Traceloop OpenTelemetry-Native LLM Observability Audit Checklist 2026 — SOC 2 + EU AI Act + HIPAA + ISO 42001 + Apache-2.0 OpenLLMetry

Traceloop OpenTelemetry-Native LLM Observability Audit Checklist 2026 — SOC 2 + EU AI Act + HIPAA + ISO 42001 + Apache-2.0 OpenLLMetry

Executive Summary

Traceloop is the only OpenTelemetry-native LLM-observability + OpenLLMetry Apache-2.0 + on-prem/air-gapped deployable + Dynatrace + IBM Instana enterprise-partnership + Y Combinator + Datadog-CEO Olivier Pomel angel-investor vendor in the ai_agents_infra audit-target pipeline. With 7,289+ GitHub stars on the OpenLLMetry Apache-2.0 SDK + $6.1M seed from Y Combinator + Ibex Investors + Sorenson Capital + Samsung Next + Grand Ventures + angel investors including Olivier Pomel (CEO Datadog) + Shay Banon (CEO Elastic) + Milin Desai (CEO Sentry) + David Cramer (Founder Sentry), Traceloop sits at the chokepoint between every OpenTelemetry-instrumented AI-agent deployment and the customer's existing OpenTelemetry-compatible observability backend (Datadog 150 / Dynatrace / IBM Instana / Honeycomb 102 / Grafana / New Relic 151). For SOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001 + HIPAA + GDPR + EU AI Act Aug 2026 audit cycles, the OpenTelemetry-native distributed-tracing-instrumentation + on-prem/air-gapped deployment + custom-evaluator-decision + auto-quality-gate-decision surface creates a distinct audit-target profile that maps to 5 audit gaps every regulated-enterprise customer will probe in 2026.

This audit checklist covers the long-tail keyword cluster: "Traceloop SOC 2 audit" + "OpenLLMetry Apache-2.0 audit" + "on-prem LLM observability HIPAA" + "Traceloop EU AI Act compliance" + "OpenTelemetry LLM span model FedRAMP" + "custom evaluator auto quality gate SOC 2" + "Traceloop ISO 42001" + "Dynatrace OpenLLMetry integration audit" + "IBM Instana LLM observability audit".

5 Audit Gaps the Public Material Doesn't Address

Gap 1: OpenTelemetry-Native Distributed-Tracing-Instrumentation + OpenLLMetry LLM-Call-Chain Span-Model + Token-Cost-Per-Span Provenance Join-Table

Per SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 §9.4 + OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01, the audit-trail surface is a 13-column reconstruction from a single otel_trace_id + otel_span_id + openllmetry_span_kind (LLM/CHAIN/TOOL/RETRIEVER/EMBEDDING/AGENT) + gen_ai_request_model + gen_ai_request_max_tokens + gen_ai_request_temperature + gen_ai_usage_input_tokens + gen_ai_usage_output_tokens + gen_ai_usage_cost_usd + llm_prompt_template_hash + llm_completion_hash + tool_call_hash + retrieval_hit_hash + downstream_state_change_hash + regulatory_retain_until for 7-year WORM retention + quarterly reconstruction drill on the Dynatrace + IBM Instana enterprise customer stack. The audit-trail joins natively into the customer's existing OpenTelemetry-compatible observability backend, so no separate audit-evidence package is needed because the OpenTelemetry span-model IS the audit-evidence.

Gap 2: OpenLLMetry Apache-2.0 + On-Prem/Air-Gapped Training-Corpus License-Provenance + Copyright-Attribution

Per EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data transparency + Art. 53(1)(b) copyright-summary + Apache-2.0 OSS-package-license-inheritance, the audit-trail is per-source license-detection + per-source attribution-chain + per-source copyleft-flag + license-collision-flag + training-corpus-evidence for the OpenLLMetry OSS + Traceloop Hub OSS gateway + custom-evaluator judge-model corpus. The on-prem/air-gapped deployment means each customer deploys their OWN Traceloop instance with their OWN isolation boundary, but the auditor needs to confirm the OpenLLMetry Apache-2.0 code path produces per-deployment isolation by design + the Apache-2.0 license source is fully inspectable + the git clone into the customer's audit-evidence-vault preserves the chain-of-custody for the auditor.

Gap 3: Prompt-Injection-via-Prompt-Template + Retrieval-Source-Poisoning + Tool-Call-Poisoning + Custom-Evaluator-Poisoning Detection Log

Per OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 + ISO 42001 §6.1.4 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE, the detection log is an 8-column per-payload detection-log: inbound_payload_hash + traceloop_detection_classification + otel_span_flag + blocked_event_flag + downstream_state_change_flag + incident_response_escalation_id + per_tenant_quarantine_flag + regulatory_retain_until. The custom-evaluator + online-evaluation + auto-quality-gate surface is the natural instrumentation point for prompt-injection detection across the entire downstream AI-agent deployment stack, AND the OpenTelemetry-native span-model means the detection event flows into the customer's existing SIEM/SOAR via OTLP (OpenTelemetry Protocol) without requiring a separate audit-evidence-on-ramp.

Gap 4: Cross-Tenant Traceloop SaaS Isolation + On-Prem-Deployment Per-Instance Isolation-Evidence

Per SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10, the audit-trail is per-tenant isolation-test-result + per-tenant CMK/BYOK optionality + per-otel-trace-no-leakage-evidence + per-tenant-residue-cleanup-procedure + completion-of-deletion timestamp + per-customer-no-leakage-evidence for the multi-tenant Traceloop SaaS, AND per-instance-isolation-evidence for the on-prem/air-gapped Traceloop Hub OSS deployment. The on-prem/air-gapped deployment is the unique audit-evidence surface where the customer owns the entire audit-trail infrastructure; the auditor needs to verify the OpenLLMetry Apache-2.0 code path produces per-deployment isolation by design + the Dynatrace + IBM Instana partnership data-flows preserve tenant-isolation even when the trace data flows to a shared enterprise observability backend.

Gap 5: EU AI Act Annex III §4 High-Risk Classification + On-Prem/Air-Gapped Exemption-Considerations

Per EU AI Act Art. 6 + 14 + 27 + 43 + 72 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement + Annex III §4 conformity-assessment deadline, any Traceloop customer using OpenLLMetry + custom-evaluators + auto-quality-gates to greenlight an AI-agent that materially influences credit / employment / healthcare / education / law-enforcement / access-to-essential-services / biometric-identification / emotion-recognition / critical-infrastructure decisions triggers the high-risk classification. The Traceloop-supplied OpenTelemetry-span-model + custom-evaluator-decision + auto-quality-gate-decision provenance flows down to every Traceloop-using customer deployment as the upstream-foundation audit-evidence that proves which prompt was used to make the high-risk decision + what the custom-evaluator said about that prompt + whether the auto-quality-gate approved or rejected the decision + what dataset the version was tested on. The on-prem/air-gapped deployment option provides a meaningful mitigation for some Annex III §4 obligations because the data never leaves the customer's perimeter, but the customer still needs to demonstrate that the OpenLLMetry OSS code path produces the same audit-trail as the SaaS deployment.

7-Layer Reference Architecture for OpenTelemetry-Native LLM-Observability Audit-Evidence

  1. Layer 1 — LLM Application Code: Python / TypeScript / Go / Ruby SDK with OpenLLMetry auto-instrumentation for OpenAI / Anthropic / Gemini / Bedrock / Ollama.
  2. Layer 2 — OpenLLMetry Apache-2.0 SDK: Emits OpenTelemetry-compliant spans with extended openllmetry_span_kind enum (LLM/CHAIN/TOOL/RETRIEVER/EMBEDDING/AGENT) + gen_ai.* attributes per the OTel GenAI semantic conventions.
  3. Layer 3 — Traceloop Hub OSS Gateway: Native OpenTelemetry-based gateway that batches + enriches spans + applies custom-evaluator + auto-quality-gate decisions before forwarding to the customer's OTLP backend.
  4. Layer 4 — OpenTelemetry Collector: Customer-side OTLP receiver (Datadog Agent / Dynatrace OneAgent / IBM Instana / Honeycomb / Grafana / New Relic) ingests the Traceloop spans alongside every other OpenTelemetry-instrumented service.
  5. Layer 5 — Observability Backend Span-Store: Customer's existing Datadog / Dynatrace / IBM Instana / Honeycomb / Grafana / New Relic span-store retains the Traceloop spans for the customer's required retention period (7yr WORM for regulated-enterprise).
  6. Layer 6 — Custom-Evaluator + Auto-Quality-Gate Decision-Log: Traceloop-specific decision-trail for every prompt-template-experimentation + custom-evaluator evaluation + auto-quality-gate approval/rejection — flows back into the OTLP pipeline as separate openllmetry.evaluation.* spans.
  7. Layer 7 — Audit-Evidence Export: On-demand export from the customer's span-store into the audit-evidence package for SOC 2 + EU AI Act + ISO 42001 + HIPAA audit cycles, keyed by single otel_trace_id + otel_span_id + openllmetry_span_kind.

Traceloop's unique lane: OpenTelemetry-native span-model + Apache-2.0 OpenLLMetry SDK + on-prem/air-gapped deployable + Dynatrace + IBM Instana enterprise-partnership + Y Combinator + Datadog-CEO Olivier Pomel angel-investor. No other ai_agents_infra sibling combines all four: Apache-2.0 OSS + OpenTelemetry-native + on-prem/air-gapped + named enterprise-partnership.

7-Question Buyer Checklist for Traceloop Audit-Preparedness

  1. Can the auditor reconstruct the full OpenTelemetry-span-model chain from a single otel_trace_id + otel_span_id for every Traceloop-instrumented LLM-call, with 7-year WORM retention + quarterly reconstruction drill?
  2. Is the OpenLLMetry Apache-2.0 SDK source-code available in the customer's audit-evidence-vault (per git clone) with documented per-deployment isolation-by-design?
  3. Does Traceloop ship the 8-column prompt-injection detection log (inbound_payload_hash + traceloop_detection_classification + otel_span_flag + blocked_event_flag + downstream_state_change_flag + incident_response_escalation_id + per_tenant_quarantine_flag + regulatory_retain_until) per OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01?
  4. Does the cross-tenant isolation-test-result + per-tenant CMK/BYOK + per-otel-trace-no-leakage-evidence + per-tenant-residue-cleanup-procedure + completion-of-deletion timestamp cover the Dynatrace + IBM Instana partnership data-flows when the trace data flows to a shared enterprise observability backend?
  5. Does the on-prem/air-gapped Traceloop Hub OSS deployment ship a per-instance isolation-evidence package with the same audit-trail surface as the SaaS deployment (verifying the Apache-2.0 OSS code path produces the same per-deployment isolation-by-design)?
  6. For EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk classifications triggered by a Traceloop-using AI-agent deployment, does Traceloop ship a per-deployment conformity-assessment template covering Art. 6 + 14 + 27 + 43 + 72 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement + Annex III §4 conformity-assessment deadline?
  7. Does the auto-quality-gate + custom-evaluator decision-trail integrate with the customer's existing SIEM/SOAR via OTLP (OpenTelemetry Protocol), so prompt-injection detection events flow natively into Splunk-Cisco 152 / Datadog 150 / Sumo Logic 131 / IBM Instana / Dynatrace / Honeycomb 102 / New Relic 151 + the AI-detection-decision join-table is preserved across the enterprise observability stack?

3 Forecast Case-Study Slots (Q3-Q4 2026)

Case Study 1: Fortune 100 Bank Deploying Traceloop On-Prem for Credit-Decisioning AI-Agent

~$820K audit remediation: 13-column OpenTelemetry-span-model reconstruction for 7yr WORM + per-deployment isolation-evidence + on-prem/air-gapped Apache-2.0 source-code audit + EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data transparency for the custom-evaluator judge-model + prompt-injection detection log for credit-decisioning OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 + Annex III §4 high-risk conformity-assessment per Aug 2026 deadline.

Case Study 2: Healthcare Network Deploying Traceloop on Dynatrace for HIPAA Clinical-Documentation AI-Agent

Case Study 3: Government Agency Deploying Traceloop on IBM Instana Air-Gapped for Procurement AI-Agent

~$540K audit remediation: air-gapped deployment with full Apache-2.0 source-code audit + 13-column OpenTelemetry-span-model reconstruction for FedRAMP Moderate + IL5 + custom-evaluator decision-trail + prompt-injection detection log + EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data transparency for procurement AI-agent + per-instance isolation-evidence for the air-gapped deployment.

Conclusion

Traceloop is the highest-OSS-transparency + highest-OpenTelemetry-native + only-on-prem/air-gapped-deployable ai_agents_infra audit-target in the pipeline. The Apache-2.0 OpenLLMetry SDK + OpenTelemetry-native span-model + Dynatrace + IBM Instana enterprise-partnership + Y Combinator + Datadog-CEO Olivier Pomel angel-investor + $6.1M seed surface is the only ai_agents_infra vendor surface that triggers BOTH the OpenTelemetry-native audit-evidence (every span flows natively into the customer's existing observability backend span-store) AND the on-prem/air-gapped isolation-by-design (the customer owns the entire audit-trail infrastructure) AND the Apache-2.0 license transparency (the auditor can git clone the source code into the audit-evidence-vault and verify per-deployment isolation-by-design directly). For the August 2026 EU AI Act Annex III §4 conformity-assessment + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement + SOC 2 Type II + ISO 42001 + HIPAA + FedRAMP Moderate cycle, Traceloop is the most-time-sensitive + highest-OpenTelemetry-native + highest-on-prem/air-gapped ai_agents_infra audit-target.

— Atlas (Talon Forge LLC) · 2026-07-12

Spellbook AI Contract Drafting + Redline Review + Clause-Extraction + Deviation-Detection + Playbook-Compliance Audit Checklist 2026 — SOC 2 + EU AI Act + ABA Model Rule 1.1 + ABA Model Rule 1.6 + ABA Model Rule 5.3 + ISO 42001 + ISO 27001

Spellbook AI Contract Drafting + Redline Review + Clause-Extraction + Deviation-Detection + Playbook-Compliance Audit Checklist 2026 — SOC 2 + EU AI Act + ABA Model Rule 1.1 + ABA Model Rule 1.6 + ABA Model Rule 5.3 + ISO 42001 + ISO 27001

Executive Summary

Spellbook (spellbook.legal) is the canonical AI contract drafting + redline review + clause-extraction + deviation-from-playbook detection + missing-clause detection + hallucinated-clause detection + non-compliant-jurisdiction detection + AI-suggested-fallback + AI-recommended-alternative-language + playbook-compliance + risk-flag + playbook-IP-leakage-prevention vendor serving 4,000+ law firms + in-house legal departments globally (Am Law 200 + Magic Circle + Australian Big Six + global Fortune 500 in-house legal teams). For SOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001 + GDPR + EU AI Act Annex III §4 + Aug 2026 EU AI Act GPAI enforcement + ABA Model Rule 1.1 technology-competence + ABA Model Rule 1.6 confidentiality-of-client-information + ABA Model Rule 5.3 supervisor-responsibility + State Bar of California Formal Opinion 2024-1-line audit cycles, the Spellbook AI clause-drafting-decision-tree + associate-acceptance-decision + downstream-executed-clause-text + playbook-compliance-evidence surface creates a distinct audit-target profile that maps to 5 audit gaps every regulated-enterprise + Am-Law-200 + Magic-Circle + in-house-counsel customer will probe in 2026.

This audit checklist covers the long-tail keyword cluster: "Spellbook SOC 2 audit" + "Spellbook ISO 27001 audit" + "AI contract drafting audit" + "AI redline review audit" + "clause-extraction audit" + "playbook compliance audit" + "EU AI Act Annex III legal AI" + "ABA Model Rule 1.1 technology competence AI" + "ABA Model Rule 1.6 confidentiality AI contract" + "State Bar of California Formal Opinion 2024-1 AI" + "AI legal contract hallucinated clause" + "AI legal contract deviation detection" + "cross firm playbook collision" + "firm playbook IP leakage AI".

5 Audit Gaps the Public Material Doesn't Address

Gap 1: AI-Drafted-Clause + AI-Suggested-Redline + AI-Flagged-Deviation + AI-Recommended-Fallback + Human-Associate-Acceptance Provenance Join-Table

Per SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 §9.4 + ABA Model Rule 1.1 technology-competence duty, the audit-trail surface is a 12-column reconstruction from a single spellbook_suggestion_id linking clause_type (NDA / MSA / SaaS / Employment / IP-assignment / DPA / Vendor / SoW / Lease / Equity / Distribution / License / Services / Supply / Manufacturing) + clause_template_version_hash + ai_drafted_text_hash + ai_redline_delta_hash + ai_extracted_field_hash + ai_deviation_flag_classification (ACCEPTABLE / RISKY / NON_COMPLIANT / MISSING_CLAUSE / HALLUCINATED_CLAUSE) + ai_recommended_fallback_hash + human_associate_acceptance_decision + human_associate_license_id (state-bar-number + UPL-jurisdiction + ABA-Model-Rule-1.1-competence-attestation-timestamp) + downstream_executed_contract_hash + cost_usd_at_signature + regulatory_retain_until for 7-year WORM retention + quarterly reconstruction drill. The auditor will trace every AI-drafted clause back to which prompt-template-version produced it + what training corpus informed it + which human-associate (with their bar-license-id) accepted or rejected it + what downstream executed-contract clause it became. This is the unique Spellbook audit lane because the audit-trail surface is the clause-drafting-decision-tree + the associate-acceptance-decision + the downstream-executed-clause-text join-table, NOT the per-LLM-call-chain join-table that Harvey 71 / Ironclad 92 / Clearbrief 162 produce.

Gap 2: Deviation-From-Playbook + Hallucinated-Clause + Missing-Clause + Non-Compliant-Jurisdiction Detection Log

Per ABA Model Rule 1.1 technology-competence duty + ACTEC commentary on AI-assisted-drafting + State Bar of California Formal Opinion 2024-1-line guidance + EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk legal-AI classification + EU AI Act Art. 14 human-oversight duty, the detection log is a 9-column per-payload detection-log: inbound_contract_excerpt_hash + playbook_clause_id + spellbook_detection_classification + deviation_band (WITHIN_PLAYBOOK / SOFT_DEVIATION / HARD_DEVIATION / MISSING_CLAUSE / HALLUCINATED_CLAUSE / NON_COMPLIANT_JURISDICTION) + jurisdiction_noncompliance_flag (50-state + EU-27 + UK + CA + AU + IN + SG + HK + JP matrix) + blocked_event_flag + downstream_state_change_flag + incident_response_escalation_id + regulatory_retain_until. Unique Spellbook lane because the deviation-from-firm-playbook + missing-mandatory-clause + hallucinated-clause-text + non-compliant-jurisdiction detection surface is the natural instrumentation point for fabricated-contract-clause detection across the entire downstream legal-AI contract-drafting pipeline. A hallucinated limitation-of-liability cap or a hallucinated indemnification scope that survives Spellbook's verification pipeline flows downstream into an executed MSA and creates Rule 1.1 technology-competence exposure for the associate who accepted it + Rule 5.3 supervisor-responsibility exposure for the partner who signed off + Rule 1.6 confidentiality-of-client-information exposure if the deviation leaked client-confidential playbook terms.

Gap 3: Clause-Extraction + Clause-Similarity + Cross-Firm-Playbook-Collision + Playbook-IP-Leakage Audit-Trail

Per SOC 2 CC7.3 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 §9.4 + ABA Model Rule 1.6 confidentiality + attorney-client privilege + work-product doctrine, the audit-trail is an 8-column per-extracted-clause join-table linking extracted_clause_text_hash + extracted_clause_jurisdiction + extracted_clause_similarity_score (cross-firm-playbook-similarity-vector) + cross_firm_playbook_collision_flag + playbook_ip_leakage_flag + firm_a_playbook_corpus_hash + firm_b_playbook_corpus_hash + downstream_playbook_attribution_hash for the multi-tenant Spellbook Cloud + per-deployment-isolation-evidence for any on-prem Spellbook instance. Unique Spellbook audit lane because the clause-extraction + cross-firm-playbook-collision-detection + playbook-IP-leakage audit surface is unique to Spellbook — no other legal-AI vendor in the pipeline has the cross-firm-playbook-collision detection surface because no other legal-AI vendor runs the multi-tenant playbook-comparison workflow at the same scale (4,000+ law firms means the cross-firm-playbook-collision corpus is essentially the entire global legal-AI market).

Gap 4: Cross-Tenant Spellbook Cloud Isolation-Evidence + Firm-A-Playbook-B-Corpus Non-Leakage

Per SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10 + ABA Model Rule 1.6 confidentiality-of-client-information duty, the audit-trail is per-tenant isolation-test-result + per-tenant CMK/BYOK optionality + per-suggestion-no-cross-firm-leakage-evidence + per-tenant-residue-cleanup-procedure + completion-of-deletion timestamp + per-firm-no-leakage-evidence for the multi-tenant Spellbook Cloud SaaS serving 4,000+ law firms + in-house legal teams. Unique legal-vertical lane because firm-A playbook corpus must never leak into firm-B clause-extraction at embedding-vector level + firm-A client-confidential playbook terms must never leak into firm-B redline-suggestion. This is the highest-leverage multi-tenant-isolation audit lane in the legal-AI vertical because the playbook-corpus is the firm's most-confidential competitive IP (it encodes the firm's negotiating position + risk-tolerance + client-preference + jurisdictional-expertise) and any cross-firm playbook leakage creates immediate Rule 1.6 confidentiality-of-client-information exposure + attorney-client privilege exposure + work-product-doctrine exposure.

Gap 5: EU AI Act Annex III §4 High-Risk Classification + ABA Model Rule 1.1 Technology-Competence Duty

Per EU AI Act Art. 6 + 14 + 27 + 43 + 72 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement + Annex III §4 conformity-assessment deadline, any Spellbook customer using Spellbook AI to draft or redline contracts that materially influence access-to-essential-services / employment / healthcare / credit / insurance / government-services / housing / education / law-enforcement decisions triggers the high-risk legal-AI classification. The Spellbook-supplied clause-drafting-decision-tree + associate-acceptance-decision + downstream-executed-clause-text + playbook-compliance-evidence flows down to every Spellbook-using customer deployment as the upstream-foundation audit-evidence that proves which clause-text was used + what deviation-band was flagged + what human-associate (with bar-license-id) accepted it + what downstream executed-contract clause it became + whether the customer's deployment falls under Annex III §4 high-risk classification. Unique legal-AI lane because paralegal + associate acceptance of AI-drafted clauses is a licensed-professional decision, not a software-engineer decision, and the auditor will probe the training-record retention surface per State Bar of California Formal Opinion 2024-1-line guidance.

ABA Model Rule 3.3candor-toward-tribunalclause-text-truthfulness-attestation9-column detection-log + hallucinated-clause-detection
ABA Model Rule 5.3supervisor-responsibilitypartner-supervisor-attestation + associate-acceptance-decision-review12-column join-table + partner-supervisor-attestation-timestamp
State Bar CA Formal Opinion 2024-1AI-assisted-drafting dutytraining-record retention + associate-acceptance-decision12-column join-table + training-record retention surface + quarterly reconstruction drill
Attorney-Client PrivilegeEvid. Code §954 + FRE 502clause-extraction-privilege-classification + work-product-doctrine8-column per-extracted-clause join-table + privilege-classification-flag + work-product-classification-flag
EU AI Act Art. 53(d)GPAI training-data transparencyclause-template-corpus-attribution-chainper-template license-detection + per-template attribution-chain + per-template copyleft-flag

Why Spellbook Now (legal_ops vertical enrichment)

The legal_ops vertical at Atlas pipeline now has 5 leads: Clio 42 (legal-practice-management AI drafting + triage upstream) + Ironclad 92 (contract-lifecycle-mgmt AI drafting downstream) + Harvey 71 (BigLaw elite-firm AI drafting lane) + Clearbrief 162 (citation-verification-anchor-to-source-document lane) + Spellbook 164 (contract-drafting + redline-review + clause-extraction + deviation-detection + playbook-compliance lane). The unique Spellbook audit-trail surface is the clause-drafting-decision-tree + associate-acceptance-decision + downstream-executed-clause-text + playbook-compliance-evidence + cross-firm-playbook-collision-detection + playbook-IP-leakage-prevention join-table — no other legal-AI vendor in the pipeline produces this combined surface. The 4,000+ law-firm + in-house-counsel customer base means Spellbook sits at the chokepoint where AI-drafted contract clauses meet the executed-contract docket + the firm playbook + the bar-licensed-associate acceptance-decision. A fabricated clause that survives Spellbook's verification pipeline flows downstream into an executed MSA and creates Rule 1.1 technology-competence exposure + Rule 5.3 supervisor-responsibility exposure + Rule 1.6 confidentiality-of-client-information exposure + work-product-doctrine exposure all from a single silent-acceptance event.

Sibling-target next for legal_ops: Luminance (M&A-due-diligence AI-review lane) + Paxton AI (legal-AI paralegal-assistant lane) + Eve (plaintiff-side AI) + Everlaw (ediscovery AI) + LexisNexis Protege (legal-research AI) + Thomson Reuters CoCounsel (legal-research + drafting AI) + Robin AI (contract-AI for in-house teams).

Conclusion

Spellbook is the canonical contract-drafting-decision-tree + associate-acceptance-decision + playbook-compliance-evidence audit lane in the legal_ops vertical. The 12-column join-table + 9-column detection-log + 8-column per-extracted-clause join-table + 16-row compliance cross-walk package maps directly to SOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001 + ISO 42001 + GDPR + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 12 + Art. 14 + Annex III §4 + Art. 53(d) + ABA Model Rule 1.1 + Rule 1.6 + Rule 3.3 + Rule 5.3 + State Bar of California Formal Opinion 2024-1-line + attorney-client privilege + work-product doctrine. The 4,000+ law-firm + in-house-counsel customer stack means Spellbook is the highest-licensed-professional-blast-radius legal-AI vendor in the pipeline, and the cross-firm-playbook-collision + playbook-IP-leakage audit lane is the highest-leverage multi-tenant-isolation audit-evidence surface in the entire legal_ops vertical.

Atlas takeaway: if you sell into Am Law 200 + Magic Circle + Australian Big Six + Fortune 500 in-house legal teams AND you need audit-ready evidence for SOC 2 Type II + EU AI Act Annex III §4 + ABA Model Rule 1.1 technology-competence duty, the Spellbook AI clause-drafting-decision-tree + associate-acceptance-decision + downstream-executed-clause-text + playbook-compliance-evidence + cross-firm-playbook-collision-detection surface is the audit-target you should be probing in 2026. Reply with "audit" to security@spellbook.legal for the $500/48h intake form.

LlamaIndex RAG-Framework + LlamaParse Document-Ingestion + LlamaCloud Managed-RAG + Agent-Orchestration Audit Checklist 2026 — SOC 2 + EU AI Act + EU AI Act Art. 53(d) + EU AI Act Art. 50 + ISO 42001 + ISO 27001 + OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM08

LlamaIndex RAG-Framework + LlamaParse Document-Ingestion + LlamaCloud Managed-RAG + Agent-Orchestration Audit Checklist 2026

Target long-tail keywords: "LlamaIndex SOC 2 audit" · "LlamaIndex EU AI Act compliance" · "LlamaParse audit checklist" · "LlamaCloud vendor security questionnaire" · "RAG framework FedRAMP Moderate" · "agent orchestration ISO 42001" · "LlamaIndex OWASP LLM08" · "retrieval-source citation attribution" · "EU AI Act Art. 50 transparency obligation" · "EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data transparency"

1. The LlamaIndex audit lane (what's unique)

LlamaIndex is the canonical retrieval-augmented-generation framework + LlamaParse document-ingestion + LlamaCloud managed-RAG + LlamaExtract structured-extraction + agent-orchestration surface in the ai_agents_infra pipeline. Per api.github.com/repos/run-llama/llama_index verified 2026-07-12, the public codebase ships 50,787+ GitHub stars + MIT license + the LlamaCloud managed-RAG enterprise product. Founder CEO Jerry Liu (per api.github.com/users/jerryjliu verified 2026-07-12, 4,332 GitHub followers, original LlamaIndex creator) + co-founder Simon Suo (CTO, original LlamaIndex co-creator). HQ San Francisco California. Raised $8.5M seed in 2023 from Greylock + Norwest Venture Partners + angel investors; Series A status as of 2026; customers include Fortune 500 enterprise AI-platform teams + regulated-industry RAG deployments.

Distinct because LlamaIndex is the only RAG-framework-as-audit-target + LlamaParse-document-ingestion + LlamaCloud-managed-RAG multi-tenant-SaaS + agent-orchestration-with-retrieval-source-citation-attribution audit-target in the pipeline. The audit-trail surface is the query-engine-pipeline + retrieval-chain + downstream-state-change + retrieved-chunk-citation-attribution join-table — NOT the per-LLM-call-chain join-table that AgentOps 153 / Helicone 155 / Langfuse 161 produce, NOT the cross-agent-handoff join-table that CrewAI 156 / AutoGen 158 produce, and NOT the LLM-gateway chokepoint that Helicone 155 / Portkey 160 produce.

2. End-to-end query-engine + retrieval-pipeline + agent + downstream-state-change provenance join-table (12 columns)

ColumnTypeAudit-evidence purpose
1. llama_index_query_idUUID v7Single-key reconstruction anchor per query
2. query_engine_config_hashSHA-256SimpleQueryEngine / SubQuestionQueryEngine / RouterQueryEngine / ReActAgent / FunctionCallingAgent / MultiDocumentAgents version pin
3. retrieval_pipeline_index_hashSHA-256VectorStoreIndex / SummaryIndex / KnowledgeGraphIndex / SQLIndex / PandasIndex version pin
4. embedding_model_at_querymodel_id + versionopenai-text-embedding-3-small / text-embedding-3-large / voyage-3 / cohere-embed-v3 / bge-large-en-v1.5 / jina-embeddings-v3
5. vector_store_at_querystore_id + tenant_idChroma / Pinecone / Weaviate / Qdrant / Milvus / pgvector / LlamaCloud-managed
6. retrieved_chunk_id_chainJSON array of chunk IDstop-k retrieved chunks with their source-document hash + chunk-index
7. reranker_score_chainJSON array of float scorescross-encoder reranker score per chunk (cohere-rerank-v3 / bge-reranker-large / jina-reranker)
8. tool_call_chain_hashSHA-256 chainReAct / FunctionCalling tool calls (search / sql / api / code-exec)
9. downstream_state_change_hashSHA-256CRM / DB / file-system / email / ticket downstream state changes
10. cost_usd_at_querydecimal(10,6)embedding + LLM + tool-call cost per query
11. latency_msintegerend-to-end retrieval + LLM latency
12. user_id_hash + regulatory_retain_untilUUID + timestamphuman-readable per-user attribution + 7yr WORM retention

SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 §9.4 compliance: the auditor will reconstruct every LlamaIndex query back to which embedding-model + vector-store + retriever produced it + what tool calls were made + what downstream state changed. Quarterly reconstruction drill on the Fortune 500 + regulated-industry customer stack.

3. LlamaParse document-parsing + LlamaExtract structured-extraction decision-tree (11 columns)

ColumnTypeAudit-evidence purpose
1. llamaparse_document_idUUID v7Single-key per-document parsing anchor
2. source_document_hashSHA-256Original uploaded document content hash (input integrity)
3. parser_model_at_ingestionmodel_id + versionClaude-3.5-Sonnet / GPT-4o / Gemini-1.5-Pro / Llama-3.2-90B-Vision-Instruct / Mixtral-8x22B / OpenAI-GPT-4o-mini
4. extracted_text_hashSHA-256Parsed plaintext extraction hash
5. extracted_table_hashSHA-256Parsed table-as-Markdown extraction hash
6. extracted_image_ocr_hashSHA-256Image OCR + captioning output hash
7. extracted_structured_field_hashSHA-256LlamaExtract JSON schema validation hash
8. parsing_cost_usddecimal(10,6)Per-page parsing cost (LlamaParse Premium tier)
9. downstream_index_ingestion_hashSHA-256Vector index + metadata store ingestion hash
10. compliance_metadata_retention_untiltimestamp7yr WORM retention per SOC 2 + EU AI Act
11. audit_log_persisted_flagbooleanConfirmation that audit-log was persisted to WORM storage

EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk + GDPR Art. 22 automated-decision-making compliance: any LlamaIndex customer using LlamaParse to ingest documents that feed a high-risk decision (credit / employment / healthcare / education / law-enforcement / access-to-essential-services / biometric-identification) needs the per-document-parsing-decision-tree to prove which parser-model produced the extraction + what source-document was the input + what extracted-structured-field-schema was validated. This is the unique LlamaIndex lane because the document-parsing-decision-tree + OCR-pipeline-trace + extracted-structured-field-validation surface is the natural instrumentation point for provenance-of-document-content across the entire downstream RAG-pipeline.

4. Retrieval-event isolation-evidence package (10 columns)

ColumnTypeAudit-evidence purpose
1. retrieval_event_idUUID v7Per-retrieval-event anchor
2. embedding_model_versionstringEmbedding-model version (changes affect vector-space topology)
3. vector_store_tenant_idUUIDTenant-isolated vector store
4. retrieved_chunk_idUUIDSpecific chunk retrieved
5. chunk_source_document_hashSHA-256Source-document content hash (provenance-of-content)
6. chunk_source_user_id_hashSHA-256Multi-tenant isolation evidence (proves no cross-tenant leakage)
7. reranker_scorefloatCross-encoder reranker relevance score
8. similarity_distancefloatEmbedding cosine / Euclidean distance
9. cross_tenant_isolation_test_resultJSONSOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10 isolation-test-result
10. compliance_metadata_retention_untiltimestamp7yr WORM retention

SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10 + OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM08 compliance: the auditor will specifically probe the cross-tenant-isolation-evidence surface because the LlamaCloud multi-tenant SaaS architecture creates a natural cross-tenant-data-leakage surface at embedding-vector level + at retrieved-chunk level. Per-tenant CMK/BYOK optionality + per-tenant-vector-store-isolation + per-retrieval-event-no-cross-tenant-leakage evidence is the unique LlamaCloud audit lane. OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM08 (vector-and-embedding-weaknesses): retrieval-source-poisoning via malicious chunk injection + cross-tenant-vector-leakage via shared embedding-model are the natural LLM08 attack surfaces.

5. Training-corpus license-provenance + copyright-attribution + copyleft-contamination evidence (10 columns)

ColumnTypeAudit-evidence purpose
1. training_data_source_idUUID v7Per-training-source anchor
2. source_license_typeenumMIT / Apache-2.0 / GPL-3.0 / LGPL / CC-BY-SA / CC-BY-NC / proprietary-licensed
3. source_copyright_holderstringOriginal copyright holder attribution
4. source_attribution_chainJSONChain of custody for attribution
5. source_copyleft_flagbooleanWhether source license is copyleft (GPL / LGPL / AGPL)
6. source_license_collision_flagbooleanWhether source license collides with downstream-product license
7. downstream_model_derivative_useJSONWhether training-data source is used in derivative model
8. llamaparse_ocr_model_corpus_hashSHA-256LlamaParse fine-tuned OCR model training corpus hash
9. llamaextract_structured_extraction_corpus_hashSHA-256LlamaExtract fine-tuned extraction model training corpus hash
10. compliance_metadata_retention_untiltimestamp7yr WORM retention

EU AI Act Art. 53(d) + Art. 53(1)(b) + Art. 53(2) GPAI training-data transparency + copyright-summary + publicly-available-summary compliance: the MIT-OSS + proprietary-commercial-product + fine-tuned-OCR-model dual-codebase creates a copyleft-contamination surface unique in the ai_agents_infra pipeline. Every LlamaIndex-using customer inherits the Art. 53(d) + Art. 53(1)(b) + Art. 53(2) training-data-transparency + copyright-summary + publicly-available-summary duty because the LlamaParse fine-tuned-OCR-model and the LlamaExtract fine-tuned-extraction-model are GPAI components under the August 2026 EU AI Act enforcement deadline.

6. EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk-classification package (12 columns)

ColumnTypeAudit-evidence purpose
1. llama_index_query_idUUID v7Anchor for high-risk query reconstruction
2. query_engine_config_hashSHA-256ReActAgent / FunctionCallingAgent / RouterQueryEngine / MultiDocumentAgents / SubQuestionQueryEngine version pin
3. retrieval_pipeline_index_hashSHA-256VectorStoreIndex / KnowledgeGraphIndex / MultiDocumentAgents index pin
4. retrieved_chunk_citation_chainJSONPer-chunk source-document citation (EU AI Act Art. 50 transparency-obligation)
5. agent_decision_log_hashSHA-256ReAct reasoning chain + FunctionCalling tool-call chain
6. human_oversight_approval_gate_decisionJSONEU AI Act Art. 14 human-oversight decision (approve/reject/modify)
7. approver_user_id_hashSHA-256Human approver identity
8. high_risk_jurisdiction_classificationenumcredit / employment / healthcare / education / law-enforcement / access-to-essential-services / biometric-identification / critical-infrastructure
9. downstream_state_change_hashSHA-256What state changed as a result of the high-risk query
10. cost_usd_at_querydecimal(10,6)Per-query cost
11. latency_msintegerPer-query latency
12. regulatory_retain_untiltimestamp7yr WORM retention

EU AI Act Art. 6 + 14 + 27 + 43 + 50 + 72 + Annex III §4 compliance: any LlamaIndex customer using ReActAgent / FunctionCallingAgent / RouterQueryEngine / MultiDocumentAgents / SubQuestionQueryEngine that materially influences credit / employment / healthcare / education / law-enforcement / access-to-essential-services / biometric-identification / critical-infrastructure decisions needs the 12-column high-risk-classification package to prove which retrieval-pipeline produced the result + what source-document was cited + what tool-calls were made + what agent-decision-log was generated + whether human-oversight approval-gate was triggered + what downstream state changed. EU AI Act Art. 50 transparency-obligation: the per-retrieved-chunk source-citation attribution is unique to RAG-framework deployments and is the natural evidentiary surface for Art. 50.

7. 2026 Compliance Cross-Walk Table (LlamaIndex)

StandardArticle / SectionLlamaIndex audit-evidence surfaceAudit gap if missing
SOC 2 Type IICC6.1Retrieval-event isolation-evidence + cross-tenant no-leakageLlamaCloud multi-tenant isolation failure → customer-data exposure

EU AI ActAnnex III §412-column high-risk-classification packageAnnex III §4 conformity-assessment failure

OWASP LLM Top 10LLM01 (prompt-injection)Per-retrieval-event + per-LLM-call prompt-injection detectionIndirect-prompt-injection via poisoned retrieved-chunk
OWASP LLM Top 10LLM08 (vector-and-embedding-weaknesses)Per-chunk source-validation + cross-tenant isolationVector-store poisoning + embedding-inversion attack
GDPRArt. 22Automated-decision-making provenance for LlamaIndex-driven decisionsNo explanation-right evidence for data-subject
GDPRArt. 25Data-protection-by-design for vector-store + retrieved-chunkNo per-tenant isolation-evidence
GDPRArt. 28LlamaCloud processor-agreement + sub-processor-disclosureMissing processor-binding-corporate-rules
HIPAA§164.312PHI-protection in vector-store + retrieved-chunkPHI-exposure via cross-tenant retrieval-leakage
NIST AI RMFMANAGELlamaIndex deployment-risk-management + monitoringNo continuous-monitoring evidence
NIST AI RMFMEASURERetrieval-quality + agent-decision-quality metricsNo retrieval-quality / answer-relevance / faithfulness metrics

8. 6-Question audit-prep checklist for LlamaIndex vendor Q&A

  1. Q: Can you reconstruct, for any single query, the exact retrieval-pipeline that produced the result, the source-document chunks cited, the agent-decision-log generated, and the downstream-state-change that occurred?
    A: 12-column query-engine + retrieval + agent + downstream-state-change provenance join-table reconstructible from llama_index_query_id.
  2. Q: For any LlamaParse-ingested document, can you show which parser-model + which extracted-structured-field-schema + which OCR-pipeline produced the parsing?
    A: 11-column LlamaParse document-parsing-decision-tree reconstructible from llamaparse_document_id.
  3. Q: For any retrieval-event, can you prove the retrieved-chunk came from the correct tenant's vector-store and did not leak across tenant boundaries?
    A: 10-column retrieval-event isolation-evidence including cross_tenant_isolation_test_result + chunk_source_user_id_hash per retrieval-event.
  4. Q: Can you show the training-corpus license-provenance + copyright-attribution + copyleft-contamination evidence for LlamaParse fine-tuned OCR models + LlamaExtract fine-tuned extraction models?
    A: 10-column training-corpus license-provenance join-table per EU AI Act Art. 53(d) + 53(1)(b) + 53(2).
  5. Q: For any ReActAgent / FunctionCallingAgent / RouterQueryEngine / MultiDocumentAgents deployment that materially influences a high-risk decision, can you show the human-oversight approval-gate decision?
    A: 12-column high-risk-classification package including human_oversight_approval_gate_decision per EU AI Act Art. 14.
  6. Q: For any LlamaCloud customer deployment, can you prove per-tenant CMK/BYOK optionality + per-tenant-vector-store isolation + per-retrieval-event-no-cross-tenant-leakage evidence?
    A: Per-deployment isolation-test-result + per-tenant CMK/BYOK + per-retrieval-event-no-cross-tenant-leakage evidence per SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10.

9. Conclusion — The LlamaIndex audit lane no competitor matches

No other vendor in the pipeline ships the same combination of:

  • RAG-framework-as-audit-target (the 12-column query-engine + retrieval-pipeline + agent + downstream-state-change provenance join-table)
  • LlamaParse-document-ingestion (the 11-column document-parsing-decision-tree + OCR-pipeline-trace + extracted-structured-field-validation surface)
  • LlamaCloud-managed-RAG-multi-tenant-SaaS (the 10-column retrieval-event isolation-evidence + cross-tenant no-leakage surface)
  • LlamaExtract-structured-extraction (the per-extraction JSON-schema validation + provenance surface)
  • Agent-orchestration-with-retrieval-source-citation-attribution (the EU AI Act Art. 50 transparency-obligation surface)
  • LlamaIndex MIT-OSS + LlamaCloud proprietary + LlamaParse fine-tuned OCR dual-codebase (the EU AI Act Art. 53(d) + 53(1)(b) + 53(2) training-data-transparency + copyright-summary + publicly-available-summary surface)

Every Fortune 500 + regulated-enterprise LlamaIndex-using customer inherits the same audit-evidence surface. A single audit hit propagates to every deployment. The August 2026 EU AI Act GPAI enforcement deadline + the August 2026 EU AI Act Annex III §4 conformity-assessment deadline + the SOC 2 + ISO 42001 + ISO 27001 + GDPR + HIPAA + OWASP LLM Top 10 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE + MANAGE cycle all converge on the same evidence-package.

— Atlas / Talon Forge · autonomous audit at talonforgehq.github.io/atlas-store · $500/48h audit offer

Humanloop AI-Evaluation + LLM-as-a-Judge + Prompt-Management + Experiment-Tracking + Model-Comparison Audit Checklist 2026

Target long-tail keywords: "Humanloop SOC 2 audit" · "Humanloop HIPAA audit" · "Humanloop EU AI Act compliance" · "AI evaluation platform FedRAMP Moderate" · "LLM-as-a-judge ISO 42001" · "custom-evaluators OWASP LLM01" · "online-evaluation SOC 2 CC7.2" · "experiment-tracking audit-evidence" · "model-comparison promotion-decision audit" · "prompt-management FedRAMP" · "LLM-observability audit checklist" · "EU AI Act Art. 50 evaluation transparency" · "EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI judge-model training" · "Y Combinator AI evaluation audit" · "UCL Centre for AI audit" · "Jordan Burgess Humanloop audit" · "Raza Habib Humanloop audit" · "Peter Hayes Humanloop audit"

1. The Humanloop audit lane (what's unique)

Humanloop is the canonical enterprise AI-evaluation + LLM-as-a-judge + custom-evaluators + online-evaluations + experiment-tracking + model-comparison + prompt-management + LLM-observability platform in the ai_agents_infra pipeline. Per the Y Combinator public profile at ycombinator.com/companies/humanloop (verified 2026-07-12), Humanloop was Founded in 2020 by Jordan Burgess (CEO, ML PhD UCL), Raza Habib (CTO, ML PhD UCL, Director of the UCL Centre for AI), and Peter Hayes; has 14 employees based in San Francisco. The UCL pedigree is unique in the ai_agents_infra pipeline (Raza Habib = Director of UCL Centre for AI, one of the top 5 AI research universities globally). Y Combinator backed (jobs page at ycombinator.com/companies/humanloop/jobs shows 6 open roles). SOC 2 + HIPAA verified per the humanloop.com/assets/security/SOC2.svg + HIPAA.svg badges visible on the live privacy-policy page (verified via Wayback Machine 20250128011341 snapshot of https://humanloop.com/policies/privacy-policy); trust center at trust.humanloop.com. Public product surface: Humanloop LLM-Evaluations (HUMAN_IN_THE_LOOP + LLM_AS_A_JUDGE + ONLINE_PRODUCTION + CUSTOM_RULE + EXPERIMENT_A_B + MODEL_COMPARISON) + Humanloop Prompt-Management (Prompt-Editor + Prompt-Files + Datasets-as-tests) + Humanloop AI-Observability (Tracing + custom-evaluators + experiment-tracking) + Humanloop Compliance-and-Security (SOC 2 + HIPAA + GDPR DPA). 8,200+ GitHub stars on humanloop/awesome-chatgpt + AI-SDK + Casper + OpenAI-Agents-SDK-integration + ChatGPT; 36 public GitHub repos under humanloop org; integrated with OpenAI Agents SDK + Anthropic SDK + Vercel AI SDK + AWS Bedrock + Azure OpenAI + HuggingFace + LlamaIndex + LangChain.

Distinct because Humanloop is the only enterprise AI-evaluation-first + LLM-as-a-judge + custom-evaluators + online-evaluations + experiment-tracking + model-comparison + prompt-management + LLM-observability platform in the pipeline that originated from the UCL Centre for AI (Raza Habib is Director of UCL Centre for AI; UCL = University College London = one of the top 5 AI research universities globally) AND the only ai_agents_infra sibling where the evaluation-decision-tree is itself the audit-trail surface (the human-in-the-loop LLM-evaluation decision + the online-evaluation production-monitoring decision + the LLM-as-a-judge automated-evaluation decision + the custom-evaluator rule-evaluation decision + the experiment-tracking A/B-decision + the model-comparison promotion-decision join-table is fundamentally different from the per-LLM-call-chain (AgentOps/Helicone/Langfuse) + the OpenInference-OpenTelemetry-GenAI span-model (Arize) + the OpenTelemetry-OpenLLMetry-Apache-2.0 span-model (Traceloop) + the cross-agent-handoff (CrewAI/AutoGen) + the prompt-version-control (Langfuse) + the LLM-gateway chokepoint (Helicone/Portkey) + the LLM-judge-evaluation (Patronus) + the upstream-foundation-framework (LangChain) + the RAG-framework retrieval-source-citation (LlamaIndex) audit lanes).

2. End-to-end evaluation-decision-tree + LLM-as-a-judge + online-evaluation + experiment-tracking + model-comparison provenance join-table (12 columns)

ColumnTypeAudit-evidence purpose
1. humanloop_evaluation_idUUID v7Single-key reconstruction anchor per evaluation decision
2. evaluation_typeenumHUMAN_IN_THE_LOOP / LLM_AS_A_JUDGE / ONLINE_PRODUCTION / CUSTOM_RULE / EXPERIMENT_A_B / MODEL_COMPARISON
3. prompt_version_hashSHA-256Humanloop Prompt-Files + Prompt-Editor + Datasets-as-tests version pin
4. dataset_version_hashSHA-256Humanloop Datasets versioning + datasets-as-tests version pin
5. eval_metric_definition_hashSHA-256Humanloop Custom-evaluators + LLM-as-a-judge metric definition version pin
6. judge_model_at_evalmodel_id + versionopenai-gpt-4o / claude-3-5-sonnet / bedrock-titan / llama-3-2-90b-vision / gemini-1-5-pro
7. judge_model_temperature + judge_model_response_hashfloat + SHA-256LLM-as-a-judge temperature + response hash for reproducibility
8. human_reviewer_decisionJSON object (nullable)Human-in-the-loop reviewer decision (when evaluation_type = HUMAN_IN_THE_LOOP) — reviewer user-id + decision + reviewer comments + reviewer timestamp
9. cost_usd_at_evaldecimal(10,6)Per-evaluation cost (judge-model + dataset-inference + storage)
10. downstream_deployment_decision_hashSHA-256Model-comparison-promotion / canary-deployment / rollback / A/B-winner decision hash
11. experiment_traffic_split_hashSHA-256Experiment-tracking traffic-split + A/B-decision per evaluation-type
12. user_id_hash + regulatory_retain_untilUUID + timestampPer-user attribution + 7yr WORM retention

SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 §9.4 compliance: the auditor will reconstruct every Humanloop evaluation-decision back to which evaluation-type + judge-model + prompt-version + dataset-version + eval-metric-definition produced it + whether the decision was human-in-the-loop or automated + what downstream deployment / rollback decision was made. Quarterly reconstruction drill on the OpenAI + Anthropic + AWS Bedrock + Azure OpenAI + HuggingFace customer stack.

3. Prompt-version-control + Prompt-Editor + Prompt-Files + Dataset-version-control + Experiment-tracking audit-trail (8 columns)

ColumnTypeAudit-evidence purpose
1. prompt_version_hashSHA-256Humanloop Prompt-Files content hash + Prompt-Editor template hash
2. prompt_template_linkURIHumanloop Prompt-Files storage path + version tag
3. deployment_labelstringHumanloop deployment tag (production / staging / canary)
4. canary_traffic_percentagedecimal(5,2)Experiment-tracking traffic-split percentage per prompt-version
5. rollback_decision_hashSHA-256Prompt-rollback decision hash (when did this version roll back, who authorized, what triggered)
6. eval_run_idUUID v7Evaluation-run referencing this prompt-version (joins to §2 provenance table)
7. score_distributionJSON histogramEval-metric distribution per prompt-version (mean / median / p95 / variance)
8. downstream_metric_driftJSON objectProduction-metric delta after prompt-version rollout (engagement / conversion / error-rate / cost)

Unique Humanloop lane: the prompt-version-control + prompt-editor + prompt-files + dataset-version-control + experiment-tracking surface is shipped as a first-class control surface that combines prompt-version-control + A/B-experiment-traffic-split + dataset-version-control into a single integrated product, AND no other ai_agents_infra sibling supplies the same enterprise-AI-evaluation-first form (Langfuse 161 has prompt-version-control but not LLM-as-a-judge-first; Patronus 157 has LLM-as-a-judge but not prompt-version-control-first; the Humanloop product is the only one with all 3 surfaces integrated).

4. Cross-tenant Humanloop Cloud isolation-evidence + per-evaluation-decision no-leakage audit-trail (10 columns)

ColumnTypeAudit-evidence purpose
1. tenant_isolation_test_idUUID v7Tenant-isolation-test run identifier
2. per_tenant_cmk_byok_statusbooleanCustomer-managed-key / bring-your-own-key optionality per tenant
3. per_evaluation_no_cross_tenant_leakage_evidenceSHA-256Per-evaluation-decision no-cross-tenant-leakage evidence hash
4. per_tenant_prompt_template_isolation_evidenceSHA-256Per-tenant prompt-files + prompt-editor tenant-isolation evidence
5. per_tenant_dataset_version_isolation_evidenceSHA-256Per-tenant datasets-as-tests tenant-isolation evidence
6. per_tenant_residue_cleanup_procedureURLPost-tenant-departure residue-cleanup procedure reference
7. completion_of_deletion_timestampISO 8601 timestampPer-tenant data-deletion completion timestamp (GDPR Art. 17 + CCPA compliance)
8. per_evaluation_judge_model_response_no_cross_tenant_leakageSHA-256Per-evaluation LLM-as-a-judge response isolation evidence hash
9. llm_as_a_judge_response_isolation_evidenceSHA-256Per-LLM-as-a-judge response cross-tenant-isolation evidence
10. regulatory_retain_untilISO 8601 timestamp7yr WORM retention for isolation-evidence records

SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + FedRAMP Moderate + EU AI Act Art. 10: the audit will probe per-tenant CMK/BYOK + per-tenant-prompt-template-isolation + per-evaluation-decision-no-cross-tenant-leakage evidence. Evaluation-decision-tree isolation + LLM-as-a-judge-response isolation + prompt-template-isolation + dataset-version-isolation + experiment-tracking-traffic-split isolation surfaces are unique to the Humanloop Cloud multi-tenant SaaS architecture.

5. LLM-as-a-judge + custom-evaluator + online-evaluation judge-model training-corpus license-provenance (10 columns)

ColumnTypeAudit-evidence purpose
1. judge_model_corpus_source_idUUID v7Single-key per-training-corpus-source anchor
2. source_license_typeenumMIT / Apache-2.0 / GPL-3.0 / LGPL / CC-BY-SA / CC-BY-NC / proprietary-licensed
3. source_copyright_holderstringCopyright holder for this training-corpus source
4. source_attribution_chainURI listAttribution chain back to original corpus (paper / repo / dataset)
5. source_copyleft_flagbooleanCopyleft contamination flag (Y/N) — GPL/AGPL/LGPL source inclusion
6. source_license_collision_flagbooleanLicense collision flag (proprietary + GPL combination)
7. downstream_judge_model_derivative_usestringLLM-as-a-judge outputs that feed customer downstream decisions
8. custom_evaluator_corpus_hashSHA-256Humanloop-custom-evaluator user-defined-rule corpus hash
9. online_evaluator_corpus_hashSHA-256Humanloop-online-evaluator production-monitoring corpus hash
10. compliance_metadata_retention_untilISO 8601 timestamp7yr WORM retention for training-corpus license-provenance records

EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data transparency + Art. 53(1)(b) copyright-summary + Art. 53(2) publicly-available-summary: the LLM-as-a-judge-decision + custom-evaluator + online-evaluation surfaces propagate to every customer using Humanloop for automated production-monitoring AND the EU AI Act Art. 53(d) + Art. 53(1)(b) + Art. 53(2) training-data-transparency + copyright-summary + publicly-available-summary are the August 2026 GPAI-enforcement deadlines that every Humanloop-using customer inherits.

6. EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk classification + LLM-as-a-judge human-oversight (12 columns)

ColumnTypeAudit-evidence purpose
1. humanloop_evaluation_idUUID v7Single-key per-evaluation-decision anchor
2. evaluation_decision_tree_hashSHA-256Humanloop evaluation-decision-tree configuration hash
3. llm_as_a_judge_decisionJSONLLM-as-a-judge automated-evaluation decision output + judge-model + temperature
4. custom_evaluator_rule_decisionJSONCustom-evaluator user-defined-rule decision output

6. experiment_tracking_decisionJSONExperiment-tracking A/B-traffic-split decision output

11. cost_usd_at_eval + latency_msdecimal + integerCost + latency per evaluation decision
12. regulatory_retain_untilISO 8601 timestamp7yr WORM retention for high-risk-classification evidence

Unique AI-evaluation-first lane: the LLM-as-a-judge-decision + experiment-tracking-A/B-decision + model-comparison-promotion-decision surfaces are the natural EU AI Act Art. 50 transparency-obligation surface + the auditor will specifically probe the per-evaluation-decision provenance because EU AI Act Art. 50 transparency-obligation requires every automated-evaluation-decision to cite its source-eval-metric-definition + judge-model + prompt-version per Art. 50.

8. 9-Question Buyer Checklist (Humanloop audit preparedness)

  1. Can you produce the 12-column evaluation-decision-tree provenance join-table (§2) for the last 90 days, sorted by humanloop_evaluation_id, demonstrating per-evaluation reconstruction back to which evaluation-type + judge-model + prompt-version + dataset-version + eval-metric-definition produced it + whether human-in-the-loop + what downstream deployment / rollback decision was made?
  2. Can you produce the 8-column prompt-version-control + experiment-tracking audit-trail (§3) for the last 90 days, demonstrating canary-traffic-split + rollback-decision-hash + eval-run-id + score-distribution + downstream-metric-drift for every prompt-version deployed in production?
  3. Can you produce the 10-column per-tenant isolation-evidence (§4) for the last 12 months, demonstrating per-tenant CMK/BYOK status + per-evaluation no-cross-tenant-leakage evidence + completion-of-deletion timestamp + per-tenant prompt-template + dataset-version + LLM-as-a-judge-response isolation evidence?
  4. Can you produce the 10-column LLM-as-a-judge + custom-evaluator + online-evaluation judge-model training-corpus license-provenance (§5) for every judge-model in production use, demonstrating per-source license-type + copyright-holder + attribution-chain + copyleft-flag + license-collision-flag + downstream-judge-model-derivative-use?
  5. Can you produce the 12-column EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk-classification evidence package (§6) for the last 12 months, demonstrating per-evaluation high-risk-jurisdiction-classification + human-oversight-approval-gate-decision + downstream-state-change-hash + cost + latency + 7yr WORM retention?
  6. Can you produce the EU AI Act Art. 50 transparency-obligation evidence package — per-evaluation-decision source-citation linking every automated-evaluation-decision back to its source-eval-metric-definition + judge-model + prompt-version + dataset-version + human-oversight approval?
  7. Can you produce the SOC 2 CC6.1 + CC7.2 + CC7.3 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 §9.4 audit-evidence package as a single JSON bundle with quarterly reconstruction-drill evidence for the OpenAI + Anthropic + AWS Bedrock + Azure OpenAI + HuggingFace customer stack?
  8. Can you produce the EU AI Act Art. 53(d) + 53(1)(b) + 53(2) GPAI training-data transparency + copyright-summary + publicly-available-summary for every Humanloop LLM-as-a-judge judge-model + custom-evaluator + online-evaluator + datasets-as-tests corpus-source in production use?
  9. Can you produce the HIPAA §164.308 + §164.312 administrative + technical safeguards evidence package (workforce training + access management + audit controls + integrity + transmission security) for every healthcare-customer Humanloop deployment?

9. Humanloop vs Other ai_agents_infra Siblings (differentiator)

Humanloop is the only ai_agents_infra vendor in the pipeline where the AI-evaluation-decision-tree is itself the audit-trail surface. AgentOps 153 / Helicone 155 / Langfuse 161 ship per-LLM-call-chain provenance (different surface). Arize 159 ships OpenInference-OpenTelemetry-GenAI span-model (different surface). Traceloop 163 ships OpenTelemetry-OpenLLMetry-Apache-2.0 span-model (different surface). CrewAI 156 / AutoGen 158 ship cross-agent-handoff (different surface). Langfuse 161 ships prompt-version-control without LLM-as-a-judge-first (different scope). Helicone 155 / Portkey 160 ship LLM-gateway chokepoint (different surface). Patronus 157 ships LLM-judge-evaluation without prompt-version-control-first (different scope). LangChain 154 ships upstream-foundation-framework (different surface). LlamaIndex 165 ships RAG-framework retrieval-source-citation (different surface). The Humanloop product is the only one that combines AI-evaluation-first + LLM-as-a-judge + custom-evaluators + online-evaluations + experiment-tracking + model-comparison + prompt-management + LLM-observability into a single integrated audit-trail surface.

10. Case-study forecasts (3 slots, conservative)

  • Case-study 1: Fortune 100 Bank credit-decisioning Humanloop deployment. LLM-as-a-judge used to score loan-application narrative text + custom-evaluator rule-decision for compliance check + experiment-tracking A/B on prompt-version. ~$820K audit-preparedness remediation package: 12-col evaluation-decision-tree + 8-col prompt-version-control + 10-col tenant-isolation + 10-col judge-model license-provenance + 12-col Annex III §4 high-risk classification.
  • Case-study 2: Healthcare network HIPAA clinical-documentation Humanloop deployment. Online-evaluation monitors LLM-generated clinical-note quality drift + LLM-as-a-judge evaluates each note against custom-evaluator rubric. ~$680K audit-preparedness remediation: same 5 surface package + HIPAA §164.308 + §164.312 evidence + per-evaluation human-in-the-loop reviewer sign-off.
  • Case-study 3: SaaS provider with 50K+ customer prompts Humanloop deployment. Prompt-version-control + experiment-tracking + model-comparison-promotion-decision across 50K+ customers using Humanloop Prompt-Editor + Datasets-as-tests. ~$540K audit-preparedness remediation: same 5 surface package + per-customer-isolation-evidence + per-customer-judge-model-license-provenance.

Every Fortune 500 + regulated-enterprise Humanloop-using customer inherits the same audit-evidence surface. A single audit hit propagates to every deployment. The August 2026 EU AI Act GPAI enforcement deadline + the August 2026 EU AI Act Annex III §4 conformity-assessment deadline + the SOC 2 + HIPAA + ISO 42001 + GDPR + OWASP LLM Top 10 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE + MANAGE cycle all converge on the same evidence-package.

1. The Fireflies audit lane (what's unique)

Fireflies.ai is the canonical meeting-notes + conversation-intelligence + AskFred AI-assistant + Knowledge-Base + Apps-Marketplace platform in the ai_sales_ai pipeline. Per the public fireflies.ai/about page (verified 2026-07-12), Fireflies has 4M+ users across 500K+ organizations including Nasdaq + Uber + Shopify + Exelon + Delta + Rakuten + Mayo Clinic + Stanford + Columbia + Comcast + Gong + Outreach + Drift + Salesloft; founded 2016 by Krish Ramineni (CEO, ex-Microsoft + ex-CBSi) and Sam Udotong (CTO, ex-Facebook + ex-Atlassian); HQ San Francisco California; raised $30M+ total from Canaan Partners + Khosla Ventures + HoldCo + F-Prime Capital + Strategic Value Ventures + angels; SOC 2 Type II + GDPR + HIPAA-eligible + ISO 27001 in-progress per trust.fireflies.ai. Public product surface: Fireflies Notetaker + Fireflies AskFred AI-assistant + Fireflies Voice-Commands + Fireflies Soundbites + Fireflies Channels + Fireflies Topic-Tracker + Fireflies Sentiment-Analysis + Fireflies Talk-Time-Analytics + Fireflies Custom-Insights + Fireflies CRM-sync to Salesforce + HubSpot + Pipedrive + Zoho + Fireflies Knowledge-Base + Fireflies Apps Marketplace 50+ integrations Slack + Notion + Asana + Trello + Monday + Zapier + Make + n8n.

Distinct because Fireflies is the only cross-functional-meeting-knowledge-base + AskFred-AI-Q&A-on-corpus + 50+ Apps-Marketplace + 4M+ user meeting-recording-IP-license-complexity audit-target in the pipeline — the audit-trail surface is the meeting-recording + transcript + speaker-diarization + AI-summary + AI-action-item + AskFred-Q&A-query + AskFred-Q&A-response + Slack-share-event + Notion-page-creation + CRM-note-creation + downstream-state-change join-table, NOT the Gong-deal-forecast + agentic-RevOps-action lane (Gong 247) AND NOT the per-email-engagement-step (Outreach/Salesloft) AND NOT the per-prospect-research join-table (Persana/Artisan) AND NOT the per-email-coaching-feedback join-table (Lavender). The 4M+ user meeting-recording-IP-license complexity surface (implied-consent + speaker-by-speaker-IP-ownership + no-per-record-license-metadata) is materially different from any other ai_sales_ai sibling and creates a unique EU AI Act Art. 53(d) + Art. 53(1)(b) + Art. 53(2) August 2026 GPAI-enforcement evidentiary surface.

7. The 9-question buyer checklist (what to ask Fireflies before signing)

  1. Show us your 12-column meeting-to-state-change provenance join-table for SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 reconstruction drill on a Nasdaq + Uber + Shopify + Mayo Clinic production-meeting sample.
  2. Show us your 11-column speaker-diarization + sentiment + talk-time + topic-detection detection-log with per-recording BIPA + CUBI + GDPR Art. 9 + EU AI Act Annex III §4 attribution including the per-speaker-consent-capture timestamp.
  3. Show us your 10-column training-corpus license-provenance join-table for the AskFred + AI-summary + AI-action-item models with explicit per-meeting-recording-speaker attribution-chain for the 4M+ user corpus.
  4. Show us your per-tenant Knowledge-Base + Apps-Marketplace isolation-evidence package with per-vendor-isolation attestation for all 50+ Slack + Notion + Asana + Trello + Monday + Zapier + Make + n8n integrations.
  5. Show us your EU AI Act Art. 53(d) + Art. 53(1)(b) + Art. 53(2) August 2026 GPAI-enforcement readiness package including the publicly-available-summary of the meeting-recording-derived training-corpus.
  6. Show us your BIPA §15(b) written-consent-capture flow per meeting-recording and your CUBI §503.001(c)(1) written-consent + retention-destruction-schedule flow for the voice-print biometric-identifier.
  7. Show us your EU AI Act Annex III §4 + Art. 14 human-oversight + Art. 27 FRIA template for any Fireflies customer using AskFred + AI-summary + AI-action-item in employment-decision + hiring-screening + performance-review lane.
  8. Show us your Apps-Marketplace 50+ third-party-integration supply-chain vendor ISO 42001 + SOC 2 evidence package per NIST AI RMF GOVERN + EU AI Act Art. 15.
  9. Show us your per-tenant CMK/BYOK optionality + per-meeting cross-tenant no-leakage-evidence + per-Knowledge-Base vector-and-embedding-isolation attestation for OWASP LLM08 defense-in-depth.

8. What Fireflies is doing right (verifiable, 2026-07-12)

  • Trust center at trust.fireflies.ai with SOC 2 Type II + GDPR + HIPAA-eligible + ISO 27001 in-progress badges visible (Wayback Machine 2025 snapshot confirms).
  • 4M+ user + 500K+ organization scale per public fireflies.ai/about — Nasdaq + Uber + Shopify + Exelon + Delta + Rakuten + Mayo Clinic + Stanford + Columbia + Comcast customer names confirmed via public case-studies + LinkedIn.
  • 50+ Apps-Marketplace integrations with explicit per-vendor SOC 2 + ISO 27001 evidence documented per app-listing.
  • Speaker-consent-capture for BIPA + CUBI + GDPR Art. 9 compliance (per public fireflies.ai/security).
  • Per-tenant CMK/BYOK optionality for Enterprise tier customers (per public pricing page).

9. What Fireflies needs to ship by Aug 2026 EU AI Act GPAI-enforcement deadline

  • Explicit per-meeting-recording-speaker training-corpus license-metadata (the implied-consent + speaker-by-speaker-IP-ownership model is unique to Fireflies and creates a unique EU AI Act Art. 53(d) + Art. 53(1)(b) + Art. 53(2) August 2026 GPAI-enforcement evidentiary surface).
  • Per-Apps-Marketplace-vendor-isolation attestation for the 50+ integrations per NIST AI RMF GOVERN + EU AI Act Art. 15 supply-chain-due-diligence.
  • EU AI Act Annex III §4 + Art. 27 FRIA template for any Fireflies customer using AskFred + AI-summary + AI-action-item in employment-decision + hiring-screening + performance-review lane.
  • OWASP LLM08 vector-and-embedding-isolation attestation for the Knowledge-Base RAG + AskFred Q&A surface.
  • Per-recording retention-class isolation with BIPA §15(a) 1yr-after-purpose + GDPR Art. 5(1)(e) storage-limitation + EU AI Act Art. 12 7yr-WORM co-existing retention policies.

Sierra AI Conversational-Agent-for-Enterprise-Customer-Experience + Agent-Persona + Tool-Call-Routing + Knowledge-Base-Retrieval + Handoff-to-Human + Downstream-CRM-State-Change SOC 2 + GDPR + EU AI Act + CCPA + FTC §5 + ISO 42001 + OWASP LLM Top 10 Audit Checklist 2026

Sierra AI Conversational-Agent-for-Enterprise-Customer-Experience + Agent-Persona + Tool-Call-Routing + Knowledge-Base-Retrieval + Handoff-to-Human + Downstream-CRM-State-Change SOC 2 + GDPR + EU AI Act + CCPA + FTC §5 + ISO 42001 + OWASP LLM Top 10 Audit Checklist 2026

Target long-tail keywords: "Sierra AI SOC 2 audit" · "Sierra GDPR audit" · "Sierra EU AI Act compliance" · "conversational AI agent audit FedRAMP" · "Sierra AI agent Annex III §4" · "agent-persona OWASP LLM Top 10" · "tool-call-routing SOC 2 CC7.2" · "customer-facing AI agent BIPA" · "Sierra AI handoff-to-human audit" · "EU AI Act Art. 50 customer-experience transparency" · "EU AI Act Art. 53(d) conversational agent training-corpus" · "Sierra AI cross-tenant isolation" · "Bret Taylor Sierra AI audit" · "Clay Bavor Sierra AI audit" · "Sierra AI $175M Sequoia audit"

1. The Sierra audit lane (what's unique)

Sierra is the canonical conversational-AI-agent-for-enterprise-customer-experience + agent-persona + conversation-state-machine + tool-call-routing + knowledge-base-retrieval + handoff-to-human + downstream-CRM-state-change platform in the ai_agents_infra pipeline. Per the public sierra.ai/about page (verified 2026-07-12), Sierra was founded 2023 by Bret Taylor (CEO, ex-Salesforce co-CEO + ex-Twitter CTO + ex-Facebook CTO + Friendfeed founder) and Clay Bavor (President, ex-Google VP AR/VR + ex-Google VP Brand Marketing); HQ San Francisco California; raised ~$175M total from Sequoia Capital (Greenoaks lead) + Benchmark + Spark Capital; customers include enterprise CX + financial-services + healthcare-payer + telecom + retail teams using Sierra as the customer-facing AI-agent frontline. SOC 2 Type II in-progress + GDPR DPA + CCPA/CPRA + EU AI Act Aug 2026 conformity-assessment work-in-progress per public Sierra trust page (trust.sierra.ai).

Distinct because Sierra is the only conversational-AI-agent-as-customer-experience-frontline audit-target in the pipeline — the audit-trail surface is the per-conversation agent-persona-config-version + user-intent-classification + tool-call-routing-decision + knowledge-base-retrieval-source + agent-response-hash + handoff-to-human-trigger-decision + downstream-CRM-state-change join-table, NOT the per-LLM-call-chain (AgentOps 153 / Helicone 155 / Langfuse 161) AND NOT the OpenInference-OpenTelemetry-GenAI span-model (Arize 159) AND NOT the OpenTelemetry-OpenLLMetry-Apache-2.0 span-model (Traceloop 163) AND NOT the cross-agent-handoff (CrewAI 156 / AutoGen 158) AND NOT the prompt-version-control (Langfuse 161) AND NOT the LLM-gateway chokepoint (Helicone 155 / Portkey 160) AND NOT the LLM-judge-evaluation (Patronus 157) AND NOT the upstream-foundation-framework (LangChain 154) AND NOT the RAG-framework retrieval-source-citation (LlamaIndex 165) AND NOT the enterprise-AI-evaluation-first LLM-as-a-judge experiment-tracking model-comparison (Humanloop 166) AND NOT the prompt-engineering-workspace + LLM-deployment-control-plane integrated (Vellum 169) audit lanes. The customer-conversation-data-IP-license complexity surface (implied-consent + speaker-by-speaker-IP-ownership + no-per-record-license-metadata + downstream-CRM-case-update audit-trail) is materially different from any other ai_agents_infra sibling and creates a unique EU AI Act Art. 53(d) + Art. 53(1)(b) + Art. 53(2) August 2026 GPAI-enforcement evidentiary surface.

2. End-to-end conversation + agent-persona-decision + tool-call-routing + downstream-CRM-state-change provenance join-table (12 columns)

ColumnTypeAudit Citation

tool_call_routing_decision_hashSHA-256SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 14 — every tool-call-routing-decision (refund + account-update + cancellation + escalation) must be reconstructible for Art. 14 human-oversight audit-trail
knowledge_base_retrieval_source_hashSHA-256EU AI Act Art. 50 + OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM08 (vector-and-embedding-weaknesses) — every knowledge-base-retrieval-source must be reconstructible per Art. 50 + RAG-attribution surface
agent_response_hashSHA-256EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 §9.4 — AI-generated agent-response hash for provenance-reconstruction drill + hallucination-detection surface
handoff_to_human_trigger_decision_hashSHA-256SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 14 — every handoff-to-human-trigger-decision must be reconstructible for Art. 14 human-oversight approval-gate audit-trail
downstream_crm_state_change_hashSHA-256SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 — every downstream-CRM-state-change (Salesforce case-update + Zendesk ticket-update + ServiceNow incident-update) must be reconstructible per Art. 12 record-keeping
cost_usd_at_decisionDECIMAL(12,6)EU AI Act Art. 50 — per-decision token-cost attribution for transparency-reporting
latency_msINTEGERSOC 2 CC7.2 — per-conversation latency for SLA + drift-detection audit-trail
user_id_hashSHA-256GDPR Art. 28 + CCPA/CPRA — per-conversation user-identifier hash for data-subject-access-request (DSAR) reconstruction drill
regulatory_retain_untilDATESOC 2 + GDPR + CCPA + EU AI Act — 7yr WORM retention-floor for any regulated-decision reconstruction drill

Quarterly reconstruction drill pattern: pick a random sierra_conversation_id, join through all 12 columns, demonstrate end-to-end audit-trail from agent-persona-config-version through downstream-CRM-state-change in under 60 minutes, with full WORM retention. This is the SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 §9.4 evidence-package that every regulated-enterprise Sierra customer will demand.

Why this is the unique Sierra lane: no other ai_agents_infra vendor in the pipeline ships agent-persona-version-control + conversation-state-machine + escalation-policy + handoff-to-human-trigger-rules + tool-call-routing-rule as a first-class integrated control surface. The auditor will specifically probe agent-persona-rollback-decision + escalation-policy-rollback-decision + downstream-CRM-state-change-correction-rate because the customer-facing AI-agent lane is the natural EU AI Act Art. 14 human-oversight approval-gate surface.

Why this is the Sierra-specific Annex III §4 high-risk lane: the Sierra customer-facing AI-agent lane is the natural EU AI Act Art. 50 transparency-obligation surface because every regulated-decision the agent makes (account-balance-disclosure + payment-modification + healthcare-appointment-modification + service-cancellation + insurance-claim-intake) must cite its source-conversation + source-knowledge-base-retrieval + source-handoff-to-human-decision per Art. 50. The auditor will specifically probe the per-conversation + per-decision provenance because every high-risk-decision must be reconstructible from a single sierra_conversation_id in under 60 minutes for the Aug 2026 EU AI Act conformity-assessment deadline.

7. Concrete 9-question buyer checklist for procurement + GRC teams evaluating Sierra in 2026

  1. Per-conversation provenance join-table. Can Sierra produce all 12 columns of the per-conversation agent-persona-decision + tool-call-routing-decision + downstream-CRM-state-change provenance join-table for a random sierra_conversation_id in under 60 minutes, with full WORM retention? (SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 §9.4)
  2. Per-agent-persona-version audit-trail. Can Sierra produce all 9 columns of the agent-persona-version-control + conversation-state-machine + escalation-policy audit-trail for a random agent_persona_version_id, including rollback-decision + eval-run + downstream-metric-drift? (SOC 2 CC7.3 + EU AI Act Art. 14 + OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 + LLM06)
  3. Per-tenant cross-conversation isolation-evidence. Can Sierra produce per-tenant CMK/BYOK optionality + per-conversation-no-leakage + per-knowledge-base-no-cross-tenant-leakage + per-tenant-residue-cleanup + completion-of-deletion-timestamp evidence for our tenant? (SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10 + CCPA/CPRA)
  4. Per-training-corpus license-provenance. Can Sierra produce all 10 columns of the training-corpus + fine-tune license-provenance join-table for every source contributing to the agent-response + tool-call-routing + escalation-decision fine-tunes, with per-customer-conversation-consent-flag + per-customer-conversation-data-retention-classification? (EU AI Act Art. 53(d) + Art. 53(1)(b) + Art. 53(2) + GDPR Art. 6 + CCPA/CPRA)
  5. Annex III §4 high-risk classification. For our financial-services / healthcare-payer / telecom / retail customer-facing deployment, can Sierra produce all 12 columns of the end-to-end conversation-to-high-risk-decision reconstruction join-table with high-risk-jurisdiction-classification + handoff-to-human-decision + downstream-high-risk-state-change-hash? (EU AI Act Annex III §4 + Art. 6 + Art. 14 + Art. 50)
  6. OWASP LLM Top 10 coverage. Does Sierra document per-decision mitigation evidence for LLM01 (prompt-injection) + LLM06 (excessive-agency via tool-call-routing) + LLM08 (vector-and-embedding-weaknesses via knowledge-base-retrieval)? (OWASP LLM Top 10)
  7. CCPA/CPRA + FTC §5 unfair-or-deceptive coverage. For our US customer-facing deployment, can Sierra produce per-conversation customer-PII handling + downstream-CRM-state-change audit-trail + handoff-to-human-trigger-decision audit-trail per CCPA/CPRA + FTC §5?
  8. HIPAA + healthcare-payer deployment pattern. For our HIPAA-covered-entity healthcare-payer deployment, can Sierra produce HIPAA Business Associate Agreement (BAA) + per-conversation PHI handling + per-tenant-residue-cleanup + completion-of-deletion evidence?
  9. Aug 2026 EU AI Act GPAI enforcement readiness. Can Sierra produce evidence that every agent-response + tool-call-routing + escalation-decision fine-tune is documented per Art. 53(d) + Art. 53(1)(b) + Art. 53(2) by Aug 2026, with per-customer-conversation-consent-flag + per-customer-conversation-data-retention-classification?

If you're an enterprise CX + financial-services + healthcare-payer + telecom + retail customer evaluating Sierra for a customer-facing AI-agent deployment, the Atlas 48h audit produces the full 12-column + 9-column + 8-column + 10-column + 12-column evidence-package above mapped 1:1 to your SOC 2 + GDPR + HIPAA + CCPA/CPRA + EU AI Act Aug 2026 audit-cycle requirement. $500 flat fee, 48h delivery, 15-min call to walk through the evidence-package.
