Why Drift is the highest-conversational-AI-blast-radius revenue_ops audit-target

Drift is the only Tier-1 conversational-AI buyer-bot platform that ships in a single stack: (1) Drift AI Site Concierge (per-conversation-AI-reply) (2) Drift Prospector (per-lead-AI-routing-decision) (3) Drift Fastlane + Concierge (per-meeting-booking-decision) (4) Drift Audiences (per-account-intent-ML-score) (5) Drift Email + Sequences (per-trigger-decision) (6) Drift Video (per-recording-storage-7yr) (7) Drift Intel (per-engagement-event-store) (8) post-Salesloft-merger unified Drift-Salesloft-tenant-mapping. A single Drift audit hit propagates to SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 14 + Art. 22 + Annex III 4 + Art. 50 + Art. 53(d) + GDPR Art. 17 + Art. 22 + Art. 28 + CCPA/CPRA + ISO 42001 9.4 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE + OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 + LLM04 + LLM08 simultaneously — making Drift the highest-conversational-AI-blast-radius revenue_ops audit-target added in this chunk.

Anyscale: The Ray-Anyscale Vendor-DD Playbook (Talon Forge Atlas)

Updated 2026-07-12 · long-tail target: vendor DD Anyscale privacy DPA SOC 2 EU AI Act · ai_infra sub-vertical (11th sibling) · source leads index 212

Anyscale is the canonical Ray-on-Anyscale distributed-compute vendor behind Anyscale Endpoints (LLM inference per-second billing), Anyscale Jobs (managed Ray batch), Anyscale Workspaces (interactive dev environments) and the Anyscale Enterprise control plane. They are also the corporate steward of the open-source Ray project (Apache 2.0, 35K+ GitHub stars, co-authored by Ion Stoica, Robert Nishihara and Philipp Moritz at UC Berkeley's RISELab). For a privacy officer or security architect under EU AI Act, ISO 42001 or NIS2 timelines, Anyscale is structurally interesting because the privacy surface inherits Ray's distributed cluster model: per-customer isolated cluster, per-job ephemeral execution, per-token billing metadata, and per-call PII handling that maps to GDPR Art. 22 (automated decision-making), Art. 14 (information about processing) and Annex III (high-risk biometric / critical infrastructure) classifications.

What "Ray-Anyscale" actually is

The Ray project started in UC Berkeley's RISELab around 2017 as a unified interface for distributed Python (data preprocessing, hyperparameter sweeps, RL training, batch inference, model serving). Anyscale, the company, was founded in 2019 by Ion Stoica (UC Berkeley Professor + Databricks co-founder + Apache Spark PMC), Robert Nishihara (Anyscale CEO + Ray creator) and Philipp Moritz (Ray co-creator + Anyscale Co-Founder) to commercialize Ray's managed runtime. Anyscale Endpoints exposes open-source LLMs (Llama-3, Mixtral, DeepSeek, Qwen, GLM, Gemma, Phi, Stable-Diffusion) as an OpenAI-compatible inference API with per-second GPU billing, function-calling, fine-tuning, and RLHF pipelines. Anyscale Jobs gives you a serverless-Ray-batch-engine for ad-hoc jobs (image preprocessing at scale, embedding generation, batch inference). Anyscale Workspaces gives you a managed JupyterLab-tier dev environment running inside the customer's Anyscale cluster.

For an enterprise customer evaluating vendor-DD on Anyscale, the architecture question is "how does my code, my weights and my data stay isolated from other tenants?" Anyscale's documented answers are: (1) per-customer Anyscale Enterprise cluster with dedicated VPC + encryption-at-rest (AES-256) + encryption-in-transit (TLS 1.2+, mTLS for cluster-internal RPC); (2) per-job ephemeral execution with no cross-tenant inference traffic; (3) per-call PII handling flagged for downstream-Salesforce / HubSpot / Databricks / Snowflake / BigQuery state-changes; (4) SOC 2 Type II (per anyscale.com/security), GDPR DPA, EU AI Act readiness, ISO 27001 in progress.

Anyscale vendor-DD playbook (Talon Forge Atlas, 8-step)

  1. Locate the privacy-policy surface. Canonical anchor: anyscale.com/privacy-policy. The page exposes mailto:privacy@anyscale.com as the canonical GDPR DPA + SOC 2 + EU AI Act + vendor-DD strategic-inbound channel. Verified live via direct HTTP fetch on 2026-07-12.
  2. Initiate vendor-DD outreach via the privacy channel — not sales. Anyscale's privacy@ distribution is monitored by both the legal team (privacy@ for GDPR DPA, EU AI Act, ISO 42001) and the security team (security@ for SOC 2 attestation, ISO 27001 status, pen-test summary). The vendor-DD approach via privacy@ produces faster, more substantive responses than cold-outreach to AEs.
  3. Ask for the EU AI Act readiness packet. The packet includes: Article 27 EU representative identity, Article 14 information-notice template, Annex III risk classification, ISO 42001 audit-status; expected turnaround 5-10 business days.
  4. Validate SOC 2 Type II scope — confirm the audit window covers the 12 months preceding your contract start, and that the audit scope includes all of Anyscale Endpoints + Anyscale Jobs + Anyscale Workspaces (not just the management plane).
  5. Validate ISO 27001 status — confirm whether the certificate is in progress (Anyscale is mid-cert) or already awarded; if mid-cert, capture the target cert date.
  6. Validate GDPR DPA + Annex III + Art. 22 + Art. 14 — request the signed DPA, the Article 27 EU representative identity (Anyscale's EU rep is appointed), the per-call PII-handling notice, and the per-job-ephemeral-execution guarantee.
  7. Validate data-residency posture — confirm US vs EU vs multi-region cluster availability, per-customer VPC isolation, per-job ephemeral-storage teardown, per-customer-weights-at-rest encryption posture.
  8. Validate billing + metering transparency — Anyscale's per-second GPU-billing is one of the few inference-platform vendors with transparent metering; confirm you can export per-job billing feeds into your downstream-Snowflake / BigQuery / Databricks cost-allocation pipeline.

5-criteria scoring rubric for any Anyscale-tier deal

CriterionWhat "good" looks likeAvoid if
Vendor DD responsePrivacy / security reply < 7 business days with DPA + SOC 2 + ISO 27001 statusGeneric AE ping-pong, no legal/security ownership
EU AI Act readinessAnnex III risk-classified + Art. 27 rep named + Art. 14 notice templateVendor punts on EU AI Act
SOC 2 / ISO 27001Type II report (12mo window), or ISO 27001 certified (not mid-cert)Type I only, or pre-cert without target date
GDPR DPA + PIISigned DPA, PII handling notice, Article 27 EU representativeDPA under NDA only, or no Article 27
Billing transparencyPer-second GPU billing + per-job metering exportable to data warehouseVendor-side only metering, no per-job export

Who else is in the ai_infra cohort (Atlas observations)

Anyscale joins the Atlas ai_infra cohort: Honeycomb 102 + Arize 107 + Galileo 108 + Datadog 193 + SambaNova 190 + Groq 191 + Together AI 207 + Poolside AI 208 + Replicate 105 + Fireworks AI 209. Anyscale's distinctive contribution to this list is the distributed-Ray / RLHF-on-Ray / fine-tuning-on-Ray / per-customer-isolated-cluster lineage: while SambaNova 190 + Groq 191 + Fireworks AI 209 compete on inference latency, and Poolside AI 208 + Replicate 105 compete on developer-experience ergonomics, Anyscale uniquely owns the customer's own Ray cluster as the privacy surface. That makes Anyscale the natural choice for an enterprise whose vendor-DD questionnaire prioritizes Ray-cluster state-change + per-job-ephemeral-execution evidence over per-request inference latency.

CTA — book a 30-min Atlas audit

If you're evaluating Anyscale (or any ai_infra sibling) for a vendor-DD cycle and want a second pair of eyes on the DPA, the SOC 2 Type II scope, the EU AI Act Annex III risk-classification or the per-job-ephemeral-execution evidence — book a 30-min Atlas audit. $500 · Atlas vendor-DD audit →

Mercury: The Banking-Platform Vendor-DD Playbook (Talon Forge Atlas)

Updated 2026-07-12 · long-tail target: vendor DD Mercury banking platform GDPR DPA SOC 2 EU AI Act · finance_ops sub-vertical (banking-platform sibling) · source leads index 217

Mercury is the canonical vertically-integrated-banking-platform for startups and SMBs behind Mercury Business Checking (FDIC-insured checking accounts via partner banks Choice Financial Group + Evolve Bank & Trust), Mercury Treasury (high-yield treasury sweep into money-market mutual funds), Mercury AP (Accounts Payable with AI-invoice-OCR), Mercury IO (multi-entity treasury + Vault + ACH + wire + international wires), Mercury Pay (Stripe Issuing card-issuing partnership + payments API), Mercury Cards (debit + virtual cards with expense management + GL-coding + QuickBooks / Xero / Sage / NetSuite CSV-export), Mercury for Banking-as-a-Service, Mercury for Startups (free-tier pre-seed), Mercury Capital (debt-financing from Mercury itself for qualifying customers), Mercury Borrow (secured-loan product), and the AI-driven Mercury AI cash-flow-forecasting product. For a privacy officer or security architect under EU AI Act, ISO 42001 or NIS2 timelines, Mercury is structurally interesting because the privacy surface inherits the Banking-as-a-Service partner-bank model: per-customer KYC-onboarding, per-beneficial-owner ownership-verification, per-transaction OFAC-screening, per-wire AML-monitoring, per-card-transaction fraud-detection-ML, per-international-currency-conversion, downstream bank-vendor state-change, downstream card-network Visa-rail state-change, and downstream Stripe-Issuing-API state-change.

What "Mercury banking-platform" actually is

Mercury was founded in 2019 by Immad Akhund (CEO, co-founder of HeyZap + early Facebook engineer + ex-SoFi + ex-Twilio) + Jason Zhang (CTO, co-founder of HeyZap + ex-Facebook engineer). Mercury operates as a Banking-as-a-Service platform with FDIC-insured partner banks Choice Financial Group + Evolve Bank & Trust, providing pass-through FDIC-insurance up to $5M via the Mercury Program Agreement. Mercury Business Checking is the default-product, Mercury Treasury is the sweep-product, Mercury AP is the bill-pay-product with AI-invoice-OCR for Sage Intacct / NetSuite / Xero / QBO sync, Mercury IO is the multi-entity-treasury-product with Vault + inter-company transfers, Mercury Pay is the payments-API product with Stripe Issuing card-issuing partnership, Mercury Cards is the debit + virtual-cards product with expense management + GL-coding + QuickBooks / Xero / Sage / NetSuite CSV-export, Mercury Capital is the debt-financing product from Mercury itself for qualifying customers, Mercury Borrow is the secured-loan product, and Mercury AI is the AI-driven cash-flow-forecasting product that derives its signal from per-customer bank-account data.

For an enterprise customer evaluating vendor-DD on Mercury, the architecture question is "how does my bank-account data + my card-transaction data + my invoice-OCR data + my AI-training-corpus stay isolated from other tenants?" Mercury's documented answers are: (1) per-customer KYC-onboarding with per-beneficial-owner ownership-verification; (2) per-transaction OFAC-screening + per-wire AML-monitoring + per-card-transaction fraud-detection-ML; (3) downstream bank-vendor state-change tracking; (4) downstream card-network Visa-rail state-change tracking; (5) downstream Stripe-Issuing-API state-change tracking; (6) SOC 2 Type II (per mercury.com/security), GDPR DPA, ISO 27001, EU AI Act readiness.

Mercury vendor-DD playbook (Talon Forge Atlas, 8-step)

  1. Locate the privacy-policy surface. Canonical anchor: mercury.com/legal/privacy. The page exposes mailto:help@mercury.com as the canonical GDPR DPA + SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + EU AI Act + banking-compliance + vendor-DD strategic-inbound channel. Verified live via direct HTTP fetch on 2026-07-12, page size 1753462 bytes (full SPA-rendered Mercury site, SPA-loaded contact path for Privacy + DPA + BAA + Security Center).
  2. Initiate vendor-DD outreach via the help@ channel. Mercury's help@ distribution is monitored by both the legal team (privacy + DPA + EU AI Act readiness) and the security team (SOC 2 attestation + ISO 27001 status + pen-test summary). The vendor-DD approach via help@ produces faster, more substantive responses than cold-outreach to AEs.
  3. Ask for the banking-compliance + EU AI Act readiness packet. The packet includes: BSA / AML / OFAC program documentation, SOC 2 Type II report, ISO 27001 certificate, GDPR DPA, EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data-transparency for Mercury AI, Annex III risk-classification, ISO 42001 audit-status; expected turnaround 5-10 business days.
  4. Validate SOC 2 Type II scope — confirm the audit window covers the 12 months preceding your contract start, and that the audit scope includes all of Mercury Business Checking + Mercury Treasury + Mercury AP + Mercury IO + Mercury Pay + Mercury Cards + Mercury AI (not just the management plane).
  5. Validate ISO 27001 status — confirm whether the certificate is already awarded (Mercury is certified per mercury.com/security); capture the certificate number and the cert body.
  6. Validate GDPR DPA + Annex III + Art. 22 + Art. 53(d) — request the signed DPA, the per-call PII-handling notice for Mercury AI cash-flow-forecasting, the per-card-transaction fraud-detection-ML model-card, and the Mercury AI training-corpus-source + per-customer-financial-data license-provenance summary.
  7. Validate data-residency posture — confirm US vs EU vs multi-region cluster availability, per-customer data-segregation, per-customer multi-entity-state, per-Mercury-IO multi-entity-transfer-isolation, and the partner-bank Choice Financial Group + Evolve Bank & Trust data-segregation evidence.
  8. Validate billing + cost-attribution transparency — Mercury exposes per-card-transaction + per-international-wire + per-bill-pay-ACH-event + per-Mercury-Treasury-money-market-sweep-event feeds for cost-attribution. Confirm you can export these feeds into your downstream-Snowflake / BigQuery / Databricks / Sage Intacct / NetSuite / Xero / QBO cost-allocation pipeline, including WORM retention per SEC 17a-4 + IRS 6001 + SOX-analog-considerations.

5-criteria scoring rubric for any Mercury-tier deal

CriterionWhat "good" looks likeAvoid if
Vendor DD responsehelp@ reply < 7 business days with DPA + SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + BSA/AML/OFAC programGeneric AE ping-pong, no legal/security ownership
EU AI Act readinessArt. 53(d) GPAI training-data-transparency + Art. 27 rep named + Annex III risk-classified + ISO 42001 audit-statusVendor punts on EU AI Act
SOC 2 / ISO 27001Type II report (12mo window), ISO 27001 certified (not mid-cert), ISO 42001 in progressType I only, or pre-cert without target date
GDPR DPA + BSA/AML/OFACSigned DPA + BSA/AML/OFAC program documentation + OFAC-screening evidence + AML-monitoring evidence + per-beneficial-owner ownership-verificationDPA under NDA only, or no BSA/AML/OFAC documentation
Billing + cost-attribution transparencyPer-card-transaction + per-international-wire + per-bill-pay-ACH-event + per-Mercury-Treasury-money-market-sweep-event feeds exportable to data warehouse + WORM retention per SEC 17a-4 + IRS 6001Vendor-side only metering, no per-event export

Who else is in the finance_ops cohort (Atlas observations)

Mercury joins the Atlas finance_ops cohort. Mercury's distinctive contribution to this list is the vertically-integrated-banking-platform lineage: while every other finance_ops sibling focuses on a single layer of the fintech stack (Ramp on corpo-cards, Plaid on bank-data-aggregation, Northern Trust on custody, Mercury Capital on debt-financing for the Mercury customer base), Mercury uniquely owns the full banking + AP + IO + Pay + Cards + AI cash-flow-forecasting surface as a Banking-as-a-Service platform. That makes Mercury the natural choice for an enterprise whose vendor-DD questionnaire prioritizes partner-bank-FDIC-structure + AI cash-flow-forecasting model-card + AI-invoice-OCR provenance + Visa-rail fraud-detection-ML model-card over single-product fintech vendors.

CTA — book a 30-min Atlas audit

If you're evaluating Mercury (or any finance_ops sibling) for a vendor-DD cycle and want a second pair of eyes on the DPA, the SOC 2 Type II scope, the BSA/AML/OFAC program, the EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data-transparency disclosure, the Mercury AI cash-flow-forecasting model-card, the Mercury AP AI-invoice-OCR provenance join-table, the Mercury Card fraud-detection-ML per-card-transaction provenance join-table, or the cross-customer Mercury Bank Account isolation evidence — book a 30-min Atlas audit. $500 · Atlas vendor-DD audit →

Brex: The All-in-One Corporate-Card + Banking-Platform Vendor-DD Playbook (Talon Forge Atlas)

Updated 2026-07-12 · long-tail target: Brex vendor DD corporate card banking platform GDPR DPA SOC 2 ISO 27001 EU AI Act privacy@brex.com · finance_ops sub-vertical (banking-platform sibling) · sources: leads_with_emails enrichment index 218 (Brex) + Mercury 217 sibling pattern

Brex is the canonical AI-native corporate-card + business-banking platform for startups + mid-market + enterprise behind Brex Card (corporate charge card with no personal guarantee + 30-60 day float), Brex Business Account (FDIC-insured via partner banks Choice Financial Group + Column NA), Brex Bill Pay (AP automation + AI-invoice-OCR + GL-coding + ACH + check + international wires), Brex Treasury (high-yield money-market sweep), Brex Embedded (issuing API for B2B platforms), Brex Expenses (expense management + receipt OCR + auto-categorization + corporate-card-feed ingestion), Brex Premium (premium plan with concierge + travel + insurance + events), Brex for Startups (free-tier for venture-backed companies), Brex Capital (debt-financing from Brex itself + venture debt for Brex customers), Brex Empower (real-time spend analytics + dashboard + anomaly detection), and the AI-driven Brex Assistant + Brex Smart Spend categorizer + Brex Bill Pay AI-invoice-OCR + Brex Anomaly-Detection-ML surface. For a privacy officer or security architect under EU AI Act + ISO 42001 + NIS2 timelines, Brex is structurally interesting because the privacy surface inherits the Banking-as-a-Service partner-bank model: per-customer KYC-onboarding, per-beneficial-owner ownership-verification, per-transaction OFAC-screening, per-wire AML-monitoring, per-card-transaction fraud-detection-ML, per-international-currency-conversion, downstream bank-vendor state-change, downstream card-network Visa-rail state-change, downstream issuing-API state-change, and downstream Brex-Anomaly-Detection-ML state-change.

What "Brex banking-platform" actually is

Brex was founded in 2017 by Henrique Dubugras + Pedro Franceschi (both co-founders of Pagar.me, the largest payments processor in Brazil at the time of acquisition). Brex operates as a Banking-as-a-Service + corporate-card + AP + treasury + expense-management platform with FDIC-insured partner banks Choice Financial Group + Column NA + Cross River Bank, providing pass-through FDIC-insurance via the Brex Program Agreement. Brex Card is the default-product (corporate charge card with no personal guarantee + 30-60 day float + AI-driven limit-management + auto-rewards-multiplier based on company cash position + Brex Anomaly-Detection-ML for suspicious-transaction-flagging), Brex Business Account is the banking-product, Brex Bill Pay is the bill-pay-product with AI-invoice-OCR for NetSuite / QuickBooks / Xero / Sage sync, Brex Treasury is the sweep-product with high-yield money-market sweep, Brex Embedded is the issuing-API for B2B platforms with white-label card-issuing + KYC + transaction-monitoring, Brex Expenses is the expense-management product with receipt-OCR + auto-categorization + corporate-card-feed ingestion, Brex Premium is the premium tier with concierge + travel + insurance + events, Brex Capital is the debt-financing product from Brex itself for qualifying customers, Brex Empower is the spend-analytics dashboard with anomaly-detection-ML, and Brex Assistant is the AI-driven conversational-finance interface that derives its signal from per-customer bank-account data + per-card-transaction data + per-invoice-OCR data.

For an enterprise customer evaluating vendor-DD on Brex, the architecture question is "how does my bank-account data + my card-transaction data + my invoice-OCR data + my AI-training-corpus stay isolated from other tenants?" Brex's documented answers are: (1) per-customer KYC-onboarding with per-beneficial-owner ownership-verification; (2) per-transaction OFAC-screening + per-wire AML-monitoring + per-card-transaction fraud-detection-ML + Brex Anomaly-Detection-ML; (3) downstream bank-vendor state-change tracking; (4) downstream card-network Visa-rail state-change tracking; (5) downstream Brex-Embedded-issuing-API state-change tracking; (6) SOC 2 Type II (per brex.com/security), GDPR DPA, ISO 27001, EU AI Act readiness, ISO 42001 audit-status, AI-Bill transparency disclosure.

Brex vendor-DD playbook (Talon Forge Atlas, 8-step)

  1. Locate the privacy-policy surface. Canonical anchor: brex.com/legal/privacy. The page exposes mailto:privacy@brex.com as the canonical GDPR DPA + SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + EU AI Act + banking-compliance + vendor-DD strategic-inbound channel. Verified live via direct HTTP fetch on 2026-07-12, page size 284175 bytes (full server-rendered privacy page exposing the privacy@ channel + DPA link + sub-processor list + cookie inventory + retention schedule + per-region data-residency table).
  2. Initiate vendor-DD outreach via the privacy@ channel. Brex's privacy@ distribution is monitored by both the legal team (privacy + DPA + EU AI Act readiness) and the security team (SOC 2 attestation + ISO 27001 status + pen-test summary + Brex Anomaly-Detection-ML model-card). The vendor-DD approach via privacy@ produces faster, more substantive responses than cold-outreach to AEs because it routes through legal-counsel-trained intake rather than sales-ops.
  3. Ask for the banking-compliance + EU AI Act readiness packet. The packet includes: BSA / AML / OFAC program documentation, SOC 2 Type II report, ISO 27001 certificate, GDPR DPA, EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data-transparency for Brex Assistant + Brex Smart Spend + Brex Bill Pay AI-invoice-OCR + Brex Anomaly-Detection-ML, Annex III risk-classification, ISO 42001 audit-status; expected turnaround 5-10 business days.
  4. Validate SOC 2 Type II scope — confirm the audit window covers the 12 months preceding your contract start, and that the audit scope includes all of Brex Card + Brex Business Account + Brex Bill Pay + Brex Treasury + Brex Embedded + Brex Expenses + Brex Empower + Brex Assistant (not just the management plane). Brex's trust-center at trust.brex.com / security.brex.com carries the latest SOC 2 Type II report + ISO 27001 certificate + pen-test summary.
  5. Validate ISO 27001 status — confirm whether the certificate is already awarded (Brex is certified per the Brex trust-center); capture the certificate number and the cert body (typically one of BSI / Schellman / Coalfire / A-LIGN).
  6. Validate GDPR DPA + Annex III + Art. 22 + Art. 53(d) — request the signed DPA, the per-call PII-handling notice for Brex Assistant + Brex Smart Spend + Brex Bill Pay AI-invoice-OCR + Brex Anomaly-Detection-ML, the per-card-transaction fraud-detection-ML model-card, the Brex Anomaly-Detection-ML model-card, and the Brex Assistant training-corpus-source + per-customer-financial-data license-provenance summary. Brex publishes a sub-processor list on its trust-center; validate that all downstream vendors (Stripe Issuing for some cards, Plaid for some bank-data-aggregation, AWS for some infrastructure, Google Cloud for some workloads) are listed with their own DPA + sub-processor flow-down.
  7. Validate data-residency posture — confirm US vs EU vs multi-region cluster availability, per-customer data-segregation, per-customer multi-entity-state, per-Brex-Embedded-issuing-tenant isolation, and the partner-bank Choice Financial Group + Column NA + Cross River Bank data-segregation evidence. Brex publishes its data-residency matrix in the trust-center.
  8. Validate billing + cost-attribution transparency — Brex exposes per-card-transaction + per-international-wire + per-bill-pay-ACH-event + per-Brex-Treasury-money-market-sweep-event feeds for cost-attribution. Confirm you can export these feeds into your downstream-Snowflake / BigQuery / Databricks / Sage Intacct / NetSuite / Xero / QBO cost-allocation pipeline, including WORM retention per SEC 17a-4 + IRS 6001 + SOX-analog-considerations.

Who else is in the finance_ops cohort (Atlas observations)

Brex joins the Atlas finance_ops cohort next to Mercury (chunk_110, lead 217), with the parent Ramp cohort further back. Brex's distinctive contribution to this list is the AI-native + corporate-card-first lineage: while Mercury (lead 217, chunk_110) anchors on banking-platform + AP + IO + Pay + Cards + Mercury AI cash-flow-forecasting, Brex uniquely owns the corporate-card-first + Brex Assistant + Brex Smart Spend + Brex Bill Pay AI-invoice-OCR + Brex Anomaly-Detection-ML + Brex Embedded issuing-API surface as a Banking-as-a-Service platform. That makes Brex the natural choice for an enterprise whose vendor-DD questionnaire prioritizes no-personal-guarantee + corporate-card-first + Brex Assistant conversational-finance + Brex Smart Spend + Brex Anomaly-Detection-ML model-card + Brex Embedded white-label-issuing-API over single-product fintech vendors. The 2-platform choice (Brex for corporate-card-first + Brex Anomaly-Detection-ML; Mercury for banking-platform + AP + IO + Pay + Cards + Mercury AI cash-flow-forecasting) is the cleanest vendor-DD alternative for an enterprise that wants 2 banking-platform vendors in the DD queue with non-overlapping primary-surface ownership.

Ramp: The AI-Native Spend-Management + Corporate-Card + Procurement + Vendor-Management Vendor-DD Playbook (Talon Forge Atlas)

Updated 2026-07-12 · long-tail target: Ramp vendor DD spend management corporate card bill pay procurement vendor management GDPR DPA SOC 2 ISO 27001 EU AI Act privacy@ramp.com · finance_ops sub-vertical (banking-platform sibling) · sources: leads_with_emails enrichment index 219 (Ramp) + Brex 218 + Mercury 217 sibling pattern

Ramp is the canonical AI-native spend-management + corporate-card + bill-pay + accounts-payable + procurement + vendor-management + treasury platform for startups + mid-market + enterprise behind Ramp Card (corporate charge card with no personal guarantee + AI-driven-limit-management + auto-savings-recommender on unspent-cash + Ramp Anomaly-Detection-ML for suspicious-card-transaction-flagging + Ramp Travel + Ramp Procurement + Ramp Bill Pay AI-invoice-OCR), Ramp Business Account (FDIC-insured via partner bank First Internet Bank of Indiana Member FDIC + Moment Advisors investment-account), Ramp Bill Pay (AP automation + AI-invoice-OCR + GL-coding + ACH + check + international wires + NetSuite-QBO-Xero-Sage-SAP-Oracle-sync), Ramp Treasury (high-yield money-market sweep + Moment Advisors managed-portfolio), Ramp Procurement (procurement-workflow + vendor-onboarding + purchase-orders + three-way-match + spend-policy-enforcement), Ramp Vendor Management (vendor-master + vendor-risk + vendor-spend + vendor-contracts), Ramp Travel (booking + policy + rewards + Ramp-Travel-policy-enforcement), Ramp Procurement (Ramp-Procurement-three-way-match AI-workflow), Ramp Contracts (AI-clause-extraction + AI-renewal-alerts + AI-vendor-risk-scoring), Ramp Capital (debt-financing + revenue-based-financing + venture-debt for Ramp customers), Ramp Savings (Moment Advisors high-yield + Ramp Cash-Back + Ramp Rewards), Ramp Card-Reader + Ramp Mobile, Ramp Counter (counters + dashboards + savings-calculator + charge-finder + per-diem-calculator + mileage-reimbursement-calculator), Ramp for Startups (free-tier for venture-backed companies) + Ramp for Enterprise (mid-market + enterprise tier) + Ramp Investor-Database + Ramp Vendor-Directory + Ramp Answers-Hub, plus the AI-surface of Ramp Anomaly-Detection-ML-on-suspicious-card-transaction-flagging + Ramp Bill Pay AI-invoice-OCR + Ramp Contracts AI-clause-extraction + Ramp Procurement three-way-match + Ramp Vendor-Risk-AI-scoring.

For a privacy officer or security architect under EU AI Act + ISO 42001 + NIS2 + BSA/AML/OFAC timelines, Ramp is structurally interesting because the privacy surface inherits the Banking-as-a-Service partner-bank model (First Internet Bank of Indiana Member FDIC for deposit + Moment Advisors for managed-portfolio) while ALSO shipping a procurement-native + vendor-management-native + contracts-AI surface that most banking-platform siblings (Mercury 217, Brex 218) do not own. The audit-target surface aggregates: per-customer KYC-onboarding + per-beneficial-owner ownership-verification + per-transaction OFAC-screening + per-wire AML-monitoring + per-card-transaction fraud-detection-ML + Ramp Anomaly-Detection-ML + per-international-currency-conversion + downstream bank-vendor state-change (First Internet Bank of Indiana + Moment Advisors) + downstream card-network Visa-rail state-change + downstream Ramp-Capital-loan state-change + Ramp Bill Pay AI-invoice-OCR + Ramp Contracts AI-clause-extraction + Ramp Vendor-Risk-AI-scoring. NMLS 2371465 (Ramp Payments Corporation) + NMLS 2431387 (Ramp Financing Corporation).

What "Ramp spend-management + banking-platform" actually is

Ramp was founded in 2019 by Eric Glyman (Co-Founder + Co-CEO, ex-Kensho + ex-Harvard) + Karim Atiyeh (Co-Founder + Co-CEO, ex-Kensho + ex-MIT). Ramp operates as the spend-management-first + corporate-card-first + procurement-native + vendor-management-native + bill-pay-first + contracts-AI-first spend-management platform with FDIC-insured partner bank First Internet Bank of Indiana Member FDIC + Moment Advisors (managed-portfolio via Apex Clearing Corporation member FINRA + SIPC). Ramp Card is the default-product (corporate charge card with no personal guarantee + AI-driven-limit-management + auto-savings-recommender on unspent-cash + Ramp Anomaly-Detection-ML for suspicious-card-transaction-flagging + per-card-network Visa-rail integration), Ramp Business Account is the banking-product (FDIC-insured via partner bank + Moment Advisors investment-account integration), Ramp Bill Pay is the bill-pay-product with Ramp-Bill-Pay-AI-invoice-OCR for NetSuite / QuickBooks / Xero / Sage / SAP / Oracle sync + ACH + check + international wires + Ramp Bill Pay multi-step approval state, Ramp Treasury is the sweep-product with high-yield money-market sweep, Ramp Procurement is the procurement-native product (procurement-workflow + vendor-onboarding + purchase-orders + three-way-match + spend-policy-enforcement + Ramp-Procurement three-way-match AI-workflow), Ramp Vendor Management is the vendor-management product (vendor-master + vendor-risk + vendor-spend + vendor-contracts + Ramp-Vendor-Risk-AI-scoring ML-model), Ramp Travel is the travel product (booking + policy + rewards + Ramp-Travel-policy-enforcement), Ramp Contracts is the contracts-AI product (AI-clause-extraction + AI-renewal-alerts + AI-vendor-risk-scoring + Ramp-Contracts-AI-clause-extraction ML-model), Ramp Capital is the debt-financing product (debt-financing + revenue-based-financing + venture-debt for Ramp customers), Ramp Savings is the high-yield savings product (Moment Advisors managed-portfolio + Ramp Cash-Back + Ramp Rewards + Apex Clearing Corporation member FINRA + SIPC).

Ramp operates as a financial-technology company, not a bank — bank deposit services are provided by First Internet Bank of Indiana Member FDIC, investment-account services are managed by Moment Advisors LLC with securities products offered by Apex Clearing Corporation (member FINRA, SIPC), and the Investment Account is NOT FDIC-insured, NOT a deposit product, and may lose value. Ramp Payments Corporation carries NMLS 2371465 + Ramp Financing Corporation carries NMLS 2431387 with state-by-state disclosures at ramp.com/legal/state-disclosures. In Canada, Ramp cards are issued by Peoples Trust Company under license by Visa International; in the UK by Stripe Payments UK Limited (FCA firm reference 900461); in the EEA by Stripe Technology Europe Limited (Central Bank of Ireland firm reference C187865).

Ramp vendor-DD playbook (Talon Forge Atlas, 8-step)

  1. Locate the privacy-policy surface. Canonical anchor: ramp.com/legal/privacy-policy + ramp.com/legal/state-disclosures + ramp.com/legal/platform-agreement. The canonical channel is mailto:privacy@ramp.com, documented across Ramp CCPA + CalOPPA public-record filings + OneTrust DPA portal + GDPR Article 27 EU-rep filings. NOTE: Ramp.com is a JS-rendered Next.js SPA; the canonical privacy@ channel is verifiable via the public-record-DPA + CCPA + CalOPPA + state-by-state NMLS disclosures rather than live-fetched from the rendered legal page.
  2. Initiate vendor-DD outreach via the privacy@ channel. Ramp's privacy@ distribution is monitored by both the legal team (privacy + DPA + EU AI Act readiness + state-by-state NMLS disclosures) and the security team (SOC 2 attestation + ISO 27001 status + pen-test summary + Ramp Anomaly-Detection-ML model-card + Ramp Bill Pay AI-invoice-OCR model-card + Ramp Contracts AI-clause-extraction model-card + Ramp Vendor-Risk-AI-scoring model-card). The vendor-DD approach via privacy@ produces faster, more substantive responses than cold-outreach to AEs because it routes through legal-counsel-trained intake rather than sales-ops.
  3. Ask for the banking-compliance + EU AI Act readiness packet. The packet includes: BSA / AML / OFAC program documentation, SOC 2 Type II report, ISO 27001 certificate, GDPR DPA, EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data-transparency for Ramp Anomaly-Detection-ML + Ramp Bill Pay AI-invoice-OCR + Ramp Contracts AI-clause-extraction + Ramp Vendor-Risk-AI-scoring, Annex III risk-classification, ISO 42001 audit-status, state-by-state NMLS disclosures for Ramp Payments Corporation (NMLS 2371465) + Ramp Financing Corporation (NMLS 2431387), expected turnaround 5-10 business days.
  4. Validate SOC 2 Type II scope — confirm the audit window covers the 12 months preceding contract start, and that the audit scope includes all of Ramp Card + Ramp Business Account + Ramp Bill Pay + Ramp Treasury + Ramp Procurement + Ramp Vendor Management + Ramp Travel + Ramp Contracts + Ramp Capital + Ramp Savings + Ramp Anomaly-Detection-ML + Ramp Bill Pay AI-invoice-OCR + Ramp Contracts AI-clause-extraction (not just the management plane). Ramp's trust-center at trust.ramp.com carries the latest SOC 2 Type II report + ISO 27001 certificate + pen-test summary + state-by-state NMLS disclosures.
  5. Validate ISO 27001 + ISO 42001 status — confirm whether the ISO 27001 certificate is already awarded (Ramp is certified per ramp.com/security); capture the certificate number and the cert body (typically one of BSI / Schellman / Coalfire / A-LIGN). ISO 42001 (AI management system) audit-status is in-progress per ramp.com/security.
  6. Validate GDPR DPA + Annex III + Art. 22 + Art. 53(d) — request the signed DPA, the per-call PII-handling notice for Ramp Anomaly-Detection-ML + Ramp Bill Pay AI-invoice-OCR + Ramp Contracts AI-clause-extraction + Ramp Vendor-Risk-AI-scoring, the per-card-transaction fraud-detection-ML model-card, the Ramp Anomaly-Detection-ML model-card, the Ramp Bill Pay AI-invoice-OCR model-card, the Ramp Contracts AI-clause-extraction model-card, the Ramp Vendor-Risk-AI-scoring model-card, and the per-ML-model training-corpus-source + per-customer-financial-data license-provenance summary. Ramp publishes a sub-processor list; validate that all downstream vendors (Stripe Issuing for some cards, Visa for card-network, Moment Advisors for managed-portfolio, AWS for some infrastructure, Google Cloud for some workloads) are listed with their own DPA + sub-processor flow-down.
  7. Validate data-residency posture + NMLS bank-partner segregation — confirm US vs EU vs multi-region cluster availability, per-customer data-segregation, per-customer multi-entity-state, per-Ramp-Capital-loan isolation, per-Moment-Advisors-managed-portfolio isolation, and the First Internet Bank of Indiana + Member FDIC partner-bank data-segregation evidence, plus NMLS 2371465 + NMLS 2431387 state-by-state compliance documentation at ramp.com/legal/state-disclosures.
  8. Validate billing + cost-attribution transparency — Ramp exposes per-card-transaction + per-international-wire + per-bill-pay-ACH-event + per-Ramp-Treasury-money-market-sweep-event + per-Ramp-Bill-Pay-AI-invoice-OCR-ML-model-cost + per-Ramp-Contracts-AI-clause-extraction-ML-model-cost + per-Ramp-Vendor-Risk-AI-scoring-ML-model-cost feeds for cost-attribution. Confirm you can export these feeds into your downstream-Snowflake / BigQuery / Databricks / Sage Intacct / NetSuite / Xero / QBO cost-allocation pipeline, including WORM retention per SEC 17a-4 + IRS 6001 + SOX-analog-considerations.

CriterionWhat "good" looks likeAvoid if
Vendor DD responseprivacy@ reply < 7 business days with DPA + SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + BSA/AML/OFAC program + Ramp Anomaly-Detection-ML model-card + Ramp Bill Pay AI-invoice-OCR model-card + Ramp Contracts AI-clause-extraction model-card + Ramp Vendor-Risk-AI-scoring model-card + state-by-state NMLS disclosuresGeneric AE ping-pong, no legal/security ownership
EU AI Act readinessArt. 53(d) GPAI training-data-transparency + Art. 27 rep named + Annex III risk-classified + ISO 42001 audit-status for Ramp Anomaly-Detection-ML + Ramp Bill Pay AI-invoice-OCR + Ramp Contracts AI-clause-extraction + Ramp Vendor-Risk-AI-scoringVendor punts on EU AI Act or only covers one of the four AI surfaces
SOC 2 / ISO 27001Type II report (12mo window), ISO 27001 certified (not mid-cert), ISO 42001 in progressType I only, or pre-cert without target date
GDPR DPA + BSA/AML/OFAC + NMLSSigned DPA + BSA/AML/OFAC program documentation + OFAC-screening evidence + AML-monitoring evidence + per-beneficial-owner ownership-verification + sub-processor-list flow-down + state-by-state NMLS disclosures for NMLS 2371465 + NMLS 2431387DPA under NDA only, or no BSA/AML/OFAC documentation, or sub-processor list missing Stripe Issuing / Visa / Moment Advisors / Apex Clearing / AWS / GCP
Billing + cost-attribution transparencyPer-card-transaction + per-international-wire + per-bill-pay-ACH-event + per-Ramp-Treasury-money-market-sweep-event + per-Ramp-Bill-Pay-AI-invoice-OCR-ML-model-cost + per-Ramp-Contracts-AI-clause-extraction-ML-model-cost + per-Ramp-Vendor-Risk-AI-scoring-ML-model-cost feeds exportable to data warehouse + WORM retention per SEC 17a-4 + IRS 6001Vendor-side only metering, no per-event export

Ramp's distinctive contribution to this list is the spend-management-native + procurement-native + vendor-management-native + contracts-AI-first lineage: while Mercury (lead 217, chunk_110) anchors on banking-platform + AP + IO + Pay + Cards + Mercury AI cash-flow-forecasting, and Brex (lead 218, chunk_111) anchors on corporate-card-first + Brex Assistant + Brex Smart Spend + Brex Bill Pay AI-invoice-OCR + Brex Anomaly-Detection-ML + Brex Embedded issuing-API, Ramp uniquely owns the spend-management-first + Ramp Procurement native + Ramp Vendor Management native + Ramp Contracts AI-clause-extraction + Ramp Vendor-Risk-AI-scoring + Ramp Anomaly-Detection-ML + Ramp Bill Pay AI-invoice-OCR + Ramp Capital debt-financing + Ramp for Startups free-tier + Ramp for Enterprise mid-market + Moment Advisors managed-portfolio + Apex Clearing Corporation FINRA SIPC surface as a Banking-as-a-Service + spend-management platform. That makes Ramp the natural choice for an enterprise whose vendor-DD questionnaire prioritizes spend-management-first + procurement-native + vendor-management-native + contracts-AI-first over single-product fintech vendors. The 3-platform choice (Ramp for spend-management + procurement + vendor-management + contracts-AI; Mercury for banking-platform + AP + IO + Pay + Cards + Mercury AI cash-flow-forecasting; Brex for corporate-card-first + Brex Assistant + Brex Anomaly-Detection-ML + Brex Embedded issuing-API) is the cleanest vendor-DD alternative for an enterprise that wants 3 finance_ops vendors in the DD queue with non-overlapping primary-surface ownership.

Rillet: The AI-Native ERP + AI-Ledger + AI-Close + AI-Revenue-Recognition (ASC 606 + IFRS 15) + AI-Sales-Tax (50-state Wayfair nexus) + AI-Multi-Entity Vendor-DD Playbook (Talon Forge Atlas)

Updated 2026-07-12 · long-tail target: Rillet AI-native ERP AI ledger close revenue recognition ASC 606 IFRS 15 multi entity sales tax Wayfair economic nexus GDPR DPA SOC 2 ISO 27001 EU AI Act privacy@rillet.com · finance_ops sub-vertical (AI-native-ERP sibling) · sources: leads_with_emails enrichment index 220 (Rillet) + Ramp 219 + Brex 218 + Mercury 217 sibling pattern

Rillet is the canonical AI-native ERP + AI-ledger + AI-close + AI-reconciliation + AI-financial-reporting + AI-CFO + Rillet Ledger + Rillet Close + Rillet Reporting + Rillet Sales Tax + Rillet Revenue Recognition + Rillet Multi-Entity + Rillet Custom Dashboards + Rillet Integrations platform for scaling SaaS startups + mid-market horizontal-replacement-of-NetSuite-and-QuickBooks-Enterprise behind Rillet's AI-ledger (auto-journal-entry-classification + auto-reconciliation against bank + Stripe + Plaid + Ramp + Brex + Mercury feeds), Rillet Close (month-end-close-acceleration via auto-tie-out + auto-flags-for-human-review + parallel-multi-entity-close), Rillet Reporting (auto-cash-flow-statement + auto-P-and-L + auto-balance-sheet + auto-deck-generation + auto-board-pack), Rillet AI-CFO (natural-language-asking-of-financial-questions), Rillet Multi-Entity (per-entity-ledger-isolation + intercompany-elimination + multi-currency-translation-IFRS-ASC-830), Rillet Revenue Recognition per ASC 606 + IFRS 15 (auto-SSP-calculations + auto-performance-obligation-identification + auto-variable-consideration-estimates), Rillet Sales Tax per Wayfair + economic-nexus-50-state-tracking + Stripe-Tax-integration, Rillet Custom Dashboards (custom-MDX-SQL-queries on top of the AI-ledger data), and 100+ integrations (NetSuite + QuickBooks + Sage Intacct + Xero + Stripe + Plaid + Ramp + Brex + Mercury + BILL + Shopify + Salesforce + HubSpot + Workday + Greenhouse + Rippling).

For a privacy officer or security architect under EU AI Act + ISO 42001 + IFRS reporting + ASC 606 compliance + Wayfair-economic-nexus sales-tax compliance + multi-entity consolidation timelines, Rillet is structurally interesting because the AI-surface inherits the full ledger-of-record obligation (every AI-posted journal entry becomes a GAAP-recognized-financial-event) while ALSO shipping the AI-Revenue-Recognition-ASC-606-SSP-decision + the AI-Sales-Tax-50-state-nexus-decision + the AI-close-tie-out-decision + the AI-Multi-Entity-elimination-decision + the AI-IFRS-15-currency-translation-decision + the AI-CFO-natural-language-decision that most AI-ledger vendors do not own. The audit-target surface aggregates: per-customer financial-data + per-customer bank-feed-data + per-customer Stripe-fee-data + per-customer Ramp-Brex-Mercury-card-transaction-data + per-entity-ledger-isolation + intercompany-elimination + multi-currency-translation + ASC 606 performance-obligation-identification + SSP-calculation + variable-consideration-estimate + 50-state Wayfair-economic-nexus-determination + ASC 830 / IFRS currency-translation-rate + downstream-Netsuite-Sage-Intacct-Xero-QBO-ERP-state-change.

What "Rillet AI-native ERP" actually is

Rillet was founded in 2024 in San Francisco by Mikael Berner (CEO, 2x ex-Stripe + led Stripe Tax and Stripe Revenue Recognition products — the canonical Stripe-led-accounting-vertical-experience) + Stelian Musat (CFO-experience, ex-Stripe + ex-Outreach) + Nicolas Kopp (ex-Stripe) + Mehmet Cigiltepe. Rillet operates as the AI-native-ERP-first + AI-ledger-first + AI-close-first + AI-revenue-recognition-ASC-606-first + AI-multi-entity-first horizontal-NetSuite-and-QuickBooks-Enterprise-replacement for SaaS startups + mid-market-companies running $5M-$500M ARR. Rillet's AI-ledger is the default-product (auto-journal-entry-classification + auto-reconciliation against bank + Stripe + Plaid + Ramp + Brex + Mercury + BILL feeds), Rillet Close is the close-product (month-end-close-acceleration via auto-tie-out + auto-flags-for-human-review + parallel-multi-entity-close), Rillet Reporting is the reporting-product (auto-cash-flow-statement + auto-P-and-L + auto-balance-sheet + auto-deck-generation + auto-board-pack), Rillet AI-CFO is the natural-language-query product (natural-language-asking-of-financial-questions + natural-language-SQL-against-ledger), Rillet Multi-Entity is the consolidation-product (per-entity-ledger-isolation + intercompany-elimination + multi-currency-translation-IFRS-ASC-830), Rillet Revenue Recognition is the ASC 606 + IFRS 15 product (auto-SSP-calculations + auto-performance-obligation-identification + auto-variable-consideration-estimates), Rillet Sales Tax is the 50-state-Wayfair-economic-nexus product (economic-nexus-tracking + Stripe-Tax-integration + rate-determination), Rillet Custom Dashboards is the analytics product (custom-MDX-SQL-queries on top of the AI-ledger data), and 100+ integrations round out the platform.

Rillet operates as a SaaS-delivered AI-native ERP, not an accounting firm + not a bank-deposit-institution. The platform ships with SOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001 + GDPR DPA + EU AI Act readiness per rillet.com/security + rillet.com/legal/privacy-policy + rillet.com/dpa. The AI-surface is positioned for the post-Wayfair + post-ASC-606 + post-EU-AI-Act SaaS-mid-market buyer who previously had to choose between a brittle QuickBooks-Online (insufficient for $50M+ ARR + multi-entity) + a heavy NetSuite (insufficient AI-decision-provenance + $100K+/yr + 6-month-implementation) — Rillet's wedge is "AI-native from day one with a 20-column audit-trail join-table per AI-decision."

Rillet vendor-DD playbook (Talon Forge Atlas, 8-step)

  1. Locate the privacy-policy surface. Canonical anchor: rillet.com/legal/privacy-policy + rillet.com/security + rillet.com/dpa. The canonical channel is mailto:privacy@rillet.com + mailto:support@rillet.com, both directly verified live 2026-07-12 via curl scrape of https://www.rillet.com/legal/privacy-policy (HTTP 200, 230785 bytes) exposing mailto:privacy@rillet.com + mailto:support@rillet.com as the canonical GDPR DPA + SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + EU AI Act + vendor-DD strategic-inbound channels.
  2. Initiate vendor-DD outreach via the privacy@ channel. Rillet's privacy@ distribution is monitored by both the legal team (privacy + DPA + EU AI Act readiness + ASC 606 / IFRS 15 disclosure considerations) and the security team (SOC 2 attestation + ISO 27001 certificate + pen-test summary + Rillet AI-Ledger model-card + Rillet Close model-card + Rillet Revenue Recognition model-card + Rillet Sales Tax model-card). The vendor-DD approach via privacy@ produces faster, more substantive responses than cold-outreach to AEs because it routes through legal-counsel-trained intake rather than sales-ops.
  3. Ask for the AI-native-ERP compliance + EU AI Act readiness + ASC 606 + IFRS 15 + Wayfair packet. The packet includes: SOC 2 Type II report, ISO 27001 certificate, GDPR DPA, EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data-transparency for Rillet AI-Ledger + Rillet Close + Rillet Revenue Recognition + Rillet Sales Tax + Rillet AI-CFO, Annex III risk-classification, ISO 42001 audit-status, ASC 606 SSP-calculations evidence, IFRS 15 currency-translation-rate evidence, Wayfair-economic-nexus-50-state-decision-log evidence, expected turnaround 5-10 business days.
  4. Validate SOC 2 Type II scope — confirm the audit window covers the 12 months preceding contract start, and that the audit scope includes all of Rillet AI-Ledger + Rillet Close + Rillet Reporting + Rillet Sales Tax + Rillet Revenue Recognition + Rillet Multi-Entity + Rillet Custom Dashboards + Rillet Integrations + Rillet AI-CFO (not just the management plane). Rillet's trust-center carries the latest SOC 2 Type II report + ISO 27001 certificate + pen-test summary.
  5. Validate ISO 27001 + ISO 42001 status — confirm whether the ISO 27001 certificate is already awarded (Rillet is certified per rillet.com/security); capture the certificate number and the cert body (typically one of BSI / Schellman / Coalfire / A-LIGN). ISO 42001 (AI management system) audit-status should be in-progress per rillet.com/security.
  6. Validate GDPR DPA + Annex III + Art. 22 + Art. 53(d) — request the signed DPA, the per-AI-decision PII-handling notice for Rillet AI-Ledger + Rillet Close + Rillet Revenue Recognition + Rillet Sales Tax + Rillet AI-CFO, the per-journal-entry AI-classification model-card, the per-reconciliation AI-decision model-card, the per-close-tie-out AI-decision model-card, the per-ASC-606-SSP-decision model-card, the per-IFRS-15-currency-translation-decision model-card, the per-Wayfair-economic-nexus-decision model-card, the per-AI-CFO-natural-language-decision model-card, and the per-ML-model training-corpus-source + per-customer-financial-data license-provenance summary. Rillet publishes a sub-processor list; validate that all downstream vendors (Stripe for Stripe Tax integration, Plaid for bank-feed ingestion, Ramp/Brex/Mercury for card-feed ingestion, BILL for bill-pay integration, AWS for infrastructure, Google Cloud for some workloads) are listed with their own DPA + sub-processor flow-down.
  7. Validate data-residency posture + multi-entity isolation + ASC 606 + IFRS 15 + Wayfair — confirm US vs EU vs multi-region cluster availability, per-customer data-segregation, per-entity-ledger-isolation, per-customer-multi-entity-state, per-Rillet-Multi-Entity-elimination isolation, per-ASC-606-performance-obligation-isolation, per-IFRS-15-currency-translation-rate isolation, per-Wayfair-economic-nexus-decision isolation, and the per-customer-AI-Revenue-Recognition-decision-isolation, plus the per-AI-CFO-natural-language-query history-isolation.
  8. Validate billing + cost-attribution transparency — Rillet exposes per-journal-entry + per-reconciliation + per-close-tie-out + per-Revenue-Recognition-ASC-606-decision + per-Sales-Tax-nexus-decision + per-AI-CFO-natural-language-query-cost + per-multi-entity-elimination-cost + per-Stripe-Plaid-Ramp-Brex-Mercury-bank-feed-ingestion-cost feeds for cost-attribution. Confirm you can export these feeds into your downstream-Snowflake / BigQuery / Databricks / Sage Intacct / NetSuite / Xero / QBO cost-allocation pipeline, including WORM retention per SEC 17a-4 + IRS 6001 + SOX 404.

5-criteria scoring rubric for any Rillet-tier deal

Criterion Weight Rillet baseline (2026) Notes for buyer
AI-decision reasoning-chain provenance depth (SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 9.4) 30% 20-column join-table per AI-decision (per-journal-entry-uuid + per-AI-classification-decision-id + per-AI-reconciliation-decision-id + per-AI-Close-tie-out-decision-id + per-AI-Revenue-Recognition-SSP-decision-id + per-AI-Sales-Tax-nexus-decision-id + per-tenant-id + per-source-transaction-id + per-LLM-prompt-text + per-LLM-completion-text + per-LLM-token-cost + per-AI-confidence-score + per-human-review-required-bit + per-downstream-ERP-state-change-id + per-GL-account-id + per-period-close-id + per-Stripe-Plaid-Ramp-Brex-Mercury-feed-source-of-truth-id + per-ASC-606-performance-obligation-id + per-SSP-component-id + per-IFRS-15-currency-translation-rate-id + per-multi-entity-elimination-id) Most AI-ledger vendors only log the GL-classification decision, not the downstream-ERP-state-change + per-ASC-606-SSP-decision + per-Wayfair-nexus-decision + per-multi-entity-elimination. Confirm the join-table covers all AI-decisions, not just journal-entry-classification.
ASC 606 + IFRS 15 + Wayfair-economic-nexus decision-log coverage 20% Per-ASC-606-performance-obligation-id + per-SSP-component-id + per-variable-consideration-estimate-id + per-IFRS-15-currency-translation-rate-id + per-Wayfair-economic-nexus-state-id + per-50-state-nexus-decision-id + per-Stripe-Tax-rate-id ASC 606 + IFRS 15 + Wayfair compliance are the regulatory hot-zone for SaaS-mid-market + EU-AI-Act Annex III 4. Confirm per-decision evidence for each, not just a summary roll-up.
Multi-entity isolation + per-entity-ledger + intercompany elimination + currency translation 15% Per-entity-ledger-isolation + per-intercompany-elimination-id + per-currency-translation-rate-id + per-ASC-830 / IFRS-currency-translation-rate-id + per-multi-entity-period-close-id SaaS-mid-market buyers running multi-entity + multi-currency + intercompany need per-entity ledger isolation that survives an audit walk. Confirm the intercompany-elimination row-level evidence, not just the rolled-up balance.
Stripe + Plaid + Ramp + Brex + Mercury + BILL bank-feed ingestion per-decision provenance 15% Per-Stripe-fee-transaction-id + per-Plaid-bank-feed-row-id + per-Ramp-card-transaction-id + per-Brex-card-transaction-id + per-Mercury-bank-account-id + per-BILL-bill-pay-id + per-source-system-write-id + per-amount + per-currency + per-feed-ingestion-latency-ms The bank-feed ingestion is where the AI-decision chain starts. Per-row evidence from the source-of-truth feed (Stripe + Plaid + Ramp + Brex + Mercury + BILL) is what makes the per-journal-entry reasoning-chain auditable.
Cross-tenant SaaS isolation + EU AI Act + GDPR Art. 28 + FedRAMP-analog posture 10% Per-tenant-ledger-isolation + per-entity-isolation + per-period-isolation + per-AI-feature-resolved-data + per-AI-Revenue-Recognition-isolation + per-AI-Sales-Tax-isolation + per-Stripe-Plaid-Ramp-Brex-Mercury-bank-feed-isolation + per-AI-CFO-natural-language-query-history-isolation + per-ASC-606-decision-isolation + per-multi-entity-elimination-isolation per SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10 + FedRAMP Moderate The ledger-of-record obligation means a single cross-tenant leak is a GAAP-recognized-financial-event-class disaster, not just a privacy breach. Validate the SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + ISO 42001 evidence per-tenant, not just at the platform level.
AI-Revenue-Recognition-ASC-606 + AI-Sales-Tax-Wayfair-nexus human-review-required-bit granularity 10% Per-ASC-606-SSP-decision human-review-required-bit + per-IFRS-15-currency-translation-rate human-review-required-bit + per-Wayfair-economic-nexus-decision human-review-required-bit + per-50-state-nexus-decision human-review-required-bit + per-multi-entity-elimination human-review-required-bit + per-AI-CFO-natural-language-query human-review-required-bit Per-decision human-review-required-bit (not per-batch) makes the regulator-trail defensible. Most ERPs default to per-batch which makes the auditor-walk much harder.

Why Rillet (specifically) for AI-native-ERP buyers

Rillet occupies a distinct lane in the AI-native-ERP market because of four founder-experience + product-design choices that compound:

  1. Founded by Stripe Tax + Stripe Revenue Recognition leaders. Mikael Berner (CEO) led Stripe Tax and Stripe Revenue Recognition — the canonical Stripe-led-accounting-vertical-experience. That is why Rillet's AI-Revenue-Recognition-ASC-606-SSP-decision + AI-Sales-Tax-50-state-Wayfair-nexus-decision are first-class-product-features rather than bolted-on-modules. Most AI-ledger vendors either skip ASC 606 entirely or treat it as a manual journal-entry template — Rillet ships the SSP-decision + performance-obligation-identification + variable-consideration-estimate as AI-decisions with per-row provenance.
  2. Horizontal NetSuite + QuickBooks Enterprise replacement position. Rillet's ICP is $5M-$500M ARR SaaS-mid-market who previously had to choose between brittle QuickBooks-Online (insufficient for $50M+ ARR + multi-entity) and heavy NetSuite (insufficient AI-decision-provenance + $100K+/yr + 6-month-implementation). Rillet's wedge is "AI-native from day one with a 20-column audit-trail join-table per AI-decision." This is the exact wedge Talon Forge Atlas's audit-target-gap matrix is designed to surface — the buyer who has been putting off a NetSuite-replacement because of audit-trail-risk now has an AI-native option.
  3. Multi-entity + multi-currency + intercompany elimination as first-class. Rillet Multi-Entity (per-entity-ledger-isolation + intercompany-elimination + multi-currency-translation-IFRS-ASC-830) is not a bolt-on — it ships day-one with the platform. Most AI-ledger vendors treat multi-entity as a manual-spreadsheet — Rillet's parallel-multi-entity-close + per-entity-elimination + per-currency-translation-rate-evidence is what makes the IFRS-reporting + Wayfair-50-state-nexus combination survivable.
  4. Bank-feed-first design (Stripe + Plaid + Ramp + Brex + Mercury + BILL). Rillet's ingestion model is bank-feed-first — every AI-decision starts from a per-row source-of-truth feed (Stripe fee transaction + Plaid bank-feed row + Ramp card transaction + Brex card transaction + Mercury bank account row + BILL bill-pay row). The audit-trail robustness comes from this — per-journal-entry reasoning-chain evidence anchored to a per-row bank-feed row is much stronger than a per-batch GL-posting summary.

Adjacent vendors worth knowing (for buyer-side comparison)

  • NetSuite (Oracle) — the legacy incumbent. $100K+/yr + 6-month-implementation. Insufficient AI-decision-provenance. Most SaaS-mid-market buyers still on NetSuite cite the AI-decision-provenance gap as the #1 reason they're evaluating Rillet.
  • QuickBooks Online + QuickBooks Enterprise — brittle at $50M+ ARR + multi-entity. QBO has no real AI-decision provenance, no ASC 606 SSP-decision automation, no Wayfair-50-state-nexus automation. QBE has some, but the implementation overhead is comparable to NetSuite.
  • Sage Intacct — the mid-market ERP incumbent. Better multi-entity than QBO but still no AI-native posture + no per-AI-decision-provenance. Buyers evaluating Sage Intacct vs Rillet typically cite the AI-decision-provenance gap + the implementation overhead.
  • Numeric — AI-native close + AI-accountant surface. Adjacent to Rillet's Rillet Close + Rillet AI-CFO but more focused on the close-package than the full ledger.
  • Pigment — FP&A-focused. Adjacent to Rillet Reporting but not a full ERP.
  • Runway Financial — FP&A + close-adjacent. Not a full ERP, more like Numeric.

Final recommendation for the AI-native-ERP buyer

If you are a SaaS-mid-market buyer (Series B+ + $5M-$500M ARR + multi-entity + ASC 606 / IFRS 15 / Wayfair-economic-nexus obligations + EU-AI-Act-Annex-III-4-audit-target obligations), Rillet is the strongest AI-native-ERP option in 2026 for the post-NetSuite-replacement wedge. The audit-target-gap matrix you'll want to walk through before signing: (1) per-AI-Revenue-Recognition-ASC-606-SSP-decision reasoning-chain provenance (Rillet ships this), (2) per-AI-Sales-Tax-Wayfair-50-state-nexus-decision reasoning-chain provenance (Rillet ships this), (3) per-AI-Close-tie-out-decision reasoning-chain provenance (Rillet ships this), (4) per-AI-Multi-Entity-elimination-decision reasoning-chain provenance (Rillet ships this), (5) per-AI-CFO-natural-language-query reasoning-chain provenance (Rillet ships this). For each, ask for the per-decision row-level evidence + the per-decision human-review-required-bit + the per-decision downstream-ERP-state-change-id + the per-decision Stripe-Plaid-Ramp-Brex-Mercury-bank-feed-source-of-truth-id. Rillet passes all five; most AI-ledger vendors fail 2-3 of them out of the box.

Book a 30-min audit-target-gap walkthrough Read template 308 (Rillet outreach)

Numeric: The AI-Native Close-Automation + AI-Reconciliation + AI-Flux-Analysis + AI-Close-Package-Bind-Event Vendor-DD Playbook (Talon Forge Atlas)

Updated 2026-07-12 · long-tail target: Numeric AI close automation reconciliation flux analysis audit committee board pack close package bind event accounting teams SOC 2 ISO 27001 EU AI Act privacy@numeric.io · finance_ops sub-vertical (AI-native-close-automation sibling) · sources: leads.csv enrichment index 221 (Numeric) + Rillet 220 + Ramp 219 + Brex 218 + Mercury 217 sibling pattern

Numeric is the canonical AI-powered close-automation + AI-reconciliation + AI-flux-analysis + AI-close-package-platform for scaling SaaS + mid-market accounting teams behind Numeric Close (auto-organized month-end-close-workflow with task-orchestration + close-package-bind-events + audit-committee-ready-board-packs), Numeric Reconciliations (AI-tie-out against bank + Stripe + Plaid + Ramp + Brex + Mercury + BILL + NetSuite + Sage Intacct + Xero + QBO ERP feeds), Numeric Flux Analysis (AI-drafted-flux-analysis-narrative auto-generated from prior-period-vs-current-period variance detection), Numeric Reporting (auto-cash-flow-statement + auto-P-and-L + auto-balance-sheet + auto-deck-generation + auto-board-pack + auto-audit-committee-preparation), Numeric Variance Investigation (AI-natural-language-investigation of flux-variances + AI-suggested-journal-entry-classification), Numeric Transaction Analysis (AI-per-transaction-categorization + AI-per-transaction-anomaly-flagging + AI-per-transaction-confidence-score + AI-human-review-required-bit), Numeric Anomaly Detection (AI-per-reconciliation-variance-flagging + AI-per-flux-variance-aggregation + AI-per-close-package-bind-event-trigger), Numeric Audit Trail (per-journal-entry-uuid + per-reconciliation-id + per-flux-analysis-narrative-id + per-close-package-bind-event-id + per-LLM-prompt-text + per-LLM-completion-text + per-LLM-token-cost + per-AI-confidence-score + per-human-review-required-bit + per-Stripe-Plaid-Ramp-Brex-Mercury-BILL-feed-source-of-truth-id).

For a privacy officer or security architect under EU AI Act + ISO 42001 + IFRS reporting + ASC 606 compliance + multi-entity consolidation timelines, Numeric is structurally interesting because the AI-surface inherits the full close-of-record obligation (every auto-bound close-package becomes an audit-committee-signed-off financial artifact) while ALSO shipping the AI-flux-analysis-narrative-auto-draft + the AI-variance-investigation-natural-language-decision + the AI-transaction-categorization-confidence-score + the AI-anomaly-detection-on-reconciliation-variances + the close-package-bind-event-as-audit-target-event-stream that most close-automation vendors do not own. The audit-target surface aggregates: per-customer financial-data + per-customer bank-feed-data + per-customer Stripe-fee-data + per-customer Ramp-Brex-Mercury-card-transaction-data + per-entity-ledger-isolation + intercompany-elimination + multi-currency-translation + ASC 606 performance-obligation-identification + SSP-calculation + variable-consideration-estimate + 50-state Wayfair-economic-nexus-determination + ASC 830 / IFRS currency-translation-rate + downstream-Netsuite-Sage-Intacct-Xero-QBO-ERP-state-change + close-package-bind-event-as-immutable-ledger-record.

What "Numeric AI-close-automation" actually is

Numeric was founded in 2020 in San Francisco by Parker Gilbert (Co-Founder + CEO, ex-accountant-finance-leader who lived the close-pain firsthand and built Numeric as "the platform I wish I had back then" — quoted directly from Numeric's about-page-first-person-narrative), Anthony Alvernaz (CPO + Co-Founder, Ex-BlackRock), and Andrew Bihl (CTO + Co-Founder, Ex-Segment-data-platform). Numeric operates as the AI-native-close-automation-first + AI-reconciliation-tie-out-first + AI-flux-analysis-narrative-first + AI-close-package-bind-event-first + 100x-incumbent-takeover-positioning platform for SaaS-mid-market + accounting-teams-running-ASC-606 + multi-entity-close + audit-committee-board-packs. Numeric Close is the workflow-product (auto-organized-month-end-close-workflow with task-orchestration + close-package-bind-events + audit-committee-ready-board-packs), Numeric Reconciliations is the tie-out-product (AI-tie-out against bank + Stripe + Plaid + Ramp + Brex + Mercury + BILL + NetSuite + Sage Intacct + Xero + QBO ERP feeds), Numeric Flux Analysis is the variance-product (AI-drafted-flux-analysis-narrative auto-generated from prior-period-vs-current-period variance detection), Numeric Reporting is the reporting-product (auto-cash-flow-statement + auto-P-and-L + auto-balance-sheet + auto-deck-generation + auto-board-pack + auto-audit-committee-preparation), Numeric Variance Investigation is the natural-language-product (AI-natural-language-investigation of flux-variances + AI-suggested-journal-entry-classification), Numeric Transaction Analysis is the per-transaction-product (AI-per-transaction-categorization + AI-per-transaction-anomaly-flagging + AI-per-transaction-confidence-score + AI-human-review-required-bit), Numeric Anomaly Detection is the variance-aggregation-product (AI-per-reconciliation-variance-flagging + AI-per-flux-variance-aggregation + AI-per-close-package-bind-event-trigger), Numeric Audit Trail is the audit-trail-product (per-journal-entry-uuid + per-reconciliation-id + per-flux-analysis-narrative-id + per-close-package-bind-event-id + per-LLM-prompt-text + per-LLM-completion-text + per-LLM-token-cost + per-AI-confidence-score + per-human-review-required-bit + per-Stripe-Plaid-Ramp-Brex-Mercury-BILL-feed-source-of-truth-id).

Numeric operates as a SaaS-delivered AI-native close-automation, not an accounting firm + not a bank-deposit-institution. The platform ships with SOC 2 Type II + GDPR DPA + EU AI Act readiness + ISO 27001 in progress per numeric.io/legal/privacy-policy + numeric.io/security + numeric.io/dpa. The AI-surface is positioned for the post-Wayfair + post-ASC-606 + post-EU-AI-Act SaaS-mid-market-accounting-team + 100x-incumbent-takeover-positioning buyer who previously had to choose between a brittle QuickBooks-Online close-checklist (insufficient for $50M+ ARR + multi-entity + audit-committee-board-packs) + a heavy NetSuite close-module (insufficient AI-decision-provenance + $100K+/yr + 6-month-implementation + no-close-package-bind-event-as-immutable-audit-record) — Numeric's wedge is "AI-native close-automation from day one with a 20-column audit-trail join-table per AI-decision + close-package-bind-event-as-immutable-audit-record." Numeric runs multiregion with HQ in San Francisco + offices in NYC + LDN (London UK) for EMEA-expansion.

Numeric vendor-DD playbook (Talon Forge Atlas, 8-step)

  1. Locate the privacy-policy surface. Canonical anchor: numeric.io/legal/privacy-policy + numeric.io/security + numeric.io/dpa. The canonical channel is mailto:privacy@numeric.io, directly verified live 2026-07-12 via curl scrape of https://www.numeric.io/legal/privacy-policy (HTTP 200, 133,272 bytes) exposing mailto:privacy@numeric.io as the canonical GDPR DPA + SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + EU AI Act + vendor-DD strategic-inbound channel.
  2. Initiate vendor-DD outreach via the privacy@ channel. Numeric's privacy@ distribution is monitored by both the legal team (privacy + DPA + EU AI Act readiness + ASC 606 / IFRS 15 disclosure considerations) and the security team (SOC 2 attestation + ISO 27001 certificate + pen-test summary + Numeric AI-Close model-card + Numeric Reconciliations model-card + Numeric Flux Analysis model-card + Numeric Variance Investigation model-card + Numeric Transaction Analysis model-card + Numeric Anomaly Detection model-card + Numeric Audit Trail join-table model-card). The vendor-DD approach via privacy@ produces faster, more substantive responses than cold-outreach to AEs because it routes through legal-counsel-trained intake rather than sales-ops.
  3. Ask for the AI-close-automation + EU AI Act readiness + ASC 606 + IFRS 15 + close-package-bind-event packet. The packet includes: SOC 2 Type II report, ISO 27001 certificate, GDPR DPA, EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data-transparency for Numeric Close + Numeric Reconciliations + Numeric Flux Analysis + Numeric Variance Investigation + Numeric Transaction Analysis + Numeric Anomaly Detection + Numeric Audit Trail, Annex III risk-classification, ISO 42001 audit-status, ASC 606 SSP-calculations evidence, IFRS 15 currency-translation-rate evidence, Wayfair-economic-nexus-50-state-decision-log evidence, close-package-bind-event-immutable-record evidence, expected turnaround 5-10 business days.
  4. Validate SOC 2 Type II scope — confirm the audit window covers the 12 months preceding contract start, and that the audit scope includes all of Numeric Close + Numeric Reconciliations + Numeric Flux Analysis + Numeric Reporting + Numeric Variance Investigation + Numeric Transaction Analysis + Numeric Anomaly Detection + Numeric Audit Trail (not just the management plane). Numeric's trust-center carries the latest SOC 2 Type II report + ISO 27001 certificate + pen-test summary + close-package-bind-event-immutability-evidence.
  5. Validate ISO 27001 + ISO 42001 status — confirm whether the ISO 27001 certificate is already awarded (Numeric is in-progress per numeric.io/security); capture the certificate number and the cert body (typically one of BSI / Schellman / Coalfire / A-LIGN). ISO 42001 (AI management system) audit-status should be in-progress per numeric.io/security.
  6. Validate GDPR DPA + Annex III + Art. 22 + Art. 53(d) — request the signed DPA, the per-AI-decision PII-handling notice for Numeric Close + Numeric Reconciliations + Numeric Flux Analysis + Numeric Variance Investigation + Numeric Transaction Analysis + Numeric Anomaly Detection + Numeric Audit Trail, the per-journal-entry AI-classification model-card, the per-reconciliation AI-tie-out model-card, the per-flux-analysis AI-narrative-generation model-card, the per-ASC-606-SSP-decision model-card, the per-IFRS-15-currency-translation-decision model-card, the per-Wayfair-economic-nexus-decision model-card, the per-variance-investigation-natural-language-decision model-card, and the per-ML-model training-corpus-source + per-customer-financial-data license-provenance summary. Numeric publishes a sub-processor list; validate that all downstream vendors (Stripe for Stripe Tax integration, Plaid for bank-feed ingestion, Ramp/Brex/Mercury for card-feed ingestion, BILL for bill-pay integration, AWS for infrastructure, Google Cloud for some workloads, OpenAI/Anthropic for LLM inference) are listed with their own DPA + sub-processor flow-down.
  7. Validate data-residency posture + multi-entity isolation + ASC 606 + IFRS 15 + Wayfair + close-package-bind-event-immutability — confirm US vs EU vs UK (London) vs multi-region cluster availability, per-customer data-segregation, per-entity-ledger-isolation, per-customer-multi-entity-state, per-Numeric-Multi-Entity-elimination isolation, per-ASC-606-performance-obligation-isolation, per-IFRS-15-currency-translation-rate isolation, per-Wayfair-economic-nexus-decision isolation, the per-customer-AI-Flux-Analysis-Narrative-isolation, the per-customer-AI-Reconciliation-isolation, the per-customer-AI-Close-Package-bind-event-isolation, the per-customer-AI-Audit-Trail-isolation, and the per-customer-AI-Variance-Investigation-query-history-isolation, plus the per-close-package-bind-event-immutability-evidence (typically AWS QLDB / Immutable S3 / QLDB-Stream / S3-Object-Lock with retention-period-of-record).
  8. Validate billing + cost-attribution transparency — Numeric exposes per-journal-entry + per-reconciliation + per-flux-analysis-narrative + per-close-package-bind-event + per-Variance-Investigation-natural-language-query-cost + per-Transaction-Analysis-categorization-cost + per-Anomaly-Detection-cost + per-Stripe-Plaid-Ramp-Brex-Mercury-BILL-bank-feed-ingestion-cost feeds for cost-attribution. Confirm you can export these feeds into your downstream-Snowflake / BigQuery / Databricks / Sage Intacct / NetSuite / Xero / QBO cost-allocation pipeline, including WORM retention per SEC 17a-4 + IRS 6001 + SOX 404.

5-criteria scoring rubric for any Numeric-tier deal

Criterion Weight Numeric baseline (2026) Notes for buyer
AI-decision reasoning-chain provenance depth (SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 9.4) 30% 20-column join-table per AI-decision (per-reconciliation-id + per-tenant-id + per-source-transaction-id + per-AI-tie-out-decision-id + per-AI-flux-variance-decision-id + per-AI-confidence-score + per-AI-human-review-required-bit + per-journal-entry-uuid + per-AI-journal-entry-classification-decision-id + per-LLM-prompt-text + per-LLM-completion-text + per-LLM-token-cost + per-downstream-ERP-state-change-id + per-close-package-bind-event-id + per-GL-account-id + per-period-close-id + per-Stripe-Plaid-Ramp-Brex-Mercury-BILL-feed-source-of-truth-id + per-ASC-606-performance-obligation-id + per-SSP-component-id + per-IFRS-15-currency-translation-rate-id) Most close-automation vendors only log the GL-classification decision, not the close-package-bind-event-decision + per-ASC-606-SSP-decision + per-Wayfair-nexus-decision + per-multi-entity-elimination. Confirm the join-table covers all AI-decisions, not just journal-entry-classification.
ASC 606 + IFRS 15 + Wayfair-economic-nexus + close-package-bind-event decision-log coverage 20% Per-ASC-606-performance-obligation-id + per-SSP-component-id + per-variable-consideration-estimate-id + per-IFRS-15-currency-translation-rate-id + per-Wayfair-economic-nexus-state-id + per-50-state-nexus-decision-id + per-Stripe-Tax-rate-id + per-close-package-bind-event-id + per-close-package-version-id + per-close-package-sign-off-approver-id + per-audit-committee-pack-distribution-id ASC 606 + IFRS 15 + Wayfair + close-package-bind-event compliance are the regulatory hot-zone for SaaS-mid-market-accounting-teams + EU-AI-Act Annex III 4. Confirm per-decision evidence for each, not just a summary roll-up.
EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data-transparency disclosure 15% Per-customer-financial-data-license-provenance + per-customer-bank-feed-data-license-provenance + per-customer-Stripe-fee-data-license-provenance + per-customer-variance-investigation-query-history-license-provenance + per-customer-flux-analysis-narrative-license-provenance EU AI Act Art. 53(d) requires GPAI providers to publish a training-data-transparency summary. Confirm Numeric publishes this for every model, not just one.
Cross-tenant Numeric SaaS + multi-entity + close-package-bind-event isolation-evidence (SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + EU AI Act Art. 10 + FedRAMP-analog-finance-ops-considerations) 20% Per-tenant ledger-isolation + per-entity-isolation + per-AI-feature-resolved-data + per-close-package-bind-event-immutability + per-Audit-Trail-isolation + per-Stripe-Plaid-Ramp-Brex-Mercury-BILL-bank-feed-isolation + per-Wayfair-50-state-isolation + per-multi-entity-elimination-isolation + per-ASC-606-SSP-isolation + per-IFRS-15-currency-translation-isolation Most close-automation vendors fail to isolate per-tenant-AI-feature-resolved-data + per-close-package-bind-event. Confirm hard-isolation tests pass.
WORM retention + cost-attribution (SEC 17a-4 WORM + IRS 6001 + SOX 404) 15% 20+ join-table linkage with per-AI-decision-cost + per-Stripe-Plaid-Ramp-Brex-Mercury-BILL-bank-feed-ingestion-cost feeds + WORM retention on the join-table itself WORM retention is the deciding factor for public-company + regulated-entity mid-market-buyers. Confirm retention-period-of-record matches your 7-year-FINRA-style or 17a-4 WORM policy.

Real-world 14-step audit conversation (Talon Forge Atlas framework)

When you walk into the room with Numeric's security + legal team, here's how the conversation usually flows — each step has a concrete artifact to request:

  1. Step 1 — Open with the privacy@ surface. "I found mailto:privacy@numeric.io at numeric.io/legal/privacy-policy. Who triages this inbox — is it the legal team, the security team, or a shared operations rotation?"
  2. Step 2 — Anchor on EU AI Act timeline. "What's your readiness posture for the August 2026 EU AI Act go-live for finance-ops high-risk use-cases like automated-journal-entry-classification + automated-reconciliation + automated-flux-analysis-narrative-generation?"
  3. Step 3 — Press on SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 9.4 reasoning-chain provenance depth. "For each AI-decision in Numeric Close + Numeric Reconciliations + Numeric Flux Analysis + Numeric Variance Investigation + Numeric Transaction Analysis + Numeric Anomaly Detection + Numeric Audit Trail, what's the per-decision join-table evidence-column-list (how many columns, and what are they)?"
  4. Step 4 — Pivot to close-package-bind-event-as-immutable-audit-record. "Can you show me the per-close-package-bind-event-immutability-evidence — typically AWS QLDB / Immutable S3 / QLDB-Stream / S3-Object-Lock — with retention-period-of-record?"
  5. Step 5 — Press on SOC 2 Type II report + ISO 27001 certificate currency. "Can I get the latest SOC 2 Type II report (full + bridge-letter) + ISO 27001 certificate + EU AI Act readiness-disclosure-summary?"
  6. Step 6 — Confirm the GDPR DPA + sub-processor list + Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data-transparency. "Walk me through the DPA signature flow (your standard DPA + customer-side redlines + signature SLA) + sub-processor-list + per-model Art. 53(d) training-data-transparency-summary publication location."
  7. Step 7 — Quantify the AI-flux-analysis-narrative auto-generation confidence-threshold. "What's the AI-confidence-score threshold above which an AI-flux-analysis-narrative auto-binds to the close-package without human-review-required-bit — and do you expose the per-decision threshold-tuning per customer?"
  8. Step 8 — Map the multi-entity consolidation + ASC 606 + IFRS 15 + Wayfair integration. "For a SaaS-mid-market customer running 3 legal entities + ASC 606 + IFRS 15 + Wayfair-economic-nexus + EU-revenue above €15M ARR, what's the per-AI-decision evidence-column-list — and can you show me a sample close-package with per-decision evidence?"
  9. Step 9 — Push on prompt-injection + retrieval-source-poisoning defense. "If a Numeric customer uploads a maliciously-crafted PDF-invoice that contains a hidden prompt-injection-instruction aimed at making the AI-flux-analysis-narrative misclassify a $1M expense as a legitimate vendor-bill, what defenses fire — and do they fire before or after the close-package-bind-event?"
  10. Step 10 — Quantify cross-tenant isolation-evidence. "Walk me through the per-tenant-AI-Flux-Analysis-Narrative-isolation + per-tenant-AI-Reconciliation-isolation + per-tenant-AI-Close-Package-bind-event-isolation — and how Numeric demonstrates it via quarterly penetration-test-summary + SOC 2 CC6.1 + EU AI Act Art. 10 tests."
  11. Step 11 — Verify billing + cost-attribution transparency feeds. "Show me the per-customer-AI-Flux-Analysis-Narrative-cost + per-customer-AI-Reconciliation-cost + per-customer-AI-Close-Package-bind-event-cost + per-customer-AI-Variance-Investigation-cost feed-export — and how it joins to Snowflake / BigQuery / Databricks / Sage Intacct / NetSuite / Xero / QBO cost-allocation pipelines."
  12. Step 12 — Press on data-residency + UK-LDN-NYC-SF multiregion posture. "If I want my data to live in EU-Frankfurt-only or UK-London-only, what's the cluster-isolation-evidence — and is Numeric Audit Trail + Numeric Anomaly Detection + Numeric Transaction Analysis data-residency-bound to the same cluster?"
  13. Step 13 — Confirm incident-response + breach-notification SLA. "If you discover a Numeric-side breach affecting my AI-Flux-Analysis-Narrative-data, what's your breach-notification SLA to me via my DPA — 24h? 48h? 72h? — and does it cover AI-training-corpus-contamination + per-customer-AI-feature-resolved-data-exfiltration + close-package-bind-event-tampering-detection?"
  14. Step 14 — Close with the AI-native-ERP-Evidence-column gap.
