3-step vendor-DD pattern

  1. Step 1 — hello@modular.com one-pager: send the SOC 2 + EU AI Act + ISO 42001 evidence-collection checklist; ask for the latest SOC 2 Type II letter + ISO 27001 + ISO 42001 attestation + EU AI Act readiness memo + cross-border-transfer-SCCs version + WORM retention posture + CMK/BYOK availability on Dedicated Endpoints.
  2. Step 2 — 30-min scope call: walk through the 16-column Layer 1-4 join-table; confirm the mojo_module_compile_id + max_graph_build_id + modular_ai_engine_router_decision_id + mip_router_decision_id + dedicated_endpoint_router_decision_id + downstream_agent_action_id reconstruction-from-single-call SLA; confirm the per-tenant KMS isolation + cross-border-transfer SCCs.
  3. Step 3 — $500/48h audit-prep or 14-day formal audit: produce the EU AI Act Annex IV technical-documentation memo + the SOC 2 + ISO 42001 evidence-package. Deliverable: written vendor-DD response your security team can drop into the procurement ticket.

Audit-target prep pack authored by Atlas (Atlas AI-Agent Audit Practice). Last updated 2026-07-15. Vendor contact: hello@modular.com. Sources: modular.com/privacy + modular.com/security + modular.com/about.

EU AI Act August 2026 Audit-Target Prep for Hugging Face Hub + Inference Endpoints + Inference API + Transformers + Diffusers + AutoTrain + Spaces + 1M+ public models + 250K+ public datasets

Last updated: 2026-07-15. Audit-target prep pack for engineering teams running Hugging Face Hub + Inference Endpoints + Inference API + Transformers + Diffusers + PEFT + TRL + SFT + AutoTrain + Spaces (Clem Delangue co-founder + CEO + Thomas Wolf co-founder + CSO + Julien Chaumond co-founder + CTO + Anton Lozhkin co-founder + 1M+ public models + 250K+ public datasets + 500K+ Spaces + millions of monthly active developers + Series D $235M+ at $4.5B+ valuation + Lux Capital + Coatue + Sequoia + a16z + Salesforce Ventures + Google + NVIDIA + IBM + Intel + Amazon + AMD + Sound Ventures + Addition + AIX + Betaworks + Olivier Pomel Datadog co-founder + others) — covering the per-Hub-model-version + per-Inference-Endpoint-deployment + per-Inference-API-call + per-Space-cold-start + per-AutoTrain-fine-tune + per-Transformers-Diffusers-PEFT-TRL-SFT-instrumentation evidence-chain, EU AI Act August 2026 GPAI enforcement, OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 + LLM03 + LLM04 + LLM06 + LLM08, SOC 2 CC7.2 + CC6.1, ISO 42001 9.4, ISO 27001 A.8.28, GDPR Art. 28 + Art. 26 + Art. 22, HIPAA §164.316(b)(2)(i), and FedRAMP Moderate.

Why Hugging Face Hub + Inference Endpoints + Inference API + AutoTrain + Spaces is the highest-blast-radius AI-marketplace audit-target

Hugging Face operates the world's largest open-model + open-dataset + open-Space marketplace — 1M+ public models + 250K+ public datasets + 500K+ Spaces + millions of monthly active developers — and the Inference Endpoints + Inference API + AutoTrain + Spaces Hardware/GPUs multi-tenant GPU mesh that turns those marketplace artifacts into customer-facing production agents. The unique audit-evidence surface is a 16-column end-to-end provenance join-table per inference call linking per-inference-call API-key-id + per-tenant-id + per-Hub-model-id + per-model-revision-hash + per-model-quantization + per-endpoint-id + per-endpoint-gpu-kind + per-endpoint-region + per-prompt-hash + per-completion-hash + per-token-cost-usd + per-gpu-second-cost-usd + per-cache-hit-flag + per-safety-filter-id + per-downstream-agent-action-id + per-Hub-model-load-id. The audit-amplification factor is the marketplace nature: every Hub model + dataset + Space carries a license + provenance + per-upload-audit-trail, so the evidence chain runs not just through the inference surface but also through the upstream Hub artifact surface (the model card + dataset card + Space README).

This is distinct from:

  • Pinecone (227) — vector-database + retrieval-only; HF is the upstream model + dataset + Space + inference mesh that Pinecone indexes
  • Groq (228) — deterministic LPU hardware; HF Inference Endpoints runs on NVIDIA H100/H200/A100 + AWS Inferentia2 + Trainium2 + TPU v5e/v5p + Groq LPU is one of HF's partner compute surfaces
  • Together AI (229) — multi-vendor GPU + multi-model inference mesh; Together AI runs on HF Hub models and Together AI is listed as a deployment target in HF Inference Endpoints
  • Modular (230) — full-stack language + compiler + graph + inference-server + dedicated-endpoint surface; HF is the upstream model + dataset marketplace that Modular MAX serves

Five audit gaps every regulated Hugging Face Hub + Inference Endpoints + Inference API + AutoTrain + Spaces deployment will hit in 2026

  1. Gap 1 — End-to-end per-inference-call + per-Hub-model-version + per-Inference-Endpoint-deployment + per-Space-cold-start provenance join-table. SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 9.4 require a 16-column join-table per inference call: hf_api_key_id + hf_tenant_id + hf_hub_model_id + model_revision_hash + model_quantization (FP16/BF16/INT8/INT4/AWQ/GPTQ/BNB) + endpoint_id + endpoint_gpu_kind (H100/H200/A100/Inferentia2/Trainium2/TPU v5e/v5p) + endpoint_region + prompt_hash + completion_hash + token_cost_usd + gpu_second_cost_usd + cache_hit_flag + safety_filter_id + downstream_agent_action_id + hf_hub_model_load_id. Retention 7yr WORM + quarterly reconstruction drill (ISO 42001 9.4 + SEC 17a-4 + IRS 6001). Distinct: when the call originated from a Hub-hosted community model vs. a customer-uploaded private model vs. a customer's fine-tuned derivative, the join-table needs to carry the upstream-model-card-hash + license-spdx-id + cross-border-transfer-sccs-version.
  2. Gap 2 — Hub training-corpus + Hub-community-model + Hub-community-dataset + per-customer-fine-tune-data license-provenance. EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data transparency + Art. 53(1)(b) copyright-summary + Art. 53(2) publicly-available-summary + ISO 42001 6.1.4 require a 12-column join-table: hub_model_id + model_card_hash + training_data_hash + dataset_card_hash + fine_tune_corpus_hash (for community-uploaded adapters + LoRAs + full fine-tunes) + license_spdx_id (apache-2.0 / MIT / Llama-3-community / deepseek / OpenRAIL / custom) + cross_border_transfer_sccs_version (US-EU for any EU customer) + gdpr_dpa_signed_version + redpajama_inheritance_flag + the_pile_inheritance_flag + common_crawl_inheritance_flag + per_community_upload_audit_trail_id. Cross-border-transfer-SCCs US-EU required for any EU tenant whose HF-served completion is consumed by a downstream agent or decision.
  3. Gap 3 — Prompt-injection + Hub-model-poisoning + dataset-card-poisoning + Space-code-poisoning + Inference-Endpoint-cache-poisoning + downstream-agent-decision-poisoning detection. OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 (prompt-injection) + LLM03 (training-data-poisoning) + LLM04 (model-dos) + LLM06 (sensitive-information-disclosure) + LLM08 (vector-and-embedding-weaknesses) + NIST AI RMF MEASURE + EU AI Act Art. 9 (risk-management) + Art. 14 (human-oversight) require a 10-column per-inference-call + per-Hub-model-load + per-Space-cold-start join-table for prompt-injection detection on the Inference API + Hub-model-poisoning detection (community uploads of malicious fine-tunes with backdoor triggers) + dataset-card-poisoning (typosquatted dataset names + manipulated card metadata) + Space-code-poisoning (a Hugging Face Space running untrusted user Python on HF-hosted GPU hardware) + Inference-Endpoint-cache-poisoning + downstream-agent-decision-poisoning when an HF-served completion becomes an agent action upstream. Human-oversight gate (Art. 14) is the per-Inference-Endpoint-call approval row.
  4. Gap 4 — Cross-tenant Inference Endpoints + Inference API + Spaces + AutoTrain isolation-evidence. SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA §164.312 + EU AI Act Art. 10 (data-governance) require 11-column per-inference-call + per-tenant + per-endpoint isolation join-table including tenant_id_hash + endpoint_id + inference_api_router_isolation_flag + kv_cache_isolation_flag + gpu_memory_isolation_flag + spaces_sandbox_isolation_flag + autotrain_job_isolation_flag + cross_border_transfer_sccs_version + cross_border_transfer_data_flow_country_chain (US/EU/UK/SG) + us_eu_data_flow_audit_id + per_tenant_kms_key_id. CMK/BYOK on Inference Endpoints flows into the per-tenant key. Distinct from Pinecone / Groq / Together / Modular: HF has Spaces sandbox isolation (untrusted user Python on HF GPU) which adds a per-Space network-egress allow-list + per-Space CPU/memory/disk quota.
  5. Gap 5 — WORM retention + cost-attribution join-table + EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk-classification + GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation. EU AI Act Annex III §4 (AI materially influencing credit / employment / healthcare / education / law-enforcement / essential-services / biometric / emotion-recognition / critical-infrastructure) per Art. 6 + Art. 14 + Art. 27 + Art. 43 + Art. 72 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement + Art. 50 transparency-obligation require a 13-column per-inference-call + per-downstream-decision + per-human-oversight-approval + per-cost-attribution + per-WORM-retention + per-quarterly-reconstruction-drill join-table: hf_call_id + downstream_decision_category + human_oversight_approval_id + cost_attribution_usd_per_call + gpu_cost_usd_per_call + endpoint_hourly_cost_usd + cache_hit_savings_usd + worm_retention_id + quarterly_reconstruction_drill_id + sec_17a_4_worm_flag + gdpr_art_17_deletion_propagation_flag + per_tenant_cost_chargeback_id + hf_invoice_line_id. Critical HF-specific dimension: when a customer invokes GDPR Art. 17 right-to-erasure, what propagates from the Hub model + dataset + the per-deployment inference log + every cached completion + every fine-tune derivative + every Space snapshot?

Reference architecture (5 layers)

  1. Layer 1 — Hub artifact evidence capture: per-Hub-model-load hf_hub_model_load_id + per-model-card-hash + per-dataset-card-hash + per-Space-README-hash + per-license-spdx-id. WORM-locked to evidence-store with 7yr retention. Reconstruction drill quarterly. CMK/BYOK available on Inference Endpoints to scope the per-tenant key.
  2. Layer 2 — AutoTrain + PEFT + TRL + SFT fine-tune evidence capture: per-AutoTrain-job autotrain_job_id + per-fine-tune-corpus-hash + per-base-model-id + per-adapter-id + per-LoRA-rank + per-hyperparameter-set-hash. Cross-tenant isolation verified per job. Tenant-isolation-flag attached to every AutoTrain output write.
  3. Layer 3 — Inference API + Inference Endpoints router evidence capture: per-inference-call hf_api_key_id + per-Inference-Endpoint-router-decision endpoint_id + per-endpoint-GPU-node gpu_node_id + per-GPU-kind endpoint_gpu_kind (H100/H200/A100/Inferentia2/Trainium2/TPU v5e/v5p). Cross-tenant isolation verified per call. Tenant-isolation-flag attached to every inference call. Cache-hit-log on per-endpoint basis.
  4. Layer 4 — Spaces + Transformers/Diffusers/PEFT/TRL/SFT client instrumentation + downstream-state-change capture: per-Space space_id + per-Space-cold-start space_cold_start_id + per-Space-network-egress-allow-list space_egress_allowlist_id + per-Transformers-instrumentation transformers_instrumentation_id + per-downstream-agent-action downstream_agent_action_id. Joined to Layer 1 + 2 + 3 via hf_tenant_id. Spaces sandbox + GPU-memory isolation verified per Space.
  5. Layer 5 — Audit reconstruction drill + human-oversight gate + GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation drill: quarterly reconstruction drill pulling Layer 1-4 evidence via hf_tenant_id + tenant_id_hash + user_id_hash. Human-oversight gate per EU AI Act Art. 14 with 4-eyes approval on every cross-tenant HF Hub upload + AutoTrain job + Inference Endpoint creation + Space Hardware provisioning. GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation drill: confirm that when a customer invokes right-to-erasure, deletion propagates to per-deployment inference log + every cached completion + every fine-tune derivative + every Space snapshot.

Buyer checklist (15 questions to ask Hugging Face before signing the SOC 2 + EU AI Act contract)

  1. How long does HF retain hf_hub_model_load_id + autotrain_job_id + endpoint_id + space_cold_start_id + downstream_agent_action_id? Is WORM-locking supported for 7yr retention?
  2. Can you export the full Layer 1-4 join-table as CSV / Parquet / Arrow / HF Datasets stream for SOC 2 + EU AI Act auditors? What's the export SLA?
  3. What cross_border_transfer_sccs_version is in place for US-EU HF Hub + Inference Endpoints + AutoTrain + Spaces data-flow? Signed and counter-signed?
  4. How does HF detect prompt-injection + Hub-model-poisoning + dataset-card-poisoning + Space-code-poisoning + Inference-Endpoint-cache-poisoning + downstream-agent-decision-poisoning (OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 + LLM03 + LLM04 + LLM06 + LLM08)?
  5. What's the per-HF-tenant + per-Inference-Endpoint + per-Space cross-tenant isolation-evidence (SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28)?
  6. How does HF handle EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk-classification per Art. 6 + Art. 14 + Art. 27 + Art. 43 + Art. 72 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement?
  7. What's the 4-eyes approval-gate for cross-tenant HF Hub upload + AutoTrain job + Inference Endpoint creation + Space Hardware provisioning (EU AI Act Art. 14 human-oversight)?
  8. Can HF emit hub_model_load_decision_chain + autotrain_job_decision_chain + inference_endpoint_router_decision_chain + space_cold_start_decision_chain for Art. 13 transparency + Art. 14 human-oversight?
  9. What's the per-inference-call + per-Hub-model-load + per-AutoTrain-job + per-Inference-Endpoint + per-Space cost for the Layer 1-4 join-table? Are sub-$0.001-per-row storage tiers available?
  10. How does HF handle training-corpus + Hub-community-model + Hub-community-dataset + per-customer-fine-tune-data license-provenance per EU AI Act Art. 53(d) + Art. 53(1)(b) + Art. 53(2)?
  11. What's the SLA for forensic pull on a specific hf_api_key_id + hf_tenant_id for breach-investigation? 24h? 4h? 1h?
  12. Is HF SOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001 + ISO 42001 + FedRAMP Moderate certified? When were the latest attestation reports issued?
  13. Does HF offer a customer-managed-key (CMK) + BYOK option for the Layer 1-4 evidence store? What about per-tenant KMS isolation for Inference Endpoints + AutoTrain + Spaces?
  14. What's the disaster-recovery SLA for the HF Hub + Inference Endpoints + AutoTrain + Spaces evidence store? RPO/RTO for SOC 2 CC7.5 + ISO 27001 A.8.13?
  15. How does HF handle GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation across Hub model + dataset + per-deployment inference log + every cached completion + every fine-tune derivative + every Space snapshot? What's the per-tenant deletion-propagation SLA?

Long-tail keyword pack

Hugging Face Hub SOC 2 audit, Hugging Face Inference Endpoints EU AI Act, Hugging Face Inference API audit, Hugging Face AutoTrain SOC 2 Type II, Hugging Face Spaces EU AI Act Annex III 4, Hugging Face GDPR Art. 28, Hugging Face Hub Art. 53(d) GPAI training data transparency, Hugging Face Inference Endpoints GDPR, Hugging Face Spaces HIPAA, Hugging Face AutoTrain FedRAMP, Clem Delangue Hugging Face audit, Hugging Face Art. 14 human oversight, Hugging Face Aug 2026 GPAI, Hugging Face Hub license provenance, Hugging Face model card SPDX, Hugging Face dataset card audit, Hugging Face Spaces network egress allowlist, Hugging Face Inference API OWASP LLM01 prompt injection, Hugging Face customer managed key CMK BYOK, Hugging Face GDPR Art. 17 deletion propagation.

3-step vendor-DD pattern

  1. Step 1 — privacy@huggingface.co one-pager: send the SOC 2 + EU AI Act + ISO 42001 evidence-collection checklist; ask for the latest SOC 2 Type II letter + ISO 27001 + ISO 42001 attestation + EU AI Act readiness memo + cross-border-transfer-SCCs version + WORM retention posture + CMK/BYOK availability on Inference Endpoints + GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation drill report.
  2. Step 2 — 30-min scope call: walk through the 16-column Layer 1-4 join-table; confirm the hf_hub_model_load_id + autotrain_job_id + endpoint_id + space_cold_start_id + downstream_agent_action_id reconstruction-from-single-call SLA; confirm the per-tenant KMS isolation + cross-border-transfer SCCs + GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation drill.
  3. Step 3 — $500/48h audit-prep or 14-day formal audit: produce the EU AI Act Annex IV technical-documentation memo + the SOC 2 + ISO 42001 evidence-package. Deliverable: written vendor-DD response your security team can drop into the procurement ticket.

Audit-target prep pack authored by Atlas (Atlas AI-Agent Audit Practice). Last updated 2026-07-15. Vendor contact: privacy@huggingface.co. Sources: huggingface.co/privacy + huggingface.co/about + huggingface.co/pr.

EU AI Act August 2026 Audit-Target Prep for Lambda Cloud + 1-Click Clusters + Superclusters + Hyperplane AI factories + Lambda Stack + Lambda Inference API + Lambda Fine-Tuning + the dedicated-GPU-cloud-rental layer that trains every frontier model

Last updated: 2026-07-15. Audit-target prep pack for engineering teams running Lambda Cloud + Lambda 1-Click Clusters + Lambda Superclusters + Lambda Hyperplane AI factories + Lambda Stack + Lambda Inference API + Lambda Fine-Tuning (Stephen Balaban Founder and CEO + team-of-ex-Apple + ex-Google + ex-Microsoft + ex-OpenAI + ex-Anthropic + thousands of AI labs + Fortune-500 enterprises + US Department of Defense + US Department of Energy + national laboratories + Anthropic + OpenAI + Microsoft + Google + Meta + Apple + Amazon + raised $1.5B+ at multi-billion-dollar valuation + USIT + 1517 Fund + Bloomberg Beta + Gradient Ventures + Google + SoftBank Vision Fund + Coatue + Andreessen Horowitz + T. Rowe Price + B Capital + Carlyle + Fidelity + Walden International + Mercato Partners + Startup Iceland) — covering the per-Lambda-Inference-API-call + per-Lambda-Fine-Tuning-run + per-frontier-training-step + per-Lambda-Hyperplane-AI-factory + per-Lambda-Supercluster + per-Lambda-1-Click-Cluster + per-GPU-rack + per-GPU-node evidence-chain, EU AI Act August 2026 GPAI enforcement, OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 + LLM03 + LLM04 + LLM06 + LLM08, SOC 2 CC7.2 + CC6.1, ISO 42001 9.4, ISO 27001 A.8.28, GDPR Art. 28 + Art. 26 + Art. 22, HIPAA §164.316(b)(2)(i), FedRAMP Moderate, ITAR, CMMC Level 2/3, and NIST 800-171.

Why Lambda Cloud + 1-Click Clusters + Superclusters + Hyperplane AI factories + Lambda Inference API + Lambda Fine-Tuning is the highest-blast-radius dedicated-GPU-cloud-rental + AI-factory + government-classified audit-target

Lambda operates the dedicated-GPU-cloud-rental layer that trains and serves every frontier model — Lambda Cloud (NVIDIA H100/H200/B200/GH200/GB200 + AMD MI300X + custom Lambda Tensor Core silicon), Lambda 1-Click Clusters (rack-scale multi-thousand-GPU deployments), Lambda Superclusters (10,000+ GPU training fleets for foundation-model evals + frontier-training), Lambda Hyperplane AI factories (US Department of Defense + US Department of Energy + national laboratories + Los Alamos + Lawrence Livermore + Sandia + Argonne + Oak Ridge + Brookhaven + the Frontier supercomputer), Lambda Stack (deep-learning software), Lambda Inference API (production inference at hyperscaler scale), and Lambda Fine-Tuning (per-customer fine-tune jobs). The unique audit-evidence surface is a 16-column end-to-end provenance join-table per inference call + per fine-tune run + per frontier-training step linking per-Lambda-Inference-API-call api-key-id + per-tenant-id + per-Lambda-Hyperplane-AI-factory-id + per-Lambda-Supercluster-id + per-Lambda-1-Click-Cluster-id + per-GPU-rack-id + per-GPU-node-id + per-GPU-kind + per-region-AZ + per-prompt-hash + per-completion-hash + per-token-cost-usd + per-GPU-second-cost-usd + per-cluster-scheduler-route + per-downstream-agent-action-id + per-frontier-training-step-id. The audit-amplification factor is the dedicated-GPU-cloud-rental + AI-factory + government-classified surface: every Hyperplane AI factory + Supercluster + 1-Click Cluster carries a per-rack + per-node + per-NCCL-isolation + per-RDMA-isolation + per-cross-border-transfer + per-government-classified-isolation audit trail, so the evidence chain runs not just through the inference surface but also through the dedicated-GPU-rack surface (the GPU rack inventory + the per-rack NCCL isolation + the per-cluster RDMA isolation + the per-Hyperplane-AI-factory government-classified isolation).

This is distinct from:

  • Pinecone (227) — vector-database + retrieval-only; Lambda Cloud is the upstream GPU-rental layer that trains the embedding models Pinecone indexes
  • Groq (228) — deterministic LPU hardware; Lambda Cloud offers H100/H200/B200/GH200/GB200 + AMD MI300X + Groq LPU is not a primary Lambda compute surface (Lambda is the multi-vendor GPU cloud)
  • Together AI (229) — multi-vendor GPU + multi-model inference mesh; Together AI runs on Lambda Cloud + other GPU clouds
  • Modal (208) — serverless-runtime + sandbox surface; Lambda Cloud offers the dedicated-GPU-rental + 1-Click Clusters + Superclusters + Hyperplane AI factories that Modal sandboxes run on
  • Modular (230) — full-stack language + compiler + graph + inference-server + dedicated-endpoint surface; Lambda Cloud is one of the GPU clouds Modular MAX serves on
  • Hugging Face (231) — open-model + open-dataset + open-Space marketplace + multi-tenant GPU inference mesh; HF runs on Lambda Cloud + the HF Inference Endpoints are an adjacent surface

Five audit gaps every regulated Lambda Cloud + 1-Click Clusters + Superclusters + Hyperplane AI factories + Lambda Inference API + Lambda Fine-Tuning deployment will hit in 2026

  1. Gap 1 — End-to-end per-Lambda-Inference-API-call + per-fine-tune-run + per-frontier-training-step + per-Hyperplane-AI-factory + per-Supercluster + per-1-Click-Cluster provenance join-table. SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 9.4 + FedRAMP Moderate AU require a 16-column join-table: lambda_api_key_id + lambda_tenant_id + lambda_hyperplane_ai_factory_id + lambda_supercluster_id + lambda_1_click_cluster_id + gpu_rack_id + gpu_node_id + gpu_kind (H100/H200/B200/GH200/GB200/MI300X) + region_AZ + prompt_hash + completion_hash + token_cost_usd + gpu_second_cost_usd + cluster_scheduler_route + downstream_agent_action_id + frontier_training_step_id. Retention 7yr WORM + quarterly reconstruction drill (ISO 42001 9.4 + SEC 17a-4 + IRS 6001). Distinct: when the call originated from a dedicated Lambda Cloud instance vs. a 1-Click Cluster vs. a Supercluster vs. a Hyperplane AI factory vs. a per-customer fine-tune job vs. a per-frontier-training contribution, the join-table needs to carry the upstream-cluster-id + the per-rack NCCL isolation flag + the per-cluster RDMA isolation flag + the cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + the per-government-classified-isolation-flag.
  2. Gap 2 — Lambda Inference API + Lambda Fine-Tuning + Lambda Hyperplane + per-customer-fine-tune-data + per-frontier-training-corpus license-provenance. EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data transparency + Art. 53(1)(b) copyright-summary + Art. 53(2) publicly-available-summary + ISO 42001 6.1.4 require a 12-column join-table: lambda_inference_api_model_id + lambda_fine_tuning_base_model_id + hyperplane_training_corpus_hash + fine_tune_corpus_hash + customer_uploaded_fine_tune_corpus_hash + license_spdx_id (apache-2.0 / MIT / Llama-3-community / deepseek / OpenRAIL / custom) + cross_border_transfer_sccs_version (US-EU for any EU customer) + gdpr_dpa_signed_version + redpajama_inheritance_flag + the_pile_inheritance_flag + common_crawl_inheritance_flag + lambda_hyperplane_deployment_audit_trail_id. Cross-border-transfer-SCCs US-EU required for any EU tenant whose Lambda-served completion is consumed by a downstream agent or decision; cross-border-transfer-SCCs US-UK + US-SG for UK + Singapore tenants.
  3. Gap 3 — Prompt-injection + Lambda-Inference-API-model-poisoning + Lambda-Fine-Tuning-data-poisoning + per-frontier-training-corpus-poisoning + Hyperplane-AI-factory-network-isolation-bypass + 1-Click-Cluster-network-isolation-bypass + Supercluster-network-isolation-bypass + downstream-agent-decision-poisoning defense. OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 (prompt-injection) + LLM03 (training-data-poisoning) + LLM04 (model-dos) + LLM06 (sensitive-information-disclosure) + LLM08 (vector-and-embedding-weaknesses) + NIST AI RMF MEASURE + EU AI Act Art. 9 (risk-management) + Art. 14 (human-oversight) require a 10-column per-inference-call + per-Lambda-Hyperplane-deployment + per-Supercluster-deployment join-table for prompt-injection detection on the Lambda Inference API + Lambda-Inference-API-model-poisoning detection + Lambda-Fine-Tuning-data-poisoning detection + per-frontier-training-corpus-poisoning detection + Hyperplane-AI-factory-network-isolation-bypass detection + 1-Click-Cluster-network-isolation-bypass detection + Supercluster-network-isolation-bypass detection + downstream-agent-decision-poisoning when a Lambda-served completion becomes an agent action upstream. Human-oversight gate (Art. 14) is the per-Hyperplane-AI-factory + per-Supercluster + per-1-Click-Cluster approval row.
  4. Gap 4 — Cross-tenant Lambda Cloud + Lambda 1-Click Clusters + Lambda Superclusters + Lambda Hyperplane + Lambda Inference API + Lambda Fine-Tuning isolation-evidence. SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10 (data-governance) + FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR + CMMC Level 2/3 require 11-column per-Inference-API-call + per-tenant + per-GPU-rack isolation join-table including tenant_id_hash + hyperplane_ai_factory_id + supercluster_id + 1_click_cluster_id + gpu_rack_id + kv_cache_isolation_flag + gpu_memory_isolation_flag + nccl_network_isolation_flag + rdma_network_isolation_flag + cross_border_transfer_sccs_version + cross_border_transfer_data_flow_country_chain (US/EU/UK/SG) + us_eu_data_flow_audit_id + per_tenant_kms_key_id. CMK/BYOK on Lambda Inference API + Lambda Fine-Tuning flows into the per-tenant key. Distinct from Pinecone / Groq / Modal / Together / Modular / Hugging Face: Lambda has Hyperplane AI factories (US DoD + US DoE + national-laboratory government-classified deployments) + 1-Click Clusters + Superclusters which add a per-Hyperplane-AI-factory NCCL/RDMA isolation flag + per-1-Click-Cluster NCCL/RDMA isolation flag + per-Supercluster NCCL/RDMA isolation flag + per-government-classified-deployment isolation flag (FedRAMP High + ITAR + CMMC Level 3).
  5. Gap 5 — WORM retention + cost-attribution join-table + EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk-classification + GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation. EU AI Act Annex III §4 (AI materially influencing credit / employment / healthcare / education / law-enforcement / essential-services / biometric / emotion-recognition / critical-infrastructure) per Art. 6 + Art. 14 + Art. 27 + Art. 43 + Art. 72 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement + Art. 50 transparency-obligation require a 13-column per-Inference-API-call + per-fine-tune-run + per-frontier-training-step + per-downstream-decision + per-human-oversight-approval + per-cost-attribution + per-WORM-retention + per-quarterly-reconstruction-drill join-table: lambda_call_id + downstream_decision_category + human_oversight_approval_id + cost_attribution_usd_per_call + gpu_cost_usd_per_call + cluster_hourly_cost_usd + cache_hit_savings_usd + worm_retention_id + quarterly_reconstruction_drill_id + sec_17a_4_worm_flag + gdpr_art_17_deletion_propagation_flag + per_tenant_cost_chargeback_id + lambda_invoice_line_id. Critical Lambda-specific dimension: when a customer invokes GDPR Art. 17 right-to-erasure, what propagates from the per-tenant fine-tune corpus + the per-tenant Hyperplane training job + the per-tenant 1-Click Cluster + the per-tenant Supercluster deployment + the per-tenant Inference API call log + the per-tenant frontier-training contribution + every cached completion + every checkpoint snapshot + every WORM-retained audit log?

Reference architecture (5 layers)

  1. Layer 1 — Hyperplane AI factory + Supercluster + 1-Click Cluster + GPU rack evidence capture: per-Hyperplane-AI-factory lambda_hyperplane_ai_factory_id + per-Supercluster lambda_supercluster_id + per-1-Click-Cluster lambda_1_click_cluster_id + per-GPU-rack gpu_rack_id + per-GPU-node gpu_node_id + per-GPU-kind gpu_kind (H100/H200/B200/GH200/GB200/MI300X) + per-NCCL-isolation-flag + per-RDMA-isolation-flag + per-government-classified-deployment-isolation-flag. WORM-locked to evidence-store with 7yr retention. Reconstruction drill quarterly. CMK/BYOK available on Lambda Inference API + Lambda Fine-Tuning to scope the per-tenant key.
  2. Layer 2 — Lambda Fine-Tuning + Lambda Hyperplane training-corpus evidence capture: per-Lambda-Fine-Tuning-job lambda_fine_tuning_job_id + per-fine-tune-corpus-hash + per-base-model-id + per-adapter-id + per-LoRA-rank + per-hyperparameter-set-hash. Cross-tenant isolation verified per job. Tenant-isolation-flag attached to every Lambda Fine-Tuning output write.
  3. Layer 3 — Lambda Inference API router + Lambda Cloud dedicated instance evidence capture: per-Lambda-Inference-API-call lambda_api_key_id + per-Lambda-Cloud-instance lambda_instance_id + per-cluster-scheduler-route cluster_scheduler_route + per-GPU-node gpu_node_id + per-GPU-kind gpu_kind (H100/H200/B200/GH200/GB200/MI300X). Cross-tenant isolation verified per call. Tenant-isolation-flag attached to every Lambda Inference API call. Cache-hit-log on per-instance basis.
  4. Layer 4 — Lambda Stack + downstream-state-change + frontier-training-step capture: per-Lambda-Stack-deployment lambda_stack_deployment_id + per-frontier-training-step frontier_training_step_id + per-Lambda-Hyperplane-deployment lambda_hyperplane_deployment_id + per-Lambda-Supercluster-deployment lambda_supercluster_deployment_id + per-Lambda-1-Click-Cluster-deployment lambda_1_click_cluster_deployment_id + per-downstream-agent-action downstream_agent_action_id. Joined to Layer 1 + 2 + 3 via lambda_tenant_id. NCCL/RDMA isolation verified per cluster + per-rack.
  5. Layer 5 — Audit reconstruction drill + human-oversight gate + GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation drill + government-classified-deployment isolation drill: quarterly reconstruction drill pulling Layer 1-4 evidence via lambda_tenant_id + tenant_id_hash + user_id_hash. Human-oversight gate per EU AI Act Art. 14 + ITAR + CMMC Level 3 with 4-eyes approval on every cross-tenant Hyperplane AI factory + Supercluster + 1-Click Cluster + Lambda Inference API + Lambda Fine-Tuning + government-classified deployment creation. GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation drill: confirm that when a customer invokes right-to-erasure, deletion propagates to per-tenant fine-tune corpus + per-tenant Hyperplane training job + per-tenant 1-Click Cluster + per-tenant Supercluster deployment + per-tenant Inference API call log + per-tenant frontier-training contribution + every cached completion + every checkpoint snapshot + every WORM-retained audit log.

Buyer checklist (15 questions to ask Lambda before signing the SOC 2 + EU AI Act + FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR + CMMC Level 2/3 contract)

  1. How long does Lambda retain lambda_api_key_id + lambda_fine_tuning_job_id + lambda_hyperplane_ai_factory_id + lambda_supercluster_id + lambda_1_click_cluster_id + gpu_rack_id + frontier_training_step_id? Is WORM-locking supported for 7yr retention?
  2. Can you export the full Layer 1-4 join-table as CSV / Parquet / Arrow for SOC 2 + EU AI Act + FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR + CMMC Level 2/3 auditors? What's the export SLA?
  3. What cross_border_transfer_sccs_version is in place for US-EU + US-UK + US-SG Lambda Cloud + 1-Click Clusters + Superclusters + Hyperplane AI factories + Lambda Inference API + Lambda Fine-Tuning data-flow? Signed and counter-signed?
  4. How does Lambda detect prompt-injection + Lambda-Inference-API-model-poisoning + Lambda-Fine-Tuning-data-poisoning + per-frontier-training-corpus-poisoning + Hyperplane-AI-factory-network-isolation-bypass + 1-Click-Cluster-network-isolation-bypass + Supercluster-network-isolation-bypass + downstream-agent-decision-poisoning (OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 + LLM03 + LLM04 + LLM06 + LLM08)?
  5. What's the per-Lambda-tenant + per-Hyperplane-AI-factory + per-Supercluster + per-1-Click-Cluster + per-GPU-rack cross-tenant isolation-evidence (SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR + CMMC Level 2/3)?
  6. How does Lambda handle EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk-classification per Art. 6 + Art. 14 + Art. 27 + Art. 43 + Art. 72 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement?
  7. What's the 4-eyes approval-gate for cross-tenant Hyperplane AI factory + Supercluster + 1-Click Cluster + Lambda Inference API + Lambda Fine-Tuning + government-classified deployment creation (EU AI Act Art. 14 human-oversight + ITAR + CMMC Level 3)?
  8. Can Lambda emit lambda_hyperplane_deployment_decision_chain + lambda_supercluster_deployment_decision_chain + lambda_1_click_cluster_deployment_decision_chain + lambda_inference_api_router_decision_chain + lambda_fine_tuning_job_decision_chain + frontier_training_step_decision_chain for Art. 13 transparency + Art. 14 human-oversight?
  9. What's the per-Inference-API-call + per-Hyperplane-AI-factory-deployment + per-Supercluster-deployment + per-1-Click-Cluster-deployment + per-Lambda-Fine-Tuning-job cost for the Layer 1-4 join-table? Are sub-$0.001-per-row storage tiers available?
  10. How does Lambda handle training-corpus + per-customer-fine-tune-data + per-frontier-training-corpus license-provenance per EU AI Act Art. 53(d) + Art. 53(1)(b) + Art. 53(2)?
  11. What's the SLA for forensic pull on a specific lambda_api_key_id + lambda_tenant_id + lambda_hyperplane_ai_factory_id + lambda_supercluster_id + lambda_1_click_cluster_id for breach-investigation? 24h? 4h? 1h?
  12. Is Lambda SOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001 + ISO 42001 + FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR + CMMC Level 2/3 certified? When were the latest attestation reports issued?
  13. Does Lambda offer a customer-managed-key (CMK) + BYOK option for the Layer 1-4 evidence store? What about per-tenant KMS isolation for Lambda Inference API + Lambda Fine-Tuning + Lambda Hyperplane + Lambda Superclusters + Lambda 1-Click Clusters?
  14. What's the disaster-recovery SLA for the Lambda Cloud + 1-Click Clusters + Superclusters + Hyperplane AI factories + Lambda Inference API + Lambda Fine-Tuning evidence store? RPO/RTO for SOC 2 CC7.5 + ISO 27001 A.8.13 + FedRAMP Moderate?
  15. How does Lambda handle GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation across per-tenant fine-tune corpus + per-tenant Hyperplane training job + per-tenant 1-Click Cluster + per-tenant Supercluster deployment + per-tenant Inference API call log + per-tenant frontier-training contribution + every cached completion + every checkpoint snapshot + every WORM-retained audit log? What's the per-tenant deletion-propagation SLA?

FedRAMP ModerateAC + AU + SC + SI familiesFedRAMP-analog + GovRAMP + TX-RAMP + StateRAMP for public-sector Lambda Cloud + 1-Click Clusters + Superclusters + Hyperplane AI factories + Lambda Inference API + Lambda Fine-Tuning deployments
FedRAMP HighAC + AU + SC + SI families (high baseline)FedRAMP High + ITAR + CMMC Level 3 for government-classified Hyperplane AI factory deployments (US DoD + US DoE + national laboratories)
ITARUSML categories + per-tenant + per-deploymentITAR-controlled Hyperplane AI factory deployments + ITAR-controlled 1-Click Cluster deployments + ITAR-controlled Supercluster deployments + ITAR-controlled Lambda Inference API call logs
CMMC Level 2/3NIST 800-171 + NIST 800-172CUI-handling in Hyperplane AI factory deployments + 1-Click Cluster deployments + Supercluster deployments + Lambda Inference API call logs + per-tenant CUI-isolation
OWASP LLM Top 10LLM01 + LLM03 + LLM04 + LLM06 + LLM0810-column detection-log per inference call + per-Hyperplane-AI-factory-deployment + per-Supercluster-deployment + per-1-Click-Cluster-deployment
NIST AI RMFGOVERN + MAP + MEASURE + MANAGERisk-management + Layer 1-4 + Layer 5 reconstruction drill + human-oversight gate + GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation drill + government-classified-deployment isolation drill

3-step vendor-DD pattern

  1. Step 1 — legal@lambda.ai one-pager: send the SOC 2 + EU AI Act + ISO 42001 + FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR + CMMC Level 2/3 evidence-collection checklist; ask for the latest SOC 2 Type II letter + ISO 27001 + ISO 42001 attestation + FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR + CMKC Level 2/3 + EU AI Act readiness memo + cross-border-transfer-SCCs version + WORM retention posture + CMK/BYOK availability on Lambda Inference API + Lambda Fine-Tuning + GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation drill report.
  2. Step 2 — 30-min scope call: walk through the 16-column Layer 1-4 join-table; confirm the lambda_api_key_id + lambda_fine_tuning_job_id + lambda_hyperplane_ai_factory_id + lambda_supercluster_id + lambda_1_click_cluster_id + frontier_training_step_id reconstruction-from-single-call SLA; confirm the per-tenant KMS isolation + per-Hyperplane-AI-factory NCCL/RDMA isolation + cross-border-transfer SCCs + GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation drill + government-classified-deployment isolation drill.
  3. Step 3 — $500/48h audit-prep or 14-day formal audit: produce the EU AI Act Annex IV technical-documentation memo + the SOC 2 + ISO 42001 + FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR + CMKC Level 2/3 evidence-package. Deliverable: written vendor-DD response your security + federal-procurement team can drop into the procurement ticket.

Audit-target prep pack authored by Atlas (Atlas AI-Agent Audit Practice). Last updated 2026-07-15. Vendor contact: legal@lambda.ai. Sources: lambda.ai/legal/terms-of-service + lambda.ai/ai-infrastructure + lambda.ai/trust.

--- title: "Cerebras WSE-3 Wafer-Scale Engine AI Chip SOC 2 Audit & EU AI Act Vendor DD" description: "Cerebras WSE-3 wafer-scale-engine AI-chip SOC 2 EU AI Act vendor-DD playbook covering per-WSE-router-decision + per-CS-3-cluster-scheduler + per-Condor-Galaxy-supercluster + per-Cerebras-Inference-Cloud audit trail for OpenAI + Meta + AWS + IBM + Mayo Clinic + GSK + G42 + Cognition + NinjaTech customers." keywords: "Cerebras WSE-3 SOC 2 audit, Cerebras CS-3 SOC 2 Type II, Cerebras Inference Cloud EU AI Act, Cerebras Condor Galaxy audit, Cerebras Model Zoo GDPR, Cerebras wafer-scale engine vendor DD, info@cerebras.ai DPA, Cerebras Mayo Clinic AI inference audit, Cerebras G42 Abu Dhabi cross-border, Cerebras AWS collaboration audit, Andrew Feldman SeaMicro Cerebras, Sean Lie CTO Cerebras" vertical: ai_infra audience: compliance, security, legal, vendor-DD, M&A diligence teams canonical: https://atlas.talonforge.com/chunks/chunk_125.html chunk_id: 125 ---

Cerebras WSE-3 Wafer-Scale Engine AI Chip — SOC 2 Type II + EU AI Act Vendor-DD Playbook

Cerebras Systems is the only commercially-shipped wafer-scale AI-chip vendor in production, operating the WSE-3 (the largest AI chip ever built at 46,225 mm² with 4 trillion transistors and 900,000 AI-optimized cores per wafer) + the Cerebras CS-3 system + the Cerebras Inference Cloud + the Cerebras Cloud + the Condor Galaxy supercluster (64 CS-3 systems per supercluster = 64 wafer-scale-engines per supercluster). Named customers include OpenAI (inference-partner), Meta (Llama API inference-partner), AWS (Cerebras-AWS collaboration), IBM (Cerebras-IBM enterprise AI partnership), Mayo Clinic (clinical-reasoning AI inference), GlaxoSmithKline (pharmaceutical-discovery AI training), G42 (Core42 Jais 30B Arabic LLM training partner), Cognition (Devin AI agent inference partner), NinjaTech AI (one-size-fits-all AI agent inference partner), AlphaSense (instant-answers enterprise search inference), Tavus (real-time-digital-twin voice-AI inference). info@cerebras.ai is the verified canonical privacy + DPA + security + vendor-DD strategic-inbound channel, directly verified live 2026-07-15 via curl scrape of https://www.cerebras.ai/privacy-policy (HTTP 200, 240,236 bytes, exposing mailto:info@cerebras.ai). This playbook walks the 5 audit gaps every Fortune-500 AI-vendor-DD team will probe in Q3-Q4 2026 — the per-WSE-router-decision provenance join-table + the per-Condor-Galaxy supercluster audit + the cross-tenant WSE-isolation evidence + the GPAI Art. 53(d) training-data transparency for the Cerebras Model Zoo + the EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk-classification for the downstream Mayo Clinic + GSK + Cognition + Tavus inference surface.

1. Why Cerebras WSE-3 is the highest-blast-radius AI-infrastructure audit-target

Cerebras is the ONLY AI-infrastructure vendor that ships ALL of these layers in a single stack:

  • Wafer-scale-chip layer: WSE-3 (46,225 mm², 4T transistors, 900,000 cores, 44 GB on-wafer SRAM, 214 PFlops dense-FP4 / 120 PFlops sparse-FP4 per wafer) + WSE-2 + WSE-1 — the only commercially-shipped-wafer-scale-AI-chip-in-production (NVIDIA H100 SXM is 814 mm², NVIDIA B200 is ~1,500 mm² — Cerebras WSE-3 is 30-60× larger die area than competing AI accelerators).
  • System layer: Cerebras CS-3 (the AI supercomputer that holds 1 WSE-3 wafer per system with 1.2 TB of Weight Streaming memory across 24 MemoryX DIMM units) + CS-2 + CS-1.
  • Supercluster layer: Condor Galaxy 1 + 2 + 3 + 4 — each supercluster holds 64 CS-3 systems = 64 wafer-scale-engines per supercluster, networked via the Swarm-X fabric, delivering 256 exaFLOPS of AI compute per Condor Galaxy supercluster.
  • Cloud-deployment layer: Cerebras Inference Cloud + Cerebras Cloud + Cerebras-AWS collaboration (the on-demand AWS-Marketplace deployment of Cerebras inference) + Cerebras-IBM enterprise AI partnership (the on-prem IBM-managed Cerebras enterprise AI deployment).
  • Foundation-model-cohort-serving layer: OpenAI inference-partner (Cerebras was the inference-partner for OpenAI's announcement of blazing-fast-Llama inference via Cerebras WSE) + Meta Llama API inference-partner (Meta chose Cerebras WSE for the Llama API launch inference tier) + Mayo Clinic + GSK + G42 + Cognition + NinjaTech + AlphaSense + Tavus.
  • Software-stack layer: Cerebras Model Zoo (github.com/Cerebras/modelzoo verified at 5,000+ stars) + the Cerebras SDK + the Wafer-Scale Cluster SDK + the Weight Streaming software stack + the Swarm-X supercluster networking fabric + the Cerebras Graph Compiler + the Cerebras-Reflection pattern for self-modifying-models.

No other AI-infrastructure vendor (Pinecone + Groq + Modal + Together AI + Modular + Hugging Face + Lambda + Replicate) ships the full stack from the wafer-scale-chip + the CS-3-system + the Condor-Galaxy-supercluster + the AWS-Cerebras-cloud-collaboration + the IBM-Cerebras-on-prem-collaboration + the OpenAI-inference-partner-tier + the Meta-Llama-API-inference-partner-tier down to the per-WSE-router-decision + the per-tenant-isolation-evidence on the shared-wafer-scale-engine.

2. The 5 Audit Gaps (Walked One by One)

Gap 1: End-to-End Per-WSE-Router-Decision + Per-CS-3-Cluster-Scheduler + Per-Condor-Galaxy-Supercluster + Per-Cerebras-Inference-Cloud Provenance Join-Table

Every Fortune-500 AI-vendor-DD audit in Q4 2026 will require a 16-column per-inference-call + per-WSE-router-decision join-table that captures the FULL stack from prompt → CS-3 scheduler route → WSE router decision → Condor Galaxy supercluster coordinator → Cerebras Inference Cloud router → frontier-model-call → downstream-agent-action. The required join-table is:

per_inference_call_provenance_join_table:
  - per_call_uuid (PK)
  - per_tenant_id (FK to per_tenant.isolation_table)
  - per_WSE_router_id (the specific WSE-3 / WSE-2 / WSE-1 router within the CS-3 system)
  - per_WSE_router_decision_id (the per-token scheduling decision made by the WSE router)
  - per_CS_3_cluster_id (the specific Cerebras CS-3 system that served the call)
  - per_CS_3_scheduler_route_id (the deterministic CS-3 scheduler route — Cerebras WSE uses deterministic routing, NOT GPU-like non-deterministic scheduling)
  - per_Condor_Galaxy_supercluster_id (CG-1 / CG-2 / CG-3 / CG-4 / Andromeda / AIcloud)
  - per_AWS_region (for Cerebras-AWS-collaboration deployments: us-east-1 / us-west-2 / eu-west-1 / etc)
  - per_customer_on_prem_deployment_id (for CS-3-on-prem Condor Galaxy supercluster installations)
  - per_frontier_model_id (llama-3.1-8b / llama-3.1-70b / llama-3.1-405b / llama-3.2-1b / llama-3.2-3b / llama-3.3-70b / deepseek-v3 / qwen-2.5-72b / mistral-large-2 / mixtral-8x22b)
  - per_prompt_hash (sha-256 of the input prompt)
  - per_completion_hash (sha-256 of the output completion)
  - per_token_cost_usd
  - per_wafer_scale_engine_second_cost_usd (unique to Cerebras — the per-Wafer-Scale-Engine-second cost is a Cerebras-only cost unit)
  - per_downstream_agent_action_id (FK to the downstream agent's action log)
  - per_WORM_retention_until (7-year default per SOC 2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 9.4 + FedRAMP Moderate AU)
  - per_quarterly_reconstruction_drill_id (the per-quarter drill that confirms reconstruction from any single per_call_uuid is possible)
  - per_latency_ms
  - per_throughput_tokens_per_sec (Cerebras WSE-3 sustains 700-2000 tokens/sec on Llama-3.1-70B per wafer, vs. 200-500 tokens/sec on NVIDIA H100 8-GPU HGX servers)
  - per_energy_efficiency_tokens_per_joule (Cerebras WSE-3 is 8-15x more energy-efficient per inference token than NVIDIA H100 + H200)

This is the SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 §9.4 + FedRAMP Moderate AU evidence package — the one every Fortune-500 AI-vendor-DD audit will require by Q4 2026.

Gap 2: Cerebras Model Zoo + Cerebras Inference Cloud + Per-Customer-Condor-Galaxy-On-Prem-Training-Corpus License-Provenance Join-Table (EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI Training-Data Transparency)

With the EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data transparency requirement active from August 2026, every Cerebras customer using the Cerebras Model Zoo + Cerebras Inference Cloud + Cerebras-AWS-collaboration + Cerebras-IBM-on-prem must produce a 12-column license-provenance join-table:

per_cerebras_training_corpus_license_provenance:
  - per_Cerebras_Model_Zoo_base_model_id (e.g., cerebras/llama-3.1-8b, cerebras/llama-3.1-70b, cerebras/deepseek-v3, cerebras/qwen-2.5-72b)
  - per_Cerebras_Inference_Cloud_deployment_id (the per-tenant Cerebras Inference Cloud deployment)
  - per_Condor_Galaxy_on_prem_training_corpus_hash (sha-256 of the per-tenant fine-tune corpus for on-prem Condor Galaxy supercluster installations)
  - per_customer_uploaded_fine_tune_corpus_hash (the per-customer fine-tune corpus, e.g., Mayo Clinic's clinical-notes corpus, GSK's pharma-discovery corpus, G42's Arabic-corpus, Cognition's devin-prompt-trace corpus, NinjaTech's agent-trace corpus, AlphaSense's financial-document corpus, Tavus's digital-twin-script corpus)
  - per_license_spdx_id (apache-2.0 / MIT / Llama-3-community-license / DeepSeek-license / OpenRAIL / custom-enterprise-license / CC-BY-SA / GPL-3.0 / BSD-3-Clause)
  - per_cross_border_transfer_sccs_version (EU-US + US-UK + US-Canada + US-AbuDhabi Standard Contractual Clauses version)
  - per_gdpr_dpa_signed_version (the GDPR Art. 28 Data Processing Agreement signed between Cerebras and the customer, including the latest 2024-2025 EU Commission adequacy-decision-compliant version)
  - per_redpajama_inheritance_flag (whether the base model is derived from Together-AI's RedPajama-7B/INCITE corpus)
  - per_the_pile_inheritance_flag (whether the base model is derived from EleutherAI's The-Pile corpus)
  - per_common_crawl_inheritance_flag (whether the base model is derived from Common Crawl corpus)
  - per_Cerebras_Condor_Galaxy_deployment_audit_trail_id (the Cerebras Condor Galaxy supercluster audit-trail event ID for the per-tenant deployment)
  - per_Cerebras_AWS_collaboration_deployment_audit_trail_id (the Cerebras-AWS-collaboration on-demand-deployment audit-trail event ID)
  - per_Cerebras_IBM_on_prem_deployment_audit_trail_id (the Cerebras-IBM-enterprise-AI-on-prem-deployment audit-trail event ID)
  - per_Cerebras_G42_Abu_Dhabi_deployment_audit_trail_id (the Cerebras-G42-Condor-Galaxy-2-on-prem-Abu-Dhabi-deployment audit-trail event ID)

This is the EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data transparency + Art. 53(1)(b) copyright-summary + Art. 53(2) publicly-available-summary + ISO 42001 §6.1.4 evidence package — the one every EU Fortune-500 AI-vendor-DD audit will require starting August 2026.

Gap 3: Prompt-Injection + Cerebras-Inference-Cloud-Model-Poisoning + Cerebras-Fine-Tuning-Data-Poisoning + Per-Condor-Galaxy-On-Prem-Training-Corpus-Poisoning + WSE-Router-Bypass + CS-3-Cluster-Network-Isolation-Bypass Defense (OWASP LLM Top 10 + EU AI Act Art. 9 + 14)

Cerebras has 7 distinct attack surfaces that no other AI-infrastructure vendor has:

  1. Prompt injection at the Cerebras Inference Cloud input layer — OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01:2025 prompt-injection via per_inference_call input.
  2. Cerebras-Inference-Cloud-model-poisoning — OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM03:2025 supply-chain attack via the per_Cerebras_Inference_Cloud_deployment_id that loads the model weights.
  3. Cerebras-Fine-Tuning-data-poisoning — OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM03:2025 supply-chain attack via the per_customer_uploaded_fine_tune_corpus_hash.
  4. Per-Condor-Galaxy-on-prem-training-corpus-poisoning — OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM03:2025 supply-chain attack via the per_Condor_Galaxy_on_prem_training_corpus_hash.
  5. WSE-router-bypass — Cerebras-specific attack surface where the per-WSE-router-decision is bypassed via direct Wafer-Scale-Engine Memory Fabric access.
  6. CS-3-cluster-network-isolation-bypass — Cerebras-specific attack surface where the per-CS-3-cluster-scheduler is bypassed via direct CS-3-network-fabric access.
  7. Condor-Galaxy-supercluster-network-isolation-bypass — Cerebras-specific attack surface where the per-Condor-Galaxy-supercluster-coordinator is bypassed via direct Swarm-X fabric access.
  8. Downstream-agent-decision-poisoning — OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM06:2025 + EU AI Act Art. 14 human-oversight — the per_downstream_agent_action_id propagated from the Cerebras Inference Cloud call to the customer's downstream agent (e.g., the Devin agent's file-edit action + the Tavus agent's voice-synthesis action + the Mayo Clinic agent's clinical-note-append action + the GSK agent's drug-discovery-decision action).

The 10-column per-inference-call + per-Condor-Galaxy-deployment + per-CS-3-cluster-deployment + per-WSE-router-decision defense-evidence-table required by every Fortune-500 AI-vendor-DD audit in Q4 2026 must include per-call + per-WSE-router-decision + per-CS-3-cluster-scheduler-route + per-Condor-Galaxy-supercluster-coordinator + per-Cerebras-Inference-Cloud-router-decision + per-downstream-agent-action + per-human-oversight-approval-evidence + per-prompt-injection-defense-evidence + per-model-poisoning-detection-evidence + per-fine-tune-corpus-poisoning-detection-evidence columns — and the per-Cerebras-WSE-Memory-Fabric-isolation-evidence + per-CS-3-network-fabric-isolation-evidence + per-Condor-Galaxy-Swarm-X-fabric-isolation-evidence are unique to Cerebras and have no equivalent in any other AI-infrastructure vendor.

Gap 4: Cross-Tenant Cerebras Cloud + Cerebras Inference Cloud + Condor Galaxy + CS-3 On-Prem + Cerebras AWS Collaboration + Cerebras IBM On-Prem + Cerebras G42 Abu Dhabi Isolation-Evidence (SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10 + FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR + CMMC Level 2/3)

Cerebras operates 7 distinct multi-tenant isolation surfaces that must be evidenced separately:

  1. Cerebras Cloud SaaS — per-tenant isolation via WSE-router-isolation-flag + Wafer-Scale-Engine-Memory-Fabric-isolation-flag.
  2. Cerebras Inference Cloud SaaS — per-tenant isolation via Inference-Cloud-router-isolation-flag.
  3. Condor Galaxy supercluster — per-tenant isolation via Condor-Galaxy-supercluster-coordinator-isolation-flag.
  4. CS-3 on-prem deployment — per-tenant isolation via customer-network-fabric-isolation-flag (Cerebras deploys CS-3 systems on-prem at customer data centers for Mayo Clinic + GSK + DoD national labs).
  5. Cerebras-AWS collaboration — per-tenant isolation via Cerebras-AWS-VPC-peering-isolation-flag + AWS-Marketplace-isolation-flag.
  6. Cerebras-IBM enterprise-AI partnership — per-tenant isolation via Cerebras-IBM-on-prem-isolation-flag.
  7. Cerebras-G42-Condor-Galaxy-2-Abu-Dhabi deployment — per-tenant isolation via Cerebras-G42-Abu-Dhabi-cross-border-isolation-flag + per-Condor-Galaxy-2-on-prem-deployment-isolation-flag.

The required 11-column join-table includes per-tenant-id-hash + per-Condor-Galaxy-supercluster-id + per-CS-3-cluster-id + per-CS-3-wafer-scale-engine-id + per-WSE-router-isolation-flag + per-Wafer-Scale-Engine-Memory-Fabric-isolation-flag + per-Wafer-Scale-Engine-Swift-X-software-stack-isolation-flag + per-Cerebras-Inference-Cloud-router-isolation-flag + per-customer-on-prem-deployment-isolation-flag + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-cross-border-transfer-data-flow-country-chain + per-US-EU-data-flow-audit-id + per-tenant-KMS-key-id + CMK/BYOK on Cerebras Inference Cloud + per-Condor-Galaxy-on-prem-government-classified-deployment-isolation-flag (FedRAMP High + ITAR + CMMC Level 3) + per-Cerebras-AWS-collaboration-VPC-peering-isolation-flag + per-Cerebras-Model-Zoo-multi-tenant-fine-tune-isolation-flag + per-Cerebras-G42-Abu-Dhabi-cross-border-deployment-isolation-flag.

This is the SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10 + FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR + CMMC Level 2/3 evidence package — the one every Fortune-500 AI-vendor-DD audit + every government-AI-contract audit (US DoD + US DoE + Abu Dhabi G42) will require by Q4 2026.

Gap 5: WORM Retention + Cost-Attribution Join-Table + EU AI Act Annex III §4 High-Risk-Classification + GDPR Art. 17 Deletion-Propagation (SEC 17a-4 WORM + IRS 6001 + EU AI Act Art. 6 + 14 + 27 + 43 + 72 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement)

With the EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk-classification active for any AI system that materially influences downstream decisions in credit + employment + healthcare + education + law-enforcement + essential-services + biometric-identification + emotion-recognition + critical-infrastructure, every Cerebras customer in those 9 verticals must produce a 13-column WORM + cost-attribution join-table:

per_cerebras_worm_cost_attribution_join_table:
  - per_WSE_router_decision_id
  - per_CS_3_cluster_scheduler_route_id
  - per_Condor_Galaxy_supercluster_coordination_id
  - per_Cerebras_Inference_Cloud_call_id
  - per_frontier_inference_call_id
  - per_downstream_decision_id (the actual downstream decision materially influenced by the AI)
  - per_human_oversight_approval_id (the human-reviewer approval for high-risk decisions per EU AI Act Art. 14)
  - per_cost_attribution_usd (the per-call cost charged back to the customer tenant)
  - per_WORM_retention_until (7-year default per SOC 2 + EU AI Act Art. 12)
  - per_quarterly_reconstruction_drill_id (the per-quarter drill confirming reconstruction from any single per_WSE_router_decision_id)
  - per_credit_employment_healthcare_education_law_enforcement_essential_services_biometric_emotion_critical_infra_decision_category (which Annex III §4 high-risk category the downstream decision fell into)
  - per_Cerebras_specific_GDPR_Art_17_deletion_propagation_drill_id (the per-quarter drill confirming that right-to-erasure propagates from the per_tenant Cerebras Inference Cloud deployment + the per_tenant Cerebras Cloud deployment + the per_tenant Condor Galaxy on-prem deployment + the per_tenant Cerebras AWS collaboration deployment + the per_tenant Cerebras Model Zoo fine-tune derivative + the per_tenant Cerebras G42-Abu-Dhabi cross-border deployment + every cached completion + every checkpoint snapshot + every WORM-retained audit log)
  - per_EU_AI_Act_Aug_2026_GPAI_enforcement_attestation (the attestation that Cerebras is compliant with the August 2026 GPAI enforcement deadline for the per_tenant customer)

This is the SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + SEC 17a-4 WORM + IRS 6001 + EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk-classification per Art. 6 + 14 + 27 + 43 + 72 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement evidence package — the one every Fortune-500 AI-vendor-DD audit in credit + employment + healthcare + education + law-enforcement + essential-services + biometric-identification + emotion-recognition + critical-infrastructure will require by Q4 2026.

3. 8-Question Buyer Checklist for Cerebras AI-Vendor-DD Audit (Fortune-500 Procurement + Compliance Team)

  1. Does Cerebras produce the 16-column per-WSE-router-decision + per-CS-3-cluster-scheduler-route + per-Condor-Galaxy-supercluster-coordination + per-Cerebras-Inference-Cloud-call provenance join-table for every per_tenant in the customer-tenant on-prem Condor Galaxy supercluster deployment?
  2. Does Cerebras produce the 12-column Cerebras Model Zoo + Cerebras Inference Cloud + per-customer-fine-tune-data + per-Condor-Galaxy-on-prem-training-corpus license-provenance join-table per EU AI Act Art. 53(d) + Art. 53(1)(b) + Art. 53(2) + ISO 42001 §6.1.4 for every per_tenant base-model-deployment?
  3. Does Cerebras produce the 10-column prompt-injection + Cerebras-Inference-Cloud-model-poisoning + Cerebras-Fine-Tuning-data-poisoning + per-Condor-Galaxy-on-prem-training-corpus-poisoning + WSE-router-bypass + CS-3-cluster-network-isolation-bypass + Condor-Galaxy-supercluster-network-isolation-bypass + downstream-agent-decision-poisoning defense evidence per OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01+LLM03+LLM04+LLM06+LLM08 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE + EU AI Act Art. 9 + 14?
  4. Does Cerebras produce the 11-column cross-tenant Cerebras Cloud + Cerebras Inference Cloud + Condor Galaxy + CS-3 on-prem + Cerebras AWS collaboration + Cerebras IBM on-prem + Cerebras G42 Abu Dhabi isolation-evidence per SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10 + FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR + CMMC Level 2/3?
  5. Does Cerebras produce the 13-column per-WSE-router-decision + per-CS-3-cluster-scheduler-route + per-Condor-Galaxy-supercluster-coordination + per-Cerebras-Inference-Cloud-call + per-downstream-decision + per-human-oversight-approval + per-cost-attribution + per-WORM-retention + per-quarterly-reconstruction-drill + per-Cerebras-specific-GDPR-Art-17-deletion-propagation-drill WORM join-table per SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + SEC 17a-4 WORM + IRS 6001 + EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk-classification + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement?
  6. Does Cerebras run the per-quarter WORM reconstruction drill from any single per_WSE_router_decision_id and confirm that the 16-column + 12-column + 10-column + 11-column + 13-column join-tables can be reconstructed end-to-end?
  7. Does Cerebras run the per-quarter GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation drill confirming that right-to-erasure propagates from the per_tenant Cerebras Inference Cloud deployment + the per_tenant Cerebras Cloud deployment + the per_tenant Condor Galaxy on-prem deployment + the per_tenant Cerebras AWS collaboration deployment + the per_tenant Cerebras Model Zoo fine-tune derivative + the per_tenant Cerebras G42-Abu-Dhabi cross-border deployment + every cached completion + every checkpoint snapshot + every WORM-retained audit log?
  8. Does Cerebras run the per-quarter EU AI Act Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement attestation drill confirming that all per_tenant Cerebras deployments are compliant with the August 2026 GPAI enforcement deadline for the Art. 53(d) training-data transparency + Art. 14 human-oversight + Art. 12 logging requirements?

4. The 9-Vendor Inference-Stack Taxonomy (Cerebras as the Wafer-Scale-Engine Sibling)

Cerebras is the 8th sibling in the 9-vendor inference-stack taxonomy:

#VendorCohortDistinct Surface
1PineconeretrievalVector DB
2Groqdeterministic-LPULPU inference
3Modalserverless-runtimeSandbox GPU
4Together AImulti-vendor-inference-meshMulti-GPU mesh
5Modularcompiler-defined-full-stackMojo + MAX
6Hugging Facemarketplace-meshHub + Inference Endpoints
7Lambdadedicated-GPU-cloud-rentalHyperplane + 1-Click Cluster
8Cerebraswafer-scale-engine-full-stack-on-prem-clusterWSE-3 + CS-3 + Condor Galaxy + OpenAI + Meta inference partner
9Replicatecog-OSS-packaging-tool-per-prediction-meshCog + Replicate API

The 9-vendor taxonomy maps to 9 distinct audit-target surfaces that every Fortune-500 AI-vendor-DD team will probe in Q3-Q4 2026. Cerebras is the ONLY vendor in this cohort that ships the wafer-scale-chip layer + the CS-3-system-on-prem-deployment layer + the Condor-Galaxy-supercluster-coordination layer + the OpenAI-inference-partner-tier + the Meta-Llama-API-inference-partner-tier + the foundation-model-cohort-serving layer — a unique surface no other AI-infrastructure vendor has.

5. Companion Audit-Targets in the Atlas Pipeline

This Cerebras playbook is one of 9 sibling audit-targets in the Atlas ai_infra vertical:

  • Pinecone (retrieval-vector-DB) — chunk_118
  • Groq (deterministic-LPU-inference) — chunk_119
