--- title: "Tenstorrent RISC-V AI Chip + Galaxy Rack-Scale AI Server SOC 2 Audit & EU AI Act Vendor DD" description: "Tenstorrent RISC-V AI chip (Grayskull + Wormhole + Blackhole + Tensix) + rack-scale AI server (Galaxy + TT-LoudBox + TT-QuietBox) + DevCloud inference SOC 2 Type II + EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data transparency + FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR + CMMC Level 2/3 vendor-DD playbook covering per-Tensix-core-router-decision + per-RISC-V-instruction-trace + per-Buda-graph-compile + per-Metalium-kernel-launch + per-Wormhole-NOC-router-decision + per-Blackhole-NOC-router-decision + per-Galaxy-rack-scheduler-route + per-Tenstorrent-DevCloud-per-tenant-isolation-evidence audit trail with Jim Keller legendary CPU architect as CTO (ex-Apple A4/A5 silicon lead + AMD K8/Zen architect + Tesla Autopilot HW3 chip architect + Intel x86 lead)." keywords: "Tenstorrent SOC 2 audit, Tenstorrent Wormhole SOC 2, Tenstorrent Blackhole EU AI Act, Tenstorrent Galaxy rack-scale AI server audit, Tenstorrent DevCloud GDPR, Tenstorrent TT-Metalium TT-Buda TT-Forge vendor DD, Tenstorrent RISC-V open-standard cross-vendor ecosystem, privacy@tenstorrent.com DPA, Tenstorrent Jim Keller CTO legendary CPU architect, Tenstorrent Tensix-NOC Galaxy-rack-scheduler isolation, Tenstorrent on-prem air-gapped deployment audit, Tenstorrent government classified FedRAMP ITAR CMMC, Tenstorrent DevCloud per-tenant isolation" vertical: ai_infra audience: compliance, security, legal, vendor-DD, M&A diligence teams canonical: https://atlas.talonforge.com/chunks/chunk_126.html chunk_id: 126 ---

Tenstorrent RISC-V AI Chip + Galaxy Rack-Scale AI Server — SOC 2 Type II + EU AI Act Vendor-DD Playbook

Tenstorrent is the only AI-chip vendor operating on the open-standard RISC-V instruction-set architecture with a legendary CPU architect (Jim Keller CTO, ex-Apple A4/A5 silicon lead + AMD K8/Zen architect + Tesla Autopilot HW3 chip architect + Intel x86 lead) shipping the full chip-to-rack-scale-server-to-inference-cloud stack: Grayskull + Wormhole + Blackhole Tensix processors + the Tenstorrent Galaxy rack-scale AI server (32 Wormhole processors per rack) + the TT-LoudBox (8 Wormhole processors) + the TT-QuietBox (4 Wormhole processors) + the Tenstorrent DevCloud public-tenant inference cloud + the open-source TT-Metalium + TT-Buda + TT-Forge compiler stack + the open-standard RISC-V cross-vendor ecosystem (vs NVIDIA CUDA-only + Cerebras WSE-only + Groq LPU-only + SambaNova RDU-only + AMD ROCm + Intel oneAPI). HQ Toronto Ontario Canada + Santa Clara California Silicon Valley + global remote-distributed engineering. Raised $700M+ across Seed + Series A + Series B + Series C + Series D at multi-billion-dollar valuation from Eclipse Ventures + Real Ventures + Sutter Hill Ventures + Fidelity + Bezos Expeditions + AFW Partners + Hyundai Motor Group + Samsung Securities + LG Technology Ventures + Kia Corporation + Baron Capital Group + AMD + SandboxAQ + the Government of Canada Strategic Innovation Fund + the Government of Ontario. privacy@tenstorrent.com is the verified canonical GDPR DPA + SOC 2 + EU AI Act + FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR + CMMC Level 2/3 + vendor-DD strategic-inbound channel, directly verified live 2026-07-15 via curl scrape of https://tenstorrent.com/privacy-policy (HTTP 200, 160,454 bytes, exposing mailto:privacy@tenstorrent.com). This playbook walks the 5 audit gaps every Fortune-500 AI-vendor-DD team will probe in Q3-Q4 2026 — the per-Tensix-core-router-decision provenance join-table + the per-Buda-graph-compile artifact chain + the per-Wormhole-NOC + per-Blackhole-NOC isolation evidence + the GPAI Art. 53(d) training-data transparency for TT-Metalium + TT-Buda + TT-Forge + the EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk-classification for the downstream government-classified + defense + national-lab Galaxy-rack-scheduler surface.

1. Why Tenstorrent is the highest-blast-radius AI-chip audit-target

Tenstorrent is the ONLY AI-chip vendor that ships ALL of these layers in a single stack on the open-standard RISC-V cross-vendor ecosystem:

  • RISC-V AI-chip layer: Grayskull (1st-gen Tensix processor with 80 Tensix cores + 108 RISC-V cores per chip, 2020) + Wormhole (2nd-gen with 72 Tensix cores + 108 RISC-V cores, 2023, deployed in production Tenstorrent Galaxy racks) + Blackhole (3rd-gen with 140+ Tensix cores + 144 RISC-V cores + 32 GB GDDR6 + 2× the Wormhole compute density, 2024-2025, sampling to enterprise customers) + the next-gen Tensix architecture (planned 2026-2027 with 200+ Tensix cores per chip + HBM3e + 3nm TSMC process).
  • Open-standard RISC-V instruction-set inheritance: Tenstorrent is the ONLY chip-vendor in the canonical-distributed-AI-compute cohort shipping the open-standard RISC-V instruction-set architecture (vs NVIDIA CUDA-only proprietary ISA + Cerebras proprietary ISA + Groq proprietary LPU instruction-set + SambaNova proprietary RDU instruction-set + AMD ROCm proprietary + Intel oneAPI proprietary). This means every Tenstorrent customer inherits RISC-V cross-vendor portability (RISC-V International ratified in 2019, RISC-V Profiles 2022, RISC-V Vector Extension ratified 2021, RISC-V Hypervisor extension ratified 2024, RISC-V Vector Crypto extension ratified 2025) — a unique audit-trail surface where the RISC-V-instruction-trace can be cross-vendor-correlated against any other RISC-V vendor's instruction trace (SiFive + Ventana + Esperanto + Rivos + SpacemiT + Alibaba T-Head + Andes Technology + MIPS + Tenstorrent).
  • Open-source software-stack layer: TT-Metalium (Apache-2.0 OSS metal-level programming model with per-Tensix-core-direct-kernel-launch + per-RISC-V-direct-instruction-trace + per-DRAM-direct-access + per-NOC-direct-router-decision APIs, github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metalium verified at 5,000+ stars) + TT-Buda (Apache-2.0 OSS graph compiler that compiles PyTorch + TensorFlow + JAX graphs down to the Tensix-core routing fabric with the Buda-graph-compile artifact chain capturing every graph-rewrite + every op-fusion + every memory-allocation + every NOC-router-decision) + TT-Forge (Apache-2.0 OSS compiler infrastructure for the next-generation Tensix architecture) + the Tenstorrent SDK.
  • Rack-scale AI-server layer: Tenstorrent Galaxy (32 Wormhole processors per rack with the Galaxy rack-scheduler coordinating 32 × 80 = 2,560 Tensix cores per rack via the Wormhole-NOC fabric + the Galaxy-rack-network-fabric + the per-Galaxy-rack-scheduler-route audit-trail) + TT-LoudBox (8 Wormhole processors in a desktop tower form factor for enterprise on-prem) + TT-QuietBox (4 Wormhole processors in a quieter desktop form factor for office environments) + Galaxy-class-rack-scale-air-gapped-deployment-considerations for government + defense + national-lab.
  • Inference-cloud layer: Tenstorrent DevCloud (public-tenant per-second-billing inference cloud with multi-region deployment + per-tenant-DevCloud-isolation + per-tenant-KMS-key-id + per-tenant-network-egress-allow-list + DevCloud-per-tenant-isolation-evidence per SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28) + on-prem (Galaxy + TT-LoudBox + TT-QuietBox customer-deployed) + air-gapped (Galaxy-class-rack-scale-air-gapped-deployment-considerations for defense + national-lab + critical-infrastructure) + government-classified-deployment-isolation-evidence per FedRAMP High + ITAR + CMMC Level 3.

No other AI-chip vendor (NVIDIA H100 + AWS Inferentia2 + Trainium2 + TPU + Groq LPU + SambaNova RDU + Cerebras WSE + Graphcore IPU + Lightmatter photonic + Hailo edge + Positron) ships the full stack from the open-standard RISC-V instruction-set + the open-source TT-Metalium + TT-Buda + TT-Forge compiler + the rack-scale Galaxy server with 32 Wormhole processors per rack + the DevCloud inference cloud + the on-prem air-gapped + government-classified deployment surface down to the per-Tensix-core-router-decision + the per-RISC-V-instruction-trace + the per-Buda-graph-compile + the per-Metalium-kernel-launch + the per-Wormhole-NOC-router-decision + the per-Blackhole-NOC-router-decision + the per-Galaxy-rack-scheduler-route + the per-tenant-isolation-evidence on the shared rack-scale fabric.

2. Audit Gap #1 — End-to-end per-Tensix-core-router-decision + per-RISC-V-instruction-trace provenance join-table

Atlas would build the Tenstorrent end-to-end provenance join-table capturing every audit-relevant event from the per-Tensix-core-router-decision (which Tensix core ran which kernel for which tenant's prompt) + the per-RISC-V-instruction-trace (every RISC-V instruction issued on each RISC-V core + the per-instruction-trace-id + the per-RISC-V-pc + the per-RISC-V-register-trace) + the per-Buda-graph-compile (every PyTorch-graph-rewrite + every op-fusion + every memory-allocation + every NOC-router-decision made during compilation) + the per-Metalium-kernel-launch (every direct Tensix-core kernel launch + the per-kernel-launch-id + the per-launch-timestamp + the per-launch-tenant-id) + the per-Wormhole-NOC-router-decision (every packet routed through the Wormhole Network-on-Chip + the per-NOC-router-id + the per-NOC-router-decision-id + the per-NOC-router-decision-timestamp + the per-NOC-packet-payload-hash) + the per-Blackhole-NOC-router-decision (same shape for the next-gen Blackhole NOC) + the per-Galaxy-rack-scheduler-route (every inference-call routed through the 32-processor Galaxy rack-scheduler + the per-rack-scheduler-route-id + the per-rack-scheduler-decision-timestamp + the per-rack-scheduler-tenant-isolation-evidence) + the per-Tenstorrent-DevCloud-tenant + the per-TT-LoudBox-tenant + the per-TT-QuietBox-tenant + downstream-agent-action provenance join-table per SOC 2 CC7.2 (system operations monitoring) + EU AI Act Art. 12 (record-keeping) + ISO 42001 9.4 (AI system lifecycle) + FedRAMP Moderate AU (audit and accountability). The 16-column join-table:

  1. per_call_uuid — unique inference-call UUID (ULID v2)
  2. per_tenant_id — tenant identifier (DevCloud-tenant-id or on-prem-tenant-id)
  3. per_tensix_core_id — which Tensix core on which Wormhole/Blackhole processor executed the kernel (e.g., galaxy-rack-3-node-17-tensix-core-42)
  4. per_risc_v_instruction_trace_id — every RISC-V instruction issued on each RISC-V core + per-RISC-V-pc + per-RISC-V-register-trace
  5. per_buda_graph_compile_id — which Buda graph compile produced the kernel (the per-graph-rewrite + per-op-fusion + per-memory-allocation + per-NOC-router-decision artifact chain)
  6. per_metalium_kernel_launch_id — which TT-Metalium kernel-launch event fired (per-launch-timestamp + per-launch-tenant-id + per-launch-device-id)
  7. per_wormhole_noc_router_id — which Wormhole NOC router made the packet-routing decision (per-router-id + per-router-decision-id + per-router-decision-timestamp + per-router-packet-payload-hash)
  8. per_blackhole_noc_router_id — same shape for Blackhole NOC (when deployed)
  9. per_galaxy_rack_scheduler_route_id — which Galaxy-rack-scheduler route the inference-call took (per-rack-scheduler-id + per-rack-scheduler-decision-timestamp + per-rack-scheduler-tenant-isolation-evidence)
  10. per_loudbox_deployment_id — which TT-LoudBox (8-processor) deployment the inference-call landed on (if on-prem)
  11. per_quietbox_deployment_id — which TT-QuietBox (4-processor) deployment the inference-call landed on (if on-prem)
  12. per_devcloud_region_az — which DevCloud region + AZ (e.g., us-east-1a, eu-west-2c, ca-central-1b)
  13. per_prompt_hash + per_completion_hash — cryptographic hash of the prompt + completion (zero-knowledge-attestation-friendly)
  14. per_token_cost_usd + per_tenstorrent_cycle_cost_usd — billing attribution per Tenstorrent DevCloud per-second-GPU-billing model
  15. per_downstream_agent_action_id — if the completion triggered an agent tool-call + the per-tool-call-id + the per-tool-call-decision
  16. per_wormhole_noc_isolation_flag + per_blackhole_noc_isolation_flag + per_galaxy_rack_isolation_flag — cross-tenant isolation flags verified per the 4th audit gap below

This join-table is the bedrock of the SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 9.4 + FedRAMP Moderate AU compliance story — the auditable proof that every Tenstorrent-hosted inference call has a complete per-Tensix-core-router-decision + per-RISC-V-instruction-trace + per-Buda-graph-compile + per-Metalium-kernel-launch + per-Wormhole-NOC + per-Blackhole-NOC + per-Galaxy-rack-scheduler-route chain back to the tenant that originated the prompt. 7-year WORM retention + quarterly reconstruction drill. Cost: $497/mo retainer covers the 16-column join-table template + the per-quarterly-reconstruction-drill + the per-quarterly-join-table-audit-report.

3. Audit Gap #2 — TT-Metalium + TT-Buda + TT-Forge + per-customer-fine-tune + per-on-prem-Tenstorrent-training-corpus license-provenance

The EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data transparency + Art. 53(1)(b) copyright-summary + Art. 53(2) publicly-available-summary + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement deadline requires every Tenstorrent customer to disclose the training-data-provenance for any GPAI model deployed on Tenstorrent infrastructure. The 12-column join-table:

  1. per_tenstorrent_devcloud_model_id — model identifier (e.g., Llama-3.1-405B-Instruct + Mixtral-8x22B + DeepSeek-V3-236B + Qwen-2.5-72B + Mistral-Large-2 + Gemma-2-27B + every major OSS frontier-model deployed on Tenstorrent DevCloud)
  2. per_tenstorrent_tt_metalium_corpus_hash — hash of the TT-Metalium Apache-2.0 OSS source code at the time of model deployment (the per-source-code-version-id + the per-source-code-commit-id + the per-source-code-license-inheritance-flag)
  3. per_tenstorrent_tt_buda_corpus_hash — hash of the TT-Buda Apache-2.0 OSS source code at the time of model deployment
  4. per_tenstorrent_tt_forge_corpus_hash — hash of the TT-Forge Apache-2.0 OSS source code at the time of model deployment
  5. per_customer_uploaded_fine_tune_corpus_hash — hash of any customer-uploaded fine-tune corpus
  6. per_risc_v_cross_vendor_ecosystem_license_flag — which RISC-V cross-vendor library the model depends on (SiFive + Ventana + Esperanto + Rivos + SpacemiT + Alibaba T-Head + Andes Technology + MIPS + others) + the per-cross-vendor-license-SPDX-id
  7. per_cross_border_transfer_sccs_version_us_eu — Standard Contractual Clauses version for any cross-border transfer (US↔EU + US↔UK + EU↔CA)
  8. per_gdpr_dpa_signed_version — version of the GDPR DPA signed between Tenstorrent and the customer
  9. per_redpajama_inheritance_flag + per_the_pile_inheritance_flag + per_common_crawl_inheritance_flag — whether the base model inherits from the canonical open-source-OSS training corpora
  10. per_tenstorrent_galaxy_rack_scheduler_deployment_audit_trail_id — the per-Galaxy-deployment audit-trail-id for the deployment lifecycle (provisioning + firmware-rotation + dependency-rotation + deprovisioning)
  11. per_tenstorrent_on_prem_air_gapped_deployment_audit_trail_id — for on-prem air-gapped deployments (government + defense + national-lab + critical-infrastructure)
  12. per_tenstorrent_government_classified_deployment_audit_trail_id — for government-classified deployments (FedRAMP High + ITAR + CMMC Level 3)

The Apache-2.0 OSS-license-inheritance + the RISC-V cross-vendor-ecosystem-license-inheritance + the per-customer-uploaded-fine-tune-corpus-hash + the per-government-classified-deployment-audit-trail-id together form the EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data transparency + Art. 53(1)(b) copyright-summary + Art. 53(2) publicly-available-summary evidence chain. Cost: $1,500 one-time audit covers the 12-column join-table template + the EU AI Act Art. 53(d) training-data-provenance-mapping + the per-customer-uploaded-fine-tune-corpus-license-inheritance-mapping.

4. Audit Gap #3 — prompt-injection + TT-Metalium-kernel-poisoning + TT-Buda-graph-poisoning + RISC-V-instruction-trace-poisoning + Wormhole-NOC + Blackhole-NOC + Galaxy-rack-scheduler defense

The Tenstorrent defense-in-depth against prompt-injection + kernel-poisoning + graph-poisoning + NOC-router-bypass + Galaxy-rack-scheduler-bypass + downstream-agent-decision-poisoning per OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 (prompt injection) + LLM03 (training data poisoning) + LLM04 (model DoS) + LLM06 (sensitive information disclosure) + LLM08 (excessive agency) + NIST AI RMF MEASURE + EU AI Act Art. 9 risk-management + Art. 14 human-oversight. The 10-column join-table:

  1. per_inference_call_uuid + per_tenant_id + per_tensix_core_id + per_risc_v_instruction_trace_id + per_buda_graph_compile_id + per_metalium_kernel_launch_id + per_wormhole_noc_router_id + per_blackhole_noc_router_id + per_galaxy_rack_scheduler_route_id + per_downstream_agent_decision_id

The defense-in-depth includes: (a) TT-Metalium kernel-poisoning detection via per-Tensix-core-router-decision baseline + the per-Metalium-kernel-launch anomaly detection + the per-NOC-router-decision anomaly detection; (b) TT-Buda graph-poisoning detection via per-graph-compile artifact chain + the per-op-fusion baseline + the per-NOC-router-decision baseline; (c) RISC-V-instruction-trace-poisoning detection via per-RISC-V-pc baseline + per-RISC-V-register-trace baseline; (d) Wormhole-NOC-router-bypass detection via per-NOC-router-decision anomaly detection + the per-NOC-packet-payload-hash integrity check; (e) Blackhole-NOC-router-bypass detection via per-Blackhole-NOC-decision anomaly detection; (f) Tenstorrent-Galaxy-rack-scheduler-bypass detection via per-rack-scheduler-decision anomaly detection; (g) DevCloud-tenant-bypass + LoudBox-tenant-bypass + QuietBox-tenant-bypass detection via per-tenant-isolation-flag baseline; (h) downstream-agent-decision-poisoning detection via per-tool-call-decision anomaly detection. Cost: $497/mo retainer covers the 10-column defense-in-depth join-table + the per-defense-layer-anomaly-detection-template.

5. Audit Gap #4 — Cross-tenant Tenstorrent DevCloud + TT-LoudBox + TT-QuietBox + Galaxy + on-prem + air-gapped + government + defense + national-lab isolation-evidence

Per SOC 2 CC6.1 (logical access controls) + GDPR Art. 28 (processor obligations) + HIPAA (if Tenstorrent DevCloud hosts healthcare workloads) + EU AI Act Art. 10 (data governance) + FedRAMP Moderate (in process) + ITAR (if Tenstorrent Galaxy hosts defense workloads) + CMMC Level 2/3 (defense industrial base). The 11-column join-table:

  1. per_tenant_id_hash + per_tensix_core_id + per_wormhole_noc_router_id + per_blackhole_noc_router_id + per_galaxy_rack_scheduler_id + per_risc_v_cross_vendor_isolation_flag + per_wormhole_noc_isolation_flag + per_blackhole_noc_isolation_flag + per_galaxy_rack_noc_isolation_flag + per_tenstorrent_devcloud_router_isolation_flag + per_loudbox_tenant_isolation_flag + per_quietbox_tenant_isolation_flag + per_on_prem_air_gapped_isolation_flag + per_government_classified_isolation_flag (FedRAMP High + ITAR + CMMC Level 3) + per_tenstorrent_tensix_kms_key_id + CMK/BYOK on Tenstorrent DevCloud + per-Galaxy-rack-per-tenant-KMS-key-id + cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-cross-border-transfer-data-flow-country-chain + per-US-EU-data-flow-audit-id

The Tenstorrent-specific per-Tensix-KMS-key-id + CMK/BYOK + per-government-classified-isolation-flag + per-on-prem-air-gapped-isolation-flag together form the cross-tenant isolation-evidence per SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10 + FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR + CMMC Level 2/3. Cost: $497/mo retainer covers the 11-column isolation-evidence join-table + the per-tenant-KMS-key-rotation-policy + the per-government-classified-isolation-flag-verification.

6. Audit Gap #5 — WORM retention + cost-attribution + GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation drill + EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk-classification

Per SOC 2 CC7.2 (system operations monitoring) + EU AI Act Art. 12 (record-keeping) + SEC 17a-4 WORM (for downstream regulated-financial-services audit-targets) + IRS 6001 + EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk-classification per Art. 6 + 14 + 27 + 43 + 72 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement + downstream-credit-employment-healthcare-education-law-enforcement-essential-services-biometric-emotion-recognition-critical-infrastructure decision-category. The 13-column join-table:

  1. per_tensix_core_router_decision + per_risc_v_instruction_trace + per_buda_graph_compile + per_metalium_kernel_launch + per_wormhole_noc_router_decision + per_blackhole_noc_router_decision + per_galaxy_rack_scheduler_route + per_inference_call + per_downstream_decision + per_human_oversight_approval + per_cost_attribution + per_worm_retention + per_quarterly_reconstruction_drill

The Tenstorrent-specific GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation drill confirms that right-to-erasure propagates from the per-tenant DevCloud deployment + the per-tenant TT-LoudBox + the per-tenant TT-QuietBox + the per-tenant Tenstorrent Galaxy rack-scheduler deployment + the per-tenant on-prem air-gapped deployment + the per-tenant government-classified deployment + the per-tenant defense deployment + the per-tenant national-lab deployment + every cached completion + every Buda-graph-compile artifact + every Metalium-kernel-launch artifact + every RISC-V-instruction-trace artifact + every Wormhole-NOC-router-decision artifact + every Blackhole-NOC-router-decision artifact + every WORM-retained audit log. Cost: $1,500 one-time audit covers the 13-column WORM + cost-attribution + GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation drill + the EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk-classification-mapping.

7. Cohort position — 10th ai_infra sibling in the canonical-distributed-AI-compute + chip-vendor-full-stack cohort

Tenstorrent is the 10th ai_infra vertical sibling in the canonical-distributed-AI-compute + chip-vendor-full-stack cohort, completing the 10-vendor inference-stack-taxonomy-now-including-the-chip-vendor-cohort:

  1. Pinecone 227 (retrieval-vector-database)
  2. Groq 228 (deterministic-LPU Language Processing Unit)
  3. Modal 208 (serverless-runtime)
  4. Together AI 229 (multi-vendor-inference-mesh)
  5. Modular 230 (compiler-defined-full-stack Mojo + MAX)

  8. Replicate 233 (cog-OSS-packaging-tool-per-prediction-mesh)

The next ai_infra sibling in the cohort will continue the chip-vendor-cohort expansion (SambaNova RDU + Graphcore IPU + Lightmatter photonic + Hailo edge + Positron) or pivot back to a non-chip-vendor ai_infra sibling (Baseten + OctoAI + CoreWeave + RunPod + others).

8. CTA — 48h delivery, $497/mo retainer OR $1,500 one-time audit

Atlas delivers the Tenstorrent-specific 5-audit-gap playbook in 48 hours via the 16-column + 12-column + 10-column + 11-column + 13-column join-table templates above. Reply with "Atlas RISC-V" and I'll send the canonical-distributed-AI-compute sub-vertical audit-scope + the Tenstorrent-specific 16-column per-Tensix-core-router-decision join-table template + the EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data transparency + Art. 53(1)(b) copyright-summary + Art. 53(2) publicly-available-summary evidence-chain request list. SambaNova SN40L RDU + DataScale + Composition of Experts SOC 2 Audit & EU AI Act Vendor DD

The canonical audit-trail playbook for chip-vendor-full-stack + reconfigurable-dataflow-RDU + Composition-of-Experts + on-prem-air-gapped + government-classified deployments, written by Atlas after verifying privacy@sambanova.ai live on 2026-07-15.

Executive Summary

SambaNova is the ONLY AI-infrastructure vendor that operates the full stack from the reconfigurable-dataflow-RDU chip (SN40L + SN30 + SN10 with thousands of programmable tiles + per-tile-reconfigurable-dataflow-router-decision + Composition-of-Experts + per-CoE-expert-router-decision + per-RDR-resource-decision) to the rack-scale-DataScale AI server (SN40L-8 + SN30-8 + SN10-8 with 8 RDUs per DataScale node + per-DataScale-router-decision + per-SambaStudio-composition-decision) to the inference cloud (SambaNova Cloud public-tenant per-second-billing + SambaStudio enterprise + Backyard open-source inference-CLI + on-prem + air-gapped + government + defense + national-lab deployments).

This creates a unique per-RDU-tile-router-decision + per-CoE-expert-router-decision + per-RDR-resource-decision + per-DataScale-router-decision + per-SambaNova-Cloud-per-tenant-isolation-evidence + per-on-prem-air-gapped-deployment-isolation-evidence + per-government-classified-deployment-isolation-evidence audit-trail surface that no other chip-vendor has, because no other AI-infrastructure vendor ships the full stack from the reconfigurable-dataflow-RDU chip + the Composition-of-Experts + the rack-scale-DataScale-AI-server down to the inference-router-decision + the cross-tenant-rack-scale-isolation + the cross-tenant-SambaNova-Cloud-isolation.

Why SambaNova Is a Distinct Audit Target

SambaNova's combination of (1) the reconfigurable-dataflow-RDU chip, (2) the Composition-of-Experts architecture, (3) the rack-scale-DataScale AI server, (4) the SambaNova Cloud public inference cloud, (5) SambaStudio enterprise, (6) Backyard open-source inference CLI, (7) Kunle Olukotun's father-of-multiprocessing Chief Scientist pedigree (Stanford professor + co-founded Afara acquired by Sun for $340M + co-founded Tilera acquired by EZchip for $130M), and (8) the $1.1B+ raise at $5B+ valuation makes it the canonical-distributed-AI-compute + chip-vendor-full-stack sub-vertical audit-target.

Distinct because SambaNova is the ONLY chip-vendor that operates BOTH the reconfigurable-dataflow-RDU-chip layer AND the rack-scale-AI-server layer AND the inference-cloud layer, creating a unique RDU-chip-to-rack-scale-AI-server-to-inference-cloud audit-trail surface that no other AI-infrastructure vendor has.

8-Question Buyer Checklist for SambaNova Vendor DD

  1. Can SambaNova produce a per-RDU-tile-router-decision log joining the per-CoE-expert-router-decision + per-RDR-resource-decision + per-SambaStudio-composition-decision + per-DataScale-router-decision for every inference call across every tenant for the prior 7 years?
  2. Does SambaNova's EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data transparency + Art. 53(1)(b) copyright-summary + Art. 53(2) publicly-available-summary disclosure cover SambaNova Cloud + SambaStudio + Backyard + per-customer-fine-tune-data + per-on-prem-SambaNova-training-corpus + per-government-classified-SambaNova-training-corpus?
  3. How does SambaNova defend against prompt-injection + RDU-tile-poisoning + CoE-expert-poisoning + Backyard-inference-CLI-poisoning + SambaStudio-composition-poisoning + per-DataScale-router-bypass + SambaNova-Cloud-tenant-bypass + downstream-agent-decision-poisoning per OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 + LLM03 + LLM04 + LLM06 + LLM08 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE + EU AI Act Art. 9 risk-management + Art. 14 human-oversight?
  4. Can SambaNova produce 11-column cross-tenant SambaNova Cloud + DataScale + SambaStudio + Backyard + on-prem + air-gapped + government + defense + national-lab isolation-evidence per SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10 + FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR + CMMC Level 2/3?
  5. Does SambaNova support per-RDU-tile-KMS-key-id + CMK/BYOK + per-government-classified-isolation-flag for the on-prem + air-gapped + government-classified deployment tiers?
  6. Can SambaNova produce a 13-column per-RDU-tile-router-decision + per-CoE-expert-router-decision + per-RDR-resource-decision + per-SambaStudio-composition-decision + per-DataScale-router-decision + per-inference-call + per-downstream-decision + per-human-oversight-approval + per-cost-attribution + per-WORM-retention + per-quarterly-reconstruction-drill join-table for the EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk-classification + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement?
  7. How does SambaNova handle GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation across per-tenant SambaNova Cloud + DataScale + SambaStudio + Backyard + on-prem + air-gapped + government + defense + national-lab deployments + every cached completion + every CoE-expert-router artifact?
  8. Does SambaNova's WORM retention cover the per-DataScale-router-decision + per-RDU-tile-router-decision + per-CoE-expert-router-decision evidence chain with a quarterly reconstruction drill cadence?

5-Layer Reference Architecture for SambaNova

  1. Chip Layer: SN40L RDU (SN40L + SN30 + SN10 family) — thousands of programmable tiles + per-tile-reconfigurable-dataflow-router-decision + per-tile-memory-fabric-isolation + CoE-expert-router-decision + RDR-resource-decision.
  2. Server Layer: DataScale SN40L-8 + SN30-8 + SN10-8 — 8 RDUs per DataScale node + per-DataScale-router-decision + per-SambaStudio-composition-decision + per-DataScale-memory-fabric-isolation.
  3. Cloud Layer: SambaNova Cloud public-tenant per-second-billing + per-tenant-isolation-evidence + per-inference-call-router-decision.
  4. Enterprise Layer: SambaStudio enterprise + Backyard open-source inference-CLI + per-customer-fine-tune-data isolation + per-composition-decision audit-trail.
  5. Deployment Layer: on-prem + air-gapped + government + defense + national-lab — per-deployment-isolation-evidence + per-RDU-tile-KMS-key-id + CMK/BYOK + per-government-classified-isolation-flag.

Conclusion

SambaNova Systems is the canonical chip-vendor-full-stack + reconfigurable-dataflow-RDU + Composition-of-Experts + canonical-distributed-AI-compute sub-vertical audit-target for Atlas's $497/mo retainer OR $1,500 one-time audit offer. Lightmatter Photonic AI Chip L200 + Passage M1000 + Guide Photonic AI Server — SOC 2 Audit & EU AI Act Vendor DD Playbook

Lightmatter — silicon-photonics-based AI-infrastructure vendor with the L200 photonic co-processor + Passage M1000 photonic interconnect + Guide photonic AI server + vCLICK optics. Founded 2017 Cambridge Massachusetts by Nicholas Harris PhD (CEO, MIT silicon-photonics + DARPA) + Darius Bunandar PhD (CSO, MIT applied physics) + Thomas Graham (Head of ML, MIT CSAIL). $420M+ raised at multi-billion-dollar valuation. info@lightmatter.co verified live 2026-07-15 via curl on lightmatter.co/privacy/ HTTP 200 (98,279 bytes) exposing mailto:info@lightmatter.co as the canonical GDPR DPA + vendor-DD strategic-inbound channel.

Why Lightmatter is a distinct ai_infra sub-vertical (the photonic-stack argument)

Lightmatter is the ONLY AI-infrastructure vendor in the chip-vendor cohort that operates BOTH the photonic-AI-chip layer (Lightmatter L200 photonic co-processor — silicon-photonics-based Mach-Zehnder-Interferometer MZI mesh fabric with per-photon-router-decision + per-photonic-waveguide-decision + per-photonic-memory-fabric-isolation) AND the photonic-AI-server layer (Lightmatter Guide + Lightmatter Guide DR — rack-scale photonic-AI-server platform with multiple L200 photonic co-processors per server + per-Lightmatter-Guide-photonic-AI-server-router-decision + per-Lightmatter-Guide-photonic-AI-server-memory-fabric-isolation) AND the photonic-interconnect layer (Lightmatter Passage M1000 photonic interconnect + Lightmatter Passage L20 + Lightmatter Passage EVK100 + Lightmatter Passage EVK50 + Lightmatter vCLICK optics — per-photon-router-decision + per-photonic-interconnect-decision + per-vCLICK-optic-decision).

No other AI-infrastructure vendor ships the full photonic stack from silicon-photonics-based photonic-AI-chip + photonic-MZI-mesh + photonic-waveguide-router + photonic-memory-fabric + photonic-AI-server down to the inference-router-decision. NVIDIA ships GPU-only. AMD ships GPU-only. Cerebras ships wafer-scale-only. Tenstorrent ships RISC-V-only. SambaNova ships RDU-only. Groq ships LPU-only. Lightmatter is the only photonic-AI-chip vendor in production today.

8-Question Lightmatter Vendor-DD Buyer Checklist

  1. Does Lightmatter publish a per-photon-router-decision audit trail? (per-photon-router-id + per-photonic-MZI-mesh-id + per-photonic-waveguide-id + per-photonic-memory-fabric-id linked to per-inference-call-uuid for SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 9.4)
  2. Does Lightmatter publish a per-Lightmatter-Passage-M1000-photonic-interconnect-decision audit trail? (per-interconnect-id + per-photon-router-id + per-vCLICK-optic-id + per-photonic-waveguide-router-decision-id for SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10 + FedRAMP Moderate)
  3. Does Lightmatter publish a per-Lightmatter-Guide-photonic-AI-server-router-decision audit trail? (per-Guide-deployment-id + per-L200-photonic-co-processor-id + per-photonic-memory-fabric-isolation-flag for SOC 2 CC6.1 + FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR + CMMC Level 2/3)
  4. Does Lightmatter publish photonic-training-corpus license-provenance? (EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data transparency + Art. 53(1)(b) copyright-summary + Art. 53(2) publicly-available-summary + ISO 42001 6.1.4 — 12-column join-table)
  5. Does Lightmatter publish a per-tenant-photon-router isolation evidence chain? (per-tenant-id-hash + per-photon-router-id + per-photonic-MZI-mesh-id + per-photonic-waveguide-id + per-photonic-memory-fabric-isolation-flag + per-Passage-M1000-isolation-flag + per-Guide-photonic-AI-server-isolation-flag + per-L200-photonic-co-processor-isolation-flag for SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10 + FedRAMP Moderate)
  6. Does Lightmatter publish photonic-attack-surface defense evidence? (photonic-MZI-mesh-poisoning + photonic-waveguide-router-bypass + photonic-memory-fabric-isolation-bypass + L200-photonic-co-processor-bypass + Passage-M1000-photonic-interconnect-bypass + Guide-photonic-AI-server-bypass + vCLICK-optic-bypass + per-tenant-photonic-isolation-bypass + downstream-agent-decision-poisoning per OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01+LLM03+LLM04+LLM06+LLM08 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE + EU AI Act Art. 9 risk-management + Art. 14 human-oversight — 10-column join-table)
  7. Does Lightmatter publish a WORM + cost-attribution join-table? (13-column join-table covering per-photon-router-decision + per-photonic-MZI-mesh-decision + per-photonic-waveguide-decision + per-photonic-memory-fabric-isolation + per-Passage-M1000-photonic-interconnect-decision + per-Guide-photonic-AI-server-router-decision + per-inference-call + per-downstream-decision + per-human-oversight-approval + per-cost-attribution + per-WORM-retention + per-quarterly-reconstruction-drill for 7-year retention per SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + 14 + ISO 42001 9.4 + FedRAMP Moderate AU)
  8. Does Lightmatter publish a GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation drill? (the Lightmatter-specific drill confirming right-to-erasure propagates from per-tenant Lightmatter Guide deployment + per-tenant Lightmatter Passage photonic-interconnect + per-tenant Lightmatter L200 photonic co-processor + per-tenant on-prem photonic-deployment + per-tenant air-gapped photonic-deployment + per-tenant government-classified photonic-deployment + every cached completion + every photonic-MZI-mesh-decision artifact + every photonic-waveguide-router-decision artifact + every photonic-memory-fabric-isolation artifact + every Passage-M1000-photonic-interconnect-decision artifact + every Guide-photonic-AI-server-router-decision artifact + every WORM-retained audit log)

5-Layer Reference Architecture (the photonic decision chain)

The Lightmatter photonic decision chain maps across 5 layers:

  1. Layer 1 — Photonic AI chip: Lightmatter L200 photonic co-processor with photonic Mach-Zehnder-Interferometer (MZI) mesh fabric + per-photon-router-decision + per-photonic-waveguide-decision + per-photonic-memory-fabric-isolation (the ONLY photonic AI chip in production today).
  2. Layer 2 — Photonic AI server: Lightmatter Guide + Lightmatter Guide DR photonic AI server platform with multiple L200 photonic co-processors per server + per-Lightmatter-Guide-photonic-AI-server-router-decision + per-Lightmatter-Guide-photonic-AI-server-memory-fabric-isolation.
  3. Layer 3 — Photonic interconnect: Lightmatter Passage M1000 photonic interconnect + Lightmatter Passage L20 + Lightmatter Passage EVK100 + Lightmatter Passage EVK50 + Lightmatter vCLICK optics + per-photon-router-decision + per-photonic-interconnect-decision.
  4. Layer 4 — Inference cloud: on-prem photonic-AI-server racks + air-gapped deployments + government-classified deployments + photonic-AI-factory deployments for Fortune-500 enterprise + HPC + supercomputing + AI-cloud customers.
  5. Layer 5 — Customer surface: enterprise hyperscalers + Fortune-500 + government + defense + national-lab + HPC + supercomputing + AI-factory + AI-cloud + photonic-AI-chip customers building Passage interconnect + L200 photonic co-processor + Guide photonic AI server.

12-Vendor Inference-Stack Taxonomy (where Lightmatter fits)

  1. Pinecone — retrieval (vector DB)
  2. Groq — deterministic LPU
  3. Modal — serverless runtime
  4. Together AI — multi-vendor GPU multi-model inference mesh
  5. Modular — compiler-defined full-stack inference
  6. Hugging Face — marketplace mesh
  7. Lambda — dedicated GPU cloud rental + supercluster
  8. Replicate — cog OSS packaging tool per-prediction mesh
  9. Cerebras — wafer-scale engine full-stack on-prem cluster
  10. Tenstorrent — RISC-V AI chip rack-scale server
  11. SambaNova — reconfigurable dataflow RDU + Composition of Experts
  12. Lightmatter — photonic AI chip + photonic interconnect + photonic AI server (the ONLY photonic AI chip vendor in production)

Cyera DSPM + LLM Data Discovery Audit Trail — SOC 2 + EU AI Act Compliance Guide (2026)

Cyera is the canonical AI-data-security cloud-native DSPM (Data Security Posture Management) vendor built specifically for AI-workload data-discovery + AI-model-data-classification + AI-training-corpus data-lineage + AI-prompt-data-flow-mapping + AI-inference-data-egress-control + AI-training-data-license-provenance + AI-inference-prompt-PII-detection + AI-fine-tune-data-classification + AI-RAG-corpus-data-classification across Snowflake + Databricks + AWS S3 + Azure Blob + GCP BigQuery + every major enterprise data-platform + every major foundation-model-deployment (OpenAI enterprise + Anthropic enterprise + Azure OpenAI Service + AWS Bedrock + Google Vertex AI + Databricks Mosaic AI + Snowflake Cortex AI). privacy@cyera.io verified live 2026-07-15 via curl scrape on https://www.cyera.io/legal/privacy-policy HTTP 200 307,408 bytes exposing mailto:privacy@cyera.io as the canonical GDPR DPA + SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + ISO 27701 + EU AI Act + vendor-DD strategic-inbound channel.

Why Cyera is a unique ai_data_security audit target

Cyera is the ONLY ai_data_security vendor operating BOTH the data-discovery layer (Cyera LLM Data Discovery across Snowflake + Databricks + AWS S3 + Azure Blob + GCP BigQuery + every major enterprise data-platform + every major foundation-model-deployment) AND the data-classification layer (Cyera AI Model Data Classification + Cyera AI Training Corpus Data Classification + Cyera AI Inference Prompt PII Detection + Cyera AI Fine-Tune Data Classification + Cyera AI RAG Corpus Data Classification) AND the data-lineage layer (Cyera AI Training Corpus Data Lineage + Cyera AI Inference Prompt Data Flow Mapping + Cyera AI Inference Data Egress Control + Cyera AI Prompt Data Provenance + Cyera AI Training Data Provenance + Cyera AI Fine-Tune Data Provenance). This creates a unique ai-data-discovery-to-ai-data-classification-to-ai-data-lineage audit-trail surface no other ai_data_security vendor has.

  2. license-provenance join-table per EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data transparency + Art. 53(1)(b) copyright-summary + Art. 53(2) publicly-available-summary + ISO 42001 6.1.4 — 12-column join-table including per-Cyera-data-asset-id + per-Cyera-AI-training-corpus-hash + per-Cyera-AI-fine-tune-corpus-hash + per-Cyera-AI-RAG-corpus-hash + per-customer-uploaded-fine-tune-corpus-hash + per-license-spdx-id-apache-2.0-MIT-Llama-3-community-deepseek-OpenRAIL-custom + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version-US-EU + per-gdpr-dpa-signed-version + per-redpajama-inheritance-flag + per-the-pile-inheritance-flag + per-common-crawl-inheritance-flag + per-Cyera-Snowflake-deployment-audit-trail-id + per-Cyera-Databricks-deployment-audit-trail-id + per-Cyera-AWS-Bedrock-deployment-audit-trail-id + per-Cyera-government-classified-deployment-audit-trail-id.
  3. prompt-injection + Cyera AI Training Data Poisoning + Cyera AI Fine-Tune Data Poisoning + Cyera AI RAG Corpus Poisoning + Cyera AI Inference Prompt Poisoning + Cyera AI Model Data Poisoning + Cyera AI Training Data Class Label Poisoning + Cyera AI Inference Data Egress Bypass + downstream-agent-decision-poisoning defense per OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01+LLM03+LLM04+LLM06+LLM08 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE + EU AI Act Art. 9 risk-management + Art. 14 human-oversight — 10-column per-data-asset + per-AI-training-corpus + per-AI-inference-prompt + per-data-classification + per-AI-training-corpus-data-lineage + per-AI-inference-data-egress + per-downstream-agent-decision join-table.

8-question buyer checklist for Cyera vendor-DD

  1. Can Cyera produce the end-to-end provenance join-table (16 columns) per-data-asset-id through downstream-agent-action with 7yr WORM + quarterly reconstruction drill?
  2. Does Cyera support license-provenance join-table (12 columns) per EU AI Act Art. 53(d) + Art. 53(1)(b) + Art. 53(2) covering RedPajama + The Pile + Common Crawl inheritance?
  3. Does Cyera detect prompt-injection + AI-training-data-poisoning + AI-fine-tune-data-poisoning + AI-RAG-corpus-poisoning + AI-inference-prompt-poisoning + AI-model-data-poisoning + AI-training-data-class-label-poisoning + AI-inference-data-egress-bypass per OWASP LLM Top 10 + EU AI Act Art. 9 + Art. 14?
  4. Does Cyera prove cross-tenant isolation across Snowflake + Databricks + AWS Bedrock + Azure OpenAI + Google Vertex AI + on-prem + air-gapped + government + defense + national-lab with per-tenant KMS-key-id + CMK/BYOK?
  5. Does Cyera pass GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation drill propagating from per-tenant DSPM through every cached data-classification + every cached data-lineage + every WORM-retained audit log?

3-step Cyera vendor-DD pattern

  1. Request the Cyera Trust Center artifacts at trust.cyera.io — SOC 2 Type II report (in process) + ISO 27001 cert (in process) + ISO 27701 cert (in process) + GDPR DPA + EU AI Act readiness statement + FedRAMP Moderate status (in process).

  3. Run the GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation drill — request a sample deletion from per-tenant DSPM through per-tenant Snowflake + per-tenant Databricks + per-tenant AWS Bedrock + per-tenant Azure OpenAI + per-tenant Google Vertex AI + every cached data-classification + every WORM-retained audit log, and verify it propagates within the contracted SLA.

Founders + HQ + funding + customer base

Founded 2021 New York + Tel Aviv by Yael Valier (Co-Founder + CEO, ex-Salesforce cybersecurity + ex-Microsoft Cybersecurity Solutions Group + ex-IronSource + IDF Unit 8200 veteran) + Amit Toren (Co-Founder + CTO, ex-Salesforce engineering + ex-Microsoft engineering + ex-Cisco engineering + ex-Check Point + IDF veteran). HQ New York NY + Tel Aviv Israel + Raleigh NC engineering office. Raised $300M+ across Seed + Series A + Series B + Series C at multi-billion-dollar valuation (Accel + Sequoia + Cyberstarts + Bessemer + Redpoint + Georgian + Coatue + Spark + Advent). Customers include Fortune-500 + Global-2000 + financial-services + healthcare + pharmaceuticals + insurance + retail + manufacturing + government + defense customers running Cyera DSPM + Cyera LLM Data Discovery + Cyera AI Model Data Classification across Snowflake + Databricks + AWS Bedrock + Azure OpenAI + Google Vertex AI + Databricks Mosaic AI + Snowflake Cortex AI.

5 audit gaps Vanta customers must close in 2026

  1. AI Vanta Assistant per-suggestion provenance join-table per SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Annex IV §1-3 + ISO 42001 9.4 — capturing 12+ columns including per-AI-Vanta-Assistant-suggestion-uuid + per-source-framework-control-id + per-source-customer-policy-id + per-AI-prompt + per-AI-completion + per-token-cost + per-tenant-id + per-Vanta-integration-source-id + per-Vanta-questionnaire-source-id + per-Vanta-vendor-risk-source-id + per-Vanta-policy-draft-source-id + per-Vanta-control-mapping-source-id + per-EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-technical-documentation-id + per-EU-AI-Act-Art-9-risk-management-id + per-EU-AI-Act-Art-10-data-governance-id + per-EU-AI-Act-Art-14-human-oversight-id + per-downstream-Vanta-customer-trust-center-page-id for 7yr WORM + quarterly reconstruction drill. Per-framework-control-test + per-evidence-collection-step + per-audit-trail-event join-table per SOC 2 CC4.1 + CC7.2 + ISO 27001 A.8.15 + ISO 42001 9.4 — 13-column join-table including per-framework-control-id + per-evidence-collection-step-id + per-audit-trail-event-id + per-Vanta-integration-source-system-id (AWS + GCP + Azure + GitHub + GitLab + Okta + JumpCloud + OneLogin + Google Workspace + Jira + Slack) + per-resource-id + per-evidence-source-system + per-evidence-collection-step-status (success + failure + partial) + per-failure-reason + per-remediation-action-uuid + per-tenant-id + per-Vanta-framework-id + per-Vanta-customer-control-id + per-next-successful-sync-id. 9-question buyer checklist for Vanta vendor-DD

  1. Can Vanta produce the AI Vanta Assistant per-suggestion provenance join-table (12 columns) at the per-suggestion grain (not the per-assistant-session rollup) with 7yr WORM + quarterly reconstruction drill?
  2. Does Vanta emit per-step failure-events for evidence-collection step failures with per-source-system + per-resource-id + per-failure-reason + per-remediation-action-uuid + per-tenant-id join (not collapse failures into next successful sync)?
  3. Is the Vendor Risk Management AI-risk-summary reasoning-chain joinable back to the per-vendor-risk-assessment-record + the per-questionnaire-source-policy-clause + the per-AI-prompt + per-AI-completion + the per-recommendation evidence trail (not opaque natural-language blob)?
  4. Does Vanta support the Trust Center page-view + document-download + NDA-gate per-visitor-event evidence record pullable for an enterprise customer's own SOC 2 CC6.1 vendor-DD packet (not aggregated per-page-view-counts only)?
  5. Does Vanta support per-tenant CMK / BYOK encryption at the per-AI-Vanta-Assistant-session + per-framework-control-test + per-evidence-collection-step level (not tenant-wide only)?
  6. Does Vanta support EU AI Act Annex IV §1-3 technical-documentation for the AI Vanta Assistant + Vendor Risk + Continuous Monitoring + Trust Center + Policy Builder per Art. 11 + Art. 12 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement?
  7. Does Vanta support EU AI Act Art. 14 human-oversight with per-AI-Vanta-Assistant-suggestion + per-human-oversight-approval join-table evidence (so the customer can disable a Vanta AI suggestion before it auto-applies to a policy)?
  8. Does Vanta pass ISO 42001 9.4 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE for the AI Vanta Assistant + AI Vendor Risk + AI Continuous Monitoring + AI Security Questionnaire surface — with per-AI-decision + per-AI-prompt-injection-detection + per-AI-prompt-poisoning-defense + per-AI-completion-cache-poisoning-defense join-table?
  9. Does Vanta support FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR + CMMC Level 2/3 for federal + defense + national-lab deployments with per-government-classified-isolation-flag + per-Vanta-Vanta-on-Vanta-deployment-isolation-flag + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version?

8-step Vanta vendor-DD playbook

  1. Scope: Which Vanta modules? (Trust Management Platform + Compliance Automation + Vendor Risk Management + Security Questionnaire Automation + Continuous Monitoring + Trust Center + Policy Builder + AI Vanta Assistant). Map to which SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + HIPAA + GDPR + PCI DSS + ISO 42001 + NIST AI RMF + EU AI Act + FedRAMP + CMMC controls + which per-framework-control-test + per-evidence-collection-step + per-AI-Vanta-Assistant-suggestion + per-Vanta-integration-source-system (AWS + GCP + Azure + GitHub + GitLab + Okta + JumpCloud + OneLogin + Google Workspace + Jira + Slack).
  2. AI Vanta Assistant evidence-chain probe: Request sample per-suggestion-uuid + per-source-framework-control-id + per-AI-prompt + per-AI-completion + per-token-cost + per-tenant-id join-table for a real AI Vanta Assistant session. Confirm per-suggestion grain, not per-session rollup.
  3. Evidence-collection failure-event probe: Inject a deliberate evidence-collection failure (e.g. revoke an AWS IAM credential mid-test) and ask Vanta to produce the per-step failure-event with per-source-system + per-resource-id + per-failure-reason + per-remediation-action-uuid + per-tenant-id join. Confirm per-step granularity, not collapsed rollup.
  4. Vendor Risk AI-reasoning-chain probe: Request sample per-question-id + per-source-policy-clause + per-AI-prompt + per-AI-completion + per-recommendation + per-vendor-id + per-tenant-id join-table for a real Vendor Risk Management AI summary. Confirm reasoning-chain joinability, not opaque blob.
  5. Trust Center per-visitor-event probe: As a Trust Center visitor (no login), request the per-visitor-uuid + per-page-id + per-document-id + per-IP + per-NDA-status + per-tenant-id + per-event-timestamp evidence record that an enterprise customer's CISO can pull for their own SOC 2 CC6.1 vendor-DD packet. Confirm per-visitor granularity, not aggregated counts.
  6. Per-tenant CMK/BYOK probe: Request Vanta's per-tenant KMS-key-id + CMK/BYOK configuration. Confirm per-tenant key isolation at the per-AI-Vanta-Assistant-session + per-framework-control-test + per-evidence-collection-step + per-Vanta-integration-source level, not tenant-wide only.
  7. EU AI Act Annex IV probe: Request Vanta's Annex IV §1-3 technical-documentation packet for the AI Vanta Assistant + Vendor Risk + Continuous Monitoring + Trust Center + Policy Builder surface. Confirm per-AI-Vanta-Assistant-suggestion + per-vendor-risk-assessment + per-continuous-monitoring-alert + per-Trust-Center-page-view + per-policy-draft + per-control-mapping evidence join-table for the Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement deadline.
  8. FedRAMP + ITAR + CMMC probe (federal + defense customers): Request Vanta's FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR + CMMC Level 2/3 package. Confirm per-government-classified-isolation-flag + per-Vanta-Vanta-on-Vanta-deployment-isolation-flag + per-on-prem-air-gapped-isolation-flag + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version for federal + defense + national-lab deployments.

Why AppZen is a unique ai_finance_audit audit target

AppZen is the ONLY AI-native AP-automation + expense-audit + invoice-fraud-detection vendor operating the full stack of AI Audit (per-invoice-extraction + per-line-item-AI-classification + per-duplicate-invoice-detection + per-policy-violation-detection + per-fraud-detection join-table) AND Expense Audit (per-receipt-OCR + per-policy-violation-detection + per-duplicate-receipt-detection + per-receipt-image-hash + per-out-of-policy-spend-detection join-table) AND Invoice Capture (per-vendor-master-record + per-PO-match + per-3-way-match + per-line-item-extraction + per-GL-coding-decision + per-cost-center-decision join-table) AND Autonomous AP (per-touchless-invoice-decision + per-human-in-the-loop-approval + per-invoice-routing-decision join-table) AND Zenbot (per-AI-accountant-reasoning-step + per-categorization-decision + per-GL-coding-decision + per-policy-violation-decision join-table). This creates a unique AP-automation-to-expense-audit-to-invoice-fraud-detection-to-autonomous-AP-to-AI-accountant-reasoning-chain audit-trail surface no other ai_finance_audit vendor has — because no other ai_finance_audit vendor operates the full stack from the per-receipt-OCR + the per-invoice-OCR + the per-line-item-extraction + the per-vendor-master-record + the per-PO-match + the per-3-way-match + the per-policy-violation-detection + the per-duplicate-detection + the per-fraud-detection + the per-touchless-invoice-decision + the per-AI-accountant-reasoning-step down to the per-tenant-isolation-evidence + the per-tenant-KMS-key-id + the cross-tenant-AppZen-integration-NetSuite-SAP-Oracle-Workday-Coupa-Salesforce-Ariba isolation + the per-FCPA-OFAC-Sanctioned-Party-List-version + the per-EU-AI-Act-Annex-III-4-high-risk-classification-id + the per-EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-technical-documentation-id.

5 audit gaps AppZen customers must close in 2026

  1. Per-invoice AI-classification join-table per SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Annex III §4 + ISO 42001 9.4 — capturing 14+ columns including per-invoice-uuid + per-invoice-AI-classification-decision + per-vendor-id + per-GL-code-decision + per-cost-center-decision + per-tax-code-decision + per-project-decision + per-OCR-confidence-score + per-line-item-extraction-confidence + per-tenant-id + per-AppZen-tenant-isolation-key + per-vendor-master-record-version + per-policy-rule-version + per-EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-technical-documentation-id + per-EU-AI-Act-Art-9-risk-management-id + per-EU-AI-Act-Art-10-data-governance-id + per-EU-AI-Act-Art-14-human-oversight-id + per-downstream-ERP-id (NetSuite + SAP + Oracle + Workday + Coupa + Ariba) for 7yr WORM + quarterly reconstruction drill. Distinguishes per-invoice-decision grain from per-customer-month rollup grain — auditors open with this question.
  2. Per-Expense-Audit policy-violation join-table per SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 14 (human oversight) + FCPA + Anti-Bribery + Anti-Corruption — 13-column join-table including per-expense-report-uuid + per-receipt-uuid + per-receipt-image-hash + per-OCR-confidence-score + per-policy-violation-decision + per-policy-rule-version + per-policy-rule-effective-date + per-human-override-decision + per-AI-confidence-score + per-receipt-image-upload-event-id + per-tenant-id + per-cross-tenant-isolation-key + per-out-of-policy-spend-amount. Distinguishes per-policy-violation-event grain from collapsed-monthly-rollup grain — auditors open with this exact question.
  3. Autonomous AP + Zenbot touchless-invoice-decision join-table per SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 9.4 + SOX §404 — 12-column join-table including per-touchless-invoice-decision-uuid + per-AI-rationale (GL-code + cost-center + tax-code + project + vendor + amount + currency) + per-tenant-isolation-key + per-cross-tenant-AI-model-version-pinned-decision + per-WORM-retention-timestamp + per-quarterly-reconstruction-drill-pass-event + per-human-in-the-loop-approval-status + per-downstream-ERP-touchpoint-id + per-Zenbot-reasoning-step-id + per-AppZen-policy-rule-version + per-touchless-invoice-amount + per-vendor-risk-score. Distinguishes per-touchless-invoice-decision grain joinable back to upstream OCR + line-item-extraction + vendor-master-lookup + policy-lookup + 3-way-match from per-customer-month-detached-blob grain — auditors open with this question.
  4. Per-invoice fraud-detection join-table per FCPA + OFAC + Anti-Money-Laundering + SOX §802 + EU AI Act Annex III §4 high-risk — 15-column join-table including per-fraud-decision-uuid + per-fraud-type (duplicate-invoice + fictitious-vendor + inflated-amount + split-invoice + shell-vendor + OFAC-match + sanctioned-party-match + bid-rigging + price-fixing + kickback) + per-fraud-confidence-score + per-vendor-master-record-version + per-PO-record-version + per-receiving-record-version + per-3-way-match-decision + per-policy-rule-version + per-vendor-master-history-version + per-sanctioned-party-list-version + per-tenant-id + per-cross-tenant-isolation-key + per-human-investigator-id + per-investigation-status + per-remediation-action + per-FCPA-disclosure-status. Distinguishes per-fraud-decision joinable back to upstream vendor-master + PO + receiving + 3-way-match + policy-rule evidence from per-customer-month-detached-fraud-alert-blob grain — auditors open with this question.
  5. Per-Zenbot AI-accountant-reasoning-step + per-GL-coding-decision join-table per SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + Art. 14 + Art. 53(d) + ISO 42001 9.4 + OWASP LLM Top 10 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE — 14-column join-table including per-Zenbot-reasoning-step-uuid + per-AI-prompt + per-AI-completion + per-AI-model-version-pinned + per-training-corpus-license-provenance + per-GL-coding-decision + per-cost-center-decision + per-tax-code-decision + per-project-decision + per-policy-violation-decision + per-prompt-injection-defense-evidence + per-MCP-router-decision + per-cross-tenant-MCP-router-isolation + per-tenant-id. Ask AppZen to walk through one real invoice-fraud-decision (duplicate-invoice + fictitious-vendor + inflated-amount + split-invoice + shell-vendor + OFAC-match + sanctioned-party-match) back to the upstream vendor-master-record + PO-record + receiving-record + 3-way-match + policy-rule-version + sanctioned-party-list-version evidence chain.

Moveworks vs Microsoft 365 Copilot for Service Desk: 2026 Comparison for Enterprise IT Buyers

If you're a CIO or VP of IT at a Fortune 500 company evaluating AI employee-service-desk platforms in 2026, the decision has narrowed sharply. The two credible enterprise-grade options are Moveworks Copilot and Microsoft 365 Copilot for Service Desk — and the differences matter more than the marketing pages suggest.

Why this comparison exists

We track 3500+ enterprise Moveworks customers and the 75% of Fortune 100 that already hold Microsoft 365 E5 licenses (which now bundle Copilot for Service Desk as a free add-on). The procurement question isn't "which is better?" — it's "which is better for your specific IT support volume, existing Microsoft footprint, and AI governance posture?"

The 5 vendor-DD questions that catch both vendors out

Every CIO we work with asks the same 5 questions. Both vendors have weak spots in their answers. Here's the framework:

  1. How does your employee-copilot onboarding differentiate against Microsoft 365 Copilot for Service Desk (which ships bundled to every E5 customer)?
  2. Which Tier-1 enterprise customer has publicly disclosed ROI metrics from your platform replacing ServiceNow Virtual Agent?
  3. How do you handle the "out-of-policy" LLM-output edge cases when an employee asks the copilot to reset a password that the IT team has explicitly blocked for their role?
  4. What's your defensible moat against an in-house LLM + LangChain + Slack-bot stack that any Fortune 500 IT team can build in 6 weeks with one engineer?
  5. How does your FedRAMP Moderate / High authorization play with the new FedRAMP High requirements for AI workloads under the 2025 OMB memo?

Moveworks Copilot — strengths and weaknesses in 2026

Strengths:

  • Multi-system integration depth: Moveworks natively integrates with 60+ enterprise systems (ServiceNow, Workday, Salesforce, Jira, Confluence, SharePoint, Slack, Teams, Okta, Active Directory, etc.) out of the box. Microsoft 365 Copilot for Service Desk is primarily scoped to the Microsoft Graph API + ServiceNow ITSM via Microsoft's connectors.
  • FedRAMP Moderate (and pursuing High): Moveworks has held FedRAMP Moderate since 2023 and is currently pursuing High. This is critical for federal contractors and healthcare-covered entities.
  • Production scale: 3500+ paying enterprise customers including LinkedIn, Toyota, Atlassian, Hearst, Snowflake, DocuSign, Broadcom, Western Digital, GitLab, Equinix, Humana, Verisk, Lumen, Autodesk, BMC, Cox, PayPal, Unity, Twitter, Verizon, HP, ServiceNow, Adobe, Akamai, Airbnb, Uber, Pinterest, T-Mobile.
  • Procurement-ready compliance: SOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001 + ISO 27701 + GDPR DPA + HIPAA + FedRAMP Moderate, all on the public trust page.
  • Multi-language support: 100+ languages natively supported, vs Microsoft's primary English + 8-12 major languages in Copilot for Service Desk.

Weaknesses:

  • Cost: Moveworks Copilot pricing is enterprise-quoted, typically $40-80/employee/year depending on volume, contract length, and modules (IT Support, HR Helpdesk, Workplace Automation). Microsoft 365 Copilot for Service Desk is bundled into E5 (~$57/user/month) — effectively "free" for E5 customers.
  • Microsoft 365 integration: If you're a heavy Microsoft shop, Moveworks integrates with Teams + Outlook + SharePoint, but the experience is not as native as Microsoft 365 Copilot (which lives inside the Microsoft Graph).
  • Setup time: Moveworks implementations typically take 8-16 weeks for full deployment. Microsoft 365 Copilot for Service Desk can be enabled in days for existing ServiceNow + Microsoft 365 customers.

Microsoft 365 Copilot for Service Desk — strengths and weaknesses

Strengths:

  • Bundled into E5: If you're already paying for Microsoft 365 E5 ($57/user/month), Copilot for Service Desk is effectively included. Moveworks adds an incremental $40-80/employee/year on top.
  • Native Microsoft Graph integration: Deep integration with Outlook, Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive, and the Microsoft 365 admin center. No third-party API limits.
  • Time-to-value: For existing ServiceNow ITSM + Microsoft 365 customers, Copilot for Service Desk can be live in 2-4 weeks.
  • Microsoft's compliance posture: Microsoft holds FedRAMP High, IL5, and the broadest set of compliance certifications in the industry.

Weaknesses:

  • Limited third-party system reach: Beyond ServiceNow ITSM and a handful of approved connectors, Copilot for Service Desk doesn't natively reach the 60+ enterprise systems that Moveworks integrates with.
  • Smaller deployment base: Microsoft 365 Copilot for Service Desk launched in 2024 — production deployment data is limited compared to Moveworks' 3500+ customer base.
  • Less mature employee-experience patterns: Microsoft's out-of-the-box employee-service templates are less developed than Moveworks' 8+ years of production deployments.
  • Microsoft roadmap risk: Microsoft's pricing and feature roadmap for Copilot has changed multiple times since 2023 — procurement teams cite this as a planning risk.

The decision matrix (when to pick which)

If your situation is...Pick
You're a heavy Microsoft 365 E5 shop with simple IT support needsMicrosoft 365 Copilot for Service Desk
You have 30+ enterprise systems outside Microsoft and need deep integrationMoveworks Copilot
You're in a regulated industry (federal, healthcare, financial services) needing FedRAMP HighBoth qualify — but Moveworks' FedRAMP Moderate is well-established while Microsoft 365 Copilot for Service Desk's FedRAMP High authorization is newer
You need 100+ language support for global workforceMoveworks Copilot
You have a small IT team and need fast time-to-valueMicrosoft 365 Copilot for Service Desk
You're replacing an existing ServiceNow Virtual Agent deploymentMoveworks Copilot (more mature migration tooling)
You want a single-vendor procurement story across IT + HR + workplaceMoveworks Copilot (bundled IT Support + HR Helpdesk + Workplace Automation modules)
You want to avoid incremental per-employee AI vendor spendMicrosoft 365 Copilot for Service Desk (bundled in E5)

3-step vendor-DD playbook for the Moveworks vs Microsoft decision

  1. Map your existing system footprint — list every enterprise system your IT + HR + workplace teams use. If more than 30% are non-Microsoft, Moveworks has the integration advantage. If 70%+ are Microsoft and you have E5, the bundled Microsoft economics win.
  2. Calculate the 3-year TCO — Moveworks typically lands at $40-80/employee/year × 3 years. Microsoft 365 Copilot for Service Desk is included in E5 but you still pay for ServiceNow ITSM licensing. Build a 3-year TCO model that includes implementation, integration, and ongoing support costs.
  3. Run a 90-day pilot — both vendors offer enterprise pilots. Scope the pilot to 500-1000 employees, one IT-support workflow (password resets + VPN access + software requests), and one HR-helpdesk workflow (PTO balance + benefits enrollment + payroll questions). Measure deflection rate, CSAT, and time-to-resolution. The pilot results will be decisive.

The 5 vendor-DD questions to put in your RFP

If you're issuing an RFP for an AI employee-service-desk platform in 2026, copy these 5 questions into your RFP document. Both Moveworks and Microsoft will have answers, but the depth of those answers will tell you which vendor has actually thought through the procurement question.

What about the in-house build option?

The fourth option — building an in-house LLM + LangChain + Slack-bot stack — gets floated in every CIO conversation. It's technically feasible (any Fortune 500 IT team can build a working prototype in 6 weeks with one engineer) but the operational burden is significant:

  • Maintenance: LLM provider outages, model upgrades, and security patches require dedicated engineering headcount that most IT teams don't have.
  • Compliance: SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + HIPAA + FedRAMP for an in-house AI service is a 12-18 month project on its own.
  • Integration depth: Moveworks' 60+ native integrations represent years of partnership engineering. Building the same breadth in-house is a 2-3 year project.
  • Continuous learning: Moveworks processes millions of employee requests per month across 3500+ customers — the in-house team would be building from scratch.

Unless you have a 5+ person AI engineering team with 18 months of runway and a clear business case for owning the AI stack end-to-end, the build-vs-buy math favors buying.

The bottom line

Moveworks Copilot and Microsoft 365 Copilot for Service Desk are both credible enterprise-grade AI employee-service-desk platforms in 2026. The decision depends on your existing Microsoft footprint, integration breadth needs, compliance posture, and budget for incremental AI vendor spend. Run the 5 vendor-DD questions, build the 3-year TCO model, and run the 90-day pilot — the data will tell you which vendor fits your specific situation.

For a deeper competitive-positioning audit on Moveworks Copilot vs Microsoft 365 Copilot for Service Desk vs the in-house build option, reply to support@moveworks.com with "audit" and we'll send the 48-hour scoping doc ($500 flat, 1-page battle-card delivered).

Kore.ai vs Moveworks: 2026 AI Agent Bake-Off for Enterprise IT Buyers

If you're a CIO or VP of IT at a Fortune 500 evaluating AI employee-service-desk + customer-service + banking-assistant platforms in 2026, the credible enterprise-grade pure-play options are narrowing fast. Kore.ai and Moveworks are the two largest pure-play AI agent platforms by enterprise customer count and the differences matter more than the marketing pages suggest — especially under the EU AI Act Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement clock and the new FedRAMP 2025 OMB memo for AI workloads.

8-row decision matrix: when to pick which

Decision axisKore.aiMoveworks
Customer count500+ enterprise3,500+ enterprise
Valuation$1.5B+ (Series E)$2.1B (Series C)
Vertical focusCustomer service + IT + HR + Banking + Healthcare + Contact centerEmployee service desk + IT support + HR helpdesk
Banking-vertical productKore.ai BankAssist (production)Not a vertical product
EU AI Act Aug 2026 readinessSOC 2 + ISO 27001 + ISO 27701 + HIPAA + GDPR + EU AI Act ready postureSOC 2 + ISO 27001 + ISO 27701 + HIPAA + GDPR + FedRAMP Moderate
FedRAMP postureFedRAMP-ready (not yet authorized)FedRAMP Moderate authorized (since 2023)
Build-vs-buy defensibility2-engineer + LangGraph + ServiceNow stack matches Tier-1 featuresSame 2-engineer + LangChain + Slack-bot stack matches Tier-1 features
Per-ticket TCO at 1M tickets/yr~$1.40/ticket~$1.20/ticket (lower per-ticket at scale)

3-step vendor-DD playbook

  1. Step 1 — Map your footprint. Are you a regulated banking customer needing BankAssist-grade vertical depth? If yes, Kore.ai wins the bake-off by default — Moveworks doesn't ship a banking-vertical product. If you're a regulated federal contractor needing FedRAMP Moderate AI workloads, Moveworks wins by default — Kore.ai is FedRAMP-ready but not yet authorized. If neither, you have a real bake-off and the remaining 7 axes decide.
  2. Step 2 — 3-year TCO. Kore.ai at 1M tickets/yr is ~$1.40/ticket × 1M = $1.4M/yr. Moveworks at 1M tickets/yr is ~$1.20/ticket × 1M = $1.2M/yr. The 17% delta is roughly $600K over 3 years — material but not the deciding factor. The deciding factor is the EU AI Act Aug 2026 readiness gap: Kore.ai's ISO 27701 + EU AI Act ready posture closes the audit-trail gap that Moveworks's ISO 27701 + FedRAMP Moderate posture doesn't directly cover for EU customers.
  3. Step 3 — 90-day pilot. Run both Kore.ai and Moveworks on the same 5-ticket basket: (a) password reset for a blocked role, (b) credit-card dispute intake, (c) health-insurance enrollment change, (d) cross-border vendor onboarding, (e) AI-agent audit-trail export. The vendor that ships the per-conversational-AI-decision audit trail Table Stakes (16-column join-table below) wins the 90-day pilot. The vendor that doesn't loses the procurement cycle.

5 RFP-ready vendor-DD questions

  1. Ship the per-conversational-AI-decision audit trail (16 columns: AI-decision-uuid, AI-prompt, AI-completion, AI-model-version-pinned, AI-rationale, policy-rule-version, training-corpus-license-provenance, human-handoff-event, out-of-policy-output-blocking-event, prompt-injection-defense-evidence, MCP-router-decision, cross-tenant-isolation-key, WORM-retention-timestamp, tenant-KMS-key-id, cross-border-transfer-SCCs-version, quarterly-reconstruction-drill-pass-event) for the top-3 use cases in your RFP. Vendors that can't ship this lose the 2026 bake-off.
  2. What's your defensible moat against an in-house LLM + LangGraph + Slack-bot + ServiceNow ITSM stack? The 2-engineer 8-week build cost is roughly $2.1M/yr per 1M tickets — that's the floor below which buy-side TCO has to land to win the bake-off.
  3. What's your EU AI Act Annex IV §1-3 GPAI technical-documentation evidence pack? Aug 2026 enforcement is 6 weeks out — vendors without the per-training-corpus-license-provenance + per-AI-decision-audit-trail lose EU regulated bake-offs.
  4. What's your FedRAMP posture for AI workloads? FedRAMP Moderate authorized is the 2026 federal-contractor Table Stakes; FedRAMP High authorization under the 2025 OMB memo is the 2027 federal contractor Table Stakes.
  5. What's your cross-tenant isolation architecture? Per-tenant KMS key + per-tenant-isolation-key + per-cross-tenant-isolation-key + per-WORM-retention-timestamp + per-quarterly-reconstruction-drill is the 2026 regulated-customer Table Stakes.

In-house build-vs-buy section

The 2-engineer in-house LLM + LangGraph + Slack-bot + ServiceNow ITSM stack is the 2026 build-side baseline. Stack cost: ~$2.1M/yr per 1M tickets (2 senior engineers at $400K loaded + GPU rental at $800K + LangSmith observability at $80K + ServiceNow license at $400K + per-ticket GPU inference cost at $420K). Buy-side TCO at the same scale: Kore.ai ~$1.40/ticket = $1.4M/yr; Moveworks ~$1.20/ticket = $1.2M/yr. The buy-side math wins by ~33% *if* the procurement team can defend the AI-agent containment-rate + escalation-rate metrics for 2 consecutive renewal cycles.

The math breaks if the buy-side vendor can't ship the containment-rate + escalation-rate audit trail Table Stakes. That's the audit gap a $500/48h evidence-pack audit covers — and the audit gap that decides the 2026 bake-off.

5-audit-gap map (Kore.ai + Moveworks specific)

  1. Audit gap 1 — per-conversational-AI-decision audit trail. Neither Kore.ai nor Moveworks publicly exposes the 16-column per-decision audit trail. SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Annex IV §1-3 + ISO 42001 9.4 + HIPAA §164.312 + GDPR Art. 28 + OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 + LLM06 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE all require it. Procurement teams that ask for it in 2026 bake-offs save 30-60 days per cycle.
  2. Audit gap 2 — per-training-corpus-license-provenance. EU AI Act Art. 53(d) requires GPAI vendors to disclose training-data transparency. Neither Kore.ai nor Moveworks publishes the per-AI-model-version-pinned training-corpus-license-provenance trail. Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement is 6 weeks out — the audit gap that decides EU regulated bake-offs.
  3. Audit gap 3 — per-prompt-injection-defense-evidence. OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 (prompt injection) is the #1 LLM risk in 2026. Neither Kore.ai nor Moveworks publishes the per-prompt-injection-defense-evidence trail. NIST AI RMF MEASURE requires it for federal-contractor bake-offs.
  4. Audit gap 4 — per-out-of-policy-output-blocking-event. Out-of-policy LLM-output handling is the vCISO question that catches every AI agent vendor. Neither Kore.ai nor Moveworks publishes the per-out-of-policy-output-blocking-event trail. The audit gap that decides vCISO approval.
  5. Audit gap 5 — per-quarterly-reconstruction-drill-pass-event. Quarterly AI-decision reconstruction drills are the new Table Stakes for SOC 2 + EU AI Act + ISO 42001 + FedRAMP. Neither Kore.ai nor Moveworks publishes the per-quarterly-reconstruction-drill trail. The audit gap that decides regulated-customer bake-offs.

Aisera vs Moveworks vs Kore.ai — agentic AI service desk bake-off 2026 (audit-trail + EU AI Act Annex IV + EU-U.S. DPF)

Quick read for enterprise CIOs + CISOs + VPs of IT + GRC leaders evaluating agentic-AI service-desk + IT-helpdesk + HR-helpdesk + customer-service + AI-workflow-automation in 2026: The 3 leading agentic-AI service-desk platforms — Aisera (Muddu Sudhakar, Santa Clara CA, 100+ Fortune 2000 customers, EU-U.S. DPF + UK Extension + Swiss-U.S. DPF certified), Moveworks (Bhavin Shah, Mountain View CA, 3500+ enterprise customers, FedRAMP Moderate), and Kore.ai (Raj Koneru, Orlando FL, 500+ enterprise customers, EU AI Act Aug 2026 ready) — converge on a similar product surface (AI Copilot + AI Agent + IT Service Desk + HR Service Delivery + Customer Service + AI Workflows + ticket-deflection + knowledge-management) but diverge sharply on audit-trail evidence, EU AI Act Annex IV §1-§3 GPAI Aug 2026 enforcement readiness, and cross-border data-transfer posture. This guide walks the 8-row decision matrix + 5-audit-gap map + per-AI-decision join-table + 3-step vendor-DD playbook + 5 RFP-ready vendor-DD questions for the enterprise buyer who needs a defensible vendor-DD before the 2 August 2026 GPAI enforcement deadline.
