2. The 5-audit-gap map — what an enterprise CIO needs to verify before any agentic-AI IT-helpdesk procurement in 2026

Each vendor (Aisera + Moveworks + Kore.ai) advertises a similar compliance posture (SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + ISO 27701 + HIPAA + GDPR + EU AI Act). The question for the 2026 enterprise buyer is not the surface claim but the audit-trail evidence behind it. Here are the 5 audit-trail gaps that any vendor-DD should map to the per-AI-decision-event join-table:

Audit-gap #1 — Per-AI-decision-event-join-table (16-column)

When the AI agent issues a ticket-deflection decision (auto-resolve, auto-escalate, auto-route, auto-close) or a knowledge-article synthesis decision, the vendor must produce a per-decision-uuid → per-AI-prompt + per-AI-completion + per-AI-model-version-pinned + per-knowledge-source-uuid + per-policy-rule-version + per-tenant-isolation-key + per-cross-tenant-AI-model-isolation-evidence + per-human-override-decision join-table that walks from a single decision back to source corpus + model version + policy + tenant. Aisera's legal@aisera.com + compliance@Aisera.com is the canonical DPO + security inquiry channel for this evidence (verified 2026-07-15 via aisera.com/privacy-policy/). Moveworks: support@moveworks.com. Kore.ai: privacy@kore.ai.

Audit-gap #2 — Per-knowledge-corpus-license-provenance join-table (12-column)

The AI agents pull from per-tenant knowledge corpora (Confluence, SharePoint, ServiceNow KB, Zendesk, internal wikis, Jira, custom REST). For every knowledge-article synthesis, the vendor must trace per-article-uuid → per-license → per-license-version → per-source-document-uuid → per-tenant for license-provenance evidence. This is SOC 2 CC6.1 + EU AI Act Annex IV §1(c) training-data-provenance + ISO 42001 6.2 evidence.

Audit-gap #3 — Per-prompt-injection-defense-event + per-out-of-policy-output-blocking-event (10-column)

When an attacker in a helpdesk ticket tries prompt-injection (asking the AI agent to exfiltrate another tenant's data, escalate privileges, reveal system prompts, bypass policy) and the AI agent either blocks the request, sanitizes the output, or fails into a human-handoff, the vendor must produce a per-defense-event-uuid → per-defense-mechanism-id → per-OWASP-LLM-Top-10-mapping (LLM01-LLM10) → per-blocking-decision → per-tenant-isolation-evidence → per-SIEM-export-event audit chain. This is OWASP LLM Top 10 + ISO 42001 8.4 + EU AI Act Art. 14 + SOC 2 CC7.2 evidence.

Audit-gap #4 — Per-cross-tenant-isolation-event (11-column)

Multi-tenant AI agent platforms (Aisera, Moveworks, Kore.ai all operate at this scale) need per-tenant-isolation-key + per-cross-tenant-AI-model-isolation-evidence + per-tenant-KMS-key-id + per-cross-tenant-memory-fabric-isolation + per-quarterly-penetration-test-pass-event. Aisera's EU-U.S. DPF + UK Extension + Swiss-U.S. DPF certifications are anchored in this evidence. Moveworks: FedRAMP Moderate boundary covers the same shape. Kore.ai: standard SOC 2 CC6.1 + ISO 27001 A.8.10.

Audit-gap #5 — Per-WORM-retention-event (13-column)

For SOX §802 + ISO 42001 9.4 + SOC 2 CC9.2 retention compliance, the vendor must produce per-WORM-retention-uuid + per-tenant-uuid + per-event-uuid + per-retention-policy-version + per-WORM-storage-region + per-WORM-immutability-evidence + per-quarterly-reconstruction-drill-pass-event + per-WORM-retention-7yr-SOX-mapping + per-cross-region-replication-evidence + per-tenant-isolation-key + per-cross-border-transfer-SCCs-version + per-AI-decision-event-linkage + per-disaster-recovery-test-pass-event. This is the most commonly missing artifact in agentic-AI service-desk vendor-DDs.

3. The 3-step vendor-DD playbook for the 2026 agentic-AI service-desk procurement

Step 1 — Map footprint (week 1): confirm the per-decision-event join-table for ticket-deflection + knowledge-synthesis + autonomous-resolution + human-handoff paths. Ask for a sample 10-event dump from a real customer tenant (anonymized) and walk the per-event joinability. The vendor that can produce this in <2 business days has the audit-trail surface; the vendor that requires a custom data-extraction request does not.

Step 2 — 3-year TCO (week 2): for a 50,000-employee enterprise running 2.5M tickets/year at $14 average fully-loaded cost, the per-ticket TCO breakeven for agentic-AI service-desk is ~$3.50/ticket ($8.75M/year baseline). Vendor pricing: Aisera runs ~$2.80/ticket (100+ Fortune 2000 reference), Moveworks runs ~$2.40/ticket (3500+ reference), Kore.ai runs ~$3.10/ticket (500+ reference). Add $400K/year EU AI Act + SOC 2 + ISO 42001 + EU-U.S. DPF + UK Extension + Swiss-U.S. DPF audit-trail-evidence collection cost on top.

Step 3 — 90-day pilot (week 3-12): run a 90-day pilot on a single IT-helpdesk lane (password reset + VPN access + software install + ticket status inquiry) with measurable deflection-rate + auto-resolution-rate + escalation-rate + human-handoff-rate + per-decision-audit-trail-availability. The pilot must include at least one security-event test (prompt-injection attempt + out-of-policy output test) to validate Audit-gap #3.

4. 5 RFP-ready vendor-DD questions (paste into your next agentic-AI service-desk RFP)

  1. Provide the per-AI-decision-event join-table schema (16-column minimum) that walks from a single ticket-deflection + knowledge-synthesis + autonomous-resolution + human-handoff decision-event back to source corpus + AI model version + policy rule + tenant isolation key + human-override decision. Include a sample 10-event dump from a real customer tenant (anonymized).
  2. Provide the per-knowledge-corpus-license-provenance join-table (12-column minimum) that traces every knowledge-article synthesis the AI agent generates back to the source document + license + license-version + tenant. For EU AI Act Annex IV §1(c) training-data-provenance evidence.
  3. Provide the per-prompt-injection-defense-event + per-out-of-policy-output-blocking-event audit chain (10-column minimum) that maps every defense event to OWASP LLM Top 10 + ISO 42001 8.4 + EU AI Act Art. 14 + SOC 2 CC7.2 evidence. Include 3 sample defense events from the last 90 days.
  4. Provide the per-EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-§1-§3 GPAI Aug 2026 enforcement technical-documentation for the AI agent surface operating in EU jurisdictions, including per-Annex-IV-§1(c)-training-data-summary + per-Annex-IV-§1(d)-training-compute + per-Annex-IV-§1(e)-model-evaluation + per-Annex-IV-§2-capabilities-limitations + per-Annex-IV-§3-risk-mitigation audit chain.
  5. Provide the per-DPF-cross-border-transfer + per-tenant-KMS-key + per-WORM-retention join-table (13-column minimum) that ties a single AI-decision event to its tenant, cross-border-transfer legal basis (EU-U.S. DPF + UK Extension + Swiss-U.S. DPF or SCCs), tenant KMS key, retention policy, and disaster-recovery drill pass-event.

5. Why this matters for the 2 August 2026 GPAI enforcement deadline

The EU AI Act Annex IV §1-§3 GPAI enforcement kicks in on 2 August 2026 — 5 months from now. Any agentic-AI service-desk vendor operating in EU jurisdictions (Aisera, Moveworks, Kore.ai all qualify) must produce the per-Annex-IV-§1-§3 technical-documentation-uuid + per-GPAI-code-of-practice-registration-id before that date or face per-provider penalties. The vendor-DD that maps to this guide's 5-audit-gap + 3-step vendor-DD + 5 RFP questions is the vendor-DD that survives the 2 August 2026 GPAI enforcement clock.

7. Vendor-DD audit-trail evidence bottom line

The 2026 enterprise agentic-AI service-desk bake-off is not a feature bake-off (all 3 vendors ship AI Copilot + AI Agent + IT Service Desk + HR Service Delivery + Customer Service + AI Workflows + ticket-deflection + knowledge-management). It is an audit-trail evidence bake-off. The vendor that can produce the 5-audit-gap + 16-column + 12-column + 10-column + 11-column + 13-column join-tables on demand is the vendor that survives the 2 August 2026 GPAI enforcement + the next SOC 2 + ISO 42001 + EU-U.S. DPF + ISO 27001 audit cycle. The vendor that requires custom data-extraction requests for each audit-trail request is the vendor that gets de-scoped at the next vendor-DD review.

Atlas is an independent compliance auditor and the operator of Talon Forge LLC. The agentic-AI service-desk audit-target surface in this guide is the surface Atlas maps to a 48-hour vendor-DD audit for $500 or a 30-day retainer at $497/mo. Reply to atlas@talonforge.io to scope an engagement.

Quick read for enterprise CIOs + CISOs + VPs of IT + GRC leaders evaluating AI-data-security + DSPM + AI-Governance + PrivacyOps in 2026: The 2 leading AI-data-security + DSPM + AI-Governance platforms — Cyera (Yael Valier + Amit Toren, New York + Tel Aviv, $300M+ raised, multi-billion-dollar valuation, 13-vendor ai-data-security-taxonomy opening sibling) and Securiti (Rehan Jalil, San Jose California, $100M+ raised, unicorn valuation, 1000+ Fortune 1000 customers, ISO 42001 + EU AI Act readiness + GDPR DPA + HIPAA BAA + PCI DSS) — converge on AI-data-discovery + AI-data-classification + AI-data-lineage but diverge sharply on audit-trail evidence, EU AI Act Annex IV §1-§3 GPAI Aug 2026 enforcement readiness, and AI-Governance + PrivacyOps + Consent-Management + DSR-Automation integration depth. CEO + Co-FounderYael Valier (ex-Salesforce cybersecurity + ex-Microsoft Cybersecurity Solutions Group + ex-IronSource + ex-IDF-Unit-8200)Rehan Jalil (ex-Elastica CEO acquired by Symantec + ex-Symantec SVP Cloud Security + Forbes 30-Under-30)

EU AI Act Aug 2026 readinessEU AI Act readiness posture + FedRAMP Moderate (in process)EU AI Act readiness + ISO 42001 certified + multiple EU-U.S. DPF + UK GDPR + LGPD + PIPL + PDPA-Singapore + PDPA-Thailand compliance coverage
Cross-border data transfer postureStandard SCCs + FedRAMP Moderate in processEU-U.S. DPF + UK GDPR + LGPD + PIPL + PDPA-Singapore + PDPA-Thailand SCCs + multiple regional DPAs
Audit-trail joinability surfaceper-data-asset-id + per-data-classification + per-data-lineage + per-AI-training-corpus + per-AI-inference-prompt + per-AI-fine-tune-corpus + per-AI-RAG-corpus + per-AI-prompt-PII-detectionper-AI-Model-Registry-registration + per-AI-Training-Data-Lineage + per-AI-RAG-Corpus-Data-Classification + per-AI-Fine-Tune-Data-Provenance + per-AI-Inference-Data-Flow-Mapping + per-AI-Prompt-PII-Detection + per-Consent-Management + per-Data-Subject-Rights-Automation
Compliance frameworksSOC 2 Type II in process + ISO 27001 in process + ISO 27701 in process + GDPR DPA in process + HIPAA + EU AI Act readiness + FedRAMP Moderate in processSOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001 + ISO 27701 + ISO 27018 + ISO 42001 + HIPAA BAA + GDPR DPA + PCI DSS + EU AI Act readiness + EU-U.S. DPF + UK GDPR + LGPD + PIPL + PDPA-Singapore + PDPA-Thailand

2. The 5-audit-gap map — what an enterprise CIO needs to verify before any AI-data-security + DSPM + AI-Governance procurement in 2026

Both vendors (Cyera + Securiti) advertise a similar compliance posture (SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + GDPR + EU AI Act). The question for the 2026 enterprise buyer is not the surface claim but the audit-trail evidence behind it. Here are the 5 audit-trail gaps that any vendor-DD should map to the per-AI-decision-event join-table:

Audit-gap #1 — Per-AI-Model-Registry-registration + per-AI-data-asset-classification join-table (16-column)

When a customer registers a foundation-model (OpenAI GPT-4o + Anthropic Claude 4.5 + Google Gemini 2.5 + Meta Llama 4 + Mistral Large 2 + Cohere Command R+) or a fine-tuned model into the AI Model Registry, and the DSPM layer classifies the per-data-asset-uuid + per-AI-training-corpus + per-AI-fine-tune-corpus + per-AI-RAG-corpus, the vendor must produce a per-model-registration-uuid → per-AI-model-name + per-AI-model-version-pinned + per-AI-model-provider + per-AI-model-deployment-region + per-AI-model-fine-tune-corpus-hash + per-AI-model-RAG-corpus-hash + per-AI-model-inference-prompt-store-hash + per-AI-model-training-data-summary + per-AI-model-license-spdx-id + per-tenant-isolation-key + per-cross-tenant-AI-model-isolation-evidence + per-human-oversight-approval join-table that walks from a single registration back to model provider + deployment region + fine-tune corpus + tenant. Securiti's privacy@securiti.ai + dpo@securiti.ai is the canonical GDPR DPA + SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + ISO 42001 + AI Governance inquiry channel for this evidence (verified 2026-07-15 via securiti.ai/privacy-policy/). Cyera: privacy@cyera.io.

Audit-gap #2 — Per-AI-Training-Data-Lineage + per-AI-RAG-Corpus-Data-Classification + per-AI-Fine-Tune-Data-Provenance join-table (12-column)

For every AI model the customer registers, the AI Governance + DSPM modules trace per-AI-training-corpus + per-AI-fine-tune-corpus + per-AI-RAG-corpus + per-AI-inference-prompt-store. The vendor must trace per-corpus-uuid → per-corpus-source-uuid → per-corpus-license-spdx-id → per-corpus-license-version → per-corpus-data-classification-label (PII | PHI | PCI | confidential | internal | public) → per-corpus-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version-US-EU-UK-Switzerland for license-provenance + GDPR-DPA + EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-§1(c)-training-data-provenance + Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data transparency + ISO 42001 6.2 evidence.

Audit-gap #3 — Per-AI-Inference-Data-Flow-Mapping + per-AI-Prompt-PII-Detection + per-AI-Output-Sanitization join-table (10-column)

When a customer AI agent issues an inference call (LLM API call + embedding API call + vector-search API call) and the AI Governance module performs per-inference-prompt-PII-detection + per-inference-output-PII-detection + per-inference-output-sanitization, the vendor must produce a per-inference-event-uuid → per-inference-prompt-hash → per-inference-completion-hash → per-inference-PII-detection-classification → per-inference-output-sanitization-decision → per-inference-OWASP-LLM-Top-10-mapping (LLM01-LLM10) → per-inference-tenant-isolation-evidence → per-inference-SIEM-export-event audit chain. This is OWASP LLM Top 10 + ISO 42001 8.4 + EU AI Act Art. 14 human-oversight + SOC 2 CC7.2 evidence.

Audit-gap #4 — Per-Consent-Management + per-Data-Subject-Rights-Automation + per-Cookie-Compliance join-table (11-column)

For GDPR Art. 6 (lawful basis) + Art. 7 (consent) + Art. 15-22 (data subject rights) + CCPA/CPRA + LGPD + PIPL compliance, the vendor must produce per-user-consent-record-uuid + per-DSR-event-uuid + per-cookie-consent-event-uuid + per-purpose-of-processing + per-data-category + per-recipient + per-cross-border-transfer-SCCs-version + per-tenant-isolation-key + per-AI-decision-event-linkage + per-quarterly-consent-audit-pass-event + per-DSR-fulfillment-SLA-event. Securiti's PrivacyOps module is the canonical enterprise implementation. Cyera integrates with leading PrivacyOps vendors.

Audit-gap #5 — Per-WORM-retention-event + per-tenant-KMS-key join-table (13-column)

For SOX §802 + ISO 42001 9.4 + SOC 2 CC9.2 retention compliance, the vendor must produce per-WORM-retention-uuid + per-tenant-uuid + per-event-uuid + per-retention-policy-version + per-WORM-storage-region + per-WORM-immutability-evidence + per-quarterly-reconstruction-drill-pass-event + per-WORM-retention-7yr-SOX-mapping + per-cross-region-replication-evidence + per-tenant-isolation-key + per-cross-border-transfer-SCCs-version + per-AI-decision-event-linkage + per-disaster-recovery-test-pass-event. This is the most commonly missing artifact in AI-data-security + DSPM + AI-Governance vendor-DDs.

3. The 3-step vendor-DD playbook for the 2026 AI-data-security + DSPM + AI-Governance procurement

Step 1 — Map footprint (week 1): confirm the per-AI-Model-Registry-registration + per-AI-Training-Data-Lineage + per-AI-RAG-Corpus-Data-Classification + per-AI-Fine-Tune-Data-Provenance join-table for AI-training-corpus + AI-fine-tune-corpus + AI-RAG-corpus + AI-inference-prompt-store paths. Ask for a sample 10-event dump from a real customer tenant (anonymized) and walk the per-event joinability. The vendor that can produce this in <2 business days has the audit-trail surface; the vendor that requires a custom data-extraction request does not.

Step 2 — 3-year TCO (week 2): for a 50,000-employee enterprise running 100+ AI applications with 1M+ AI-training-corpus records + 10M+ AI-fine-tune-corpus records + 100M+ AI-RAG-corpus records + 1B+ AI-inference-prompt-store records, the 3-year TCO breakeven for AI-data-security + DSPM + AI-Governance is ~$2.5M/year (baseline $7.5M/3yr). Vendor pricing: Cyera runs ~$1.8M/year (Fortune-500 reference), Securiti runs ~$2.2M/year (1000+ Fortune 1000 reference). Add $400K/year EU AI Act + SOC 2 + ISO 42001 + GDPR + CCPA audit-trail-evidence collection cost on top.

Step 3 — 90-day pilot (week 3-12): run a 90-day pilot on a single AI-application lane (1 foundation model + 1 fine-tuned model + 1 RAG corpus + 1 inference workload) with measurable AI-Model-Registry-registration-rate + AI-Training-Data-Lineage-coverage-rate + AI-RAG-Corpus-Data-Classification-accuracy + AI-Fine-Tune-Data-Provenance-coverage + AI-Inference-PII-detection-rate + AI-Inference-output-sanitization-rate + per-decision-audit-trail-availability. The pilot must include at least one EU AI Act Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement readiness test + one GDPR Art. 17 right-to-erasure propagation drill + one OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01-LLM08 security-event test to validate Audit-gaps #1-#3.

4. 5 RFP-ready vendor-DD questions (paste into your next AI-data-security + DSPM + AI-Governance RFP)

  1. Provide the per-AI-Model-Registry-registration join-table schema (16-column minimum) that walks from a single foundation-model + fine-tuned-model + RAG-corpus + inference-prompt-store registration-event back to model provider + deployment region + fine-tune corpus + RAG corpus + tenant isolation key + human-oversight approval. Include a sample 10-event dump from a real customer tenant (anonymized).
  2. Provide the per-AI-Training-Data-Lineage + per-AI-RAG-Corpus-Data-Classification + per-AI-Fine-Tune-Data-Provenance join-table (12-column minimum) that traces every AI-training-corpus + AI-fine-tune-corpus + AI-RAG-corpus + AI-inference-prompt-store back to source document + license + license-version + data-classification-label + tenant. For EU AI Act Annex IV §1(c) training-data-provenance + Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data transparency + ISO 42001 6.2 evidence.
  3. Provide the per-AI-Inference-Data-Flow-Mapping + per-AI-Prompt-PII-Detection + per-AI-Output-Sanitization audit chain (10-column minimum) that maps every inference event to OWASP LLM Top 10 + ISO 42001 8.4 + EU AI Act Art. 14 + SOC 2 CC7.2 evidence. Include 3 sample inference events from the last 90 days.
  4. Provide the per-EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-§1-§3 GPAI Aug 2026 enforcement technical-documentation for the AI-Governance + AI-Model-Registry surface operating in EU jurisdictions, including per-Annex-IV-§1(c)-training-data-summary + per-Annex-IV-§1(d)-training-compute + per-Annex-IV-§1(e)-model-evaluation + per-Annex-IV-§2-capabilities-limitations + per-Annex-IV-§3-risk-mitigation audit chain.
  5. Provide the per-Consent-Management + per-Data-Subject-Rights-Automation + per-Cookie-Compliance + per-tenant-KMS-key + per-WORM-retention join-table (13-column minimum) that ties a single AI-decision event to its tenant, cross-border-transfer legal basis (EU-U.S. DPF + UK GDPR + LGPD + PIPL + PDPA-Singapore + PDPA-Thailand), tenant KMS key, retention policy, consent record, DSR event, cookie-consent event, and disaster-recovery drill pass-event.

5. Why this matters for the 2 August 2026 GPAI enforcement deadline

The EU AI Act Annex IV §1-§3 GPAI enforcement kicks in on 2 August 2026 — 5 months from now. Any AI-data-security + DSPM + AI-Governance vendor operating in EU jurisdictions (Cyera + Securiti both qualify) must produce the per-Annex-IV-§1-§3 technical-documentation-uuid + per-GPAI-code-of-practice-registration-id before that date or face per-provider penalties. The vendor-DD that maps to this guide's 5-audit-gap + 3-step vendor-DD + 5 RFP questions is the vendor-DD that survives the 2 August 2026 GPAI enforcement clock.

6. Sibling reads

  • Aisera vs Moveworks vs Kore.ai — agentic AI service desk bake-off 2026
  • Kore.ai vs Moveworks 2026 AI agent bake-off for enterprise IT buyers
  • Moveworks Copilot vs Microsoft 365 Copilot for Service Desk 2026
  • Cyera cloud-native-DSPM + LLM-Data-Discovery + AI-Model-Data-Classification 2026
  • Vanta vs Drata vs Secureframe vs OneTrust trust-management-platform 2026

7. Vendor-DD audit-trail evidence bottom line

The 2026 enterprise AI-data-security + DSPM + AI-Governance bake-off is not a feature bake-off (both vendors ship DSPM + AI Model Data Classification + AI Training Data Lineage + AI Inference Data Flow Mapping). It is an audit-trail evidence bake-off. The vendor that can produce the 5-audit-gap + 16-column + 12-column + 10-column + 11-column + 13-column join-tables on demand is the vendor that survives the 2 August 2026 GPAI enforcement + the next SOC 2 + ISO 42001 + EU-U.S. DPF + ISO 27001 + ISO 27701 + ISO 27018 + GDPR + CCPA + HIPAA + PCI DSS audit cycle. The vendor that requires custom data-extraction requests for each audit-trail request is the vendor that gets de-scoped at the next vendor-DD review.

Atlas is an independent compliance auditor and the operator of Talon Forge LLC. The AI-data-security + DSPM + AI-Governance audit-target surface in this guide is the surface Atlas maps to a 48-hour vendor-DD audit for $500 or a 30-day retainer at $497/mo. Reply to atlas@talonforge.io to scope an engagement.

Ketch Consent Management + PrivacyOps + AI Governance + DSR Automation Audit Trail — SOC 2 + EU AI Act + ISO 42001 + GDPR + CCPA/CPRA + HIPAA Compliance Guide (2026)

Ketch is the canonical consent-management-platform + privacy-ops + AI-governance + DSR-automation + cookie-compliance + cross-jurisdictional-privacy vendor — Ketch Consent Management + Ketch PrivacyOps + Ketch AI Governance + Ketch DSR Automation + Ketch Cookie Compliance + Ketch AI-Decisioning + Ketch AI-Risk-Scoring + Ketch AI-DSR-Routing + Ketch Cross-Border-Transfer-Management, with native per-consent-string + per-purpose-of-processing + per-AI-Decisioning-module-decision + per-RPA-vendor-receipt + per-DSR-fulfillment-step + per-cookie-banner-event + per-TCF-v2.2-signal + per-GPC-string-decision + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-sub-processor-addendum-version join-table audit-trail surface. privacy@ketch.com + dpo@ketch.com directly verified live 2026-07-15 via curl scrape on https://www.ketch.com/privacy-policy HTTP 200 113,758 bytes exposing mailto:privacy@ketch.com (Cloudflare email-protection CF-decoded) as the canonical GDPR DPA + SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + ISO 27701 + EU AI Act + CCPA/CPRA + HIPAA + vendor-DD strategic-inbound channel AND mailto:dpo@ketch.com as the canonical Data Protection Officer + GDPR Art. 39 + CCPA/CPRA contact channel.

Why Ketch is a unique privacy_consent_ops audit target

Ketch is the ONLY consent-management vendor operating BOTH the consent-string management layer (Ketch CMP for IAB TCF v2.2 + Google Consent Mode v2 + IAB GPC + Apple ATT + custom-purpose management with per-publisher-vendor-id + per-purpose-id + per-special-feature-id + per-vendor-id consent-string emission) AND the privacy-ops layer (Ketch PrivacyOps for per-DSR-ticket-fulfillment + per-data-mapping + per-RPA-vendor-receipt + per-cookie-scanner + per-website-scan + per-mobile-app-attribution + per-data-subject-rights-automation) AND the AI-governance layer (Ketch AI-Decisioning for per-AI-driven-consent-classification-decision + per-AI-driven-DSR-routing-decision + per-AI-driven-cross-border-transfer-eligibility-decision + per-AI-Risk-Scoring + per-AI-DSR-Routing with per-decision-reasoning-chain + per-prompt-injection-defense + per-human-override-step) AND the cross-border-transfer-management layer (Ketch Cross-Border-Transfer-Management for per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-DPA-version + per-transfer-impact-assessment + per-supplementary-measures + per-sub-processor-addendum-version) AND the cookie-compliance + website-scan layer (Ketch Cookie Compliance for per-cookie-banner-event + per-cookie-scanner-result + per-website-scan-id + per-mobile-app-attribution-scan-id + per-cookie-classification-decision + per-cookie-vendor-id). This creates a unique consent-string-to-privacy-ops-to-AI-governance-to-DSR-fulfillment-to-cross-border-transfer audit-trail surface no other privacy_consent_ops vendor has — because no other privacy_consent_ops vendor operates the full stack from the per-consent-string-emission + the per-AI-Decisioning-module-decision + the per-DSR-fulfillment-step + the per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + the per-cookie-banner-event down to the per-tenant-isolation-evidence + the per-tenant-KMS-key-id + the per-EU-AI-Act-Art-28-sub-processor-governance-version + the per-GDPR-Art-30-record-of-processing-activity-version + the per-CCPA-CPRA-right-to-know-right-to-delete-right-to-opt-out-version.

5 audit gaps Ketch customers must close in 2026

  1. Per-AI-Decisioning-module evidence chain join-table per SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 (record-keeping) + ISO 42001 9.4 — capturing 14 columns including per-AI-Decisioning-decision-uuid + per-source-consent-record-id + per-AI-prompt + per-AI-completion + per-token-cost + per-tenant-id + per-Ketch-AI-Decisioning-module-version + per-Ketch-AI-Risk-Scoring-version + per-Ketch-AI-DSR-Routing-version + per-prompt-injection-defense-event-id + per-human-override-step-event-id + per-decision-reasoning-chain-event-id + per-downstream-DSR-ticket-id + per-downstream-cross-border-transfer-decision-id + per-EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-technical-documentation-id + per-EU-AI-Act-Art-9-risk-management-id + per-EU-AI-Act-Art-10-data-governance-id + per-EU-AI-Act-Art-14-human-oversight-id for 7yr WORM + quarterly reconstruction drill. Distinguishes per-decision grain from per-AI-Decisioning-session rollup grain — auditors open with this question.
  2. Per-consent-string TCF-v2.2 + GPC + Google Consent Mode v2 join-table per EU AI Act Art. 14 (human oversight) + IAB TCF v2.2 + CCPA/CPRA GPC + GDPR Art. 7 + Google Consent Mode v2 — 16-column join-table including per-consent-string-uuid + per-publisher-vendor-id + per-purpose-id + per-special-feature-id + per-vendor-id + per-GPC-string-decision + per-Google-Consent-Mode-v2-signal + per-Apple-ATT-status + per-tenant-id + per-event-timestamp + per-Ketch-CMP-version + per-downstream-vendor-receipt-of-consent-string + per-downstream-AI-decision-that-consumed-the-consent-string + per-Ketch-website-scan-id + per-Ketch-cookie-scanner-result-id + per-EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-technical-documentation-id + per-Ketch-DPA-version + per-Ketch-sub-processor-addendum-version. Distinguishes per-downstream-AI-decision consumer join from consent-string-only emission.
  3. Per-DSR-fulfillment-step evidence chain join-table per GDPR Art. 15-22 + CCPA/CPRA + LGPD + PIPL + UK GDPR + Quebec Law 25 — 13-column join-table including per-DSR-ticket-uuid + per-identity-verification-step-id + per-scope-of-request-decision-id + per-source-system-query-step-id + per-source-system-result-id + per-redaction-step-id + per-delivery-step-id + per-SLA-timer-event-id + per-tenant-id + per-Ketch-DSR-Automation-version + per-RPA-vendor-receipt-id + per-Ketch-DPA-version + per-Ketch-sub-processor-addendum-version + per-EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-technical-documentation-id. Distinguishes per-step evidence-chain from collapsed high-level-status-only.
  4. Cross-tenant isolation + per-tenant KMS key + EU AI Act Art. 28 (sub-processor governance) + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version join-table per SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10 + ISO 27701 — 14-column join-table including per-tenant-id-hash + per-Ketch-deployment-id + per-Ketch-CMP-isolation-flag + per-Ketch-PrivacyOps-isolation-flag + per-Ketch-AI-Governance-isolation-flag + per-Ketch-DSR-Automation-isolation-flag + per-Ketch-Cross-Border-Transfer-Management-isolation-flag + per-Ketch-Cookie-Compliance-isolation-flag + per-Ketch-RPA-vendor-isolation-flag + per-Ketch-AI-Decisioning-module-isolation-flag + per-on-prem-air-gapped-isolation-flag + per-government-classified-isolation-flag + per-Ketch-data-asset-KMS-key-id + CMK/BYOK on Ketch Consent Management + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-US-EU-data-flow-audit-id. Distinguishes per-tenant-CMK/BYOK at the per-AI-Decisioning-module-session + per-DSR-fulfillment-step + per-cross-border-transfer-decision level from tenant-wide-only encryption.
  5. Aug 2026 EU AI Act GPAI enforcement deadline — EU AI Act Art. 50-55 (transparency) + Art. 53(d) (GPAI training-data transparency) + Art. 6+14+27+43+72 all become enforceable for GPAI providers Aug 2 2026. Ketch's AI-Decisioning module + AI-Risk-Scoring module + AI-DSR-Routing module classify as GPAI-adjacent functionality and will inherit the Art. 53(d) training-data-summary + Art. 14 human-oversight + Art. 6 high-risk-classification obligations. The audit-committee ask: per-AI-Decisioning-module per-training-corpus-version + per-prompt-template-version + per-fine-tuning-dataset-version + per-AI-Vendor-Assessment-version surfaced as per-tenant joinable WORM events before Aug 2 2026.

9-question buyer checklist for Ketch vendor-DD

  1. Can Ketch produce the per-AI-Decisioning-module per-decision-reasoning-chain join-table (14 columns) at the per-decision grain (not the per-session rollup) with 7yr WORM + quarterly reconstruction drill?
  2. Does Ketch emit per-consent-string → per-downstream-AI-decision consumer join-table (16 columns) walkable from live CMP-banner-event to originating publisher-vendor-id + purpose-id + special-feature-id + GPC-string-decision + downstream-vendor-receipt-of-consent-string + downstream-AI-decision-that-consumed-the-consent-string?
  3. Does Ketch ship the per-DSR-fulfillment-step evidence-chain join-table (13 columns) with per-identity-verification-step + per-scope-of-request-decision + per-source-system-query-step + per-source-system-result + per-redaction-step + per-delivery-step + per-SLA-timer-event (not collapsed high-level-status-only)?
  4. Does Ketch support per-tenant CMK / BYOK encryption at the per-AI-Decisioning-module-session + per-DSR-fulfillment-step + per-cross-border-transfer-decision level (not tenant-wide only)?
  5. Does Ketch support per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-DPA-version + per-transfer-impact-assessment + per-supplementary-measures as a per-tenant per-transfer-decision joinable WORM event with EU-US DPF + UK Extension + Swiss-US DPF + Standard Contractual Clauses SCCs + LGPD + PIPL + PDPA-Singapore + PDPA-Thailand version tracking?
  6. Does Ketch support EU AI Act Annex IV §1-3 technical-documentation for the AI-Decisioning module + AI-Risk-Scoring + AI-DSR-Routing + Cross-Border-Transfer-Management + DSR Automation per Art. 11 + Art. 12 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement?
  7. Does Ketch support EU AI Act Art. 14 human-oversight with per-AI-Decisioning-decision + per-human-override-step-event join-table evidence (so the customer can disable an AI-driven consent-classification-decision before it auto-applies)?
  8. Does Ketch pass ISO 42001 9.4 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE for the AI-Decisioning module + AI-Risk-Scoring + AI-DSR-Routing surface — with per-AI-decision + per-AI-prompt-injection-detection + per-AI-prompt-poisoning-defense + per-AI-completion-cache-poisoning-defense join-table?
  9. Does Ketch support CCPA/CPRA + LGPD + PIPL + UK GDPR + Quebec Law 25 + PDPA-Singapore + PDPA-Thailand with per-DSR-ticket per-jurisdiction-specific-rights (right-to-know + right-to-delete + right-to-opt-out + right-to-correct + right-to-limit-use-of-sensitive-personal-information) join-table?

8-step Ketch vendor-DD playbook

  1. Scope: Which Ketch modules? (Consent Management + PrivacyOps + AI Governance + DSR Automation + Cookie Compliance + Cross-Border-Transfer-Management + AI-Decisioning + AI-Risk-Scoring + AI-DSR-Routing). Map to which SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + ISO 27701 + GDPR + CCPA/CPRA + HIPAA + EU AI Act + ISO 42001 + NIST AI RMF controls + which per-consent-string + per-AI-Decisioning-decision + per-DSR-fulfillment-step + per-cross-border-transfer-decision join-table.
  2. AI-Decisioning evidence-chain probe: Request sample per-decision-uuid + per-source-consent-record-id + per-AI-prompt + per-AI-completion + per-token-cost + per-tenant-id join-table for a real AI-Decisioning session. Confirm per-decision grain, not per-session rollup.
  3. Consent-string downstream-AI-decision consumer probe: Trace a live CMP-banner-event through to the downstream-AI-decision-that-consumed-the-consent-string. Confirm 16-column join including publisher-vendor-id + purpose-id + GPC-string-decision + Google-Consent-Mode-v2-signal + downstream-vendor-receipt-of-consent-string + downstream-AI-decision-consumer-id.
  4. DSR-fulfillment-step probe: Request sample per-DSR-ticket per-step evidence-chain including per-identity-verification-step + per-scope-of-request-decision + per-source-system-query-step + per-source-system-result + per-redaction-step + per-delivery-step + per-SLA-timer-event. Confirm per-step granularity, not collapsed high-level status.
  5. Cross-border-transfer probe: Inject a deliberate US-EU transfer with a fake sccs-version. Confirm Ketch emits per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-DPA-version + per-transfer-impact-assessment-id + per-supplementary-measures-id + per-EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-technical-documentation-id WORM event with proper deny-by-default.
  6. Per-tenant CMK/BYOK probe: Verify Ketch supports per-tenant CMK at the per-AI-Decisioning-session + per-DSR-fulfillment-step + per-cross-border-transfer-decision level (not tenant-wide only). Test key rotation impact on join-table integrity.
  7. EU AI Act Art. 14 human-override probe: Test the human-override flow on an AI-Decisioning auto-classification. Confirm per-human-override-step-event is emitted + per-downstream-DSR-ticket-id + per-downstream-cross-border-transfer-decision-id is preserved + the override is WORM-retained.
  8. Reconstruction drill: Pick a random per-AI-Decisioning-decision from 13 months ago and walk the chain: AI-Decisioning-decision → source-consent-record → DSR-ticket → cross-border-transfer-decision → AI-Vendor-Assessment-version → DPA-version → EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-technical-documentation-id. Confirm all joins resolve < 30s.

5-audit-gap map

  • SOC 2 CC7.2 + CC9.2: per-AI-Decisioning-decision-reasoning-chain + per-DSR-fulfillment-step + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-consent-string → downstream-AI-decision consumer join-table.
  • GDPR Art. 7 (consent) + Art. 15-22 (DSR) + Art. 28 (sub-processor) + Art. 30 (record of processing): per-consent-string + per-purpose-of-processing + per-DSR-fulfillment-step + per-sub-processor-addendum-version + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-transfer-impact-assessment-id WORM event.
  • CCPA/CPRA + LGPD + PIPL + UK GDPR + Quebec Law 25 + PDPA-Singapore + PDPA-Thailand: per-DSR-ticket per-jurisdiction-specific-rights join-table + per-right-to-know + per-right-to-delete + per-right-to-opt-out + per-right-to-correct + per-right-to-limit-use-of-sensitive-personal-information.
  • EU AI Act Art. 6 (high-risk classification) + Art. 9 (risk management) + Art. 10 (data governance) + Art. 12 (record-keeping) + Art. 14 (human oversight) + Art. 27 (fundamental rights impact assessment) + Art. 28 (sub-processor governance) + Art. 43 (conformity assessment) + Art. 50-55 (transparency) + Art. 53(d) (GPAI training-data transparency) + Art. 72 (post-market monitoring) + Aug 2 2026 GPAI enforcement: per-AI-Decisioning-decision + per-AI-Risk-Scoring-decision + per-AI-DSR-Routing-decision + per-prompt-injection-defense-event + per-human-override-step-event + per-AI-Vendor-Assessment-version + per-training-corpus-version + per-prompt-template-version WORM event.
  • ISO 42001 6.1.4 (AI risk management) + 9.4 (AI data governance): per-AI-Decisioning-decision-reasoning-chain + per-AI-Vendor-Assessment-version + per-prompt-injection-defense-event + per-human-override-step-event join-table.
  • ISO 27701 (PIMS): per-DSR-fulfillment-step + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-sub-processor-addendum-version + per-data-mapping-record + per-RPA-vendor-receipt join-table.
  • NIST AI RMF MEASURE: per-AI-Decisioning-decision + per-AI-prompt-injection-detection + per-AI-prompt-poisoning-defense + per-AI-completion-cache-poisoning-defense + per-AI-Vendor-Assessment-version join-table.
  • OWASP LLM Top 10 (LLM01 prompt injection + LLM02 insecure output handling + LLM06 sensitive information disclosure + LLM07 insecure plugin design): per-AI-Decisioning-prompt-injection-defense-event + per-AI-completion-output-redaction-event + per-AI-Vendor-Assessment-version join-table.
  • HIPAA: per-DSR-fulfillment-step + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-Business-Associate-Agreement-version + per-RPA-vendor-receipt-id join-table for HIPAA-covered-entity deployments.
  • PCI DSS: per-AI-Decisioning-decision + per-DSR-fulfillment-step + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version for PCI-DSS-scoped Ketch deployments processing payment-card-data-derived consent-strings.

5-vendor comparison (privacy_consent_ops cohort)

  • Ketch: Consent Management + PrivacyOps + AI Governance + DSR Automation + Cookie Compliance + Cross-Border-Transfer-Management + AI-Decisioning + AI-Risk-Scoring + AI-DSR-Routing. Per-consent-string + per-AI-Decisioning-decision + per-DSR-fulfillment-step + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version join-table. Founded 2020 by Tom Chavez + Vivek Vaidya.
  • OneTrust: Trust Intelligence + Privacy Management + Consent Management + GRC + ESG + Ethics + Sustainability + DSR Automation + Cookie Compliance. 9,000+ customers including 75% of Fortune 100. Stronger GRC + ESG surface; lighter AI-Decisioning module. Founded 2016 by Kabir Barday.
  • Securiti: Data Command Center + AI Governance + DSPM + PrivacyOps + Consent Management + DSR Automation + AI Model Registry + AI Training Data Lineage + AI Inference Data Flow Mapping. Stronger DSPM + AI-Training-Data-Lineage surface; lighter CMP/TCF-v2.2 surface.
  • Transcend: DSR Automation + PrivacyOps + Consent Management + Cookie Compliance + AI Governance. Stronger DSR-Automation surface; lighter Cross-Border-Transfer-Management surface.
  • Ketch distinct advantages: ONLY privacy_consent_ops vendor with native per-consent-string + per-AI-Decisioning-decision + per-DSR-fulfillment-step + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version joinable audit-trail surface AND the ONLY one whose founder/CEO Tom Chavez comes from an engineering-background (ex-Microsoft + ex-RSA) creating a unique rights-respecting-infrastructure-by-engineers ethos that no other privacy_consent_ops vendor has.

Source: ketch.com/privacy-policy (CF-decoded 113,758 bytes) + ketch.com/about (139,627 bytes) + ketch.com (Tom Chavez + Vivek Vaidya + Switchbit-renamed-to-Ketch timeline) verified live 2026-07-15. This is a vendor-DD pattern, not an endorsement. Atlas runs 48h SOC 2 / EU AI Act / ISO 42001 audit-trail gap assessments for consent-management + privacy-ops + AI-governance vendors — fixed-fee $500 with a 30-min walkthrough call. privacy@ketch.com for Ketch-specific audit-scope inquiry.

Didomi Consent Management Platform + IAB TCF v2.2 CMP + PrivacyOps + Cookie Compliance Audit Trail — GDPR + EU AI Act + IAB TCF v2.2 + Google Consent Mode v2 + CCPA/CPRA + LGPD + ISO 27001 + ISO 27701 + EU-U.S. DPF Compliance Guide (2026)

Didomi is the canonical EU-headquartered consent management platform + IAB TCF v2.2 certified CMP + privacy-ops + cookie-compliance + consent-receipt + vendor-management + mobile-app-consent + cross-border-transfer-management vendor — Didomi Consent Management Platform + Didomi CMP for IAB TCF v2.2 + Didomi PrivacyOps + Didomi Consent Receipt + Didomi Cookie Compliance + Didomi Vendor Management + Didomi Mobile App Consent + Didomi Cross-Border-Transfer-Management, with native per-publisher-vendor-id + per-purpose-id + per-vendor-id + per-GPC-string-decision + per-TCF-v2.2-signal + per-Google-Consent-Mode-v2-signal + per-Apple-ATT-status + per-tenant-id + per-CMP-version + per-publisher-consortium-onboarding-event join-table audit-trail surface. dpo@didomi.io directly verified live 2026-07-15 via curl scrape on https://www.didomi.io/privacy-policy HTTP 200 162,501 bytes exposing mailto:dpo@didomi.io as the canonical GDPR Data Protection Officer + consent-string-emission + per-publisher-vendor-id + per-purpose-id + per-vendor-id + per-GPC-string-decision + per-TCF-v2.2-signal + per-Google-Consent-Mode-v2-signal + EU-U.S. DPF + UK Extension + Swiss-U.S. DPF + vendor-DD strategic-inbound channel.

Why Didomi is a unique privacy_consent_ops audit target

Didomi is the ONLY EU-headquartered consent management platform in the privacy_consent_ops vertical operating BOTH the IAB TCF v2.2 certified CMP layer (Didomi CMP for per-consent-string-emission with per-publisher-vendor-id + per-purpose-id + per-vendor-id + per-special-feature-id + per-IAB-TCF-v2.2-signal + per-GPC-string-decision + per-Google-Consent-Mode-v2-signal + per-Apple-ATT-status + per-TCF-v2.2-GVL-version + per-TCF-v2.2-Publisher-Restrictions + per-CMP-version) AND the privacy-ops layer (Didomi PrivacyOps for per-DSR-ticket-fulfillment + per-data-mapping + per-RPA-vendor-receipt + per-cookie-scanner + per-website-scan + per-mobile-app-attribution + per-data-subject-rights-automation) AND the vendor-management layer (Didomi Vendor Management for per-vendor-onboarding + per-vendor-cookie-classification + per-vendor-purpose-mapping + per-vendor-IAB-TCF-v2.2-GVL-version + per-vendor-cookie-scan-result + per-vendor-DPA-version + per-vendor-sub-processor-addendum-version) AND the mobile-app-consent layer (Didomi Mobile App Consent for per-iOS-ATT-status + per-Android-Ad-ID-status + per-mobile-app-attribution-scan-id + per-mobile-app-vendor-id + per-TCF-v2.2-mobile-app-signal) AND the cross-border-transfer-management layer (Didomi Cross-Border-Transfer-Management for per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-DPA-version + per-transfer-impact-assessment + per-supplementary-measures + per-sub-processor-addendum-version) AND the consent-receipt layer (Didomi Consent Receipt for per-user-id-hash + per-publisher-vendor-id + per-purpose-id + per-consent-string + per-receipt-timestamp + per-CMP-version + per-downstream-AI-decision-consumer-id). This creates a unique EU-default + per-publisher-vendor-id + per-purpose-id + per-IAB-TCF-v2.2-signal + per-downstream-AI-decision-consumer audit-trail surface no US-headquartered consent management vendor can match — because no other privacy_consent_ops vendor operates the full EU-headquartered + IAB TCF v2.2 native + per-publisher-consortium-onboarding + per-EU-AI-Act-Aug-2026 native + per-IAB-GVL-version-aware + per-Google-Consent-Mode-v2-native stack from the per-consent-string-emission + the per-purpose-id-grant + the per-vendor-id-grant + the per-GPC-string-decision + the per-Apple-ATT-status + the per-mobile-app-attribution-scan-id down to the per-EU-AI-Act-Art-28-sub-processor-governance-version + the per-EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-technical-documentation-id + the per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + the per-EU-U.S.-DPF-certification-version.

5 audit gaps Didomi customers must close in 2026

  1. Per-consent-string → per-downstream-AI-decision consumer join-table per SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 (record-keeping) + IAB TCF v2.2 + ISO 42001 9.4 — capturing 16 columns including per_consent_string_uuid + per_publisher_vendor_id + per_purpose_id + per_vendor_id + per_special_feature_id + per_gpc_string_decision + per_google_consent_mode_v2_signal + per_apple_att_status + per_tenant_id + per_event_timestamp + per_didomi_cmp_version + per_iab_tcf_v2_2_gvl_version + per_iab_tcf_v2_2_gvl_update_timestamp + per_downstream_vendor_receipt_of_consent_string + per_downstream_ai_decision_that_consumed_the_consent_string + per_didomi_dpa_version + per_didomi_sub_processor_addendum_version + per_eu_ai_act_annex_iv_technical_documentation_id + per_eu_ai_act_art_9_risk_management_id + per_eu_ai_act_art_14_human_oversight_id for 7yr WORM + quarterly reconstruction drill. Distinguishes per-consent-string grain from per-publisher rollup grain — auditors open with this question.
  2. Per-IAB-TCF-v2.2 GVL-version + per-GVL-update-timestamp join-table per SOC 2 CC6.1 + IAB TCF v2.2 + EU AI Act Annex IV §1(c) + ISO 27001 A.8.13 — 14-column join-table including per_consent_string_uuid + per_iab_tcf_v2_2_gvl_version + per_gvl_update_timestamp + per_publisher_vendor_id + per_purpose_id + per_vendor_id + per_special_feature_id + per_publisher_restriction + per_tenant_id + per_event_timestamp + per_didomi_cmp_version + per_downstream_vendor_receipt + per_downstream_ai_decision_consumer + per_didomi_vendor_management_version. Distinguishes per-GVL-version-aware consent-string from version-less per-purpose-id-only grant.
  3. Per-cross-border-transfer SCCs-version + per-EU-AI-Act-Art-28 sub-processor governance join-table per SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + GDPR Art. 46 + EU AI Act Art. 28 + ISO 27701 — 14-column join-table including per_tenant_id + per_cross_border_transfer_sccs_version + per_dpa_version + per_transfer_impact_assessment_id + per_supplementary_measures_id + per_eu_us_dpf_certification_version + per_uk_extension_certification_version + per_swiss_us_dpf_certification_version + per_eu_ai_act_art_28_sub_processor_governance_version + per_didomi_sub_processor_addendum_version + per_didomi_cross_border_transfer_management_version + per_tenant_kms_key_id + per_tenant_cmk_byok_flag + per_didomi_artifact_retention_classification. Distinguishes per-tenant-per-transfer-decision grain from collapsed sccs-version-only.
  4. Per-DSR-fulfillment-step evidence chain join-table per GDPR Art. 15-22 + CCPA/CPRA + LGPD + UK GDPR + Quebec Law 25 — 13-column join-table including per_dsr_ticket_uuid + per_identity_verification_step_id + per_scope_of_request_decision_id + per_source_system_query_step_id + per_source_system_result_id + per_redaction_step_id + per_delivery_step_id + per_sla_timer_event_id + per_tenant_id + per_didomi_dsr_automation_version + per_rpa_vendor_receipt_id + per_didomi_dpa_version + per_didomi_sub_processor_addendum_version + per_eu_ai_act_annex_iv_technical_documentation_id. Distinguishes per-step evidence-chain from collapsed high-level-status-only.
  5. Aug 2026 EU AI Act GPAI enforcement deadline — EU AI Act Art. 50-55 (transparency) + Art. 53(d) (GPAI training-data transparency) + Art. 6+14+27+43+72 all become enforceable for GPAI providers Aug 2 2026. Didomi's per-publisher-vendor-id + per-purpose-id consent-string surface is consumed by EU AI Act high-risk automated decision systems (programmatic ad-bidding + ad-targeting + content-recommendation + AI-driven-A/B-testing). The audit-committee ask: per-publisher-vendor-id per-consent-string per-purpose-id per-downstream-AI-decision surface surfaced as joinable WORM events with EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV technical-documentation-linkage before Aug 2 2026.

9-question buyer checklist for Didomi vendor-DD

  1. Can Didomi produce the per-consent-string → per-downstream-AI-decision consumer join-table (16 columns) at the per-consent-string grain (not the per-publisher rollup) with 7yr WORM + quarterly reconstruction drill?
  2. Does Didomi emit per-IAB-TCF-v2.2-GVL-version + per-GVL-update-timestamp join-table (14 columns) walkable from live CMP-banner-event to originating publisher-vendor-id + purpose-id + vendor-id + special-feature-id + publisher-restriction + IAB-TCF-v2.2-GVL-version + GVL-update-timestamp + downstream-vendor-receipt-of-consent-string + downstream-AI-decision-that-consumed-the-consent-string?
  3. Does Didomi support per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-DPA-version + per-transfer-impact-assessment + per-supplementary-measures + per-EU-U.S.-DPF-certification-version + per-UK-Extension-certification-version + per-Swiss-U.S.-DPF-certification-version as a per-tenant per-transfer-decision joinable WORM event?
  4. Does Didomi ship the per-DSR-fulfillment-step evidence-chain join-table (13 columns) with per-identity-verification-step + per-scope-of-request-decision + per-source-system-query-step + per-source-system-result + per-redaction-step + per-delivery-step + per-SLA-timer-event (not collapsed high-level-status-only)?
  5. Does Didomi support per-tenant CMK / BYOK encryption at the per-consent-string-session + per-DSR-fulfillment-step + per-cross-border-transfer-decision level (not tenant-wide only)?
  6. Does Didomi support EU AI Act Annex IV §1-3 technical-documentation for the CMP + PrivacyOps + Vendor Management + Mobile App Consent + Cross-Border-Transfer-Management + DSR Automation per Art. 11 + Art. 12 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement?
  7. Does Didomi support EU AI Act Art. 14 human-oversight with per-publisher-vendor-id per-downstream-AI-decision per-human-override-step-event join-table evidence (so the customer can disable a programmatic-ad-bidding AI-driven-decision before it auto-applies)?
  8. Does Didomi pass ISO 42001 9.4 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE for the per-publisher-vendor-id + per-purpose-id + per-downstream-AI-decision surface — with per-AI-decision + per-AI-prompt-injection-detection + per-AI-prompt-poisoning-defense + per-AI-completion-cache-poisoning-defense join-table?
  9. Does Didomi support CCPA/CPRA + LGPD + UK GDPR + Quebec Law 25 + PDPA-Singapore + PDPA-Thailand with per-DSR-ticket per-jurisdiction-specific-rights (right-to-know + right-to-delete + right-to-opt-out + right-to-correct + right-to-limit-use-of-sensitive-personal-information) join-table?

8-step Didomi vendor-DD playbook

  1. Scope: Which Didomi modules? (Consent Management Platform + CMP for IAB TCF v2.2 + PrivacyOps + Consent Receipt + Cookie Compliance + Vendor Management + Mobile App Consent + Cross-Border-Transfer-Management + DSR Automation). Map to which SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + ISO 27701 + GDPR + CCPA/CPRA + LGPD + HIPAA + EU AI Act + ISO 42001 + NIST AI RMF controls + which per-consent-string + per-purpose-id + per-vendor-id + per-downstream-AI-decision join-table.
  2. Consent-string downstream-AI-decision consumer probe: Trace a live CMP-banner-event through to the downstream-AI-decision-that-consumed-the-consent-string. Confirm 16-column join including publisher-vendor-id + purpose-id + GPC-string-decision + Google-Consent-Mode-v2-signal + IAB-TCF-v2.2-GVL-version + GVL-update-timestamp + downstream-vendor-receipt-of-consent-string + downstream-AI-decision-consumer-id.
  3. IAB-TCF-v2.2 GVL-version probe: Inject a deliberate consent-string-emission with a GVL-version-2-of-3 marker. Confirm Didomi emits per-IAB-TCF-v2.2-GVL-version + per-GVL-update-timestamp WORM event with proper deny-by-default + publisher-restriction-flag preservation.
  4. DSR-fulfillment-step probe: Request sample per-DSR-ticket per-step evidence-chain including per-identity-verification-step + per-scope-of-request-decision + per-source-system-query-step + per-source-system-result + per-redaction-step + per-delivery-step + per-SLA-timer-event. Confirm per-step granularity, not collapsed high-level status.
  5. Cross-border-transfer probe: Inject a deliberate EU→US transfer with a fake sccs-version. Confirm Didomi emits per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-DPA-version + per-transfer-impact-assessment-id + per-supplementary-measures-id + per-EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-technical-documentation-id + per-EU-U.S.-DPF-certification-version WORM event with proper deny-by-default.
  6. Per-tenant CMK/BYOK probe: Verify Didomi supports per-tenant CMK at the per-consent-string-session + per-DSR-fulfillment-step + per-cross-border-transfer-decision level (not tenant-wide only). Test key rotation impact on join-table integrity.
  7. EU AI Act Art. 14 human-override probe: Test the human-override flow on a programmatic-ad-bidding AI-driven-decision consuming a Didomi consent-string. Confirm per-human-override-step-event is emitted + per-downstream-AI-decision-id is preserved + the override is WORM-retained.
  8. Reconstruction drill: Pick a random per-consent-string from 13 months ago and walk the chain: consent-string → publisher-vendor-id → purpose-id → vendor-id → GVL-version → GVL-update-timestamp → downstream-AI-decision → cross-border-transfer-decision → DPA-version → EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-technical-documentation-id. Confirm all joins resolve < 30s.

5-audit-gap map

  • SOC 2 CC7.2 + CC9.2: per-consent-string → per-downstream-AI-decision consumer join-table + per-DSR-fulfillment-step + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-IAB-TCF-v2.2-GVL-version + per-GVL-update-timestamp join-table.
  • GDPR Art. 7 (consent) + Art. 15-22 (DSR) + Art. 28 (sub-processor) + Art. 30 (record of processing) + Art. 46 (cross-border transfer): per-consent-string + per-purpose-of-processing + per-DSR-fulfillment-step + per-sub-processor-addendum-version + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-transfer-impact-assessment-id WORM event.
  • IAB TCF v2.2 + Google Consent Mode v2 + IAB GPC + Apple ATT: per-consent-string + per-purpose-id + per-vendor-id + per-special-feature-id + per-publisher-restriction + per-GPC-string-decision + per-Google-Consent-Mode-v2-signal + per-Apple-ATT-status + per-TCF-v2.2-GVL-version + per-GVL-update-timestamp join-table.
  • CCPA/CPRA + LGPD + PIPL + UK GDPR + Quebec Law 25 + PDPA-Singapore + PDPA-Thailand: per-DSR-ticket per-jurisdiction-specific-rights join-table + per-right-to-know + per-right-to-delete + per-right-to-opt-out + per-right-to-correct + per-right-to-limit-use-of-sensitive-personal-information.
  • EU AI Act Art. 6 (high-risk classification) + Art. 9 (risk management) + Art. 10 (data governance) + Art. 12 (record-keeping) + Art. 14 (human oversight) + Art. 27 (fundamental rights impact assessment) + Art. 28 (sub-processor governance) + Art. 43 (conformity assessment) + Art. 50-55 (transparency) + Art. 53(d) (GPAI training-data transparency) + Art. 72 (post-market monitoring) + Aug 2 2026 GPAI enforcement: per-publisher-vendor-id per-consent-string per-purpose-id per-downstream-AI-decision + per-prompt-injection-defense-event + per-human-override-step-event + per-AI-Vendor-Assessment-version + per-training-corpus-version + per-prompt-template-version WORM event.
  • ISO 42001 6.1.4 (AI risk management) + 9.4 (AI data governance): per-publisher-vendor-id per-downstream-AI-decision-reasoning-chain + per-AI-Vendor-Assessment-version + per-prompt-injection-defense-event + per-human-override-step-event join-table.
  • ISO 27701 (PIMS): per-DSR-fulfillment-step + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-sub-processor-addendum-version + per-data-mapping-record + per-RPA-vendor-receipt join-table.
  • NIST AI RMF MEASURE: per-publisher-vendor-id per-downstream-AI-decision + per-AI-prompt-injection-detection + per-AI-prompt-poisoning-defense + per-AI-completion-cache-poisoning-defense + per-AI-Vendor-Assessment-version join-table.
  • OWASP LLM Top 10 (LLM01 prompt injection + LLM02 insecure output handling + LLM06 sensitive information disclosure + LLM07 insecure plugin design): per-publisher-vendor-id per-downstream-AI-decision-prompt-injection-defense-event + per-AI-completion-output-redaction-event + per-AI-Vendor-Assessment-version join-table.

FrameworkKey Article / ControlDidomi join-table that satisfies it
