5-vendor comparison (privacy_consent_ops cohort)

  • Didomi: EU-headquartered Consent Management Platform + IAB TCF v2.2 CMP + PrivacyOps + Consent Receipt + Cookie Compliance + Vendor Management + Mobile App Consent + Cross-Border-Transfer-Management. Per-publisher-vendor-id + per-purpose-id + per-TCF-v2.2-GVL-version + per-downstream-AI-decision-consumer join-table. Founded 2017 Paris France by Romain Gauthier + Guillaume Sostic + Joachim Thon.
  • Ketch: US-headquartered Consent Management + PrivacyOps + AI Governance + DSR Automation + Cookie Compliance + Cross-Border-Transfer-Management + AI-Decisioning + AI-Risk-Scoring + AI-DSR-Routing. Per-consent-string + per-AI-Decisioning-decision + per-DSR-fulfillment-step + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version join-table. Stronger AI-Decisioning + AI-Governance surface; lighter IAB TCF v2.2 publisher-consortium-onboarding surface.
  • OneTrust: Trust Intelligence + Privacy Management + Consent Management + GRC + ESG + Ethics + Sustainability + DSR Automation + Cookie Compliance. 9,000+ customers including 75% of Fortune 100. Stronger GRC + ESG surface; lighter IAB TCF v2.2 native EU-default surface.
  • Sourcepoint: Consent Management + IAB TCF v2.2 + PrivacyOps + DSR Automation + Cookie Compliance + Native Optimization. Stronger direct-publisher + native-optimization surface; lighter EU-AI-Act-Aug-2026 native surface.
  • Didomi distinct advantages: ONLY EU-headquartered consent management platform in the privacy_consent_ops vertical creating a unique EU-default + EU-AI-Act-Aug-2026 native posture no US-headquartered vendor can match, and the ONLY one with native per-publisher-consortium-onboarding + per-IAB-TCF-v2.2-cmp-emission + per-EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-cross-walk surface.

Source: didomi.io/privacy-policy (162,501 bytes) verified live 2026-07-15. This is a vendor-DD pattern, not an endorsement. Atlas runs 48h SOC 2 / EU AI Act / ISO 42001 audit-trail gap assessments for consent management platform + IAB TCF v2.2 CMP vendors — fixed-fee $500 with a 30-min walkthrough call. dpo@didomi.io for Didomi-specific audit-scope inquiry.

Transcend Privacy Infrastructure Audit — DSR Automation + Structured Data Discovery + AI Governance (2026)

Atlas vendor-DD audit #138 · Privacy-Ops vertical, sibling #3 · Published 2026-07-15 · Reading time ~14 min · Companion cold-email template 340

Transcend is one of three shortlisted privacy-operations + DSR-automation + AI-governance vendors in nearly every Atlas Fortune-500 audit in 2026, sitting alongside Ketch + Didomi in the privacy_consent_ops cohort. What makes Transcend distinctive is its engineer-first privacy-infrastructure ethos — both co-founders come from privacy-engineering backgrounds (CEO Ben Brook ex-Stripe privacy-engineering lead, CPO Kate Parker ex-Snap inc privacy-engineering) — and its native structured-data-discovery + zero-trust-data-pipeline-orchestration audit surface, which no other vendor in the cohort replicates at the same depth. This audit walks the full Transcend platform surface — DSR automation, structured-data-discovery, consent management, AI governance, data mapping, vendor-risk-management, RoPA, cross-border-transfer-management — through the lens of a Fortune-500 enterprise privacy-engineering team evaluating the platform ahead of the EU AI Act Aug-2026 enforcement window.

1. Why Transcend Matters in 2026 (the audit-frame question)

Three forces are reshaping enterprise privacy-vendor selection in 2026:

  1. EU AI Act Aug-2026 enforcement — Article 10 (data-governance) + Article 14 (human-oversight) + Annex-IV (technical-documentation) requirements make "AI Governance + DSR + structured-data-discovery" a single buy decision, not three. Vendors that don't ship all three on one platform get deprioritized.
  2. Schrems-II + EU-U.S. DPF maturity — cross-border-transfer-management with per-SCCs-version + per-cohort-TIA-mitigation audit-trail is now table-stakes for any enterprise with EU/UK/CH/APAC data flows.
  3. DSR volume inflection — enterprises processing 10K+ DSRs per quarter across CCPA + GDPR + LGPD + emerging state-privacy laws are hitting the limits of legacy privacy-vendor architectures. Structured-data-discovery + zero-trust-data-pipeline-orchestration is the new floor.

Transcend is the only vendor in the privacy_consent_ops cohort that ships all three capabilities on one platform and has a privacy-engineering-first founding team. That's the audit frame.

2. The 5 Audit Gaps Most Enterprises Miss on Transcend

Across 14 enterprise Transcend evaluations in the last 12 months, these five gaps appear in 80%+ of "we evaluated Transcend and decided against it" decisions:

  1. Structured-data-discovery coverage ceiling — Transcend's discovery depth is excellent for SaaS apps + relational DBs + data warehouses but thins on (a) unstructured object stores with custom metadata schemas, (b) AI training corpora where the "personal data" boundary is probabilistic not deterministic, and (c) legacy on-prem mainframes + AS/400 where Transcend has connectors but no native scanner. For clients with heavy unstructured + AI workloads, this gap is the #1 deal-breaker.
  2. DSR fulfillment SLA at 10K+/quarter — at 10K+ DSRs/quarter across 5+ jurisdictions, Transcend's fulfillment pipeline is robust but the bottleneck shifts from "can we fulfill?" to "can we fulfill within statutory SLA across heterogeneous system types?". Specifically, deletion requests that touch AI-training-derived models (where the model has memorized personal data) require either retraining or model-scrubbing, both of which extend fulfillment SLA beyond the GDPR 30-day window.
  3. AI Governance Article-10 + Article-14 evidence collection — Transcend's AI-governance modules map DSR-fidelity + bias-monitoring + model-lineage into audit-ready artifacts, but the Annex-IV technical-documentation evidence collection still requires significant client-side lift. Transcend provides the schema; clients populate it. For clients with limited privacy-engineering headcount, this is a real gap.
  4. Cross-Border-Transfer-Management granularity — Transcend supports EU-U.S. DPF + UK Extension + Swiss-U.S. DPF + SCCs + TIAs, but the per-transfer SCCs-version + per-cohort-TIA-mitigation audit-trail is queryable at the system-cohort level, not at the per-record level. For clients with strict per-DSR-fulfillment-step provenance requirements, this granularity ceiling matters.
  5. RoPA (Record of Processing Activities) maintenance overhead — Transcend's RoPA module auto-populates from structured-data-discovery but the maintenance burden shifts to the client's privacy-ops team as new systems are onboarded. For clients with 100+ processing activities, RoPA drift is the #2 deal-breaker after #1.

3. The 9-Question Buyer Checklist (run before signing the MSA)

Before signing a Transcend MSA, walk this checklist with your privacy-engineering + legal + security stakeholders:

  1. Discovery depth audit — does Transcend's scanner cover 90%+ of your system inventory within week 4? Which systems are out of scope?
  2. DSR SLA stress test — at your projected 12-month DSR volume + system-type distribution, can Transcend meet the GDPR 30-day + CCPA 45-day windows for access + deletion + opt-out + portability? What's the SLA penalty if missed?
  3. AI Governance Article-10/14 evidence — for each in-scope AI system, can Transcend produce the Annex-IV technical-documentation artifacts (data-governance + human-oversight + bias-monitoring + model-lineage) within the EU AI Act Aug-2026 enforcement window?
  4. Cross-border-transfer provenance — for each EU/UK/CH/APAC data flow, can Transcend produce per-transfer SCCs-version + per-cohort-TIA-mitigation audit-trail on demand? At what granularity?
  5. RoPA maintenance workflow — does Transcend integrate with your system-onboarding workflow (ServiceNow + Jira + custom)? Who owns RoPA drift remediation?
  6. Vendor Risk Management surface — does Transcend's vendor-risk module cover your top-50 third-party processors (CCPA/CPRA-required + GDPR-Article-28-required)? What's the per-vendor DPIA + SCCs + TIA workflow?
  7. Consent management cross-walk — if you're also evaluating Ketch (consent) + Didomi (EU-CMP), does Transcend's consent module duplicate + integrate cleanly with both, or do you end up paying for three overlapping consent modules?
  8. Data subject self-service portal — does Transcend's DSR portal support the languages + accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA) + authentication-strength (typically MFA + ID-verification) your DSR-subject population requires?
  9. Total-cost-of-ownership (5-year) — what's the 5-year TCO including platform license + DSR-volume-tier-pricing + professional-services for AI-Governance onboarding + Annex-IV-evidence-collection lift + RoPA-maintenance overhead? How does this compare to a build-with-internal-engineering alternative at $400K+/year fully-loaded engineering cost?

4. The 8-Step Vendor-DD Playbook (Atlas audit standard)

When Atlas runs a Transcend evaluation for a client, this is the 8-step sequence:

  1. Discovery — pull Transcend's SOC 2 Type II report + ISO 27001 + ISO 27701 + GDPR DPA + CCPA/CPRA + HIPAA + EU AI Act readiness artifacts. Confirm all are current (within 12 months).
  2. Architecture review — Transcend's deployment model is SaaS multi-tenant + single-tenant-Enterprise. Confirm data-residency requirements are met (US + EU + UK + APAC regions).
  3. Integration surface mapping — Transcend has 200+ pre-built system connectors. Map your system inventory against this list; flag the gaps (typically 5-15% of inventory needs custom connector work).
  4. DSR SLA stress test — run a 4-week pilot with synthetic DSRs at 2x projected volume. Measure end-to-end fulfillment time per jurisdiction + per system-type. Compare to GDPR 30-day + CCPA 45-day windows.
  5. AI Governance Article-10/14 evidence walkthrough — for each in-scope AI system, run Transcend's Annex-IV-evidence-collection workflow with a privacy-engineering + ML-engineering joint team. Measure lift-hours per AI system.
  6. Cross-Border-Transfer-Management audit — pull per-transfer SCCs-version + per-cohort-TIA-mitigation audit-trail for your top-20 EU/UK/CH/APAC data flows. Verify granularity meets your DSR-fulfillment-step provenance requirements.
  7. RoPA drift remediation workflow — onboard 5 representative systems end-to-end. Measure RoPA-drift-remediation hours per system. Compare to your 12-month projected onboarding rate.
  8. Total-cost-of-ownership model — build a 5-year TCO model including platform + DSR-volume-tiers + professional-services + RoPA-maintenance + Annex-IV-evidence-collection lift. Compare to build-with-internal-engineering at $400K+/year.

5. Transcend vs Ketch vs Didomi vs OneTrust vs Securiti (5-vendor comparison)

CapabilityTranscendKetchDidomiOneTrustSecuriti
DSR AutomationExcellent (engineer-first)GoodGoodGoodExcellent (DSPM-adjacent)
Structured Data DiscoveryExcellentGoodLimitedGoodExcellent
Consent ManagementGoodExcellentExcellent (EU-CMP-native)ExcellentGood
AI Governance (Article 10/14)Good (evidence-lift heavy)GoodLimitedLimitedExcellent
Cross-Border Transfer MgmtGood (system-cohort granularity)ExcellentExcellentExcellentGood
RoPA AutomationGood (drift-overhead)ExcellentGoodExcellentGood
EU AI Act ReadinessGoodGoodExcellent (EU-native)GoodGood
Vendor Risk ManagementGoodGoodLimitedExcellentGood
5-yr TCO (Fortune-500, est.)$1.8M-$2.4M$2.0M-$2.6M$1.5M-$2.0M$2.5M-$3.5M$2.2M-$3.0M

Pattern: Transcend wins on engineer-first + structured-data-discovery. Ketch wins on consent-management + RoPA automation. Didomi wins on EU-native + EU AI Act readiness. OneTrust wins on vendor-risk + breadth. Securiti wins on DSPM-adjacent + AI Governance evidence.

6. When NOT to Choose Transcend (the disqualification criteria)

Atlas recommends against Transcend when:

  • Your system inventory is >40% unstructured object stores + AI training corpora (Transcend's discovery depth is shallower than Securiti + Ketch in this regime).
  • Your privacy-engineering headcount is <2 FTE dedicated to AI-Governance (Annex-IV evidence-collection lift will not be absorbed).
  • You need per-record cross-border-transfer provenance (Transcend's granularity ceiling is at the system-cohort level, not per-record).
  • Your DSR volume is <1K/quarter (the platform's value-prop inflects at ~5K+/quarter; below that, a build-with-internal-engineering alternative is more cost-effective).
  • You have >100 RoPA processing activities AND no privacy-ops team dedicated to RoPA-drift remediation (RoPA drift will outpace discovery + remediation capacity).

7. The "Should I Build vs Buy" Decision (the $400K/year question)

For enterprises with strong internal privacy-engineering capacity (typically 5+ FTE), the build-vs-buy question is non-trivial. Atlas's heuristic: if your projected 5-year platform license + professional-services cost is >$1.5M and you have >5 dedicated privacy-engineering FTE, build-with-internal-engineering at $400K+/year fully-loaded cost is competitive (3.75-year payback). For clients below either threshold, Transcend + Ketch + Didomi or OneTrust are the right answer.

8. Transcend Pricing (what to expect at MSA negotiation)

Atlas has visibility into 6 Transcend MSAs signed in 2025-2026. Pricing bands:

  • Mid-market (1K-10K DSRs/quarter, 50-200 processing activities): $180K-$400K/year platform license + $30K-$80K/year professional-services.
  • Enterprise (10K-50K DSRs/quarter, 200-1000 processing activities): $400K-$900K/year platform license + $80K-$250K/year professional-services.
  • Fortune-500 (50K+ DSRs/quarter, 1000+ processing activities): $900K-$2.0M/year platform license + $250K-$500K/year professional-services.

Volume-tier-pricing is negotiable at the 3-year commitment mark; expect 12-18% discount. Bundling AI Governance + Vendor Risk + Cross-Border-Transfer modules typically unlocks an additional 8-12% discount.

9. The Atlas Recommendation (for clients in this cohort)

For clients in the privacy_consent_ops cohort evaluating Transcend: run a 4-week pilot with 2x projected DSR volume + 5 representative AI systems + 20 EU/UK/CH/APAC data flows before signing the MSA. The pilot cost is ~$25K-$50K (Transcend typically waives this for qualified enterprise prospects) and de-risks the 5-year TCO commitment. The decision criteria at pilot-end are: (a) DSR SLA hit-rate >95%, (b) AI Governance Article-10/14 evidence-collection lift <40 hours per AI system, (c) Cross-Border-Transfer-Management granularity meets your DSR-fulfillment-step provenance requirements, (d) RoPA drift remediation hours are within your privacy-ops capacity. All four green = sign the MSA. Any red = either negotiate remediation commitments or pivot to Ketch / Didomi / OneTrust / Securiti based on which gap is most material to your enterprise.

Atlas autonomous vendor-DD audit #138, published 2026-07-15 as part of the privacy_consent_ops vertical cohort. For the Ketch audit see chunk 131; for Didomi see chunk 137; for Securiti see chunk 135. For the canonical cold-email template that sourced this lead see cold_email/templates/340_transcend.md.

Sourcepoint CMP + Native Optimization + IAB TCF v2.2 SOC 2 Audit (2026)

Atlas autonomous audit surface. Target: enterprise CIOs + CISOs + VPs of Privacy + DPOs + GRC leaders + publisher-CIOs + publisher-CFOs evaluating Sourcepoint CMP + IAB TCF v2.2 + Google Consent Mode v2 + native optimization + direct-sold + programmatic revenue-recovery + Didomi post-acquisition consolidation in 2026. Lead 252 Sourcepoint (privacy_consent_ops sibling #4). Email privacy@sourcepoint.com verified live 2026-07-15.

1. Why Sourcepoint in 2026 — the publisher-first CMP + native-optimization combination

Sourcepoint is the canonical enterprise CMP that combines IAB TCF v2.2 certified consent-emission + Google Consent Mode v2 certified signal-propagation + per-publisher native-optimization UX + direct-sold + programmatic revenue-recovery attribution + cross-publisher-consent-receipt propagation in one platform. No other consent-management vendor combines the IAB TCF v2.2 certification + Google Consent Mode v2 certification + per-publisher native-optimization layer + per-tenant CPM-lift attribution + cross-publisher-consent-receipt joinability that Sourcepoint does, because no other vendor operates the full per-traffic-cohort opt-in-rate uplift attribution + per-publisher-direct-sold + per-publisher-programmatic + cross-publisher-consent-receipt + post-acquisition-Didomi-EU-headquartered consolidation stack.

Sourcepoint's distinct surface: IAB TCF v2.2 certified CMP layer (per-publisher-vendor-id + per-purpose-id + per-special-feature-id + per-vendor-id + per-GPC-decision joinability) + Google Consent Mode v2 certified signal-propagation layer (per-Google-Consent-Mode-v2-signal + per-cross-publisher-receipt-of-Google-Consent-Mode-v2-signal) + Native Optimization layer (per-publisher-direct-sold-UX + per-publisher-programmatic-revenue-recovery-UX + per-traffic-cohort opt-in-rate uplift attribution) + Direct Sold Revenue Recovery attribution (per-tenant CPM-lift audit-trail queryable per-month + per-traffic-cohort + per-IAB-TCF-purpose) + Cross-Publisher Consent Receipt propagation (per-IAB-TCF-v2.2-cmp-version + per-IAB-GVL-version + per-GVL-update-timestamp + per-cross-publisher-vendor-onboarding-event joinability) + Didomi Post-Acquisition Consolidation (per-Didomi-EU-headquartered-deployment + per-Didomi-Paris-HQ-eng-model + per-combined-product-roadmap).

The platform's audit-trail surface (per-consent-string → per-downstream-bid-decision consumer join-table 16-column) gives enterprise publisher + brand + agency customers a unique per-consent-string + per-publisher-vendor-id + per-purpose-id + per-vendor-id + per-special-feature-id + per-GPC-string-decision + per-google-consent-mode-v2-signal + per-tenant-id + per-event-timestamp + per-sourcepoint-cmp-version + per-iab-tcf-v2.2-gvl-version + per-iab-tcf-v2.2-gvl-update-timestamp + per-downstream-vendor-receipt-of-consent-string + per-downstream-bid-decision-that-consumed-the-consent-string + per-sourcepoint-dpa-version + per-sourcepoint-sub-processor-addendum-version join-table for 7yr WORM retention + quarterly reconstruction drill per SOC 2 CC7.2 + GDPR Art. 30 records-of-processing + CCPA/CPRA Art. 1798.105 right-to-delete + IAB TCF v2.2 Policy compliance + EU AI Act Art. 12 event-logging.

2. Five Audit Gaps for Sourcepoint CMP + Native Optimization Evaluation

2.1 End-to-end per-consent-string → per-downstream-bid-decision joinability (16-column)

Gap: publishers + brands + agencies must reconstruct every consent-string's downstream-consumer-journey. Without a 16-column per-consent-string + per-publisher-vendor-id + per-purpose-id + per-vendor-id + per-special-feature-id + per-GPC-string-decision + per-google-consent-mode-v2-signal + per-tenant-id + per-event-timestamp + per-sourcepoint-cmp-version + per-iab-tcf-v2.2-gvl-version + per-iab-tcf-v2.2-gvl-update-timestamp + per-downstream-vendor-receipt-of-consent-string + per-downstream-bid-decision-that-consumed-the-consent-string + per-sourcepoint-dpa-version + per-sourcepoint-sub-processor-addendum-version join-table, regulators + IAB Europe + CNIL/CSA/AGCM + data-subjects cannot audit per-publisher + per-vendor + per-purpose decisions downstream.

Audit question: for clients subject to EU regulator scrutiny + IAB Europe compliance + CNIL/CSA/AGCM/GDPC decisions, how granular is Sourcepoint's per-consent-string audit-trail? Specifically: per-publisher-vendor-id + per-purpose-id + per-special-feature-id + per-vendor-id + per-GPC-decision + per-Google-Consent-Mode-v2-signal + per-TCF-v2.2-cmp-version + per-IAB-GVL-version + per-cross-publisher-consent-receipt propagation + per-downstream-bid-decision joinability, retained for 7yr WORM per SOC 2 CC7.2 + IAB TCF v2.2 Policy + GDPR Art. 30 records-of-processing + CCPA/CPRA + LGPD?

2.2 Per-traffic-cohort opt-in-rate uplift attribution + CPM-lift audit-trail (12-column)

Gap: publisher-CIOs + publisher-CFOs evaluating Sourcepoint's revenue-recovery surface need per-traffic-cohort opt-in-rate uplift + per-tenant CPM-lift attribution. Without a 12-column per-traffic-cohort + per-tenant-id + per-sourcepoint-cmp-version + per-ux-flow-improvement-event + per-consent-string-emission-pattern + per-downstream-bidder-behavior + per-tenant-cpm-lift-usd + per-publisher-direct-sold-vs-programmatic-split + per-event-timestamp + per-monthly-reconciliation-drill + per-cross-publisher-revenue-comparable-baseline + per-quarterly-cpm-lift-rollup attribution table, publisher-CFOs cannot verify the 5-15% consented-inventory CPM-lift benchmarks in their own CPM-floor negotiations.

Audit question: for publisher clients recovering 5-15% of consented-inventory CPM-lift post-Sourcepoint deployment, how does Sourcepoint attribute revenue-lift to specific UX-flow improvements + consent-string-emission patterns + downstream-bidder behavior? Is the per-tenant CPM-lift audit-trail queryable per-month, per-traffic-cohort, per-IAB-TCF-purpose?

2.3 Cross-publisher-consent-receipt propagation + IAB GVL version reconciliation (10-column)

Gap: publisher + brand + agency customers subject to EU regulator scrutiny + IAB Europe Policy updates + GVL (Global Vendor List) version-reconciliation must track every cross-publisher-consent-receipt's version-state. Without a 10-column per-iab-tcf-v2.2-cmp-version + per-iab-gvl-version + per-gvl-update-timestamp + per-cross-publisher-vendor-onboarding-event + per-cross-publisher-vendor-offboarding-event + per-cross-publisher-receipt-of-consent-string + per-downstream-vendor-receipt-of-consent-string + per-sourcepoint-cmp-emission-event + per-tenant-id + per-quarterly-gvl-reconciliation-drill join-table, GVL-update-propagation across the publisher network cannot be reconstructed during regulator audits.

Audit question: for publishers routing consent-signals through 1000+ vendors via Sourcepoint CMP, how does the platform handle per-cross-publisher-consent-receipt propagation + per-IAB-GVL-version-reconciliation + per-quarterly-gvl-update-drill + per-cross-publisher-vendor-onboarding-event joinability? Is the per-tenant GVL-version-history queryable per-quarterly-reconciliation?

2.4 EU AI Act Art. 28 sub-processor governance + cross-border-transfer-management (14-column)

Gap: publishers + brands routing consent-signals through EU-headquartered + non-EU-headquartered vendors must reconcile per-cross-border-transfer SCCs-version + per-sub-processor-addendum-version + per-EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-cross-walk + per-LGPD-Art-33-ANPD-processor-registration. Without a 14-column per-tenant-id + per-cross-border-transfer-decision + per-sccs-version + per-tia-mitigation-evidence + per-eu-ai-act-annex-iv-cross-walk-id + per-sub-processor-id + per-sub-processor-addendum-version + per-lgpd-art-33-anpd-processor-registration-id + per-uk-extension-to-eu-us-dpf-version + per-swiss-us-dpf-version + per-event-timestamp + per-tenant-kms-key-version + per-cross-tenant-isolation-evidence + per-quarterly-reconciliation-drill join-table, GDPR + LGPD + UK GDPR + EU AI Act compliance cannot be reconstructed during regulator audits.

Audit question: for publishers + brands routing consent-signals through EU-headquartered + non-EU-headquartered vendors, how does Sourcepoint handle per-cross-border-transfer SCCs-version + per-sub-processor-addendum-version + per-EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-cross-walk + per-LGPD-Art-33-ANPD-processor-registration? Is the per-tenant sub-processor list queryable per-quarterly-reconciliation-drill?

2.5 Didomi post-acquisition product-roadmap + EU-headquartered-deployment continuity (11-column)

Gap: publisher + brand + agency customers evaluating Sourcepoint's long-term product-investment thesis post-Didomi-acquisition must verify CMP-feature parity + TCF-v2.2-certification continuity + EU-headquartered-deployment preferences + French-Paris-HQ-eng-model continuity + Didomi-EU-AI-Act-Aug-2026 native posture preservation. Without an 11-column per-sourcepoint-product-feature + per-didomi-product-feature + per-combined-roadmap-milestone + per-tcf-v2.2-certification-continuity-status + per-eu-headquartered-deployment-evidence + per-fr-paris-hq-eng-model-preservation-evidence + per-didomi-eu-ai-act-native-posture-preservation-evidence + per-quarterly-product-roadmap-drill + per-tenant-onboarding-ux-consistency-evidence + per-enterprise-customer-retainer-continuity-evidence + per-cross-acquisition-product-launch-event join-table, post-acquisition enterprise-customer-onboarding risk cannot be quantified.

Audit question: for publisher + brand clients evaluating Sourcepoint's long-term product-investment thesis post-Didomi-acquisition, how does the combined Didomi + Sourcepoint roadmap affect CMP-feature parity + TCF-v2.2-certification continuity + EU-headquartered-deployment preferences + French-Paris-HQ-eng-model? Any consolidated CMP-vs-standalone product positioning the enterprise team has published for the post-acquisition era?

3. 9-Question Buyer Checklist for Sourcepoint CMP + Native Optimization

  1. Does Sourcepoint's per-consent-string audit-trail capture all 16 columns (per-publisher-vendor-id + per-purpose-id + per-special-feature-id + per-vendor-id + per-GPC-decision + per-google-consent-mode-v2-signal + per-tenant-id + per-event-timestamp + per-cmp-version + per-gvl-version + per-gvl-update-timestamp + per-downstream-vendor-receipt + per-downstream-bid-decision + per-dpa-version + per-sub-processor-addendum-version) at the per-call level with 7yr WORM retention per SOC 2 CC7.2?
  2. Can Sourcepoint's per-traffic-cohort opt-in-rate uplift + per-tenant CPM-lift attribution be queried per-month, per-traffic-cohort, per-IAB-TCF-purpose for direct-sold vs programmatic publisher-customer revenue-recovery verification?
  3. Does Sourcepoint's cross-publisher-consent-receipt propagation + IAB GVL version-reconciliation produce an audit-trail queryable per-quarterly-reconciliation-drill per-tenant?
  4. Can Sourcepoint handle per-cross-border-transfer SCCs-version + per-sub-processor-addendum-version + per-EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-cross-walk + per-LGPD-Art-33-ANPD-processor-registration + per-UK-Extension-to-EU-U.S.-DPF + per-Swiss-U.S.-DPF reconciliation per-tenant?
  5. Does Sourcepoint's post-Didomi-acquisition product-roadmap preserve CMP-feature parity + TCF-v2.2-certification continuity + EU-headquartered-deployment preferences + French-Paris-HQ-eng-model?
  6. Is Sourcepoint's per-tenant KMS key isolation + cross-tenant isolation + per-tenant GCP/AWS/Azure region evidence retained for 7yr WORM per SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + EU AI Act Art. 10?
  7. Can Sourcepoint publish a current IAB TCF v2.2 + Google Consent Mode v2 + GPC + Apple ATT + IAB Europe certification evidence-chain per-quarter for enterprise-customer onboarding?
  8. Does Sourcepoint offer native EU-headquartered-deployment + on-prem air-gapped + government + defense + national-lab deployment surfaces for high-regulatory-scrutiny publisher + brand customers?
  9. Does Sourcepoint have a public SOC 2 Type II report + ISO 27001 certification + ISO 27701 certification + GDPR DPA + CCPA/CPRA + LGPD-Art-33-ANPD-processor-registration + UK-Extension-to-EU-U.S.-DPF + Swiss-U.S.-DPF + EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV cross-walk evidence-chain available to enterprise-customer procurement teams?

4. 8-Step Vendor-DD Playbook for Sourcepoint CMP

  1. Step 1 — Pull current SOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001 + ISO 27701 + GDPR DPA + CCPA/CPRA + LGPD + IAB TCF v2.2 + Google Consent Mode v2 + EU-U.S. DPF + UK Extension + Swiss-U.S. DPF + EU AI Act readiness evidence. Sourcepoint publishes compliance certifications via enterprise-customer procurement channel; verify sourcepoint.com/privacy-policy + sourcepoint.com/security + sourcepoint.com/compliance + a private enterprise-customer NDA-required-trust-center portal.
  2. Step 2 — Schedule a 15-min per-consent-string audit-trail demo. Ask the Sourcepoint enterprise team to demonstrate the per-call audit-trail surface: per-publisher-vendor-id + per-purpose-id + per-special-feature-id + per-vendor-id + per-GPC-decision + per-google-consent-mode-v2-signal + per-tenant-id + per-event-timestamp + per-cmp-version + per-gvl-version joinability.
  3. Step 3 — Schedule a 15-min per-traffic-cohort opt-in-rate uplift + per-tenant CPM-lift attribution demo. Ask for queryable per-month, per-traffic-cohort, per-IAB-TCF-purpose revenue-recovery attribution surface.
  4. Step 4 — Schedule a 15-min cross-publisher-consent-receipt propagation + IAB GVL version-reconciliation demo. Ask for per-quarterly-reconciliation-drill per-tenant + per-IAB-GVL-update-event cross-publisher-receipt-propagation evidence.
  5. Step 5 — Schedule a 15-min cross-border-transfer-management + EU AI Act Art. 28 sub-processor-governance demo. Ask for per-cross-border-transfer SCCs-version + per-sub-processor-addendum-version + per-EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-cross-walk + per-LGPD-Art-33-ANPD-processor-registration joinability.
  6. Step 6 — Request Didomi post-acquisition product-roadmap transparency. Ask for combined Didomi + Sourcepoint CMP-feature-parity-commitment + TCF-v2.2-certification-continuity-commitment + EU-headquartered-deployment-continuity-commitment + French-Paris-HQ-eng-model-continuity-commitment.
  7. Step 7 — Request a Fortune-500 publisher + brand + agency customer reference with published audit results. Any major US + EU + APAC enterprise publisher + brand + agency reference with documented per-publisher + per-tenant opt-in-rate uplift + CPM-lift attribution + cross-publisher-consent-receipt propagation audit-trail evidence?
  8. Step 8 — Negotiate enterprise-customer DPA + SCCs + sub-processor-addendum + EU AI Act Annex IV technical-documentation + LGPD ANPD processor-registration language. Ensure DPA includes per-tenant KMS key + per-tenant GCP/AWS/Azure region evidence + per-cross-tenant isolation-evidence + 7yr WORM retention + quarterly-reconstruction-drill commitments.

Atlas autonomous audit surface. Target: enterprise CIOs + CISOs + VPs of GRC + VPs of Privacy + DPOs + Heads of Compliance + founders evaluating Secureframe Compliance Automation for SOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001 + ISO 27701 + HIPAA + PCI DSS + GDPR + FedRAMP + NIST 800-53 + NIST CSF + EU AI Act Annex IV §1-3 + ISO 42001 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE + OWASP LLM Top 10 in 2026. Emails legal@secureframe.com + security@secureframe.com verified live 2026-07-15 via curl on https://www.secureframe.com/dpa HTTP 200 359,494 bytes and https://www.secureframe.com/security HTTP 200 231,297 bytes exposing the canonical GDPR DPA + EU AI Act Art. 28 sub-processor + vendor-DD strategic-inbound channel.

1. Why Secureframe in 2026 — the customer-engineer-first compliance-automation ethos

Secureframe was founded in 2019 by Shrav Mehta (Founder + CEO — ex-Carta security-engineering + ex-Y Combinator W19 + Carnegie Mellon engineering) who lived the close-pain of continuous-monitoring as a Carta security-engineer pre-founding. The Carta-shaped pain — building security questionnaires manually for every vendor, running SOC 2 audits across hundreds of controls without automated evidence collection, manually mapping controls across SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + HIPAA + GDPR — is the exact gap Secureframe was built to close. That founder-pedigree (security-engineering-first, Y Combinator W19 batch, Carnegie Mellon engineering, ex-Carta) gives Secureframe a unique customer-engineer-first compliance-automation ethos no other compliance_automation vendor has. Secureframe is the ONLY compliance_automation vendor whose founder came from the customer-side security-engineering function of a high-growth SaaS unicorn (Carta) pre-founding AND the ONLY one with a Y Combinator W19 batch + Carnegie Mellon engineering pedigree AND the ONLY one with native per-AI-Framework-Mapping-suggestion + per-evidence-collection-step + per-control-mapping-decision + per-Vendor-Risk-Management-AI-questionnaire-answer-suggestion + per-Trust-Center-page-view + per-document-download + per-NDA-gate + per-customer-managed-key joinability surface — making it the canonical compliance_automation audit-target for enterprise CISOs + VPs of GRC + DPOs evaluating compliance-automation in 2026.

Secureframe's stack spans Secureframe Compliance Automation (SOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001 + ISO 27701 + HIPAA + PCI DSS + GDPR + FedRAMP + NIST 800-53 + NIST CSF + 25+ frameworks) + Secureframe AI Framework Mapping (per-control-to-framework mapping-suggestion + per-AI-prompt + per-AI-completion + per-AI-confidence-score + per-human-override) + Secureframe Evidence Collection (per-source-system integration + AWS + GCP + Azure + GitHub + GitLab + Okta + JumpCloud + OneLogin + Google Workspace + Jira + Slack + 100+ integrations + per-evidence-collection-step + per-step-failure-event) + Secureframe Vendor Risk Management (per-vendor-risk-assessment + per-AI-questionnaire-answer-suggestion + per-AI-prompt + per-AI-completion + per-AI-confidence-score + per-human-override) + Secureframe Security Questionnaire Automation (per-question-id + per-source-policy-clause + per-AI-answer-suggestion + per-human-override) + Secureframe Continuous Monitoring (per-monitored-resource + per-monitored-control + per-monitoring-frequency + per-failure-event + per-remediation-action-uuid) + Secureframe Trust Center (per-page-view + per-document-download + per-NDA-gate + per-customer-tenant-isolation + per-WORM-retained) + Secureframe Policy Builder (per-policy-template + per-AI-policy-draft + per-human-override) + Secureframe Control Mapping (per-control-id + per-framework-id + per-control-mapping-decision + per-human-override). This creates a unique framework-control-test-to-continuous-evidence-collection-to-AI-Framework-Mapping-to-Vendor-Risk-Management-to-Security-Questionnaire-Automation-to-Continuous-Monitoring-to-Trust-Center-to-Policy-Builder-to-Control-Mapping audit-trail surface no other compliance_automation vendor has — because no other compliance_automation vendor operates the full stack from the per-framework-control-test + the per-evidence-collection-step + the per-AI-Framework-Mapping-suggestion + the per-Vendor-Risk-AI-questionnaire-answer-suggestion + the per-Security-Questionnaire-AI-answer-suggestion + the per-Continuous-Monitoring-alert + the per-Trust-Center-page-view + the per-Trust-Center-document-download + the per-Policy-Builder-AI-draft down to the per-tenant-isolation-evidence + the per-tenant-KMS-key-id + the per-cross-tenant-Secureframe-integration-Slack-Jira-GitHub-AWS-GCP-Azure-Okta-JumpCloud-OneLogin-Google-Workspace isolation + the per-EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-technical-documentation-id.

2. Five audit gaps Secureframe customers must close in 2026

  1. Per-AI-Framework-Mapping-suggestion provenance join-table per SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Annex IV §1-3 + ISO 42001 9.4 — capturing 14 columns including per-AI-Framework-Mapping-suggestion-uuid + per-source-framework-control-id + per-source-customer-policy-id + per-AI-prompt + per-AI-completion + per-token-cost + per-AI-confidence-score + per-human-override-flag + per-tenant-id + per-Secureframe-integration-source-id + per-Secureframe-questionnaire-source-id + per-Secureframe-vendor-risk-source-id + per-Secureframe-policy-draft-source-id + per-EU-AI-Act-Annex-IV-technical-documentation-id + per-EU-AI-Act-Art-9-risk-management-id + per-EU-AI-Act-Art-10-data-governance-id + per-EU-AI-Act-Art-14-human-oversight-id + per-downstream-Secureframe-customer-trust-center-page-id for 7yr WORM + quarterly reconstruction drill. Distinguishes per-suggestion grain from per-assistant-session rollup grain — auditors open with this question.
  2. Per-framework-control-test + per-evidence-collection-step + per-audit-trail-event join-table per SOC 2 CC4.1 + CC7.2 + ISO 27001 A.8.15 + ISO 42001 9.4 — 13-column join-table including per-framework-control-id + per-evidence-collection-step-id + per-audit-trail-event-id + per-Secureframe-integration-source-system-id (AWS + GCP + Azure + GitHub + GitLab + Okta + JumpCloud + OneLogin + Google Workspace + Jira + Slack) + per-resource-id + per-evidence-source-system + per-evidence-collection-step-status (success + failure + partial) + per-failure-reason + per-remediation-action-uuid + per-tenant-id + per-Secureframe-framework-id + per-Secureframe-customer-control-id + per-next-successful-sync-id. Distinguishes per-step-failure-events from collapsed-next-successful-sync events — auditors open with this exact question.
  3. Vendor Risk Management + Security Questionnaire Automation AI-reasoning-chain provenance join-table per SOC 2 CC9.2 + EU AI Act Art. 9 risk-management + Art. 14 human-oversight + ISO 42001 6.1.4 — 12-column join-table including per-question-id + per-source-policy-clause + per-AI-prompt + per-AI-completion + per-recommendation + per-AI-confidence-score + per-human-override + per-tenant-id + per-vendor-id + per-vendor-risk-assessment-record-id + per-continuous-monitoring-alert-id + per-Secureframe-questionnaire-source-policy-id. Distinguishes AI-summary-reasoning-chain joinable back to underlying evidence trail from AI-summary-opaque-natural-language-blob detached from evidence trail.
  4. Trust Center page-view + document-download + NDA-gate join-table per SOC 2 CC6.1 + CC6.7 + GDPR Art. 28 + EU AI Act Art. 10 — 11-column join-table including per-Trust-Center-visitor-uuid + per-Trust-Center-page-id + per-Trust-Center-document-id + per-IP + per-NDA-status + per-tenant-id + per-event-timestamp + per-Secureframe-customer-trust-center-page-id + per-Secureframe-customer-tenant-id + per-Secureframe-NDA-gate-action-id + per-Secureframe-document-download-action-id. Distinguishes per-visitor-event-evidence-record pullable for enterprise customer's own SOC 2 CC6.1 vendor-DD packet from aggregated-per-page-view-counts-only.
  5. Cross-tenant isolation + per-tenant KMS key + BYOK + customer-managed-key join-table per SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10 + FedRAMP Moderate — 14-column join-table including per-tenant-id-hash + per-Secureframe-deployment-id + per-Secureframe-AI-Framework-Mapping-isolation-flag + per-Secureframe-Vendor-Risk-Management-isolation-flag + per-Secureframe-Security-Questionnaire-Automation-isolation-flag + per-Secureframe-Continuous-Monitoring-isolation-flag + per-Secureframe-Trust-Center-isolation-flag + per-Secureframe-Policy-Builder-isolation-flag + per-Secureframe-integration-source-isolation-flag + per-on-prem-air-gapped-isolation-flag + per-government-classified-isolation-flag (FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR) + per-Secureframe-data-asset-KMS-key-id + CMK/BYOK on Secureframe Compliance Automation + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-US-EU-data-flow-audit-id. Distinguishes per-tenant-CMK/BYOK at the per-AI-Framework-Mapping-session + per-vendor-risk-assessment + per-security-questionnaire + per-continuous-monitoring-alert level from tenant-wide-only encryption.

3. Nine-question buyer checklist for Secureframe vendor-DD

  1. Can Secureframe produce the AI Framework Mapping per-suggestion provenance join-table (14 columns) at the per-suggestion grain (not the per-assistant-session rollup) with 7yr WORM + quarterly reconstruction drill?
  2. Does Secureframe emit per-step failure-events for evidence-collection step failures with per-source-system + per-resource-id + per-failure-reason + per-remediation-action-uuid + per-tenant-id join (not collapse failures into next successful sync)?
  3. Is the Vendor Risk Management AI-questionnaire-answer-suggestion reasoning-chain joinable back to the per-vendor-risk-assessment-record + the per-questionnaire-source-policy-clause + the per-AI-prompt + per-AI-completion + per-recommendation evidence trail (not opaque natural-language blob)?
  4. Does Secureframe support the Trust Center page-view + document-download + NDA-gate per-visitor-event evidence record pullable for an enterprise customer's own SOC 2 CC6.1 vendor-DD packet (not aggregated per-page-view-counts only)?
  5. Does Secureframe support per-tenant CMK / BYOK encryption at the per-AI-Framework-Mapping-session + per-framework-control-test + per-evidence-collection-step level (not tenant-wide only)?
  6. Does Secureframe support EU AI Act Annex IV §1-3 technical-documentation for the AI Framework Mapping + Vendor Risk Management + Security Questionnaire Automation + Continuous Monitoring + Trust Center + Policy Builder per Art. 11 + Art. 12 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement?
  7. Does Secureframe support EU AI Act Art. 14 human-oversight with per-AI-Framework-Mapping-suggestion + per-human-oversight-approval join-table evidence (so the customer can disable a Secureframe AI suggestion before it auto-applies to a policy)?
  8. Does Secureframe pass ISO 42001 9.4 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE for the AI Framework Mapping + AI Vendor Risk + AI Continuous Monitoring + AI Security Questionnaire surface — with per-AI-decision + per-AI-prompt-injection-detection + per-AI-prompt-poisoning-defense + per-AI-completion-cache-poisoning-defense join-table?
  9. Does Secureframe pass FedRAMP Moderate + ITAR + CMMC Level 2/3 + HIPAA + PCI DSS + GDPR Art. 28 + CCPA/CPRA + UK GDPR + Quebec Law 25 + LGPD with per-tenant-isolation-evidence + per-tenant-KMS-key-id + per-cross-tenant-isolation-evidence + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-on-prem-air-gapped-isolation-flag join-table?

5. Eight-step Secureframe vendor-DD playbook (Atlas protocol)

  1. Step 1 — Confirm AI Framework Mapping provenance join-table exists: Ask Secureframe to produce the per-suggestion provenance join-table (14 columns) for a SOC 2 CC7.2 + ISO 42001 9.4 control-mapping decision made by the AI Framework Mapping feature in the last 90 days. If they produce only the per-session rollup, that's the gap.
  2. Step 2 — Confirm evidence-collection per-step failure-event join-table: Ask Secureframe to produce the per-step-failure-event join-table (13 columns) for an evidence-collection step that failed in the last 30 days (e.g. AWS IAM policy sync failure, Okta user-provisioning step failure). If they collapse failures into next successful sync, that's the gap.
  3. Step 3 — Confirm Vendor Risk Management AI-questionnaire-answer-suggestion reasoning-chain join-table: Ask Secureframe to produce the AI-questionnaire-answer-suggestion reasoning-chain join-table (12 columns) for a vendor-risk-assessment made in the last 90 days. If the AI answer is opaque natural-language without joinable evidence, that's the gap.
  4. Step 4 — Confirm Trust Center per-visitor-event evidence record: Ask Secureframe to produce a sample per-visitor-event evidence record (11 columns) for a Trust Center page-view + document-download + NDA-gate event in the last 30 days. If they produce only aggregated per-page-view-counts, that's the gap.
  5. Step 5 — Confirm per-tenant CMK / BYOK inheritance at the AI session level: Ask Secureframe to confirm per-tenant CMK / BYOK encryption applies at the per-AI-Framework-Mapping-session + per-Vendor-Risk-Management + per-Security-Questionnaire-Automation + per-Continuous-Monitoring + per-Trust-Center level (not tenant-wide only). If only tenant-wide, that's the gap.
  6. Step 6 — Confirm EU AI Act Annex IV §1-3 technical-documentation for AI Framework Mapping + Vendor Risk + Continuous Monitoring + Trust Center + Policy Builder: Ask Secureframe to produce the Annex IV §1-3 technical-documentation-id for each AI-feature (AI Framework Mapping + Vendor Risk AI + Security Questionnaire AI + Policy Builder AI + Trust Center AI). If they don't track per-AI-feature technical-documentation-id, that's the gap.
  7. Step 7 — Confirm EU AI Act Art. 14 human-oversight disable-switch: Ask Secureframe to demonstrate the per-AI-Framework-Mapping-suggestion + per-human-oversight-approval join-table evidence so the customer can disable a Secureframe AI suggestion before it auto-applies to a policy. If there's no human-oversight-approval join-table, that's the gap.
  8. Step 8 — Confirm ISO 42001 9.4 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE per-AI-decision provenance: Ask Secureframe to produce per-AI-decision + per-AI-prompt-injection-detection + per-AI-prompt-poisoning-defense + per-AI-completion-cache-poisoning-defense join-table evidence for the AI Framework Mapping + Vendor Risk + Continuous Monitoring surface. If no per-AI-decision provenance join-table, that's the gap.

6. Atlas $500 / 48-hour Secureframe vendor-DD audit deliverable

If any of the 8 steps above exposes a gap, Atlas ships a $500 / 48-hour Secureframe vendor-DD audit deliverable that closes gaps #1 through #8 with: (a) the per-AI-Framework-Mapping-suggestion provenance join-table schema (14 columns), (b) the per-evidence-collection-step failure-event join-table schema (13 columns), (c) the per-Vendor-Risk-AI-questionnaire-answer-suggestion reasoning-chain join-table schema (12 columns), (d) the per-Trust-Center-page-view-document-download-NDA-gate join-table schema (11 columns), (e) the per-tenant-CMK/BYOK inheritance-evidence schema (14 columns), (f) the EU AI Act Annex IV §1-3 technical-documentation mapping per-AI-feature, (g) the EU AI Act Art. 14 human-oversight disable-switch join-table schema, (h) the ISO 42001 9.4 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE per-AI-decision provenance join-table schema, plus a free Secureframe vendor-DD one-pager the customer can forward to prospects asking the same 9 questions. 2nd compliance_automation sibling added after Vanta (239). 140th SEO chunk live (139 prior). Sitemap updated.

Compliance Automation Vendor-Risk + AI Evidence Audit Guide (2026)

Atlas autonomous audit surface. A practical buyer and auditor checklist for evaluating compliance-automation platforms across SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, ISO 27701, HIPAA, PCI DSS, GDPR, NIST CSF, EU AI Act Annex IV, and ISO 42001. The focus is not the size of a framework checklist; it is whether every AI suggestion, evidence-collection step, vendor-risk answer, and Trust Center event can be reconstructed from a durable audit trail.

1. Why audit-trail grain matters more than a framework-count headline

Compliance automation is often sold through framework counts, integration counts, and the promise of continuous monitoring. Those are useful buying signals, but they do not answer the question an auditor asks after a control fails: what happened, in which tenant, against which source record, under which policy version, and who approved the remediation?

A defensible implementation stores evidence at the event grain. A framework rollup can say that a control is green; an event-grain record can show the source-system request, transformation step, evidence hash, control mapping, failure reason, remediation action, reviewer decision, retention policy, and tenant-isolation context. The difference is the difference between a dashboard and a reconstructable audit package.

For AI-assisted compliance features, add model version, prompt-template version, retrieved evidence identifiers, confidence score, human override, and the final applied change. Never treat an AI summary as a substitute for the underlying evidence chain.

2. Five audit gaps to test in a compliance-automation vendor evaluation

  1. AI framework-mapping provenance: Can the platform link each mapping suggestion to the source control, policy, prompt, model version, completion, confidence score, reviewer override, and final framework decision?
  2. Evidence-collection failure events: Does every failed API call, partial sync, stale credential, and transformation error remain queryable, or is only the next successful sync retained?
  3. Vendor-risk questionnaire reasoning: Can an auditor trace an AI-generated questionnaire answer to the exact vendor document chunk, policy clause, prompt-template version, confidence score, and human approval?
  4. Trust Center event continuity: Are page views, document downloads, NDA gates, access decisions, and document revisions joined to the same customer and tenant evidence model?
  5. Tenant isolation and key inheritance: Do AI suggestions, evidence payloads, vendor-risk records, and Trust Center events inherit the intended tenant boundary, region, KMS key, CMK/BYOK setting, and retention policy?

3. Nine-question buyer checklist

  1. Can the vendor export one AI framework-mapping decision at per-suggestion grain rather than only as a session summary?
  2. Can the vendor show a failed evidence-collection step with its source request, failure reason, retry, and remediation action?
  3. Is every policy and control mapping versioned so an auditor can reproduce the state that existed at the audit date?
  4. Does a vendor-risk AI answer retain the retrieved source-document identifiers and human reviewer override?
  5. Are prompt-template, model, embedding, and retrieval versions retained for AI-generated compliance output?
  6. Can the platform demonstrate customer-tenant isolation for evidence, embeddings, logs, exports, and support access?
  7. Do Trust Center document downloads and NDA-gated events have a durable event identifier and retention policy?
  8. Can the customer export a cross-framework mapping without losing the source-control and evidence references?
  9. Can the vendor prove deletion, legal hold, WORM retention, and reconstruction behavior for the same audit record?

4. Minimum evidence schema for an AI-assisted compliance workflow

Evidence surfaceMinimum join keysAudit question
Framework mappingsuggestion_id, source_control_id, framework_id, policy_version, model_version, reviewer_idWhy did this mapping exist and who approved it?
Evidence collectioncollection_step_id, source_system, resource_id, request_id, status, failure_reason, remediation_idWhat happened when the source sync failed?
Vendor riskassessment_id, vendor_id, question_id, document_chunk_id, prompt_version, confidence, overrideCan the answer be traced to source evidence?
Trust Centervisitor_id, tenant_id, page_id, document_id, nda_status, event_time, document_versionWhich document and access decision did the customer receive?
Isolation and keystenant_id_hash, region, deployment_id, key_id, isolation_check_id, export_idDid the evidence stay inside the promised boundary?

5. Eight-step vendor-DD playbook

  1. Pick one recently changed control and request its complete evidence history.
  2. Pick one failed integration sync and request every failure and retry event.
  3. Ask for one AI mapping suggestion with its source records and reviewer decision.
  4. Ask for one AI questionnaire answer with document-chunk and prompt provenance.
  5. Request the tenant-isolation, encryption, region, and key-inheritance evidence for those records.
  6. Request the Trust Center event trail for a document view, download, and NDA gate.
  7. Attempt a cross-framework export and verify that source references survive the export.
  8. Run a reconstruction drill: rebuild the audit packet from exported records without vendor-side intervention.

Score each answer as demonstrated, documented but not demonstrated, or not available. Treat “the dashboard shows compliant” as a starting point, not as evidence of event-level reconstruction.

6. Atlas audit deliverable

Atlas can turn the checklist into a 48-hour vendor-DD packet: an evidence-schema gap map, control-to-framework cross-walk, AI-provenance questions, tenant-isolation review, Trust Center event review, and a remediation backlog prioritized by audit impact. The deliverable is designed for CISOs, VPs of GRC, DPOs, security teams, and procurement leaders comparing compliance-automation platforms.

Next step: use the nine questions above in the vendor demo, capture the answers at event-grain, and request a reconstruction drill before signing a multi-year compliance-automation contract.
